question: How can I add new patients to E-Prescribe?
context:
[Article: Add New Patients to E-Prescribe | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41122742911259-Add-New-Patients-to-E-Prescribe]
Before you can prescribe medications through E-Prescribe, each patient must have a complete profile that meets prescription requirements. This includes key details such as their name, date of birth, gender, address, and at least one phone number. If any of this information is missing, you’ll be prompted to fill it in before continuing. Once a patient’s profile is linked with ScriptSure, you’ll be able to prescribe medications and view up to 12 months of their filled medication history directly in Vagaro. Prerequisites : This feature requires E-Prescribe . Add Patient to E-Prescribe To prescribe medication to your patients, you'll need to create a customer profile for them in Vagaro and then add them to E-Prescribe. To connect an existing profile to E-Prescribe, additional details may be required: First Name , Last Name , Birthday , Gender , Address , and at least one phone number. If an existing profile does not have sufficient information, you will be prompted to enter additional details when going to prescribe to your customers. Keep in mind that when you're creating new patient profiles in the future, you should have this required information entered beforehand so you don't need to later on. To add a patient to E-Prescribe Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab. Select Start Prescribing . If the patient is missing information from their profile, you will be prompted to enter additional details. If the patient's profile matches multiple patients in ScriptSure, you will be prompted to select the correct patients and link their Vagaro profile with ScriptSure. You have connected the customer's profile with ScriptSure, and you can now begin prescribing. Doing this also allows you to access up to 12 months of the patient's filled medication history. From the E-Prescribe tab in the customer's profile, you can manage the following: Prescriptions : View your patient's prescription history and prescribe new medication. Allergies : View your patient's allergies and add new ones. Pharmacy : View and add your patient's preferred pharmacies. Diagnosis : View and add any diagnoses for your patient. Vitals : View and write vitals for your patient. Remove Patient from E-Prescribe To remove a patient from E-Prescribe Go to Customers . Select the patient's profile. Go to the E-Prescribe tab. In the bottom-left corner, select Disconnect Customer . Acknowledge that the customer will no longer be linked with ScriptSure and Vagaro, then select Disconnect .
ground_truth: Create a customer profile for them in vagaro or link and existing profile to them. Ensure they have a first name last name birthday gender address and at least one phone number. Go to customers select a customers and go to the E-Prescribe tab. Select Start prescribing.

question: How do I create a blog post on mysite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Create a Blog Post | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/31549408978331-MySite-Create-a-Blog-Post]
Want to get your point across to your customers but don't have much blog writing experience? To help get your blog started, Vagaro has an embedded AI that can help write your content for you. Select the AI button in the rich text box, choose a Tone , and tell the AI what you want your post to be about.

For example, you could use AI to create a blog post on the benefits of beard oil, so I asked the AI to write me a "two-paragraph post on the benefits of beard oil," it was able to draft up an entire blog post for me formatting and everything! Also, Vagaro's AI is intelligent and knows about your business, so it will craft a post specific to your industry and offerings.

If you already have content written, you can highlight a piece of written content and then ask the AI to rewrite it for you or add more information.

To manage your blog, you can adjust various details, such as the title, author, URL, and summary to ensure your post is clear and engaging. You can also organize content using tags and categories, making it easier for readers to find related topics. Additionally, you can customize the appearance and visibility of elements like images, publication date, and read time to create a polished and professional look for your blog.

To manage your blog settings:

In your new blog post, select Manage Post Settings at the top of the screen. In Post Settings , configure your blog post: Post Name : The title of your post. Post Author : The person who wrote this post. You can leave it anonymous or select an author. You'll need to add a new employee profile to add a new author. URL Slug : The part of your web address that comes after the main website name and helps show what the page is about. Meta Description : A summary of a webpage's content that appears under the title in search engine results, helping users understand what the page is about. Title Tag : The clickable headline shown in search engine results and the browser tab, giving a clear idea of what the webpage is about. Post Tags : The keywords or phrases assigned to a blog post to categorize and organize content, making it easier for readers to find related topics. Select Edit Tags to manage existing tags or add new ones. Post Categories : The broad groupings used to organize blog content by topic or theme, helping readers navigate and explore related articles easily. Select Edit Categories to manage existing categories or add new ones. Select Save . Manage additional settings by selecting the top-most section of the blog post: Layout : Change how your hero image appears on your blog. For example, select Full to have the image cover the entire width of your blog. Hero Image Position : If your image is cut off, use this setting to adjust the vertical position of your hero image. Categories : Show or hide categories in your post. Tags : Show or hide tags in your post. Author Display : Whether to show or hide the author and their profile image. Date : Show or hide the publish date of the post. Read Time : Show or hide the read time of your post. Image Radius : Change the roundness of your image.

Publish your blog post on your website for the world to see. If you're done with your content but don't want to publish it quite yet, schedule it for a later date and time.

To publish or schedule your blog:

In your new blog post, select the Publish Post menu in the top-right corner. Choose to publish or schedule your post: Publish Post : Publish your post. Select Launch Post at the top of the screen to see your new post. Preview : See a preview of your post before publishing. Schedule Post : Select the calendar button to choose when you want your post to be published. Select the Calendar button to enter the date and time. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: select mysite, go to settings, select blogging, select create new post, in the new post textbox enter teh title of your blog post... additional details on editing... once done select publish post on the top right corner.

question: What business categories must be selected to accept FSA and HSA payments when signing up for credit card processing?
context:
[Article: Accept FSA and HSA Payments | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24312682236315-Accept-FSA-and-HSA-Payments]
To take FSA and HSA payments, you must select the Med Spa, Medical Services and Health Practitioners, or Chiropractors, Chiropractic Offices business/industry category when signing up for credit card processing . Email support@vagaro.com to request approval to process FSA/HSA cards if you already have a merchant account. In order for certain healthcare expenses to be approved under HSA/FSA guidelines, the provider must hold a valid, state-board-certified medical license. Acceptable documentation includes a medical license for a Nurse Practitioner (NP), Medical Doctor (MD), Chiropractor, Massage Therapist (MT), or Physician Assistant (PA). In the email, let us know your business type and the business code that applies to you. Med Spa (62139) Medical Services and Health Practitioners/Chiropractors (6211) Note If the provider is not listed as an employee at the business, please include the signed Medical Provider Agreement in the email in order to proceed with the request. The file formats we accept are PDF, JPG, and PNG. Vagaro devices that support HSA/FSA payments: Legacy Credit Card Computer Swiper Legacy EMV Reader and EMV Reader+ PayPro Go If you don't have any of the hardware below, you can manually enter the card number at checkout .
ground_truth: You must select the Med Spa, Medical Services and Health Practitioners, or Chiropractors, Chiropractic Offices business/industry category.

question: What are the four types of components available for building a website in MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Place and Manage Components | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/20043904453915-MySite-Place-and-Manage-Components]
When it comes to building a website, components are the building blocks that make up the visual elements of your site. You have a variety of components to choose from, including image carousels, text blocks, iFrames, and components that sync directly with your Vagaro business. There are four types of components: Text Components : Headers and text blocks on your website. You can customize up to three text blocks and four header styles in your global site stylings . Layout Components : The building blocks of your website; used to organize and separate content and keep your website organized. Element Components : Simple components, such as images, videos, and buttons. Vagaro Components : Sync from your Vagaro business and require no maintenance once added. For instance, if you add the Facility Information Vagaro component, it will appear all the information set in your Vagaro settings exactly as it appears on your Vagaro Listing Page. Any changes you make to the Facility Information within Vagaro will automatically be reflected on your website. Placing or selecting components on the editor will open the style pane and allow you to customize how that component looks. This will only update the look of the component you selected, so we recommend that you change the global site stylings on your website to make changes across your website. Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . The Style Pane Think of the style pane as the "settings" of a component. All components have a preset style applied to them when you first choose your website's style. The style panel allows you to make smaller tweaks to the look and feel of your website. Large-scale changes, like color, should be made in your site style settings. Some components overlap at some points, so it's important to know what component you're editing. You can view what component you selected at the top of the style pane. You can also view the breadcrumbs of the component you're in. Breadcrumbs allow you to keep track of where you are in your page layout. Select a hyperlink in the style pane to go to its parent component. Here's an example of what breadcrumbs look like in the style pane: Section > Column > Container . Note Changes to a component will only affect that specific component, not others of the same type. Place a Component Go to Component Types to view a list of all components. To place a component: Select MySite . Go to the page to be edited. In the left design panel, select the Components tab. A list of all components, including Vagaro components, will appear. Choose the component you want to place. Select and hold the component, then drag it to the MySite editor. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish . Each component can be styled and edited differently. We recommend that you play around with the different components on a hidden page before publishing changes to your live site. Move and Delete Components After building your website, you may want to move around or delete some components. Move Components To move a component: Select MySite . Select MySite . Find and select the component to be moved. Drag and move the component to another place on the page. An indicator appears where the component will be placed. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish . Delete Components To delete a component: Select MySite . Find and select the component to be deleted. Press the Delete key on your keyboard. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: The four types of components are: Text Components (headers and text blocks), Layout Components (to organize and separate content), Element Components (simple components like images, videos, and buttons), and Vagaro Components (sync from your Vagaro business).

question: What options do I have to filter a customer's class attendance history on the Vagaro Pro app?
context:
[Article: View a Customer's Class Attendance History | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360021459773-View-a-Customer-s-Class-Attendance-History]
From a customer's profile, you can view their class attendance history to see how often they attend their classes, which classes they've signed up for, which classes they didn't show up for, and more. You can also process a refund or change the customer's class status from their profile, or if you prefer a copy of this report, you can export their history for your records. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On the Vagaro Pro App On the Vagaro Pro app, a customer's attendance history will appear as a collection of cards. Each card represents one class session that the customer signed up for and will show the attendee's status, time of the class, instructor, class name, and price. To view a customer's class attendance history: At the bottom of the screen, select Customers . In your Customer List, search for and select a customer. Select the Classes tab. In the top-right corner, select the Filters button to filter and search the customer's attendance history. Select Status : Choose the attendee status you want to view. For example you can view each session where the customer Cancelled . Select Class : Choose the specific class you want to view. All Teachers : Choose a specific teacher the customer booked with. Start Date/End Date : Choose the date range you want to view. The system will show all classes scheduled during that range. All Businesses : For multi-location businesses , select another location to see the customer's class history at that business. Select Apply to update the customer's class history, or select Clear to remove all filters. Optionally, select a class session to view more details, like the price, points used or gained, instructor, and so on. After selecting a session, you can: Select View on Calendar to go to the class session on the Calendar. In the top-right corner, select the Action menu to change the customer's status for that class. This will not be available for class sessions that are completed. If the class session has been completed, you can refund the customer by selecting Refund . On a Computer On the web version of Vagaro, a customer's attendance history will appear as a chart, with each row representing one class session for which the customer is registered. Each column will appear additional details about the class session, such as the class's date, the attendee's Status , the class's Price , and so on. To view a customer's class attendance history: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Classes tab. Filter and search through the customer's attendance history, then select Search . In the All Dates list, choose the date range you want to view. The system will show all classes scheduled during that range. In the All Statuses list, choose the attendee status you want to view. For example, you can view each session where the customer Cancelled . In the All Classes list, choose the specific class you want to view. In the All Teachers list, choose a specific teacher the customer booked with. Tip Select Clear to remove all filters. Optionally, in the top-right corner, multi-location businesses can select another location in the All businesses list. Optionally, manage the customer's class session from their profile: Select the Action menu to change the customer's status or view the class Timeline . If the class has been completed, you can Refund the session or view the class Timeline . Select the hyperlinked (blue) text under Class Date to go to the class session on the calendar. Optionally, save a copy of the customer's class history by selecting the Download or Print buttons in the top-right corner.
ground_truth: You can filter a customer's class attendance history by selecting the Filters button and then choosing options for Status, Class, All Teachers, and Start Date/End Date.

question: What happens to my custom designs if I reset my MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Reset Your MySite and Start From Scratch | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26664576284187-MySite-Reset-Your-MySite-and-Start-From-Scratch]
Changing your website layout will reset your MySite. Resetting your MySite will restore any of the default pages, text, and images from when you first completed the MySite wizard. Doing so will remove any configuration you've set up since then, excluding any domains you purchased and connected. Vagaro will not refund any custom designs if you decide to reset your MySite. Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To reset your MySite: Select MySite . In the top-left corner, go to Settings . Select Change Layout . View all the different layouts available. To select a layout, hover over it, then select Change Layout . Select the Pages tab to view changes. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: Vagaro will not refund any custom designs if you decide to reset your MySite.

question: What details can be edited when modifying a class or workshop in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Edit a Class or Workshop | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360009036933-Edit-a-Class-or-Workshop]
When you modify a class or workshop offered on your class menu, you can rename those that were created as custom classes, but you cannot rename those that were created as a recommended Vagaro Class. Classes and workshops that have been scheduled cannot be changed into a workshop, and vice versa. Their other details are still editable.

Note Any price changes will not affect any appointments booked before the price was changed. You will need to manually edit the price of any appointment already booked on your Calendar to update the price.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

Vagaro Pro App To edit a class: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Classes Select the Class to edit, and then select Edit . Edit the class as needed. Class Name and Class Description , and Category . Resource : Any space or equipment limitations required to run the class. Add-Ons Suggested : Class add-ons that you've created that customers may want to include with their class. Tax : Select any taxes applicable to this class. Business Cost : How much it costs your business to run this class, such as supplies, materials, and rent. The cost must be less than what your service providers charge for this class or workshop. Cleanup Time : The amount of time blocked off after the class for the employee to clean up and prepare for the booking on their calendar. This reserved time is useful for back-to-back appointments. Workshop : The cost for customers to attend a class session or a workshop made up of a series of classes. Prices charged must be greater than the Business Cost . Number of Sessions : Applicable only if you'll have than more one session of the same class and offering a price break for customers who attend more than one session. Sell Price and Live Stream Price : The cost of one class session in-house or online and must be greater than the Business Cost . Optionally, select Add Price Break to allow customers to book additional classes at a discounted rate. For example, the cost of one Pilates class could be $30, but you could entice customers to return for multiple sessions by lowering the cost of a class to $25 if they also pay for the next 6 sessions in one transaction. Show Class Online : Whether customers can book this class and see its price on your Vagaro Listing Page. Duration : Amount of time blocked off on the service provider's calendar to teach the class. Points Given : Number of points that a customer earns for attending the class. Points to Redeem : Number of points a customer can redeem instead of cash to attend the class. Add Photo : Optionally, add a photo or an image of the class activity. Select Save .

Web Version To edit a class: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. On the Classes tab, find the class to be edited. Select the service's Action menu, and then select Edit . Update the class's information as needed. Add Photo : Optionally, add a photo or an image of the class activity. Class Name and Class Description , and Category . Resource : Any space or equipment limitations required to run the class. Add-Ons Suggested : Class add-ons that you've created that customers may want to include with their class. Tax : Select any taxes applicable to this class. Business Cost : How much it costs your business to run this class, such as supplies, materials, and rent. The cost must be less than what your service providers charge for this class or workshop. Cleanup Time : The amount of time blocked off after the class for the employee to clean up and prepare for the booking on their calendar. This reserved time is useful for back-to-back appointments. Show Class Online : Whether customers can book this class and see its price on your Vagaro Listing Page. Workshop : The cost for customers to attend a class session or a workshop made up of a series of classes. Prices charged must be greater than the Business Cost . Number of Sessions : Applicable only if you'll have than more one session of the same class and offering a price break for customers who attend more than one session. Sell Price and Live Stream Price : The cost of one class session in-house or online and must be greater than the Business Cost . Optionally, select Add Price Break to allow customers to book additional classes at a discounted rate. For example, the cost of one Pilates class could be $30, but you could entice customers to return for multiple sessions by lowering the cost of a class to $25 if they also pay for the next 6 sessions in one transaction. Select the employee(s) to perform the class. Performed by : Toggle on the switch beside the service provider's name to them to teach the class. Price : The amount charged to the customer for this service provider to teach the class. Duration : Amount of time blocked off on the service provider's calendar to teach the class. Points Given : Number of points that
ground_truth: When modifying a class or workshop in Vagaro, you can edit the class name, class description, category, resources, add-ons suggested, applicable taxes, business cost, cleanup time, whether the class is shown online, workshop cost, number of sessions, sell price, live stream price, duration, points given, points to redeem, and optionally add a photo.

question: What steps should I follow to reply to a customer's review on a phone?
context:
[Article: Respond to Customer Reviews | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4405382006299-Respond-to-Customer-Reviews]
If you reply to a customer's review, your comment will be posted below it and the customer will receive a notification. Customers cannot write additional comments after your reply.

On a Phone To reply to a customer's review: Select More → Reports → Review Management . To filter your report, select Filter , change the following filters as needed, and then select Run Report . Below the customer's comment, enter or generate your response to the customer. To write your own response to the customer, enter your text in the Write a reply box. To auto-generate a response that you can edit or post-as is: Select Reply with AI . Optionally, select a Tone and choose the Length of your reply. The text is regenerated each time to change a setting. You can also select Regenerate to get a different message. Select Previous Prompts to review or go back to a response that was already generated. If you need to refer to the customer's review, select Original Customer Review . Optionally, update the message as needed. We highly recommend that you read the generated response to make sure that the text is appropriate and contains the message and sentiment you want to say. Select Use this Text . Select the Post Response button. Your message is shown below the customer's comment.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To reply to a customer's review: Select Reports → Review Management . Enter or generate a response to the customer. To write your own response to the customer, select Reply , enter your Response to Review . To auto-generate a response that you can edit or post as is: Select Reply with AI . Optionally, select a Tone and choose the Length of your reply. The text is regenerated each time to change a setting. Optionally, update the message as needed. We highly recommend that you read the generated response to make sure that the text is appropriate and contains the message and sentiment you want to say. Select Use this Text . Select Post Response . Your message is shown below the customer's comment.

You can only edit or remove replies that were submitted by you and can be performed only on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro.

To edit your response to a customer:

Select Reports → Review Management . Optionally, at the top of the screen, set the filters for your report, then select Run Report . To edit a response: Select the Action menu in the reply to update, then select Edit . Update or regenerate response as needed. Select Post Response . To delete a response: Select the Action menu in the reply to remove, then select Delete . In the confirmation popup, select Delete .
ground_truth: To reply to a customer's review on a phone, select More → Reports → Review Management. Filter your report as needed and select Run Report. Below the customer's comment, enter or generate your response in the Write a reply box. You can either write your own response or select Reply with AI to auto-generate a response. After reviewing the generated response, select Use this Text and then select the Post Response button to post your message below the customer's comment.

question: What should you do if an attendee doesn't have a profile at your business while registering them for a class?
context:
[Article: Register and Manage Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29078318111387-Register-and-Manage-Class-Attendees]
Customers typically register for classes online through your Vagaro Listing Page . For walk-ins or customers who call to register, you can add them directly to the class from the calendar.

Once registered, each customer will appear in the attendee list. From there, you can quickly see their status, including whether they have checked in, prepaid, confirmed, or been approved. You can also update an attendee's status to remove them from the class or reschedule them for a different session.

Prerequisites : This feature requires a scheduled class on the calendar.

Go to your class session on the calendar to add more attendees.

Adding attendees and checking them out at the same time will only check out one attendee and will not group them together. We recommend adding the attendees and checking them out individually later.

On the Vagaro Pro App To register attendees for a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. If the attendee isn't sure which class they want to book, you can search all your class availability . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Add Attendees . Select the customers to register, then select Next . The class's details will display. Tip If the attendee doesn't have a profile at your business, you can create one for them by selecting + in the top-right corner. Optionally, make sure that the Start Date of the customer's first class is correct. Optionally, add any add-ons and payment information: To save a credit card on file, select the Card button next to the attendee's profile. To charge a deposit, enter a Deposit Due for your attendee. This option will not be available if you register multiple attendees at once. To add an add-on to the attendee's sessions, select + Add-Ons . Add your attendees: To register your attendees and check them out later, select Add Attendees . To register your attendee and prepay for their class, select Add & Checkout . Note You can't check out multiple attendees at the same time.

On a Computer To register attendees for a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. If the attendee isn't sure which class they want to book, you can search all your class availability . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Add Attendees . Select the customers to register, then select Next . Customers already enrolled will have "(enrolled)" next to their profile. If the attendee doesn't have a profile, select Create New to make one for them. Optionally, add any add-ons and payment information: To save a credit card on file, select the Card button next to the attendee's profile. To charge your attendee a deposit, enter a Deposit Due for your attendee. This option will not be available if you register multiple attendees at once. To add an add-on to the attendee's sessions, select + Add-Ons . Optionally, make sure that the Date of the class session is correct. Add your attendees: To register your attendees and check them out later, select Add Attendees . To register your attendee and prepay for their class, select Add & Checkout . Note You can't check out multiple attendees at the same time.
ground_truth: You can create a profile for them by selecting the + in the top-right corner.

question: How do I add additional stock to a partial-use product on a phone?
context:
[Article: Create Partial-Use Products for Backbar Use | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41206452277787-Create-Partial-Use-Products-for-Backbar-Use]
When new stock of a product comes in, you can change the quantity from Inventory Management.

On a Phone To add additional stock: Select More → Settings → Management . From your inventory, select your partial-use product. In the top-right corner, select Action → Add . Scan your products or Add (Containers) Manually . If you scan your product, it'll appear on screen. To add more products, keep scanning them or select Add (Containers) Manually . To change the quantity or business cost, select an item in the list. To remove a product, select X . After adding all products, select Next . If your product has the same barcode when scanned or entered, the Barcode Already Exists popup appears prompting you to increase the quantity of your product. From Barcode Already Exists , select Add Quantity . Enter the date you received this product and the Business Cost for it. Enter the Total Quantity of (Container) you're adding to the stock. Select Add to add the new stock. Select the Date you received this item, then select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add additional stock: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . From your inventory, next to your partial-use product, select Action → Add . Scan your products or Add (Containers) Manually . If you scan your product, it'll appear on screen. To add more products, keep scanning them or select Add (Containers) Manually . To edit a product, select Action → Edit . To remove a product, select Action → Remove If your product has the same barcode when scanned or entered, the Barcode Already Exists popup appears prompting you to increase the quantity of your product. From Barcode Already Exists , select Add Quantity . Enter the date you received this product and the Business Cost for it. Enter the Total Quantity of (Container) you're adding to the stock. Select Add to add the new stock.

If you enter an expiration date for a partial-use product, a Products to Discard notification will appear in the Notification Panel once the item expires. From there, you can view all expired products and discard them directly from Inventory Management.

On a Phone To discard an expired product: Select More → Settings → Management . Select the product to be discarded, then in the top-right corner, select Action → Discard Product . Choose the Date the product was discarded. The Quantity will automatically be set to the entire quantity of the product. Select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To discard an expired product: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . Next to the product to be discarded, select Action → Discard Product . From Subtract , enter the Date you discarded the product. The Quantity will automatically be set to the entire quantity of the product. Select Save .

To add a partial-use product to your service, you can select the product at checkout.

On a Phone To add an item at checkout: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select Select Customer , then choose a customers that's In Today or select from All Customers . If the customer is a walk-in, manually add items to the cart. Select Shopping Cart → Add Item → Product . For the partial-use product, select Right-Arrow > to view all items associated with the product. Select the item you plan to use for this service or class, then select Add (number of) Products . The partial-use product will be added to the customer's cart. Optionally, to change how much product you're using, swipe left on the item, then select - or + . Select Done , then check out your customer. The quantity of the product will be updated.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add an item at checkout: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select Select Customer , then choose a customers that's In Today or select from All Customers . If the customer is a walk-in, manually add items to the cart. At the bottom of the screen, select Product . For the partial-use product, select Right-Arrow > to view all items associated with the product. Select the item you plan to use for this service or class, then select Add Product . The partial-use product will be added to the customer's cart. Optionally, to change how much product you're using, change the quantity ( Qty ). Check out your customer. The quantity of the product will be updated.
ground_truth: To add additional stock on a phone, select More → Settings → Management, then choose your partial-use product from the inventory. In the top-right corner, select Action → Add. Scan your products or select Add (Containers) Manually. After adding all products, select Next. If the Barcode Already Exists popup appears, select Add Quantity, enter the date received, Business Cost, and Total Quantity, then select Add to add the new stock.

question: What criteria does Vagaro use to automatically delete customer profiles?
context:
[Article: Merge and Clean Up Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204348140-Merge-and-Clean-Up-Customers]
Turn on Auto-Cleanup Customers to let Vagaro remove customer profiles that are missing contact details or inactive for a while. This runs in the background, so you can keep running your business.

To protect important records, Vagaro won't remove a customer who has any of the following:

Future appointments Future classes or workshop sessions Gift cards that are not expired or have a balance Memberships or packages (active or inactive) Purchased products from your business Outstanding IOUs and invoices Customer notes

On a Phone To set up automatic deletion of unwanted customer profiles: Select More → Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Turn on Auto-Cleanup Customers . Select the checkboxes for the criteria Vagaro should use to remove customer profiles. Customers without an email Customers without a phone number Customers without a card on file Customers who haven't visited within a selected period Note Vagaro only removes a profile that matches every selected criterion. Vagaro removes matching customers within four hours. The {Number} Customers Removed by Auto-Cleanup section appears under the setting, where you can review or restore the removed profiles.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To set up automatic deletion of unwanted customer profiles: Select Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Turn on Auto-Cleanup Customers . Select the checkboxes for the criteria Vagaro should use to remove customer profiles. Customers without an email Customers without a phone number Customers without a card on file Customers who haven't visited within a selected period Note Vagaro only removes a profile that matches every selected criterion. Vagaro removes matching customers within four hours. The {Number} Customers Removed by Auto-Cleanup section appears under the setting, where you can review or restore the removed profiles.

You can restore customer profiles that you manually removed or that Auto-Cleanup Customers removed automatically.

Note To restore removed customer profiles, Auto-Cleanup Customers must be turned on. Turn it back on if you turned it off after the profiles were removed.

On a Phone To restore deleted customer profiles: Select More → Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Under {Number} Customers Removed by Auto-Cleanup , select View Details . Choose which customers to restore: To restore a specific set of customers, select the checkbox for each customer. To restore all customers, select the checkbox next to the search box. Select Restore Customer Profile .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To restore deleted customer profiles: Select Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Under {Number} Customers Removed by Auto-Cleanup , select View Details . Choose which customers to restore: To restore a specific set of customers, select the checkbox for each customer. To restore all customers, select the checkbox next to Customer . Select Restore Customer Profile .
ground_truth: Vagaro uses the following criteria to automatically delete customer profiles: customers without an email, customers without a phone number, customers without a card on file, and customers who haven't visited within a selected period. Vagaro only removes a profile that matches every selected criterion.

question: How can I undo a check-in at the kiosk if I accidentally checked in for the wrong appointment?
context:
[Article: Self Check-In at a Business - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003955413-Self-Check-In-at-a-Business-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
Depending on the business, you may still be able to check in at a kiosk after an appointment has started. For classes, you may even be able to check in even after the class has ended.

To check in for a service or class:

On the kiosk's home screen, scan your QR code or membership card, or select Check in to log in to your Vagaro account. In the list of your appointments, select the Check In button for the one you're checking in for. Classes are identified with the Class icon. If you have multiple appointments or classes that can be checked in today, toggle on their switches to select them, then select Check In Select Done on the confirmation screen.

Undo a Check-In If you accidentally checked in for an appointment, you can undo it and check-in again later at the correct time. To undo a check-in: On the kiosk's home screen, select Check in to sign into your Vagaro account . In your list of appointments, select the More menu beside the appointment or class that was checked in, then select Undo Check-In . The button changes to Undo Check-In . If you have multiple check-ins to undo, toggle on their switches to select them, then select Undo Check In . Select Close or Check-In . Select Done on the confirmation screen.

Cancel an Appointment Depending on the business's cancellation policy, your appointment or class session can be canceled at a kiosk before they begin. If your appointment was already checked in, you'll have to undo the check-in before you can cancel it. To cancel an appointment at a kiosk: On the kiosk's home screen, select Check in to sign into your Vagaro account . If you have any checked-in appointments, undo their check-ins by selecting their More button, then select Undo Check In . Select the More button next to an appointment or class not checked in. The Check In button changes to Cancel Appointment . Select Cancel Class or Cancel Appointment beside the appointment. If you have multiple check-ins to undo, toggle on their switches to select them. Select Close or Cancel . Select Done on the confirmation screen.
ground_truth: To undo a check-in at the kiosk, select Check in to sign into your Vagaro account. In your list of appointments, select the More menu beside the appointment or class that was checked in, then select Undo Check-In. If you have multiple check-ins to undo, toggle on their switches to select them, then select Undo Check In. Finally, select Close or Check-In and select Done on the confirmation screen.

question: What happens if I have only one Vagaro license regarding the business hours setup?
context:
[Article: Set Up Your Business Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977243390491-Set-Up-Your-Business-Profile]
for Navigation App Directions : Toggle if you want your customers' navigation apps to use the address you entered (instead of the map marker, if you moved the pin). Specify what days and times your business is open and closed: Note This toggle does not appear if you have only one Vagaro license, which is usually the business owner's. Instead, the working hours entered in the business owner's Employee Profile screen are automatically used. Business Hours (disables “Use Employee Working Hours”): Show the days and times that your business is open, regardless of when your employees are working. Toggle the Day of the Week and then enter the Opens at and Closes at times. Select Copy to All to copy the same times to every day of the week, and make minor changes, such as closing earlier on the weekend. Use Employee Working Hours (disables "Business Hours") : Shows the business's opening and closing hours based on employees working hours. For example, if one employee works on Mondays and Wednesdays from 8 AM to 2 PM, and another employee works Monday to Friday from 11 AM to 4 PM, then your opening hours would be Mondays to Fridays from 8 AM to 4 PM. Select Save . Optionally, visit your Vagaro Listing Page to preview what your customers will see, and update your information as needed.
ground_truth: If you have only one Vagaro license, which is usually the business owner's, the toggle for specifying business hours does not appear. Instead, the working hours entered in the business owner's Employee Profile screen are automatically used.

question: What steps do I need to follow to add a Vagaro Service to my service menu on a phone?
context:
[Article: Add a Vagaro Service | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977243959067-Add-a-Vagaro-Service]
When you add a Vagaro Service to your menu, you're also defining your business type with keywords in their names and descriptions. These keywords help customers find you and your services more easily in the Vagaro Marketplace, and you'll only have to update or add more details. Alternatively, you can set up custom services if you can't find a suitable Vagaro Service or want a more creative name. Sell your services by themselves, in a bundle , as a package , and in your memberships both in-house and on your Vagaro Listing Page . While managing your menu, you can search your services by entering part or all of a word to see exact and partial matches at the top of the left-side menu, then selecting the one you want to update. Alternatively, press Ctrl +K on a PC, or press Command + K on a Mac to move your cursor to the Search bar. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To add a Vagaro Service to your service menu: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Services to view your service menu. At the top of the screen, verify that the Service/Class Menu is being created for the correct employee, select the Filters button to choose another employee, or select the shown name to change it to another employee. At the bottom of the screen, select Add , and then select Service . Select Vagaro Service . Alternatively, select Custom Service to use a different name. Scroll through the list of services, select the one you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the services' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the service's category, select the one you just added. Provide the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your service after you have completed setting up your service menu): Service Name : The name of the service. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Service because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the service offers and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Service because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Sell Price : The cost of the service if performed by that service provider. Duration : The amount of time to block off on the service provider's calendar to perform that service. Select Save . To rearrange the order of the services or change the category that they belong in, drag and drop the service's handle button into place. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a Vagaro Service to your service menu: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. On the Services tab, select Add , and then select Service . Select Vagaro Service . Alternatively, select Custom Service to use a different name. Scroll through the list of services, select the one you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the services' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the service's category, select the one you just added. Provide the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your service after you have completed setting up your service menu): Title : The name of the service. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Service because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the service offers and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Service because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Select the employee(s) to perform the service. Performed By : Toggle on the switch beside the employee(s) who will perform the service. Price : How much the service costs to be performed by the service provider. The price charged must be greater than the Business Cost . Duration : The amount of time to block off on the service provider's calendar to perform that service. Select Save . Repeat steps 2 to 8 to update your other services. To rearrange the order of the services or change their categories on your Vagaro Listing Page , drag them to their new place.
ground_truth: To add a Vagaro Service to your service menu on a phone, select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu. Then select Services to view your service menu. Verify that the Service/Class Menu is for the correct employee, select Add, and then select Service. Choose Vagaro Service or Custom Service, scroll through the list of services, select the one you want to add, and then select Save.

question: What are the steps to create a compound medication in E-Prescribe?
context:
[Article: Set Up and Prescribe a Compound Medication | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41163913325979-Set-Up-and-Prescribe-a-Compound-Medication]
E-Prescribe includes the ability to add compound medications to the drug database and then use those compound medications to prescribe them for any patient. Creating a compound is similar to adding a new medication. It's like creating a "template" for what you want, or in other words, a base version of the medication users can select and then modify as needed when prescribing the medication. Any user with permission can set up a compound medication. Prerequisites : This feature requires E-Prescribe . Create a Compound Medication Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab. In Prescriptions , select Prescribe New Drug → Compound/Specialty → Setup Compound . Note If you do not see the setup button, you do not have permission to set up a compound medication. You can select an existing compound to prescribe for a patient, but you cannot create a new one. Enter a Title and a Comment for the compound, then select Add Compound . (The Title will be the name of the compound when selecting it on‐screen; this name is only visible in Vagaro and does not send to the pharmacy. You will set the name of the drug that the pharmacist will see in the next steps. The Comment is also only visible in Vagaro and will indicate to users what the medication is used for.) In your new compound, next to Compound , select Edit . The Compound screen will appear, where you can enter Ingredients , Quantity , Directions , and the Pharmacy you would like to set for the compound medication, then select Update Compound . Note The preferred name and the Ingredient 1 name can be the same. At least one ingredient must be added all compounds is enough, individual ingredients do not need to be added, but you can Repeat whatever the preferred name is in the ingredient 1 drug description.  There is a Quantity for Ingredient 1 and a Final Quantity .  Both of these will be the same when you have only 1 ingredient. Prescribe a Compound Medication Once you've created your compound medication, you can prescribe it to your customers. To prescribe a compound medication. Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab. Select Start Prescribing . If the patient is missing information from their profile, you will be prompted to enter additional details. If the patient's profile matches multiple patients in ScriptSure, you will be prompted to select the correct patients and link their Vagaro profile with ScriptSure. In Prescriptions , select Prescribe New Drug → Compound/Specialty . Select the compound you'd like to prescribe from the list. In Compound Selection , next to the dosage of medication you want to order, select Next . Note If you do not see any compound medications to select, you will either need to create a new compound medication or have an administrator do this for you. In Create Compound , verify and/or modify the prescription. Make sure to enter any required information in blank fields. Select Send to Pharmacy or Queue to Provider for Approval .
ground_truth: To create a compound medication in E-Prescribe, go to Customers and select a customer. Then, select the patient's profile and go to the E-Prescribe tab. In Prescriptions, select Prescribe New Drug → Compound/Specialty → Setup Compound. Enter a Title and a Comment for the compound, then select Add Compound. Next, select Edit next to Compound to enter Ingredients, Quantity, Directions, and the Pharmacy for the compound medication, then select Update Compound.

question: How can I jump to a specific month and year on the calendar using a phone?
context:
[Article: Jump to a Date on the Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360016457833-Jump-to-a-Date-on-the-Calendar]
Jump to a different day, week, or month to quickly view upcoming appointments and events. You can also move the calendar backward or forward by years if needed. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone There are many ways you can navigate through the calendar: Swipe left or right on the calendar to move to the previous or next day, week, or month, based on your current view . In the top-left corner, select Calendar to return to the current day, week, or month, depending on your current view . Near the top of the screen, use the < or > buttons to jump forward or backward one day, week, or month, depending on your current view . In the top-right corner, select the Menu menu, then select the Calendar Jump button to jump to a specific month and/or year. Use the < or > buttons to cycle through the different months, or select the current month with the Down-Arrow icon to choose another year or month. Select a day on the calendar or select Done to update your calendar. In the same menu, Jump by Week by selecting one of the negative or positive numbers. For example, if you select -8 , the calendar will go back eight weeks. If you select +4 , the calendar will advance four weeks. Select Today to go back to today's schedule. Select Done to update your calendar. On a Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro Swipe left or right on the calendar to move to the previous or next day, week, or month, based on your current view . Near the top-left corner, select Today to jump to the current day, week, or month, based on your current view . Next to the Today button, use the < or > buttons to cycle to the previous or next day, week, or month, based on your current view . Next to Today , select the date to use the expanded calendar tool. Within the tool, you can also jump forward or back a specific day, week, or month On a Computer There are many ways you can navigate through the calendar: In the top-left corner, use the calendar to jump to a day in the month or cycle through the different months of the year. Use the < or > buttons to cycle through months. Near the top-left corner, select Today to jump to the current day, week, or month, based on your current view . Next to the Today button, use the < or > buttons to cycle to the previous or next day, week, or month, based on your current view . Next to Today , select the date to use the expanded calendar tool. Within the tool, you can also jump forward or back a specific day, week, or month
ground_truth: On a phone, you can jump to a specific month and/or year by selecting the Menu menu in the top-right corner, then selecting the Calendar Jump button. Use the < or > buttons to cycle through the different months, or select the current month with the Down-Arrow icon to choose another year or month. After selecting a day on the calendar, select Done to update your calendar.

question: What information is displayed on the Company Pays tab for each employee?
context:
[Article: Payroll History Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003990314-Payroll-History-Report]
aro Payroll only): The total amount reimbursed to the employee. Additional Earnings : The extra amount earned in addition to their hourly wages or salary. Gross Pay Total : The total amount earned by the employee Deductions : The total amount deducted from the gross pay total for business costs . Employee Taxes : The total amount deducted for employee taxes from the gross pay total. Employee Benefits : The total amount deducted for employee benefits from the gross pay total. Payroll Due : The total amount paid to the employee for the pay period. Pay Method : If this line is listed, the employee was paid by direct deposit; select the link to view the employee's pay stub. If no pay method is listed, the employee was paid another way, and a pay stub will be available.

Company Pays Tab

The Company Pays tab shows a breakdown of the benefits and taxes that the company paid for each employee during the selected payroll frequency.

This tab and its information are only available with Vagaro Payroll .

On a Phone The following information is shown for each employee: Employees : The first and last name of each employee that your business paid for. Gross Pay Total : The gross amount of money your business paid your employee. Payment Method : How each employee received payments from your business. Reimbursements : The amount your business reimbursed each employee. Company Taxes : The total amount of taxes your business paid for your employees. Company Benefits : The total amount of benefits your business paid for each employee. Subtotal : The subtotal of all amounts paid by the company for the employee.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro The following information is shown for each employee: Employees : The first and last name of each employee that your business paid for. Gross Pay Total : The gross amount of money your business paid your employee. Payment Method : How each employee received payments from your business. Reimbursements : The amount your business reimbursed each employee. Company Taxes : The total amount of taxes your business paid for your employees. Company Benefits : The total amount of benefits your business paid for each employee. Subtotal : The subtotal of all amounts paid by the company for the employee.

Taxed & Debited Tab

The Taxed & Debited tab lists the taxes and services that were deducted from your employees' paychecks.

This tab and its information are only available with Vagaro Payroll .

On a Phone On a phone, this tab contains two sections: Taxes : The top half of this screen lists the taxes that your employees and your business owe and are paid directly to their government agencies. Tax Description : The name of the tax that was paid during the pay period by your employees and/or your business. All taxes listed in this table depend on the state that your business is located in. By Your Employees : The total amount of taxes that were deducted from your employees' paychecks. By your Company : The total amount of taxes that were debited from your business's bank account. Debited by Gusto : The bottom half of this screen lists the amounts for each service you offer to your employees. Gusto debits these amounts from your business's bank account on your behalf to pay your employees and the government agencies they're owed to. Direct Deposit : The total amount that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Reimbursement : The total amount of reimbursements that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Garnishments : The total amount of garnishments that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Taxes (Employee and Employers) : The total amount of taxes paid by both employees and your business that were debited from your business's bank account.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro On the web, tablet, Pay Desk, or Paypro, this tab contains two tables: Taxes : This table lists the taxes that your employees and your business owe and paid directly to their government agencies. Tax Description : The name of the tax that was paid during the pay period by your employees and/or your business. All taxes listed in this table depend on the state that your business is located in. By Your Employees : The total amount of taxes that were deducted from your employees' paychecks. By your Company : The total amount of taxes that were debited from your business's bank account. Debited by Gusto : The bottom half of this screen lists the amounts for each service you offer to your employees. Gusto debits these amounts from your business's bank account on your behalf to pay your employees and the government agencies they're owed to. Direct Deposit : The total amount that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Reimbursement : The total amount of reimbursements that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Garnishments : The total amount of garnishments that Gusto debited from your business' bank account. Taxes (Employee and Employers) : The
ground_truth: The Company Pays tab shows the first and last name of each employee, the gross amount of money paid to the employee, the payment method, the amount reimbursed, the total amount of taxes paid by the company for the employee, the total amount of benefits paid by the company for the employee, and the subtotal of all amounts paid by the company for the employee.

question: What steps do I need to follow to decrease the quantity of a single product on my phone?
context:
[Article: Lower Product Quantity | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29525023232539-Lower-Product-Quantity]
Maintaining your business's inventory is important to know if you're running low or out of stock of a product. When your business needs to lower products, reduce the amount of multiple products in your inventory. Lower that product's quantity in your inventory outside of customer purchases so you have a complete picture of what you currently have in stock and what you need to reorder. For your records, you can categorize removed items as lost, damaged, expired, or used for business purposes such as customer appointments. You can also enter any additional comments about why the amount was subtracted. These changes to your inventory can be tracked in the Product History report.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses, and you must already have products set up in your inventory .

Decrease the quantity of a single product.

On a Phone To decrease the quantity of a selected product: Select More → Settings → Management . Select the product to be edited. If you want to decrease the stock of a single Variant , select the Right-Arrow > next to a product to view its Variants . In the top-right corner of the screen, select the button, then select Subtract . Note On the Pay Desk or PayPro, select the Action menu in the middle of the screen. In Subtract Inventory , enter some information about why you are reducing the stock: Date : Select the date when the product was subtracted from your inventory. By default, today's date is selected. Reason : Select a reason in the list. Comment : Enter an optional comment about why the amount was subtracted. Select the Subtract - button to remove stock from your inventory. If this product has Variants , you can subtract stock from all Variants at once. Select Save . Changes to the product's stock are added to Product History .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To decrease the quantity of one or more selected products: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . Next to the product to be changed, select the Action menu, then select Subtract . If you want to increase the stock of an individual Variant , select the Right-Arrow > next to a product to view its variants. Select a Date for when you lowered the product's stock. Enter the Subtract Quantity for this product. If this product has Variants , you can subtract quantity for multiple Variants at the same time or select the X button to remove them. Select a Reason why the stock is being removed, then enter an optional comment. Select Save . Changes to the product's stock are added to Product History .
ground_truth: To decrease the quantity of a selected product on your phone, select More → Settings → Management, then select the product to be edited. If you want to decrease the stock of a single Variant, select the Right-Arrow > next to the product to view its Variants. In the top-right corner of the screen, select the button, then select Subtract. Enter the date, select a reason for the reduction, and optionally enter a comment. Finally, select the Subtract - button to remove stock from your inventory and select Save.

question: How do I access the Checkout Settings on the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Change Your Checkout Settings | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19076333010075-Change-Your-Checkout-Settings]
You can configure your Checkout Settings to change how day-to-day transactions process, what types of receipts you need, set up the Vagaro Printer, and set your tipping preferences. Any changes you make depends on the device you're on Vagaro Pro App : These settings apply only to the device you're using, not to the business. Each device can have its own checkout settings. Computer : These settings apply to all other computers connected to the business. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . To configure your Checkout Settings : Go to the Checkout screen. Open the Checkout Settings : Vagaro Pro App : In the top-right corner, select the Settings icon. Web Version : In the bottom-right corner of the screen, select the Down-arrow button, then select Checkout Settings . Configure your checkout settings: View the following articles for more on how each setting works. Set Tipping Options Require Digital Signature Contactless Checkout Receipt Preference Receipt Printers Select Save .
ground_truth: To access the Checkout Settings on the Vagaro Pro App, go to the Checkout screen and in the top-right corner, select the Settings icon.

question: What is Vagaro's public key used for in the context of data transfer?
context:

question: What information is displayed for each customer profile in the Customer List Report when viewed on a phone?
context:
[Article: Customer List Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115004772554-Customer-List-Report]
to show customers whose ages were within this range during this timeframe. After running the report, review and sort the customers in the report as separate cards or in a table by selecting their tabs: Card View : Show each customer profile in a separate card. You can sort the list by criteria and alphabetically by criteria. Grid View : Show the list of customers in a table. Sort the list of customers by selecting the column headers at the top of the report. View the customer's profile by selecting their name in the Name column. Send an email to the customer by selecting their email address in the Email column. At the top of the screen, you can select: Undo Delete : Restore a deleted customer profile. Merge : Combine two or more customer profiles that belong to the same customer. Delete : Remove a customer profile from your business. At the bottom of the screen, you can select: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only).

After running or filtering this report, you can view the customers' profiles that match your search criteria and the activities they've performed at your business.

Depending on the filters and dates you've selected, you may notice that the list of customers and the information shown for each may differ from what is currently shown in their actual Customer Profile screen. If you specify a Date Range , the report will show only customer profiles and their activities that occurred between these dates. For example, if you enter a date range from Feb 1, 2024, to February 29, 2024, your customers' profile information, such as the Amount Spent, Employees Seen, Appointments Booked, Classes Booked, Check-Ins, and so on, will only show customers' profile information during that timeframe.

On a Phone The following information will be shown for each customer profile listed in this report: Name : The customer's name. Amount Spent : The total amount of money that the customer has spent at your business. If you selected a Date Range , this number shows how much the customer spent during this timeframe. Email : The customer's email address. Address : The customer's mailing address. Cell Phone : The customer's mobile phone number. Day Phone : The customer's preferred phone number to be reached at during the day. Night Phone : The customer's preferred phone number to be reached at in the evenings. Customer Since : The date when the customer first became a customer at your business, or the date it was changed to. Last Visited : The date when the customer last completed an appointment or class. This date does not count previous visits for other purchases. Membership : The memberships that the customer has currently purchased and are currently active. Memberships received through Family & Friends sharing will not be shown. Online Booking : Select whether these customers are allowed to book online with your business, require acceptance for appointments, or are blocked from booking online with your business. Birthdate : The date the customer was born. Gender : The gender selected in the customer's profile. Referred By : The name of the person or business who referred this customer. This person or entity does not need to already be a customer. Tags : Any General Tags you've assigned to the customer. Appointments Booked : The number of appointments that the customer has booked. If you selected a Date Range , this number shows how many appointments the customer booked during this timeframe. Classes Booked : The number of classes that the customer has booked. If you selected a Date Range , this number shows how many classes the customer booked during this timeframe. Check-Ins : The number of times the customer has checked in. If you selected a Date Range , it'll show how many times a customer checked in during that timeframe. Credit Card : The card type and the last four digits of the credit card that the customer has on file. Bank : The last four digits of the customer's bank information that the customer has on file if you allow your customers to pay with Buy Now, Pay Later . Points Earned : The number of points that the customer has earned. If you selected a Date Range , it'll show the points eanred during that timeframe. No Shows/Cancellations : The number of times the customer no-showed or cancelled their appointments. If you selected a Date Range , it'll show the total number during that timeframe. Employee Seen : The service providers the customer has had appointments with.
ground_truth: The following information is shown for each customer profile listed in the report on a phone: Name, Amount Spent, Email, Address, Cell Phone, Day Phone, Night Phone, Customer Since, Last Visited, Membership, Online Booking status, Birthdate, Gender, Referred By, Tags, Appointments Booked, Classes Booked, Check-Ins, Credit Card details, Bank information, Points Earned, and No Shows/Cancellations.

question: What notifications will a customer receive after an invoice is created for them?
context:
[Article: Create and Send an Invoice at Checkout | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011037574-Create-and-Send-an-Invoice-at-Checkout]
If a customer forgets their wallet or you need to charge a deposit over the phone, create an invoice. Invoices are a safe and secure way to collect payment from customers when they're not at your business. The customer will then receive the invoice by email and text with instructions on how to pay online . You'll also get a popup informing you that the customer has an outstanding invoice the next time you check them out. After creating an invoice, your customer will receive an email, text message, and push notification notifying them of the new invoice. Vagaro will then continue to send payment reminders every seven days until the invoice is paid. Payment reminders are only sent via email. Prerequisites : Credit Card Processing is highly recommended to process invoice payments. To create and send an invoice: Select Checkout , or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Next to the Checkout button, select the down arrow button, then select Create Invoice . Fill out the invoice as needed: Invoice Title : The title of the invoice. Invoice # : The number to help find your invoices at a later time. You can't use the same Invoice # more than once. Due / Due Date : The expected payment date. Message to customer : A message about the invoice directed to the customer. Optionally, to prompt customers to add a tip, turn on Allow Tip . This option will not appear if your Checkout Settings are set to Don't Ask for Tip . Select Next . Review all items in your invoice, then select Send Invoice . Tip To make a draft to be edited at a later time, select Save for Later . The system redirects you to Invoice Management , where you can create new invoices and manage your Sent , Overdue , Paid , Void , and Draft invoices.
ground_truth: The customer will receive the invoice by email and text with instructions on how to pay online, as well as a push notification notifying them of the new invoice.

question: What are the benefits of creating Daily Deals on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Create Daily Deals for Memberships & Packages | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21356144738331-Create-Daily-Deals-for-Memberships-Packages]
Daily Deals are a great way to advertise your business to new customers and help bring back customers who haven't come in for a while. Creating a Daily Deal is free, and Vagaro does not take any commission. Once announced and approved, the deal appears on the Vagaro Marketplace , your Vagaro Listing Page , and MySite , allowing for more visibility of your business.

Prerequisites : This feature requires Credit Card Processing , the Online Shopping Cart , and to be listed on Vagaro.com .
ground_truth: Creating Daily Deals helps advertise your business to new customers, encourages returning customers, and increases visibility by appearing on the Vagaro Marketplace, your Vagaro Listing Page, and MySite.

question: How can I change the URL of a new page I add to my MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Add a New Web Page | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/20043859618843-MySite-Add-a-New-Web-Page]
Organize the content in your MySite by adding new web pages to showcase and explain different topics. For example, you can create a new page for each of your services to provide more detailed information about the benefits of each one. Create a new page on your MySite to help organize your content. Pages appear in the top navigation menu on your website and have a unique path that can be visited by your customers. For example, "www.yourdomain.com/ page ." This path can be changed by updating the URL slug of each page. Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To create a new page: Select MySite . In the left design panel, select the Pages tab. Select the Add Page button. Give your page a name. Configure your page settings: URL Slug : The page name added to your URL. For example: https://yourdomain.com/urlslug. Title Tag : The name of your page that appears in a web browser or when sharing a link. Meta Description : The description of your website shown on search engines. Open Graph Image : The image that appears when a link to your page is shared. Set as Home Page : Make this page the home page of your MySite. Hide Page From Menu : Hide the page from your top navigation bar, but still makes it accessible. Disable Page : Make this page inaccessible to customers. Select Save . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: You can change the URL of a new page by updating the URL Slug in the page settings.

question: How do I use a package to redeem a service during booking on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Redeem a Package - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360006472253-Redeem-a-Package-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
A package allows you to purchase services in bulk, typically at discounted prices. You can purchase a package that can redeem one service or buy one with a variety of items and services. To view your packages and see what services are included, see Viewing Your Packages... . Prerequisites : Ensure that you've purchased at least one package from the business you're looking to book with. Go to the businesses' booking page, log in to your account, and begin booking a service , signing up for a class , or purchasing a product . Optionally, once you've reached the checkout screen, you'll see a summary of your cart. On the right side of the screen, ensure that no points or memberships are applied to the cart. If there are, select Remove . Note On the phone, select View Order Summary at the top of the screen to view your summary. Scroll to Select Payment Method , then select Use Package Visit for the package to be used. Optionally, you can use another payment method to pay the difference if you have any additional balance. Review and agree to the businesses' cancellation or refund policy. Book your service or class or purchase your product.
ground_truth: On the checkout screen, ensure no points or memberships are applied, then select 'Use Package Visit' for the package to be used, and proceed with booking your service or class.

question: Why should I set up safe IP addresses for my team members in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Best Practices for Vagaro Security | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21876536869531-Best-Practices-for-Vagaro-Security]
In today’s world of information, everyone’s personal and financial information is always at risk from prying eyes and data thieves. Vagaro takes privacy and information security as a top priority to protect your business and customers from hackers, spammers, and cyber attacks. Our team works tirelessly with security experts and implements the latest mandates and guidelines per the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). there are many things you can do that aren’t as difficult as you may think to keep your business's and customers' information safe.

Vagaro has several features that you can take advantage of to protect your business's and employee's information . If you need help with any of these settings, contact Vagaro Support , and we will be happy to assist you.

Create an employee profile for each of your team members, including your business partners and family members who use Vagaro at your business, and ensure everyone logs in to their own account.
