Emphasize that no one should share their Vagaro login or password information so that you can accurately track who changed information on a screen and when it happened. Set up access level permissions for each role at your business and assign each team member to an access level. Not everyone needs access to all your client records and employee profiles, business’s financial information, or Vagaro features, so restrict permissions accordingly. You can create as many access levels as needed for every job in your business. For example, only business owners should be able to change business account ownership, and only accountants should be able to run your business reports. Install and use Vagaro only on your business’s computers and devices. You cannot trust that someone else won't access your Vagaro account on a library computer, a friend’s phone, or a family member’s tablet. Deactivate or delete employee profiles of team members who no longer work with you or your business. (Deleting an employee profile only removes that account from your business; it does not delete that employee's Vagaro account.) Not only does removing unused employee profiles free up a Vagaro license if your business has fewer than eight licenses, but you also prevent people who don’t work with you from logging into Vagaro. Enable auto-logout in the Access Levels screen so that the system returns to the Login screen after a specified time. Doing so prevents customers and other strangers from using Vagaro if you leave your computer or device unattended. If you allow your team members to access your Vagaro account outside of your business location, set up safe IP addresses in the Access Levels screen so they can log in to Vagaro from specific locations that you’ve allowed. Every device that connects to the Internet or a network is assigned an IP address, and permitting specific employees' devices prevents unknown users and devices from logging into your Vagaro business. Businesses that need to keep patients’ medical information on file should enable HIPAA compliance in their text messages, push notifications, and emails. Vagaro also provides additional tips and recommendations that you can implement to maintain customer privacy
ground_truth: Setting up safe IP addresses allows team members to log in only from specific, approved locations, preventing unknown users and devices from accessing your Vagaro business account.

question: How can I resend an invitation to my customers after changing my Vagaro URL?
context:
[Article: Invite Customers to Your Vagaro Listing Page | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005045413-Invite-Customers-to-Your-Vagaro-Listing-Page]
After you set up your Vagaro Listing Page , you can send customers an email invitation with a link to your booking page. If you change your Vagaro URL in your Business Profile, invite your customers again so they can still find you online and in the Vagaro Marketplace. Tip Update your Email & Text Notifications to automatically invite new customers to your Vagaro Listing Page each time you add a customer. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses on the computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro. To invite your customers to your booking site: Go to Marketing . At the top of the screen, select Invite Customers . In the confirmation pop-up, select OK . Note If you’ve invited customers before, you can send another invitation to all existing customers. For example, if you update your Vagaro URL in your Business Profile, resending the invitation sends a new welcome email with the updated URL.
ground_truth: After updating your Vagaro URL in your Business Profile, go to Marketing, select Invite Customers, and confirm to resend the invitation, which will send a new welcome email with the updated URL.

question: How do I add a product bundle in Vagaro using a barcode scanner on a computer or tablet?
context:
[Article: Add a Product Bundle to Your Inventory | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260800450750-Add-a-Product-Bundle-to-Your-Inventory]
Product bundles are sets of products sold together, such as a gift basket that includes skin care products or a “fitness pack” that includes a jump rope, resistance bands, and a bag to hold them. Adding a product bundle in Vagaro is easy—you simply need to group together products you've added to your inventory, enter a price, and then you can sell it both in-house and online.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses, and you must already have products set up in your inventory .

Here are some points to keep in mind about Product Bundles in Vagaro:

The available quantity of a bundle will default to the quantity of the lowest-stocked item in the bundle. Product bundles appear in the Inventory Management screen with the Bundle icon. You can only add products and their product variants to a bundle. Adding all the variants of a product allows customers to choose the one they want at checkout. If each variant has a different price, the final price of the bundle will update accordingly.

On a Phone To add a product bundle: Select More → Settings → Management . In the top-right corner, select Action → Add → Bundle . Scan your bundle's barcode with your camera or enter it in the text box. Select Next to continue. Enter a Bundle Name and Description . Bundle your products together: Under Product Bundle , select Add Products . Select the products to be added in bundle, then select Select Products . If you have a lot of products in your inventory, select Scan and scan a product's barcode to bring it up quickly. Enter the Sell Price for each product in the bundle, then select Done . The price of your bundle is based on the total price of each product you include. Under Bundled Products , enter the quantity ( Qty ) of each item in the bundle. If you want to change how the bundle appears online, under Bundled Products , select Reorder , select Move and drag it to a new spot, and then select Done . If you want to remove an item from the bundle, select X . Optionally, to sell this bundle online, turn on Sell Product Online and configure your settings . Select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a product bundle: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . In the top-left corner, select Add (or +) → Product Bundle Scan or enter your bundle's barcode, then select Next : With a Barcode Scanner : Plug the scanner into your device, press and hold the power button, and hold it up to the barcode until you hear a beep. The code will appear in the text box. Without a Scanner : Manually enter the product ID in the text box. On a Tablet or PayPro Mini : Select Scan , then point your device's camera at the barcode to scan it. Enter the Product Bundle Name and Description . If you're selling this product online, you can add Additional Details as collapsible content. Bundle your products together: Under Bundled Products , select Add Product . Select the products to be added to the bundle, then select Add Product . From Add Product Bundle under Bundled Products , enter the quantity ( Sell Qty ) of each item in the bundle. If you want to change the order of how the bundle shows online, select Move and drag it to a new spot. If you want to make changes to items in your bundle, select Action , then select Edit to choose another item or Delete to remove that item. Under Variants , enter Price for each product in the bundle. The final selling price of the bundle is the combined Price of all products. If you added variants of the same product in the bundle, the final price depends on which variant the customer chooses. Optionally, to sell this bundle online, turn on Sell Product Online and configure your settings . Select Save .
ground_truth: To add a product bundle with a barcode scanner, plug the scanner into your device, press and hold the power button, and hold it up to the barcode until you hear a beep. The code will appear in the text box. Then, select Next to continue.

question: What should I do if the receipt printer does not print a test receipt during setup?
context:
[Article: Set Up PayPro Duo | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/27909914522651-Set-Up-PayPro-Duo]
To onboard PayPro:

On the welcome screen, select Begin Onboarding . Follow the prompts for each Vagaro hardware, or select Skip for now to plug them in later.

Barcode Scanner Connect the barcode scanner and test that it is connected to your device. Plug the scanner’s USB cable into any USB port on the PayPro hub, then select Next . Test the barcode reader by scanning the barcode shown onscreen. If the test is successful, the screen will automatically go to the receipt printer steps.

Receipt Printer Connect and test that the receipt printer can communicate with Vagaro Pro. Plug the printer’s USB-C end of the cable into the receipt printer and the USB side of the cable into the PayPro hub, then plug the printer’s power cord into the DC port and the other end into a power outlet, then select Next . Load paper into the receipt printer, then select Next . Select Test to print a test receipt. If a receipt was printed, select Yes . The screen will automatically go to the cash drawer steps. If a test receipt wasn’t printed, select No . Make sure that the receipt printer is powered on, that all cables are fully inserted, and that the receipt paper is inserted correctly, then select Test again, or select Skip for now to try again later.

Cash Register Connect and test that Vagaro Pro can communicate with the cash register. Plug in the RJ12 cable into the port under the cash register and the other end into the receipt printer, then select Next . Select OK to test that Vagaro Pro can communicate with the register. If the register drawer opened: Select Yes . Select Next in the confirmation. The screen will automatically go to the card reader steps. If the drawer didn't open, make sure the receipt printer is powered on, all cables are fully plugged in, and the paper is loaded correctly, then select Test again.

Onboarding and pairing with PayPro Mini requires action on both devices. Make sure PayPro Mini is in its dock before pairing it with PayPro.

To onboard and pair PayPro with PayPro Mini:

On PayPro, select PayPro Mini as the payment accessory, then select Next . Note When prompted to pair with PayPro Mini, do not select Next until PayPro Mini is in pairing mode (see step 2b). On PayPro Mini: Select Begin Onboarding . Select Pair with PayPro , then select Next . PayPro Mini is ready to pair with PayPro. On PayPro, select Next to pair the devices and finish onboarding on both devices. If the test is successful, Vagaro will finish the onboarding process.

When PayPro, PayPro Mini, and your Vagaro hardware have all been plugged in and tested, the main PayPro unit will appear the following screens:

The setup progress bar shows that PayPro is finishing the setup process. The welcome screen is briefly shown before you'll log in to Vagaro. The Vagaro login screen allows you to log in and resume your business activities. On PayPro Mini, the idle screen appears. Tip Set up a screen-saver slideshow of your businesses and service/class menu to be shown as instead of the idle screen.
ground_truth: Ensure that the receipt printer is powered on, all cables are fully inserted, and the receipt paper is inserted correctly, then select Test again or choose Skip for now to try later.

question: How can I display my business phone number when there are no available appointments on my Vagaro Listing Page?
context:
[Article: Configure Your Online Appointment Rules | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347060-Configure-Your-Online-Appointment-Rules]
From setting up lead times for services and classes to how appointment times appear on your Vagaro Listing Page , customize your customers' booking experience in the Online Appointment Rules screen. Some settings you can turn on include setting appointment and class lead times, controlling the number of timeslots available to customers, and displaying your business phone number when appointments are unavailable. Other features include showing the available class seats or automatically assigning an employee, which will further facilitate processes for the clients.

With membership or package booking requirements, limited booking windows, and the need for approval over certain customers or employees, businesses can further control who can and can't book. Enable waitlisting to manually or automatically book customers to fill in last-minute availability. You can also specify and select your cancellation and rescheduling policies so customers are aware of your required notice periods and conditions for refunds.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

To select your online appointment rules:

Go to Online Appointment Rules : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Toggle the switches on or off for each setting shown below. Select Save .

The following rules can be applied when customers search for appointments on your Vagaro Listing Page :

Appointment Lead Time : The buffer time before a customer can book a specific timeslot for a service. You can enter a value in min (minutes), hrs (hours), or days . For example, set your lead time to 24 hours to prevent customers from booking an appointment on the same day. Class Lead Time : The buffer time before a customer can book a specific session for a class. You can enter a value in min (minutes), hrs (hours), or days . For example, set your lead time to 24 hours to prevent customers from registering for a class on the same day. Appointment Search Limit : The maximum number of bookable timeslots your customers can see at a time on your listing page. Search Appointment Interval : The amount of time between available timeslots on your listing page. For example, if you set an interval of 15 minutes, the available appointments for the day will be 8:00, 8:15, 8:30, 8:45, 9:00, and so on. The next available booking times will round up to the nearest 5 or 15 minutes if a customer books at, for example, 7:32 PM. Cleanup Time Preferences : Choose how to apply cleanup time when a customer books multiple services back-to-back. Use Longest Cleanup at the End : Adds a single cleanup slot after the final service in a multi-service booking. Only the longest cleanup time from the grouped services is applied at the end of the booking. Note This setting applies only to online bookings for a single customer. It does not affect classes, in-house bookings, or service bundles. If the appointments are booked separately or by multiple customers close together, this setting won’t apply. As a workaround, you can manually adjust the cleanup time in these scenarios. Apply After Each Service (Default): Cleanup time will be added to the end of each service. Show Number of Seats Remaining in Classes : Whether to show how many more customers can still sign up for the class. Display Business Phone Number When Appointments are Unavailable : Whether to prompt customers to call your business and display your business's phone number if there are no available appointments for that day.
ground_truth: Enable the setting 'Display Business Phone Number When Appointments are Unavailable' to prompt customers to call your business and show your phone number when no appointments are available.

question: How do I change the title tag for a specific page on my Vagaro MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Change the Title Tag for a Page | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26945918520987-MySite-Change-the-Title-Tag-for-a-Page]
A Title tag specifies the title of a web page. It appears on search engine results pages as the headline for a given result or on social media pages when shared. Title tags are important for usability, SEO, and social sharing. By default, the Title tag is set to the business's name on Vagaro. Note Any changes to your website's home page settings will also apply to your website's base URL. Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To change a page's title tag: Select MySite . In the left design panel, select the Pages tab. Hover over the page, then select the Gear button. Scroll down to Title Tag and enter your tag. Select Save . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: To change a page's title tag, select MySite, go to the Pages tab in the left design panel, hover over the page, click the Gear button, scroll down to Title Tag, enter your new tag, and then select Save.

question: How can I view the current balance of a gift card if the receipt is lost?
context:
[Article: Sell Gift Cards | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18627015674011-Sell-Gift-Cards]
Gift cards are a great way to help boost revenue at your business during the holidays. In Vagaro, you can sell physical and digital gift cards to your customers.

Physical gift cards can be scanned from the checkout screen to add value to those cards. You can use gift cards you've created or order custom gift cards from the Vagaro Online Store . Digital gift cards are sold in-house , on your Vagaro Listing Page , MySite , and on Facebook ; there is no need to create them in advance because Vagaro has a variety of templates to choose from. If you have your own designs, you can upload those as well . If you're having trouble, Studio 180, Vagaro's in-house design services team , can help resize and touch up digital gift card designs for you.

Each customer is restricted from purchasing more than $1,500 (or the equivalent in local currency) in gift cards per day. This limit includes individual gift certificates or packages for smaller amounts that add up to more than $1,500 or a single one for more than $1,500. Restrictions are set by FinCen and our banking partners.

If you are a customer of a business looking to purchase a gift card, please see these articles .

Prerequisites : Selling gift cards in-house is available to all businesses. Selling gift cards online requires Credit Card Processing and Online Shopping Cart .

Purchased gift cards are saved in the recipient's customer profile, so customers can always redeem them in person without a physical card. If a customer purchases a gift card for someone who is not in your system, you can provide a physical gift card or print a gift card receipt. Both options can be redeemed at your business even if the recipient does not have a profile yet. If the receipt is lost, you can go to the Gift Card Report to view all gift cards sold and check their current balance. From this report, you can also void a gift card, resend a receipt, or change its balance. If you recently moved to Vagaro, you can transfer any existing gift cards to continue accepting them at your business. In-person gift card sales are deposited to the business account owner or to any employee who has their own merchant account. Deposits are made the following business day after the gift card is purchased. Because Vagaro doesn’t treat gift card sales as service revenue, commission can’t be assigned to hourly or salaried employees for these transactions. Gift cards purchased online will be deposited into the business owner's merchant account by default. If you want customers to choose which employees should receive credit for the sale, turn on Gift Card Sales Go Directly to Employees in your Online Shopping Cart settings. Service-based gift cards are sold at the lowest price you offer for that service. Any tax that applies to the service is automatically included in the gift card’s total price.
ground_truth: You can view the current balance of a gift card by going to the Gift Card Report, which shows all gift cards sold and their balances.

question: What information do I need to enter under the Business Information section when setting up my Vagaro business profile?
context:
[Article: Set Up Your Business Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977243390491-Set-Up-Your-Business-Profile]
Your business profile is one of the first things your customers will see when booking appointments with you online. You'll want customers to have all the information they need when they schedule with you, such as your business's name, a description of what you do and offer, your location, contact information, the days you're open, and business hours.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

To set up your business profile:

Go to your Business Profile : Phone : Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Business Profile . Under the Business Information heading, enter the following information: Business Name : Your business's legal name. Business Description : Enter at least 100 characters to explain what your business does or is about, and any other information you'd like customers to know. Business Phone , Business Email , and Business Website : How customers can contact your business. Vagaro URL : Your business's name or moniker that is added to the end of www.vagaro.com for customers to visit your Vagaro Listing Page . For example, if your business is called "Rock Starz," enter RockStarz to be found online at www.vagaro.com/rockstarz . Toggle the following settings: Show Contact Information : Whether to show the email address and phone number you provided above. On your Vagaro Listing Page , customers can select the corresponding buttons to contact your business. Show Vagaro Connect Messaging : Whether to show the Vagaro Connect Message button on your Vagaro Listing Page. Toggling off this setting prevents most messages through Vagaro Connect, you may receive messages if a customer contacts your Vagaro number directly or replies to a text campaign. List Business on Vagaro.com : Whether customers can find your business on the Vagaro Marketplace and book appointments on your Vagaro Listing Page . Listing your business on the Vagaro Marketplace makes your business visible to potential customers in their search results. Note This setting is already toggled on when you create your Vagaro business. Even if you don't list your business on Vagaro.com, your customers will still be able to find your business if they bookmarked your page in their web browser or know your Vagaro Listing Page's URL. List Business on Google and/or List Business on Apple Maps : Whether customers can book with you directly from Google Maps or Apple Maps. Note To list your business on Google Maps, you must already have a Google Business account . To list your business on Apple Maps, you must already have an Apple Business account and Apple Business Connect account . It may take up to 24 hours for your business to appear on these services. List Business on Yelp : Display a "Book Now" button on your Yelp business page that links directly to your Vagaro booking page. Enable Daylight Saving? : Whether to apply daylight saving time in Vagaro for customer appointments, clock-in/out entries, reports, and other screens and where timestamps appear. This toggle appears only for businesses located in Hawaii, Arizona, and Puerto Rico, and in Saskatchewan, Canada. If this setting is toggled on to apply daylight saving time, it must also be toggled off when daylight saving time ends. Under the Business Location heading, provide the following information: Service Location : Specifies that your business is performed At My Business , At My Client's Location , or Both . Additional settings are required if you want to offer mobile services . Business Address Line 1 (and Business Address Line 2 , if applicable): Where your business is located and additional street address details, such as suite or unit number. While you type your address, the field predicts the address you are trying to enter, and you can select your address when you see it fully shown in the results. The system may prompt you to select a different address if it cannot find the one you enter. Note Business addresses cannot be a P.O. box. Tip The map automatically shows the location you entered. If the location is wrong, select Fix Incorrect Map Marker to move the pin to where it should be, and then select Save Map Marker . Use Business Address for Navigation App Directions : Toggle if you want your customers' navigation apps to use the address you entered (instead of the map marker, if you moved the pin). Specify what days and times your business is open and closed: Note This toggle does not appear if you have only one Vagaro license, which is usually the business owner's. Instead, the working hours entered in the business owner's Employee Profile screen are automatically used. Business Hours (disables “Use Employee Working Hours”): Show the days and times that your business is open, regardless of when your employees are working. Toggle the Day of the Week and then enter the Opens at and Closes at times. Select Copy to All to copy the same times to every day of the
ground_truth: You need to enter your business's legal name, a description of what your business does (at least 100 characters), contact information including phone, email, and website, and your Vagaro URL.

question: How do I add a new external link to my Vagaro MySite navigation bar?
context:
[Article: MySite: Create a Navigation Link | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/33245801195291-MySite-Create-a-Navigation-Link]
Navigation links allow you to link to external websites, such as your social media page or another website you own. Any navigation links you create will be added to the navigation bar at the top of the screen. Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To create a navigation link: Select MySite . In the left design panel, select the Pages tab. Select Add Nav Link . Configure your new navigation link: Enter a Link Name . The name you enter will appear in your navigation bar. Enter the Link URL of the external website you want to redirect customers. Optionally, toggle Open in new tab if you want to have the external page open in a new tab. Select Save . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: To add a new external link, select MySite, go to the Pages tab in the left design panel, click Add Nav Link, enter a Link Name, input the Link URL of the external website, optionally toggle Open in new tab, then click Save.

question: How can I apply a gift card to my purchase on Vagaro's website if it's not automatically applied?
context:
[Article: Redeem Gift Cards Online - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360006601033-Redeem-Gift-Cards-Online-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
You can redeem one or more gift cards purchased from a Vagaro business when you check out at that business, both in-store and online. Tip If you have difficulty applying a gift card towards your purchase, log out of Vagaro and log back in again. On a computer also try clearing your web browser's cookies and cache , then logging in again. Prerequisites : This feature requires a Vagaro account, and you must already have gift cards with a balance to be redeemed at that specific Vagaro business. On the Vagaro App To redeem gift cards when checking out on a business's Vagaro Listing Page: Go to the business's Vagaro Listing Page . Schedule a service or class , or select products to Add to Cart , then select the Shopping Cart icon. Review the items in your shopping cart, then select Proceed to Checkout . Tip If you already have a gift card in your account, they are automatically applied, as indicated by the Gift Card Applied tag below the product. Optionally, expand View Order Summary at the top of the screen to see a breakdown of your total. Scroll down to the Gift Cards heading and select the gift cards you'd like to apply toward your purchase. Selected gift cards are highlighted in blue. To remove a gift card, select it again; to use another gift card, select Add Gift Card , enter the Gift Card Number , then select Apply . If you still have a balance after selecting your gift cards, select New Payment Information to add another gift card or enter a new credit card. Select Buy Now . On a Computer To redeem gift cards when checking out on a business's Vagaro Listing Page: Go to the business's Vagaro Listing Page . Schedule a service or class , or select products to Add to Cart , then select the Shopping Cart icon. Select the shopping cart icon at the top-right of the screen. Review the items in your shopping cart, then select Proceed to Checkout . Tip If you already have a gift card in your account, they are automatically applied, as indicated by the Gift Card Applied tag below the product. Under the Gift Cards heading, select the gift cards you'd like to apply toward your purchase. Selected gift cards are highlighted in blue. To remove a gift card, select it again. Select the gift cards you want to use. The blue highlight shows the item has been selected for use. If you still have a balance after selecting your gift cards, select New Payment Information to add another gift card or enter a new credit card. Select Buy Now .
ground_truth: On a computer, go to the Vagaro Listing Page, add items to your cart, proceed to checkout, then under the Gift Cards heading, select the gift cards you'd like to apply. If needed, select Add Gift Card, enter the Gift Card Number, and click Apply.

question: What happens to my data when I deactivate the Data Lake integration?
context:
[Article: Deactivate the Data Lake Integration | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/34914557151643-Deactivate-the-Data-Lake-Integration]
The Data Lake integration can be managed from the Manage Subscription page and turned off at any time. Deactivating this integration will remove all previous, current, and future data from your business intelligence software. If you decide to reactivate the integration later, the integration must be set up with your business intelligence software again and will require rebuilding your reports and dashboards. Prerequisites : Requires the Vagaro Data Lake integration . To deactivate the data lake integration: Go to Integrations : Phone : Select More → Settings → Integrations . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Integrations . Scroll down to the Data Lake integration, then select Settings . Select Deactivate Integration .
ground_truth: Deactivating the Data Lake integration will remove all previous, current, and future data from your business intelligence software.

question: What are the recommended dimensions and aspect ratio for uploading a logo image to Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Logo Image Tips | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115006340867-Logo-Image-Tips]
When searching in Vagaro, your logo image is the first visual a potential client sees, so it is important that you choose a great image that attracts more customers. To present your business in its best light, we suggest you partner with a photographer, graphic designer, or one of our Design Team members . You can then add your logo to your Vagaro account by uploading the image to your Venue Gallery . If you'd rather create or add your own logo, we've provided some tips to help you choose a stunning visual to represent your business. Choose a photo of your studio space, work, staff, or just an image of your business logo. These subjects will best represent your business to potential clients. Make sure the image is high quality. High resolution, sharp, and not blurry. The left image is low resolution and, therefore, blurry. To ensure a high-quality image, upload an original file under 5MB that is at least 1000 x 667 pixels. Use a horizontal orientation or crop it into a 3:2 ratio. A horizontal image crops best when the image has a 3:2 aspect ratio; however, some vertical photos can also fit. In this vertical portrait, the subject's lovely features are cropped out. In this horizontal portrait, no valuable information has been cropped out. This horizontal portrait is an excellent example of a well-cropped logo image because the entire image is cropped within a 3:2 aspect ratio. Take your photo in natural, soft light. You can achieve soft light in front of a window or outside in the shade. Moving to the shade softens the subject's features. The lighting in this photo is too harsh. Direct sunlight creates unflattering shadows. Use text sparingly. Your business name is the only text needed on your logo image. Your business information is on your Vagaro Listing Page , so there is no need to display it on your logo image. The text on the left image does not distract from the image, while the text on the right image does. Business information is unnecessary on a logo image. Avoid distracting backgrounds. We want your work to be the focus. Photograph your subject against a wall, a simple background, or blurred background. The cars in this background distract from the subject. A clean background makes the subject pop out in the photo. Do not use a collage. You have a small amount of space to entice customers, so choose one great visual that will invite them to your page. Place your logo on a 3:2 canvas (1280 x 1920 pixels). A cropped logo looks unprofessional. This logo does not fit within the 3:2 aspect ratio. This logo was placed on a 3:2 canvas, and now it fits within the entire frame.
ground_truth: The recommended dimensions are at least 1000 x 667 pixels, and the image should be placed on a 3:2 canvas (1280 x 1920 pixels) to ensure proper cropping and professional appearance.

question: What information do I need to enter to add a new employee's profile in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add an Employee Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977275440795-Add-an-Employee-Profile]
by asking the selected question, and they must provide the answer entered. Enter the employee's Employee Access settings: Employee Type : The type of Vagaro user your employee is at your business. Service Provider : Team members who manage their customers and appointments on the Vagaro calendar and perform services. They may have different working Hours than the business, and they can choose to allow Online Booking and Share Calendar . There is a license fee for each service provider added, other than the business owner's, for up to seven calendars, after which it is a flat fee to add an unlimited number of service providers. Account Admin : Team members, such as receptionists, accountants, and cleanup staff, who do not provide services to customers. This employee type typically doesn't have their own calendar but they can view or modify other calendars if their access level allows. Admins can be given a calendar if you toggle Add Calendar after selecting this employee type so they can schedule personal tasks , request time off , set working hours, clock in and out , and so on. If you toggle it on again, the calendar will be restored. Warning Allowing an Admin to have a calendar requires another Vagaro license; therefore, your subscription will be updated and billed accordingly. Access Level : Specifies what the employee can see and do in Vagaro. Employee Card ID : (Optional) Enter an employee ID, or use your device to scan the employee card number. Select Save . The employee's profile is listed in the Employee Profiles screen.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add an employee profile: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Add Employee . Enter the employee's First Name , Last Name , Email , and Confirm Email . Only these fields are required; you can fill out any optional information as needed: Note If an employee already has a Vagaro account, they will be prompted to enter their Vagaro password, and that account will be added to your business. Under the Personal Information heading, enter the following information: Profile Picture : Optionally, upload a photo of this person so your customers can see what your team member looks like. Address Line 1 : The employee's street number and name. As you type, the address line will autofill with the address's city and zip code. To change the employee's country of residence, select the flag and choose the country. Address Line 2 : The employee's room, unit, suite, or apartment number if they live in a multi-unit building. Gender , Address , and Birthday : Personal employee information that is not shared with customers. Phone Number : Send text messages and notifications about their customer’s appointments. Note Employees who have toggled off text notifications in their employee profiles will not receive text messages about appointment notifications or other text messages from your business. Show contact email and phone on Vagaro : Whether to make this team member's information visible to customers on your Vagaro Listing Page in the Staff tab. Under the Company Information heading, enter the following information: Start Date : The day your employee started working at your business. Note Once added to their profile, an employee's Start Date can only be modified by the business owner or the employee's manager ( Reports To ). Reports To : This employee's manager. By default, the business owner is selected and will need to be changed if authorization is required for employee actions, such as requesting paid time off on their calendar. Employee Bio : A personal description of the employee, up to 1000 characters long, about the employee that will be viewable by customers on the business's Vagaro Listing Page . For example, share the employee's experience and training and why they are qualified to perform their services. Under Business Notifications , toggle whether your want the employee to receive Email Notifications , Text Notifications , and/or Push Notifications . Note Employees who have toggled off email notifications in their employee profiles will not receive any of these emails, text messages, or notifications. Under Marketing Notifications , toggle whether you want the employee to receive Marketing Emails from my Business , Vagaro Emails , or Vagaro Text Notifications . Select Next . Enter the employee's Login Information and Employee Access settings: Username : Automatically filled with the employee's email address. Employee Card ID : (Optional) Enter an employee ID or use your device to scan the employee card number. Password and Confirm Password : Only the employee will know what the password is. Security Question and Security Answer : If this employee contacts Vagaro Support, the agent will verify their identity by asking the selected question, and they must provide the answer entered. Employee Type : The type of Vagaro user your employee is at your business. Service Provider : Team members who manage their customers and appointments on the Vagaro calendar and perform services. There is a license fee for each service provider added, other than the business owner's, for up to seven calendars, after which it is a flat fee
ground_truth: To add a new employee's profile, you need to enter their First Name, Last Name, Email, Confirm Email, and optionally upload a Profile Picture, and provide personal details such as Address, Gender, Birthday, Phone Number, and Company Information like Start Date and Reports To. You also set their Login Information, including Username, Employee Card ID, Password, Security Question, and Employee Access settings.

question: How can I view the usage history of a customer's purchased package in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Manage Purchased Packages from a Customer's Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4406328135451-Manage-Purchased-Packages-from-a-Customer-s-Profile]
After a customer purchases a package , you can view and manage it in their customer profile or by running the Packages report . Customers can purchase as many packages as they want, even if they are all the same. In a customer's profile, you can also view their memberships' usage history, switch them to another one, manage auto-renewal, update membership details, reactivate inactive ones, as well as cancel or refund them.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro, and you must already have at least one package created and active . Optionally, Credit Card Processing is required to set up auto-renewing packages.

All packages can be edited so that their settings are specific to a customer. None of these changes will affect the original package created in the Packages screen.

Note On a phone, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro, this task can be performed on the Packages report screen.

On a Computer To update a customer's package after they've purchased it: Go to Customers and select a customer. Search for and select the customer's profile. Select the Packages tab. At the end of the row of the packages to update, select the package's Action menu, then select Edit Package . Update the customer's package information as required. Select Save .

Updating a customer's package allows a business to add or remove visits in the package.

On the Vagaro Pro App To edit a customer's package balance: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Packages tab. Select the package to view, then select Edit Balance . For Services/Classes/Add-Ons , select it, then update the number of Visits remaining , or toggle on Unlimited . For Products , select the tab, then update the Quantity Remaining , or toggle on Unlimited . Select Save .

On a Computer To edit a customer's package balance: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Packages tab. At the end of the row of the package to update, select the package's Action menu, then select Edit Balance . For Services/Classes/Add-Ons , update the number of Visits . For Products , select the tab, then update the Quantity Remaining . Select Save .

Viewing a customer's package usage shows detailed information about all packages a customer has purchased, their current balance and status, who has used it, and the activity that has occurred on it.

On the Vagaro Pro App To view a customer's package usage in their customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Packages tab. Select the package to view, then select History . Select the Back button to close the report.

On a Computer To view a customer's package usage in the customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Packages tab. At the end of the row of the package to view, select the Action menu, then select History .

You can tell whether a customer's package will auto-renew in a customer's profile by selecting the Packages tab and looking at its Status , or by running the Packages report and checking whether there are any renewals left in the Remaining Auto Renewal column. Packages that will automatically renew based on the charge frequency will have an Active status, and those that won't will have an Inactive status.

Note On a phone, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro, this task can be performed on the Packages report screen.

On a Computer To pause or resume an auto-renewing package: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Packages tab. To pause a package: In the list of packages, select the package's Action menu, then select Pause Auto Renew . In the confirmation popup, select Pause Package . To resume a customer's package: In the list of packages, select the package's Action menu, then select Resume Package . In the popup, select Resume Package . Review the information in the confirmation screen, and tell the customer that they can start using their package again immediately and charged on their next billing date. Note If the customer wants to change the billing date, the customer will have to cancel the package and purchase a new one with the new billing date. Select Resume Package .
ground_truth: To view a customer's package usage, go to the customer's profile, select the Packages tab, then select the package and click History.

question: What details are shown in the Payment History section of the Vagaro Capital Report?
context:
[Article: Check Your Vagaro Capital Balance and Payments | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41561529042587-Check-Your-Vagaro-Capital-Balance-and-Payments]
Keeping track of your Vagaro Capital cash advance is simple with the Vagaro Capital Report. This report shows your payment history, remaining balance, and how much you’ve already paid, giving you a clear picture of your progress. Tip To see how your payments are calculated, check out your daily sales in the Deposit report . Prerequisites : Apply for Vagaro Capital. Run the Report By default, the report will show this year's payments and your cash advance balance. On a Phone To run the report: Select More → Reports → Vagaro Capital . Set your Date Range of payments you want to view. Optionally, to change the merchant account you want to review, select Filters and choose another Merchant . Note You can also sort your report to show the information you want first. Optionally, to export or print your report, select Action , then choose an option: Star : Bookmark this report. Print : Print a physical copy of this report. Export PDF : Export this report as a PDF file. Export Excel : Export this report as an Excel sheet. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To run the report: Select Reports → Vagaro Capital . Set your Date Range of payments you want to view. In the Merchant list, choose the merchant account you want to review if your business has multiple merchant accounts. Select Run Report . Optionally, export or print your report: To export your report, select the Export menu, then choose to export as a PDF or Excel file. To print your report, select Print . What's in This Report? After running the report, information about your cash advance will be shown, including how much you've paid, your remaining balance, and each payment you've made. On a Phone At the top of the report, you can view information about your cash advance through Vagaro Capital. Total to Pay : The amount to be paid, including the cash advance amount and the fixed fee. Percentage of Sales : The percentage taken out of daily sales. Paid So Far : The amount you've paid towards your balance. Remaining Balance : The amount left to pay. Under Payment History , you can see the days you've made payments to your balance displayed as cards. Each card shows the payment date, the amount paid, and your remaining balance, making it easy to track your progress. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro At the top of the report, you can view information about your cash advance through Vagaro Capital. Total to Pay : The amount to be paid, including the cash advance amount and the fixed fee. Percentage of Sales : The percentage taken out of daily sales. Paid So Far : The amount you've paid towards your balance. Remaining Balance : The amount left to pay. Under Payment History , you can see the days you’ve made payments toward your balance. Each row in the table displays the payment date, the amount paid, and your remaining balance, making it easy to track your progress.
ground_truth: The Payment History section displays the days you've made payments, showing the payment date, the amount paid, and your remaining balance for each payment.

question: How can I list my business on Google and Apple Maps using Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Turn on Online Booking and List Your Business on Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000604014-Turn-on-Online-Booking-and-List-Your-Business-on-Vagaro]
Your Vagaro Listing Page is your business's booking site where your clients can book services and classes with you online, as well as purchase products, gift cards, memberships, and packages. You can list your business on the Vagaro Marketplace , Google.com, Google Maps , and Apple Maps to help drive traffic to your business. The Vagaro Listing Page uses a shared design across all businesses to keep the Marketplace consistent and easy to navigate. If you'd like more control over the look and feel of your booking page, you can create your own custom site using Vagaro MySite .

The Vagaro Marketplace search is based on the titles of your services and classes, not their descriptions or categories, so be as informative as you can. We recommend that you set up your Service/Class Menu by creating Vagaro Services and Vagaro Classes that include the best keywords, and then add more details to their titles to provide more information about what’s offered, specific words that customers may have also entered, and increase the likelihood you’ll appear in search results.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

List your business on Vagaro to add yourself to the marketplace, making it easy for new clients to discover, book, and connect with you.

On a Phone To set up your Vagaro Listing Page: Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Go to Vagaro URL and enter a unique slug for your listing page URL. Tip The slug is the part of a URL that comes after the slash, like vagaro.com /yourbusinessname , and helps clients easily find your booking page online. Toggle on List Business on Vagaro.com , which also allows you to: List Business on Google.com : Allow customers to book appointments with you when they search for you on Google.com. A Google business account is required. List Business on Apple Maps : Allow customers to book appointments with you when they search for you in Apple Maps. An Apple business account is required. Select Save . Optionally, you can continue to customize your Vagaro Listing Page for your customers: Show your business's working hours Show your contact information Show your services and classes Show your employees and booth renters Show off your business and service portfolio

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To set up your Vagaro Listing Page: Select Settings → Business Profile . Go to Vagaro URL and enter a unique slug for your listing page URL. Tip The slug is the part of a URL that comes after the slash, like vagaro.com /yourbusinessname , and helps clients easily find your booking page online. Toggle on List Business on Vagaro.com , which also allows you to: List Business on Google.com : Allow customers to book appointments with you when they search for you on Google.com. A Google business account is required. List Business on Apple Maps : Allow customers to book appointments with you when they search for you in Apple Maps. An Apple business account is required. Select Save . Optionally, you can continue to customize your Vagaro Listing Page for your customers: Show your business's working hours Show your contact information Show your services and classes Show your employees and booth renters Show off your business and service portfolio
ground_truth: To list your business on Google and Apple Maps, go to your Business Profile settings, toggle on 'List Business on Vagaro.com,' which also enables listing on Google.com and Apple Maps, provided you have the required business accounts, and then click Save.

question: How do I clear browsing history and cache in Microsoft Edge?
context:
[Article: Clear Cache and History on a Web Browser | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204346890-Clear-Cache-and-History-on-a-Web-Browser]
To clear the cache and history on a web browser if the device is running slower than usual, select one of the links below: Microsoft Edge Firefox Google Chrome Safari (Mac Only) Microsoft Edge Launch the Microsoft Edge browser. Click the action menu (3 horizontal dots) and then scroll down and select Settings . Select Privacy, search, and services on the sidebar menu. Then, under Clear browsing data , click Choose what to clear . Select the checkboxes next to each item you want to clear, including Browsing history , Download history , Cookies and other site data , and Cached images and files . Select a time range: last hour, last 24 hours, last 7 days, last 4 weeks, or all time. Click Clear now to finish. Close all browser windows and restart Microsoft Edge . Firefox Launch the Firefox browser. Click and select Settings or Options (depending on Firefox version.) Click Privacy & Security . Go to the History section on the page. Then click Clear History . Click the drop-down next to Time Range to clear. Select Everything . Select Browsing & Download History , Form & Search History , Cookies, and Cache. Then click Clear Now . Close all Firefox windows and restart. Google Chrome Launch the Google Chrome browser. Click the menu. Hover over More Tools . Then select Clear browsing data . Select the Advanced tab. Select the Time range from the drop-down list. For example, if you select All time , it will clear all your past browsing data. Select the items you want to remove. Then click CLEAR DATA . Close all browser windows and restart Google Chrome . Safari (Mac Only) Launch the Safari browser. Click Safari at the top of the screen. Then select Clear History . Select a time range. For example, if you select all history , it will clear all your past browsing data. Click Clear History . Close the browser once you're finished clearing the history. Then launch a new Safari browser window.
ground_truth: In Microsoft Edge, click the action menu (3 dots), select Settings, then Privacy, search, and services. Under Clear browsing data, click Choose what to clear, select the items you want to clear, choose a time range, and click Clear now. Finally, close all browser windows and restart Edge.

question: How can I add a new brand to my products in Vagaro if it's not already in the preset list?
context:
[Article: Add a Product Brand and Vendor | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001713713-Add-a-Product-Brand-and-Vendor]
Brands and vendors allow you to group products so they're easily found online, in your reporting , and when managing Purchase Orders . Brand : A brand is a name that represents a company's product and can help your customers identify items on your Vagaro Listing Page . Brands can also help you filter through your products when reporting on product sales, inventory, and stock . Vendor : A person or company that sells products to businesses. Assigning a vendor to your product allows you to configure Purchase Orders and Drop Shipping. The Vendor Management screen keeps all your vendors in one place to add and make changes anytime. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Add a Brand to Your Products By default, Vagaro has a preset list of popular brands to choose from. If you're brand is not in the list, you can add a custom one when creating your product. To create a brand: Go to the Inventory Management screen, then create a new product or edit an existing one . In the Brand list, select Create New Brand . Enter the Brand Name . Select Save . Select your newly created brand. Select Save . Once created, a brand can't be edited or removed from the Brand list. Add a Vendor to Your Products If you plan to set up drop shipping and purchase orders within Vagaro, add a Vendor to your product so that when an order is placed, the system will automatically send an email to your vendor with the purchase order. To create a Vendor: Go to the Inventory Management screen, then create a new product or edit an existing one . In the Vendor list, select Create New Vendor . Enter the Vendor Name . Enter any other details you have about the vendor. Note An Email must be entered if you want to process purchase orders or drop ship with the vendor. Select your newly created vendor. Select Save . Any Vendors you created will be shown in the Vendors Management screen. Manage the Vendor List From the Vendors Management screen, you can create, edit, delete, export, or print a list of your vendors. You can also view the vendor's contact information and website from this screen. Important The Vendors Management screen is only available on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro. To see all vendors you've added, go to Settings → Inventory → Vendors . From the Vendors screen, you can do the following: To find a specific vendor, enter the specific vendor in Search , then select Search . To make changes to the vendor, in the Action list, select Edit or Delete . To add a new vendor, select Create New in the top-left corner. To export your list of vendors, select Export to download it as an Excel spreadsheet or select Print to get a paper copy.
ground_truth: Go to the Inventory Management screen, create a new product or edit an existing one, select 'Create New Brand' from the Brand list, enter the Brand Name, and then select Save.

question: How can I change the hero image in my new blog post on MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Create a Blog Post | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/31549408978331-MySite-Create-a-Blog-Post]
Create and manage a blog on MySite to post news, updates, and other professional content about your business. Start crafting your post, then further refine it by changing the hero image to enhance your design. You can further refine your post by managing details such as tags, categories, and metadata, which helps ensure better organization and improves search engine optimization. You can choose to publish your post immediately or schedule it for a future date.

With the embedded Vagaro AI feature, writing for a blog can be fast and easy, even for an inexperienced writer. You only have to provide some input on what you want your content to be about, and it will provide you with polished, industry-specific content for your business. Whether you're writing a full blog post or just rewriting the content, the AI changes its tone and style based on what is required. Select the AI button in the text editor, provide a topic or idea, and let the tool generate professional-grade content. It’s a great way to save time while ensuring your blog posts resonate with your audience.

Tip Publishing a blog post will create a unique URL for the post; however, it will not appear on your MySite without creating a new page to host it. After making a new page, use the Blog List and Blog Search components to design your blog page.

Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite .

Create a new blog post for your MySite page.

To create a new blog:

Select MySite . In the top-left corner, go to Settings . Select Blogging . Select Create New Post . In the New Post textbox, enter the title of your blog post.

A hero image is a large, visually striking banner typically placed at the top of a webpage or blog post to grab attention and set the tone for the content. It often includes supportive text, like a headline or call-to-action, and is designed to leave a strong first impression. Change the image shown at the top of your blog post and use it as a thumbnail for your post.

To change your hero image:

In your new blog post, hover over the image box, then select Change Hero Image . Select an image: Search Online : Search for free images online from Unsplash. Upload Image : Upload an image from your computer. From Library : Choose a photo that you've uploaded previously to MySite. Optionally, to change how your image appears, select the image, then modify the image in the right settings panel. Layout : Change how your hero image appears on your blog. For example, select Full to have the image cover the entire width of your blog. Hero Image Position : If your image is cut off, use this setting to adjust the vertical position of your hero image. Image Radius : Change the roundness of your image.

Not only is the rich text editor the tool you use to write your content, it's also designed to make creating polished, professional content painless. Use the rich text editor to easily draft up your content and make a beautiful post. Once you select the Write your blog post text box, the editor toolbar appears at the top of the page, giving you instant access to a wide range of options, including:

Text Styling : Adjust the size, font, and color of your text. You can also make text bold or italic to emphasize key points or underline words to draw attention. Organize Content : Break up large blocks of text with bullet points or numbered lists to improve readability. Use indentation to structure your paragraphs or create a visual hierarchy. Quotations : Add inspiring quotes to your blog by adding quote blocks to your text. Hyperlinks : Highlight text and hyperlink to other pages to navigate customers to external sites or another page on your MySite. Images : Upload and embed images directly into your blog, giving readers a visual element to complement your writing. You can upload an image directly from your computer or search online for an image with Unsplash.
ground_truth: Hover over the image box in your new blog post, then select Change Hero Image. You can choose to search online, upload an image from your computer, or select from your previously uploaded images in the library. You can also modify the image's appearance, layout, position, and roundness using the right settings panel.

question: When is the confirmation request typically sent to a customer before their appointment?
context:
[Article: Examples of Appointment Notifications and Reminders | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000393854-Examples-of-Appointment-Notifications-and-Reminders]
The screenshots below show examples of appointment notifications and reminders sent to customers via email and text when appointments are booked. Appointment Details Customers receive this notification after an appointment is booked in-house or over the phone. It is sent shortly after the booking is made and provides the appointment's details. If the customer booked the appointment online, the details will be sent only via push notification; there will be no text message. However, if the customer booked the appointment online for someone on their Family & Friends list , the family or friend will receive both a push notification and a text message at the number provided for the booking. Appointment Details Email Appointment Detail Text Confirmation Request This notification lets the client confirm if they will be at the appointment. It is especially helpful in discouraging no-shows. A confirmation request is typically sent 72 hours before the scheduled appointment. Confirmation Request Email Confirmation Request Text Reminder Notification This notification is sent to remind the customer of a scheduled appointment. An appointment reminder is usually sent 24 hours before the service. Reminder Email Reminder Text Other Notifications No-Show Appointment Email No-Show Text Canceled Appointment Denied Appointment
ground_truth: A confirmation request is typically sent 72 hours before the scheduled appointment.

question: Where do I add custom code to include Google Tag Manager on my MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Add Head or Footer Code to Your Website | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26664552870043-MySite-Add-Head-or-Footer-Code-to-Your-Website]
Adding custom code to your website's header or footer lets you apply it to all pages. This can be used to set up Google Tag Manager on your MySite. Custom code added to the head will be added to the Head element of your website, and code added to your footer will be added to the Footer element inside the Body . Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To add head or footer code: Select MySite . In the top-left corner, go to Settings . Select General . Enter your code in the Head Code or Footer Code box. Select Save . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: You add custom code in the Head Code box for the header or the Footer Code box for the footer in your MySite settings, then save and publish your site.

question: What happens if I run out of email or text credits when sending a campaign?
context:
[Article: Avoid Email and Text Plan Overages | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/38605174908059-Avoid-Email-and-Text-Plan-Overages]
If you run out of email or text credits, you'll be charged an overage fee for each additional message you send. When you're about to send a campaign, such as an email or text blast or a Daily Deal announcement , a popup will appear. This popup lets you know how many messages will go over your limit and gives you the option to either upgrade your plan or accept the overage charges. The exact fee depends on your current plan . This does not apply to recurring campaigns, like before or after visit campaigns. The system will continue sending them even after you’ve reached your credit limit. If you have recurring campaigns scheduled, be sure to monitor your current usage to avoid unexpected overage fees. Here are some helpful articles you can use to keep track of your plan usage and stop further campaigns from being sent out: View This Month's Marketing Plan Usage Check Your Email and Text Marketing Billing History Pause a Recurring Marketing Campaign
ground_truth: If you run out of email or text credits, you'll be charged an overage fee for each additional message you send, and a popup will appear to inform you of the overage and options to upgrade or accept the charges.

question: Who is authorized to activate the Facebook Book Now button on my Vagaro business page?
context:
[Article: Add a Book Now Button to Facebook | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360002102633-Add-a-Book-Now-Button-to-Facebook]
Similar to customers being able to book an appointment by going to your business's Instagram, Yelp, Google Maps, or Apple Maps account, you can also add the Book Now button at the top of your Facebook business page. When customers select this button, they will be taken to your Vagaro Listing Page so they can schedule an appointment. Prerequisites : This feature is available only on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro. A Facebook business page is required, and only the Vagaro business account owner can activate this feature. If employees try to enable the integration, they'll see a popup stating " You have no rights to authorize. " Activate the Facebook Integration To activate the Facebook integration: Go to Integrations : Phone : Select More → Settings → Integrations . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Integrations . Under Facebook , select Learn More . Select Activate Facebook Booking , then in the popup, select Authorize to give Facebook access to your Vagaro account. Log into your Facebook account and continue. Select Next . Confirm the settings that Vagaro can connect to. If needed, you can select a setting to change what is mapped between Vagaro and Facebook. Verify What is Vagaro allowed to do? , then select Next . Vagaro has been integrated with Facebook. A book now button will now appear on your business page.
ground_truth: Only the Vagaro business account owner can activate the Facebook Book Now button; employees will see a popup stating 'You have no rights to authorize' if they try.

question: How can I edit a recurring appointment to change only one occurrence without affecting the series?
context:
[Article: Edit Your Customer's Appointment on the Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010358234-Edit-Your-Customer-s-Appointment-on-the-Calendar]
On Vagaro, you can update your customer’s appointments to change who's performing the service, appointment duration, price, or make the appointment repeat. If an appointment is part of a series, you can edit all appointments in the series or just a single appointment. You can only edit multiple appointments at the same time when they are part of a series. After updating the appointment, you can choose whether to notify your customer of the changes. Prerequisites : Requires a scheduled appointment on the Calendar. On the Vagaro Pro App To edit an appointment: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, the Edit Appointment popup will appear. If the service is part of a repeating series, choose to Edit This Occurrence or Edit Series . Note Keep in mind that editing an individual class or appointment that's recurring will unlink it from the series, and any future changes to the series will have no effect on this appointment or class. You can identify unlinked items on your Calendar by looking for the No Repeat icon. If the customer prepaid for this appointment, select Edit Appointment in the Edit Paid Appointment popup to confirm. From Edit Appointment , make changes to the appointment, such as updating the service, service provider, date, time, price, duration, appointment notes, or how often it repeats. If you don't want to notify customers of the change, turn off Notify Customer . Leaving this toggle on will send a push notification, an email, and a text message to your customer. Select Save . On a Computer To edit an appointment: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, the Book Appointment with... popup will appear. If the service is part of a repeating series, choose to Edit only this occurrence or Edit the series . Note Keep in mind that editing an individual class or appointment that's recurring will unlink it from the series, and any future changes to the series will have no effect on this appointment or class. You can identify unlinked items on your Calendar by looking for the No Repeat icon. If the customer prepaid for this appointment, select Edit Appointment in the Edit Paid Appointment popup to confirm. If the customer has multiple appointments scheduled that day, select Edit Appointment to confirm. From Book Appointment with... , make changes to the appointment, such as updating the service, service provider, date, time, price, duration, appointment notes, or how often it repeats. If you don't want to notify customers of the change, turn off Notify Customer . Leaving this toggle on will send a push notification, an email, and a text message to your customer. Select Save .
ground_truth: When editing a recurring appointment, select 'Edit This Occurrence' to change only that specific appointment without affecting the entire series.

question: What must be done before transferring ownership if the current owner has future bookings?
context:
[Article: Change Owner or Transfer Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347570-Change-Owner-or-Transfer-Business]
Only the business account owner of a Vagaro business can change the owner of their business account or transfer it to someone else, including other business partners, family members, and employees. If the owner still has future bookings, they must be moved to another employee's calendar before ownership can be transferred. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To reassign a business account to another owner: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Select the Account Owner profile. Under Locations , select the Action menu, then select Reassign . In the Reassign - Select Option popup, select Reassign Account Ownership , then select Continue . Note If a message says that the owner still has future bookings, select Reassign Calendar to move their appointments to another employee, or add a new employee , who does NOT have hours or services , then select OK in the confirmation popup. Enter the business account owner's password, then select Login to continue. A popup confirms that you entered the correct password. In the Reassign Account Ownership popup, select Continue to agree and confirm that the new owner will enter their credit card and assume responsibility for maintaining this Vagaro account. Enter the new owner's credit card information and billing address, then select Continue . Select how the new owner's credit card will be processed. No Credit Card Processing : Transfer the business account to the new owner without signing up for Vagaro Merchant Services or using their existing VMS account. The new owner will not be able to accept credit card payments until they sign up for Vagaro Merchant Services or they can activate their merchant account later. Select Continue to finalize the transfer the business account to the new owner. If the new owner has Card Reader Present Processing : Select Continue with this option selected to have the new owner set up Vagaro Merchant Services right away or use their existing VMS account with their new business. If they don't already have a merchant account, select Continue again on the warning screen, and the new owner can set up their account. Select Current Configuration to view how the current owner's account is set up. Review the Reassign Account Ownership summary, then select Reassign Ownership to confirm the transfer. The assigned user can now log in as the owner of the business. On a Computer To reassign a business account to another owner: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . In the row of the Account Owner profile, select the Action menu, then select Reassign . In the Reassign - Select Option popup, select Reassign Account Ownership , then select Continue . Note If a message says that the owner still has future bookings, select Reassign Calendar to move their appointments to another employee, or add a new employee , who does NOT have hours or services , then select OK in the confirmation popup. Enter the business account owner's password, then select Login to continue. A popup confirms that you entered the correct password. In the Reassign Account Ownership popup, select Continue to agree and confirm that the new owner will enter their credit card and assume responsibility for maintaining this Vagaro account. Select the New Owner and Select Access Access Level , then select Continue . If you're assigning ownership to someone who doesn't work at your business, select Add Employee and enter that person's information. Enter the new owner's credit card information and billing address, then select Continue . Select how the new owner's credit card will be processed. No Credit Card Processing : Transfer the business account to the new owner without signing up for Vagaro Merchant Services or using their existing VMS account. The new owner will not be able to accept credit card payments until they sign up for Vagaro Merchant Services or they can activate their merchant account later. Select Continue to finalize the transfer the business account to the new owner. If the new owner has Card Reader Present Processing : Select Continue with this option selected to have the new owner set up Vagaro Merchant Services right away or use their existing VMS account with their new business. If they don't already have a merchant account, select Continue again on the warning screen, and the new owner can set up their account. Select Current Configuration to view how the current owner's account is set up. Review the Reassign Account Ownership summary, then select Reassign Ownership to confirm the transfer. The assigned user can now log in as the owner of the business.
ground_truth: The future bookings must be moved to another employee's calendar or a new employee who does not have hours or services before ownership can be transferred.

question: How do I create a generic customer profile for walk-in clients in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Check Out Walk-in Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/37612023336347-Check-Out-Walk-in-Customers]
People are spontaneous—sometimes they walk into your business ready to book a service or buy a product without an appointment. That doesn’t mean you should miss out on the sale. Because Vagaro requires a customer to be selected when checking out a service, product, or class session, you’ll need a quick solution for walk-ins who don’t want to create a profile. One simple workaround is to create a generic “Walk-In” customer profile that you can reuse anytime someone drops by unannounced. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Create a Walk-in Profile To check out a walk-in customer, you'll need to create a customer profile specifically for walk-ins. On the Vagaro Pro App To create a walk-in profile: Select Customers . In the top-right corner, select the Add + button. Enter "Walk" for First Name and "In" for Last Time . Only this information is required. Note We always recommend creating a new customer profile for each walk-in customer. Select Save . The profile is added to your Customer List . On a Computer To create a walk-in profile: Select Customers . In the top-right corner, select Create New . Enter "Walk" for First Name and "In" for Last Time . Only this information is required. Select Save . The profile is added to your Customer List . Check Out Your Walk-in After creating the walk-in profile, select that profile from the Checkout screen. On the Vagaro Pro App To check out walk-in: Select Checkout . Select Select Customers , then choose the "Walk In" customer in the list. Build your walk-in's cart and check them out . On a Computer To check out walk-in: Select Checkout . Select Select Customers , then choose the "Walk In" customer in the list. Build your walk-in's cart and check them out .
ground_truth: To create a walk-in profile, select Customers, then on a mobile device tap the Add + button and enter 'Walk' as First Name and 'In' as Last Name, then select Save. On a computer, select Customers, then click Create New and enter 'Walk' for First Name and 'In' for Last Name, then Save.

question: What information is included in the Appointment Timeline for a Vagaro appointment?
context:
[Article: View Appointment Timeline and History | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347730-View-Appointment-Timeline-and-History]
The Appointment Timeline (also called Appointment History) provides a complete record of an appointment and all actions taken on it. This includes the appointment date and duration, the service added to the booking, the employee who provided the service, and any updates or changes made along the way. The timeline shows the following information: Who booked the appointment, and on what date it was created. The appointment date. The duration of the appointment. The service or class being performed. Any appointment notes ( Comment ) that the business and/or customer added. The employee performing the service. Whether the appointment was modified and who edited it. The status of the appointment. Any changes made to the appointment. On a computer, you'll also be able to see any email or text notifications sent to your customer.
