Prerequisites : This feature requires a scheduled appointment on the Calendar. On the Vagaro Pro App To see the appointment timeline: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. If the service is part of a repeating series, select Edit This Occurrence . From Edit Appointment , select Action in the top-right corner. Scroll down and select Appointment History . The Appointment History for this appointment will be shown. Each change made to the appointment will be displayed separately. On a Computer To see the appointment timeline: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the appointment repeats, choose to Edit only this occurrence , then select Edit Appointment(s) . From Book Appointment with... , select the Timeline tab. Your appointment timeline will be shown. Each change to the appointment will be displayed in a separate row. Under the Action column, you can view more details about the appointment by selecting one of the following options shown: Email : View a copy of the email notification sent to your customer. Notification : View the push notification sent to your customer. SMS : View the text message sent to your customer. Cancellation Policy : Review a copy of your cancellation policy. Depending on the appointment, not all of these links will appear.
ground_truth: The Appointment Timeline includes the appointment date and duration, the service added, the employee who provided the service, any notes added, who booked it and when, whether it was modified and by whom, the appointment status, any changes made, and on a computer, any email or text notifications sent to the customer.

question: How can I upload artwork for a custom gift card if it's not ready during the order process?
context:
[Article: Order Gift Cards from Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/30847952225179-Order-Gift-Cards-from-Vagaro]
Work with our Design Team to create a look specifically for your business. You’ll also be able to add your gift card policy and restrictions to be printed on the back of the card.

After you submit your order, a member of our Design Team will contact and work with you to create your gift card. Timely responses to our communications will ensure the prompt creation and delivery of your artwork.

Custom gift cards will be printed in 10-15 business days.

To purchase a custom gift card design:

Go to the Vagaro Store : Phone : Select More , go to the Purchase Vagaro Products banner, and then select Buy Now . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select More → Purchase Vagaro Products . Computer : Select More → Vagaro Store . Select the Custom Gift Cards tile. At the top of the screen in the Your Custom Cards banner, select Order Now . The order form will open in an Internet browser window. Under Tell us about your card artwork , select I'd like Vagaro's customer design service . Under the Front of Card heading: Select Choose File to upload the artwork for your gift card. If your artwork isn’t ready yet, you can email the file later to artwork@ecardsystems.com . Optionally, enter a Message to provide any additional artwork instructions. Under the Back of Card heading: Enter your Business Address and Phone Number . Optionally, review and update the Message about your gift card policy and restrictions. Under the Quantity & Finish heading, select your Card Quantity , Production Time , and Card Laminate Type . Select Continue . Enter your Company Info , then select Continue . Optionally, select any accessories for your gift cards, such as card sleeves, envelopes , or a card display. Select Save and Continue to Checkout . In the Cart screen, review and submit your order. If you have a Coupon Code , enter it, then select Apply Coupon . Verify the price, quantity, and order total, then select Process to Checkout . Enter your Billing Details , Shipping Details , and Credit Card information. Optionally, enter any Order notes that you'd like to bring to our attention. Select Place Order . When you receive your design proof from eCard, please review and approve the gift card design. We cannot proceed with printing and shipping your order without your approval.

Reprint your previous order for gift cards or upload new artwork with the same quantities and options as the last order. Gift card re-orders will be printed within 15 business days.

To reorder gift cards:

Go to the Vagaro Store : Phone : Select More , go to the Purchase Vagaro Products banner, and then select Buy Now . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select More → Purchase Vagaro Products . Computer : Select More → Vagaro Store . At the top of the screen in the Your Custom Cards banner, select Order Now . The order form will open in an Internet browser window. Select the Reorder Gift Cards tile. Select your Reorder Type : Identical Reprint : Reprint the same order you last placed with no changes to the design or quantity. Reprint with Changes : Place the same order as last time but with some changes to the text on the card or business information. You can specify your changes in the Message field when entering your order information later in the form. New Artwork : Reprint the same order you last placed with a new image. Otherwise, there are no changes to the quantity. Enter your order information (fields with an asterisk [*] are required): Company Name* : Enter the name of your business. Account Name : Enter any other names your business is known as, if applicable. Card Quantity : Select how many cards you are ordering. Merchandise Quantity : Select how many gift card envelopes or sleeves you are ordering, if applicable. First Name : Enter the first name of the person placing this order. Last Name : Enter the last name of the person placing this order. Email* : Enter the email address to receive your order confirmation and gift card proof. Phone Number* : Enter the phone number to be contacted if we have any questions about your order. Message : Describe the changes to your gift card from the previous order or any special instructions, questions, or additional details about your order. If you’re reordering gift cards with New Artwork , select Choose File , then select and upload your new image. If your artwork isn’t ready yet, you can email the file later to artwork@ecardsystems.com . Select Submit Order . When you receive your design proof from eCard, please review and approve the gift card design. We cannot proceed with printing and shipping your order without your approval.
ground_truth: You can email the artwork file later to artwork@ecardsystems.com if it isn’t ready at the time of your order.

question: How can I refund a partially redeemed package using the Transaction List report on a phone?
context:
[Article: Fully Refund a Partially Redeemed Package | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18535438378011-Fully-Refund-a-Partially-Redeemed-Package]
If your customer is unsatisfied with a package they've purchased, you can fully refund it, even if some of their visits or redemptions have already been used. This transaction is performed in the Transaction List report , which can show all transactions performed at your business, as well as issue refunds. Prerequisites : A customer must have already purchased and partially redeemed a package. To refund a partially redeemed package: On a Phone To refund a partially redeemed package: Select More → Reports → Transaction List . The system automatically runs the report for today's transactions. At the top of the screen, select Date Range to change the date range of the data. Select the Filters button, then select your filters to reduce the number of transactions to search, then select Run Report . In the Transaction Type list, select Packages . Optionally, in the Search bar, enter all or part of the barcode, brand name, product name, product type, and even transaction ID if you know it. Swipe left on the package to be refunded, then select Refund . Select Refund under the item. Enter the full or partial Refund Amount and the Tip amount to refund, if applicable, then select Next . Optionally, deselect Return to Stock if you are not returning the product to your inventory. Select Next . Optionally, split a refund between two payment methods, such as cash and a gift card, and then select Submit . Review the Refund Summary , then select Refund . Enter a reason for the refund, select whether to Print Receipt or Email Receipt , then select Continue . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To refund a partially redeemed package: Select Reports → Transaction List . The system automatically runs the report for today's transactions. At the top of the screen, select your filters to reduce the number of transactions to search then select Run Report . Optionally, select the Transaction Date , Service Provider (s), and Customer . Expand the Advanced Filters , then in the Transaction Type list, select Packages . Optionally, select any other filters that may help you find the package to be refunded. Hover over the package to be refunded, then select Refund . Select the customer's preferred refund method. Unavailable methods are grayed out. Enter the full or partial Refund Amount . Optionally, if a tip was included, you can enter the Tip refund. Select Next or Confirm . Optionally, split the refund between two payment methods, such as cash and a gift card, and then select Confirm . The refund method fields will update with the amount you entered. Enter the Reason for Refund , select whether to Email Receipt or Print Receipt , then select Refund .
ground_truth: To refund a partially redeemed package on a phone, select More → Reports → Transaction List, set the date range and filters, then swipe left on the package to be refunded, select Refund, enter the refund amount and tip if applicable, then proceed with the refund steps including selecting the refund method and confirming.

question: How do I duplicate a product on a mobile device in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Duplicate a Product | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/27871630917531-Duplicate-a-Product]
Duplicating a product allows you to add another similar item to your Inventory without having to create it from scratch. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses, and you must already have products set up in your inventory . On a Phone To duplicate a product: Select More → Settings → Management . Select the product to go to its Product Details , then in the top-right corner, select Action → Duplicate . The product will be duplicated, and the Add Product screen will appear. The Quantity of the product will be set to 0, and a "-1" will be added to the end of your product's Name and Barcode . Note You cannot duplicate variants . Make changes to your duplicated product as needed, then select Save to add it to your inventory. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To duplicate a product: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . Next to the product to be duplicated, select Action → Duplicate . The product will be duplicated, and the Add Product screen will appear. The Quantity of the product will be set to 0, and a "-1" will be added to the end of your product's Product Name and Barcode . Note You cannot duplicate variants . Make changes to your duplicated product as needed, then select Save to add it to your inventory.
ground_truth: To duplicate a product on a mobile device, select More → Settings → Management, choose the product, then in the top-right corner, select Action → Duplicate. Make any needed changes and then select Save to add it to your inventory.

question: How can I change my legal business name on Vagaro after account creation?
context:
[Article: Australia - Activate Credit Card Processing | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402559712283-Australia-Activate-Credit-Card-Processing]
Choose a Business/Industry Type Select the best industry type that fits your service/class offerings. You can change your industry type after your merchant account has been created.

Register Your Legal Business Name If you are a sole proprietor or individual doing business under your legal name, like Tom Smith, you usually are not required to register your business name . Merchants can change the Doing Business As (DBA) on their account, not the legal business name. If you want to change your legal business name on Vagaro, contact support with documentation supporting that change.

Choosing Your Type of Business When signing up, you must classify how you’ll use our merchant services by selecting your Business Type . If you’ve already set your business type and want to change it, send a request to support@vagaro.com . Companies and Partnerships : For-profit company/partnership Nonprofit : A non-profit company/organization.

Why Wasn't My Application Approved? If your application is denied, our Customer Support team will contact you with more. Please be on the lookout for any emails from our team. There are multiple reasons your application might have been denied: You didn't pass the verification check. question: How can I test if my Vagaro receipt printer is working correctly after setup?
context:
[Article: Setting Up Your Vagaro Receipt Printer | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/9036513964443-Setting-Up-Your-Vagaro-Receipt-Printer]
After setting up your Vagaro receipt printer, you can perform these actions:

Test Your Printer After your Vagaro receipt printer has been installed, make sure it is working correctly by printing a transaction from the Transaction List Report . The first time you select and use your printer, your printer choice will be saved. Going forward, the same printer will be used each time you select Print . To test your printer: Go to the Transaction List Report screen. Vagaro Pro App : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Reports → Sales → Transaction List . On the Web : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to Reports → Sales → Transaction List . Run a report. Select any transaction, and then select Receipt . In the receipt screen, select the arrow button next to Print , and then select your printer. If no receipt is printed, see Troubleshooting Vagaro Receipt Printer .

Connect Your Cash Drawer to Your Printer If you connect your cash drawer to your printer, then whenever a receipt is printed, the cash drawer will automatically open. To connect your cash drawer to your printer: Plug in the provided RJ12 cable into the Vagaro receipt printer and the cash drawer. Test your printer to verify that the cash drawer opens when a receipt is printed.

Rename Your Printer To rename your printer: Go to the receipt printer's settings. Vagaro Pro App : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Connected Devices . On the Web : In the Checkout screen, select the arrow button next to the Checkout , and then select Checkout Settings . Scroll down to the Receipt Printers section, and then select the receipt printer. Select Rename or Rename Printer . Enter a new name for the printer. Select Save .

Remove Your Printer To remove your printer: Go to the receipt printer's settings. On the Web : In the Checkout screen, select the arrow button next to the Checkout , and then select Checkout Settings . Scroll down to the Receipt Printers section, and then select the receipt printer. Select Remove Printer . Select Remove Printer to confirm that you want to remove it. Optionally, add another printer .
ground_truth: To test your printer, go to the Transaction List Report screen, run a report, select a transaction, then select Receipt. In the receipt screen, select the arrow next to Print and choose your printer. If no receipt prints, refer to Troubleshooting Vagaro Receipt Printer.

question: How can I customize which services are displayed in the Services component on my MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: Customize the Services Component and Hide Specific Services | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/22612637681179-MySite-Customize-the-Services-Component-and-Hide-Specific-Services]
With the Services Vagaro components, you can show off the services you sell and have your customers book directly on your MySite. This Vagaro component syncs with your Service Menu on Vagaro, so ensure you've set up your Service Menu before adding this component. Before continuing, we recommend you learn how to place components and the different Vagaro component types . Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . Choose What Services Display on Your MySite With the Services component, you can customize and choose what items on your service menu should be shown on your MySite. To choose what services appear in this component: Select MySite . In the MySite Editor, select the Services component to be customized. The right Style pane will appear for you to customize. In the Services Style panel, scroll down to the Services option and select All Services . Search for and select the services and categories to be included on your website. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish . Customize the Services Component To customize your Services component: Select MySite . In the MySite Editor, select the Review component to be customized. The right Style pane will appear for you to customize. Optionally, to make changes to the content of the component, select Edit in Vagaro . Customize the component as needed: Size : Manually set the width and height of the container. Select the Lock button to lock in an aspect ratio (shape). Location : Choose what business the Services component will generate from. For example, if you have a Service Menu for two of your businesses, select the correct Location . Allow Booking for : Choose whether to allow customers to select a location they want to book with (if you have multiple locations) or just the Location you selected. Layout : Change how each service appears on your website. You can see an example of each layout here. Services : Customize the services shown in this component. Panel Radius : Change the roundness of each panel. Panel Spacing : Change the distance between each panel. Advanced Settings : More advanced users can change the CSS , Shape , and Effects of the component. Customize the color of elements in the Services component: Font Color Title Description Price Panel Color Panel Border Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish . Layout Examples Here are some examples of the different layouts available: Default Horizontal Cards
ground_truth: In the MySite Editor, select the Services component, then scroll down to the Services option in the Style panel. Search for and select the specific services and categories you want to include on your website.

question: How long does it typically take to import my data from Timely to Vagaro after scheduling an import appointment?
context:
[Article: Import Your Business From Timely to Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/20389132330523-Import-Your-Business-From-Timely-to-Vagaro]
Transferring your business to Vagaro is a simple process for any type of business, from large multi-location businesses to solopreneurs. Here's what you can do to move your business over to Vagaro: Self-Service Import : Vagaro provides a user-friendly app and software to easily transfer your customers yourself. All you need to do is upload an Excel file of your customers, and we'll do the rest for you. Free Import Appointment : If you would prefer the professionals to help you bring over your business, you can schedule a free appointment with our Imports Team, and they'll guide you through the entire process. Multi-Location Syncing : For businesses with multiple locations, Vagaro offers a free syncing feature that allows you to easily synchronize all your businesses to a flagship location . Prerequisites : You will need to sign up for a Vagaro business account before scheduling an import. What Can I Transfer Over From Timely? Data Can I Import? Employees ✓ Contracts ✓ Notes ✓ Inventory ✓ Services ✓ Appointments ✓ Packages ✓ Memberships X Gift Cards ✓ Credit Cards on File X Reviews X Request Your Free Import and Vagaro Walkthrough To start your import from Timely, you'll need to schedule an import appointment with Vagaro and provide some information about your previous software. To book your import appointment: Go to Import Management : Phone : Select More → Settings → Import Management . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Import Management . Under Import from Other Software , select Import to schedule your appointment. In Where are you importing from , select your previous software, and then select Find a Time . Choose a time that works best for you, submit the necessary details, and then schedule your appointment. After scheduling your appointment, it typically takes one to three business days to import your information from Timely to Vagaro. Our Imports Team will contact you when your account is ready. During your import appointment, you'll learn more about Vagaro and get set up. Getting Started with Vagaro Moving from one software to another can be daunting. We're here to make it easier for you. Here are some articles and resources to help you get started with Vagaro: Vagaro Resources & Training Vagaro Support & Services What's Included in Your Vagaro Free Trial Download the Vagaro Pro App Log In to Your Vagaro Account Customer Profiles Article What is the Benefit? Create Customer Profiles Develop your customer list and keep track of all your clients. Save Customer Credit Cards on File Save a customer's card on file to charge recurring memberships and packages, or just to make checking customers out that much easier. Create Email Marketing Campaigns to Your Customers Send your customers marketing emails to promote your business. Create Text Marketing Campaigns to Your Customers Send your customers text marketing messages to promote your business. Employee Profiles Article What is the Benefit? Set Up Your Employee Profiles Create a profile for each of your employees to manage their appointments. What Are Employee Types? Learn about the two employee types in Vagaro: Admin and Service Provider. Set Up Access Levels to Restrict Employee Access Limit what pages and settings your employees have access to. Assign Employees to Access Levels Assign your employees to different Access Levels. Service/Class Menu Article What is the Benefit? Create a New Service Show off all the different services you offer. Create a New Class Show off all the different classes you offer. Schedule a Service Appointment Schedule your appointments in-house or over the phone. Schedule a Class Session Schedule your class sessions in-house or over the phone. Credit Card Processing Article What is the Benefit? Accept Credit Card Payments With Vagaro's Credit Card Processing Easily accept credit and debit cards. Comes with a free credit card reader. Our Competitive Credit Card Fees and Rates Great rates, so you can focus on growing your business. Activate Buy Now, Pay Later Buy now, pay later option for your customers. Online Shopping Cart Article What is the Benefit? Create Your Online Store Sell products and take payments online with the Online Shopping Cart. Require a Deposit When Booking Online Charge an upfront deposit to discourage no-shows and last-minute cancellations. Require a Credit Card When Booking Online Require a credit card on file to book an appointment to help speed up the checkout process and protect against cancellations. Automatically Charge Cancellation Fees Charge cancellation fees whenever a customer cancels. Vagaro Marketplace Article What is the Benefit? Your Vagaro Listing Page Get appointments without having to lift a finger. Get Featured With Vagaro Make your business more visible on the Vagaro Marketplace. Set Up Daily Deals Advertise your discounts and deals on the Vagaro marketplace.
ground_truth: It typically takes one to three business days to import your information from Timely to Vagaro after scheduling your appointment.

question: What happens to the employee's name in the customer's appointment notifications when auto-assign is enabled?
context:
[Article: Auto-Assign Employees for Online Bookings | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/15057197724443-Auto-Assign-Employees-for-Online-Bookings]
To make your customer's online booking experience as simple as possible, we recommend auto-assigning service providers to automatically select an employee based on your settings. This feature is especially useful for businesses that pay commission for services and want their bookings auto-assigned. When a customer books an appointment online, or the customer does not select a specific employee when searching for appointments, Vagaro selects an employee based on whether the employee offers the service, has online booking enabled, and is available at the chosen time. This feature only applies to online bookings for services, not classes, and to recurring appointments, though customers may not be assigned the same employee each time. Additionally, once assigned, the employee's name is removed from the customer's booking experience across all notifications and platforms. Customers will not see the selected employee's name in any of their appointment notifications via push, email, or text message, waitlists, or on the customer's Appointment screen . Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. You must already have at least one service set up, and Enable Online Booking must be toggled on for each employee to be considered for auto-assignment. To automatically assign employees when customers book online: Go to Online Appointment Rules : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Under the Booking heading: Toggle on the Auto-Assign Employee When Booking switch. Optionally, in the Online Employee Booking Order list, select how Vagaro will auto-assign employees to bookings. If you don't select an option, the First Available employee will be chosen. First Available : Select the first employee with an available timeslot that matches the customer's search criteria. The customer can review the booking before finalizing the appointment. Website Employee Lineup : Select the first employee in your Website Employee Lineup shown on the Staff tab on your Vagaro Listing Page . If that employee is unavailable, the next employee in the lineup will be selected, and so on. Most Open Schedule : Select the employee with the most availability on that day based on the total hours available, not how many appointments they have. For example, an employee with six hours available will be given priority over an employee with only four hours available. If the most-available employee is fully booked, the next employee with the second-most availability will be selected, and so on. Best Reviewed : Select the employee with the highest rating (out of five). A low-rated employee will only be booked when other employees aren't available. Alphabetical Order : Select the first available employee by first name in alphabetical order (from A to Z). Select Save
ground_truth: Once an employee is assigned through auto-assign, their name is removed from the customer's booking notifications across all platforms, including push, email, text messages, waitlists, and the Appointment screen.

question: How can I unsubscribe from marketing emails from a specific Vagaro business?
context:
[Article: Unsubscribe from Vagaro or Business Communications - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003412914-Unsubscribe-from-Vagaro-or-Business-Communications-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
If you no longer want to receive communications from a Vagaro business, you can opt out by selecting Here at the bottom of their email. You can also choose to receive marketing from businesses you want to hear from in text messages instead of by email by changing your notification settings. Opting out of marketing messages does not disable your appointment notifications unless you change those notification settings. Important By opting out or unsubscribing from a business's marketing, you risk missing out on their important announcements, special promotions, and direct messages. Prerequisites: A Vagaro user account is required to complete this procedure. Unsubscribe from a Business To unsubscribe from a business's marketing email list: At the bottom of the business's email, select Here . The Notification Settings screen in your customer profile is displayed. Select the notifications you wish to receive: Appointment Reminders : Select whether you want to receive reminders for upcoming appointments at the businesses you've booked with in an Email , Text , Push Notifications , or None . You can select any or all notification types. Subscribe to Marketing Campaigns : Select which businesses can send marketing messages to you and whether you want to receive them via Email or Text , or None at all. The businesses you see in this list are only those that you have booked services or classes with, purchased something from, or that have you on their customer list. Important Businesses may occasionally send out forms and other information before and after your appointments. Opting out of marketing campaigns may prevent you from receiving these messages. Vagaro Emails : Select whether you want to receive promotional and marketing emails from Vagaro. Vagaro Text Notification : Select whether you want to receive promotional and marking text messages from Vagaro. Select Save Changes . Stop Appointment Text Notifications Stopping appointment text notifications will block all appointment notifications, confirmation requests, and appointment details from ALL Vagaro businesses. To stop receiving all appointment notifications from Vagaro businesses: Send a text message to 89885 containing one of the following commands: STOP STOPALL UNSUBSCRIBE CANCEL END QUIT Note Message and data rates may apply. To opt back into messages from Vagaro, send another message to 89885 with one of the following commands: START UNSTOP YES Stop Marketing Messages from a Business Stopping all text messages disables your Subscribe to Marketing Campaigns setting for that business until you send another text message to unstop. Usubscribing with this method will only block marketing message from this one business, we recommend updating your notification messages to block marketing messages from all businesses. To stop marketing messages from a specific business, reply to one of their text messages with one of the commands listed below. STOP STOPALL UNSUBSCRIBE CANCEL END QUIT Note Message and data rates may apply. To opt back into messages from that business, send another message to that same number with one of the following commands: START UNSTOP YES
ground_truth: At the bottom of the business's email, select 'Here' to access your notification settings, then choose to unsubscribe from marketing emails or texts from that business.

question: How can I change the default calendar view that appears when I log in to Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Set Calendar Configuration Settings | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347700-Set-Calendar-Configuration-Settings]
Set up your calendar to work the way you do. Choose a default view, organize service providers in your preferred order, and customize how your calendar looks at a glance. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Go to Calendar Settings In Vagaro, you can access your Calendar Configuration settings from two places: within your Vagaro business settings and from the Calendar screen. From Business Settings To go to Calendar Configuration: On a Phone Go to More → Settings → Look & Feel → Calendar Configuration . On a Computer, Tablet, PayPro, or Pay Desk Go to Settings → Look & Feel → Calendar Configuration . Going to Calendar Configuration from business settings allows you to make changes for your entire business. Any changes you make to this setting will apply to all devices logged in to your business, except for the Week View Starting Day and Default View at Login . Changing these two settings will not affect any devices on the Vagaro Pro app. From the Calendar To go to Calendar Configuration: On a Phone : Go to the Calendar , then in the top right corner, select Menu → Settings . On a Computer, Tablet, PayPro, or Pay Desk Go to the Calendar , then in the top right corner, select Calendar Settings . When you open Calendar Configuration from the calendar, you can update certain calendar settings, but not all of them. On the Vagaro Pro app, only Week View Starting Day and Default View at Login are available. These two settings apply only to the device you’re using, so changes won’t affect other devices logged into your business. Overview of Each Setting When you open your Calendar Configuration settings, you will see the following options: Week View Starting Day Choose which day is shown first on the Week view . If you are closed on Mondays, you can set Tuesday as the first day, or you can choose Today to always display the current day first. This setting can be changed from the Calendar or your business settings. On the Vagaro Pro app, changing this setting from the Calendar affects only that device. In all other cases, it applies across your logged-in devices, except for a phone that has its own custom setting. Default View at Login Choose the calendar view you want to see when you log in, such as Day, Week, Month, or Agenda. This view is used only at login. After that, your calendar opens to the last view you were using. This setting can be changed from the Calendar or your business settings. If updated on the Vagaro Pro app from the Calendar, it affects only that device. In all other cases, it applies across your logged-in devices, except for devices with custom settings. Calendar Resolution Control the time interval between each time slot, with options of 5, 10, or 15 minutes. On the Vagaro Pro app, this setting can only be changed in business settings, and you must log out and back in for the changes to take effect. Calendar Line Spacing (Computer Only) Control the spacing between time slots on your calendar. Show Current Time (Computer Only) Show a red line marking the current time on the calendar. Display Resource on Calendar Show the names of resources assigned to classes or appointments on the calendar. On the Vagaro Pro app, this setting can only be changed in business settings, and you must log out and back in for the changes to take effect. Track Customer Retention Track how many new and returning customers visit your business by monitoring retention by appointment type. This data appears in the Customer Retention report, which also breaks down retention rates by appointment type and employee. On the Vagaro Pro app, this setting can only be changed in business settings, and you must log out and back in for the changes to take effect. Label All Calendar Lines (Computer Only) Display a time label for each time slot on the calendar. For example, if your Calendar Resolution is set to five minutes, times appear in five-minute increments. This setting can be changed only in business settings. Service Provider Order on Calendar Change the order in which employees appear on the calendar. This setting can be updated only in business settings. If you are using the Vagaro Pro app, you must log out and log back in for the change to take effect.
ground_truth: You can change the Default View at Login setting from either your Calendar or your business settings. This determines whether the calendar opens in Day, Week, Month, or Agenda view when you log in.

question: How can I add customer names automatically to the subject line of my email campaign?
context:
[Article: Set Up Before-Visit Email Notifications & Reminders | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26573136977051-Set-Up-Before-Visit-Email-Notifications-Reminders]
links; Vagaro will not track clicks to external websites outside of Vagaro. Repeat this step for all applicable buttons. Select Save . Modify your social links: Select the social links component. Select a Social Link Style . The system will populate the social links that are attached to your business. You can edit them from this page; however, this won't update your social links on other pages. Select Save . After you finish designing your email, select Next at the bottom of the screen. Preview your campaign, then select Next to schedule your campaign. Select Send Preview Email to send this preview to your email. Warning Sending an email preview will pull from your Email Marketing plan. In the top-right corner of the screen, select Save to save a draft of this email. Schedule and announce your campaign: Schedule : Set a time for when this campaign will usually be sent out. By default, it is set to 8:00 a.m. Agree to the acknowledgment. Select Announce and confirm the Important Notice or Usage Alert that may appear.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To create this campaign: Go to Marketing , then select Create Campaign under Email & Text Marketing . On the marketing dashboard, select Create Campaign . Next to Select Delivery Method , choose Email . Enter a Campaign Email Subject . This will appear in the subject line of the email your customer receives. Select the Vagaro AI button to enhance your subject with AI. Select the Emoji button to add emojis to the subject. Select Insert Name , then select First Name or First & Last Name to auto-populate the customer's name in the subject. Select the Before Visit campaign type. A slider will appear to select how many days before a customer's appointment you want to send the message. You'll also have to select the services or classes that apply to this campaign. Select the recipients for this campaign: All Customers : Send this campaign to all customers in your Customer List. Filtered List : Send this campaign to a specific, filtered list of customers. Specific Customers : Not available for this campaign type. Optionally, set a Schedule for when this campaign will be sent out to customers. Select Next to begin drafting your email campaign. Choose a template for your email. Each category has different templates, so we recommend looking through all of them before choosing one. Optionally, in the top-right corner of the email editor, select Style to customize the colors and border of your campaign. Select the box next to Page Background to change its color. Select the box next to Body Background to change its color. Select the box next to Border to change its color. You can also change the border thickness as well. By default, each template includes two images, buttons, and social links. You can optionally Move , Edit , or Delete them as needed. To add a module to the message, drag and drop it from the sidebar onto the design window. The Style tab on every edit pop-up provides background and border colors, border size, and padding options. Changes to the background and border color won't take effect unless you select OK in that dialog. Text modules will open a text editor where you can enter and format text for your email, insert customer names , add hyperlinks, and style the text box with background and border colors. Image modules allow you to upload an image or select one from your business's Image Gallery . You can make any image function as a link by adding a URL in the Link to box. Uploading an image will open the Vagaro Image Editor , where you can edit your image. You can also hover over or select an image selected from the gallery, then select Edit to open the editor. The Buttons module allows you to create buttons that link to pages on your Vagaro Listing page and more. You can set button color, height, width, rounded corners, text color, font, and size and add preset or custom links. Each Button module can have up to three buttons, but only the ones you configure will show on the message. Note Adding links to your campaigns allows for tracking the click-through rate and how many customers booked through the link . These metrics are only tracked for Vagaro links; Vagaro will not track clicks to external websites outside of Vagaro. The Social Buttons module lets you add links to social media accounts (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, etc.) The Themes drop-down list gives you six link style selections. Any change to the Theme is reflected in the links shown on the page and the preview. Preview your campaign: Select the Preview or Preview tabs to view how this campaign will look like on a phone or computer. Select Send Email Preview to send a preview of this campaign to your email. Warning Sending an email preview will pull from your Email Marketing plan. Select Save for Later to save this campaign as a draft. Announce your campaign by agreeing to the acknowledgment at the bottom of the screen, then select Announce .
ground_truth: Select Insert Name, then choose First Name or First & Last Name to auto-populate the customer's name in the subject.

question: How do I set up a separate merchant account for retail sales in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Merchant Account Settings - Allow Money Distribution and Retail Merchant Account | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/12373895113243-Merchant-Account-Settings-Allow-Money-Distribution-and-Retail-Merchant-Account]
The Separate Retail/Product Merchant Accounts setting allows you to sell products online or in-house and have the funds deposited into a separate merchant account. Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing . Set Up a Merchant Account for Retail Sales If your business sells products online or in-house, you can create a separate merchant account for product purchases. A separate merchant account for retail sales allows you to divide retail and service transactions into two different bank accounts. Once set up, sales from any items purchased from your Inventory will be deposited into a separate merchant account. Set up a merchant account for retail sales: Go to Credit Card Processing : Phone : Select More → Settings → Credit Card Processing . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Credit Card Processing . Turn on Route Item Sales to a Different Merchant Account . Select a merchant account that will be used for product purchases. You can create a new account by selecting Start Your Credit Card Application . Select Save .
ground_truth: Go to Credit Card Processing: Phone: Select More → Settings → Credit Card Processing, then on a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro: Select Settings → Credit Card Processing, turn on Route Item Sales to a Different Merchant Account, select a merchant account for product purchases, and click Save.

question: How can I require a credit card for customers to book a service online on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Require a Credit Card on File to Book | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/17733255316763-Require-a-Credit-Card-on-File-to-Book]
Requiring a credit card on file for customers to book a service or a class on your Vagaro Listing Page requires credit card capture to be enabled in Vagaro. Doing so also allows them to quickly pay after an appointment, and lets you automatically charge cancellation and no-show fees and take deposits . Credit card capture is an effective way to help reduce income loss and discourage last-minute cancellations and no-shows. Note Vagaro only accepts and processes deposits and fees from Vagaro Merchant Services. When booking online, customers will be prompted to enter their information before they can book. For in-house appointments, a red Required box will appear next to the customer's profile when booking their appointment. You can bypass the credit card requirement by selecting the Required box, then Book Without Credit Card . Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing and the Online Shopping Cart . Require a Credit Card to Book a Service To require a credit card for customers to book a service online: Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Services and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage based on the service price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer appointments : Customers who don't have a customer profile at your business. Old customer appointments : Customers who already have a customer profile at your business. Mobile service appointments : Customers who schedule a mobile service appointment Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. Enter your Required Appointment Cancellation Fee Policy to describe your business's cancellation policy . This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save . Require a Credit Card to Sign Up for a Class To require a credit card for customers to sign up for a class: Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Classes and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage of the class price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer class sign ups : Customers who have never taken the class before. Old customer class sign ups : Customers who have already taken the class. Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. In the Required Class Cancellation Fee Policy box, enter a cancellation policy for your business. This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save .
ground_truth: Go to Online Shopping Cart → Settings → Online Shopping Cart, then scroll down to 'Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Services' and select 'Capture Credit Card.' Provide the minimum service price, cancellation/no-show fee, cutoff time, and select customer types that need to provide a credit card, then click Save.

question: How do I activate a larger Email Marketing Plan on a computer?
context:
[Article: Activate an Email Marketing Plan | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000823427-Activate-an-Email-Marketing-Plan]
An Email Marketing Plan is a subscription that increases the number of email credits your business can use each month. When you begin your Vagaro subscription, you are automatically enrolled in the Small Email Marketing Plan that includes 1,000 credits per month. A popup will notify you if you are approaching the limit and suggest that you purchase a larger plan. You may want to select an Email Marketing Plan if you have a large customer list and wish to create business or brand-level marketing campaigns or announce your Daily Deals . Vagaro's automated messages , including welcome messages, customer invitations to visit your business, business referrals, invoice reminders, requests for customers to fill out forms , appointment notifications and reminders , or notifications for Vagaro Payroll and rent collection . Note If you are currently in a free trial , using Email Marketing or selecting another Email Marketing Plan will end your free trial and begin your monthly subscription. If you're not using the Small email marketing plan and disable this feature before your billing date, you will still be charged for the feature at the end of your billing cycle, regardless of when you deactivated it. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Email Marketing Rates The cost of a larger Email Marketing Plan depends on the number of emails your business plans to send and the country your business is located in: United States (USD) Canada (CAD) United Kingdom (GBP) Australia (AUD) Small 1,000 Credits Free No extra credit Free No extra credit Free No extra credit Free No extra credit Medium 5,000 Credits $10 $0.01/extra credit $15 $0.013/extra credit £10 £0.0075/extra credit $15 $0.014/extra credit Large 7,500 Credits $20 $0.01/extra credit $25 $0.013/extra credit £15 £0.0075/extra credit $30 $0.014/extra credit X-Large 12,000 Credits $30 $0.01/extra credit $40 $0.013/extra credit £25 £0.0075/extra credit $45 $0.014/extra credit XX-Large 50,000 Credits $60 $0.0075/extra credit $80 $0.01/extra credit £45 £0.006/extra credit $85 $0.0105/extra credit Huge 100,000 Credits $90 $0.0009/extra credit $120 $0.0085/extra credit £70 £0.005/extra credit $130 $0.009/extra credit On the Vagaro Pro App To sign up for an Email Marketing Plan: Go to More → Settings → Premium Features → Email Marketing Plans . Select a plan. On a Computer To activate a larger Email Marketing Plan: Go to Settings → Settings → Email Marketing Plans . Select a plan.
ground_truth: To activate a larger Email Marketing Plan on a computer, go to Settings → Settings → Email Marketing Plans and select a plan.

question: What is the main difference between an Email Blast campaign and automatic campaigns in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Email Campaign Types | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412652458779-Email-Campaign-Types]
In Vagaro, there are automatic and blast campaigns. Automatic campaigns continuously send emails to your customers that fit the criteria until they are deactivated. Email blasts are sent to a group of customers once and will not be sent to new customers until the campaign is reannounced. Email blasts also allow you to send an email campaign to a specific group of customers instead of all or a filtered list. Only the Email Blast campaign is a one-time blast . The remaining campaign types are automatic : Birthday Campaigns Lost Customer Campaigns Before-Visit Campaigns After-Visit Campaigns The Different Campaign Types The Email Blast campaign type is a one-time blast that bulk sends messages to a selected or filtered group of customers. The message can include anything from information about a store closure, upcoming sales and events, a daily deal, and so on. The Birthday campaign type allows you to automatically send an email before your customers' birthdays. These emails can include a promo code, a form, a link to your service page, or just a kind message. Once set up, the system will look at the birthdays of your eligible customers and send messages a set number of days before their birthdays. The Lost Customer campaign type allows you to automatically send an email to customers who haven't visited your business in a while. The message can include anything from a call to action to book an appointment, a promo code, a feedback survey form, and so on. A lost customer campaign can be set up to send messages up to 24 weeks (five and a half months) after your customers' last appointment, but these campaigns will not retroactively look back for any customers that were lost before creating this campaign. For instance, if you create a campaign for customers who haven't visited your business in two weeks, the system will not send out campaigns to anyone who hasn't visited in three or more weeks. The system will also not send out this campaign to customers who have future appointments. The Before Visit campaign type allows you to automatically send an email to a customer about their appointment or class session. The message can include anything from advertising products to be purchased in-store, a pre-visit form, or instructions on where to park and how to get to your business. Configure before-visit campaigns to only apply to a specific service or class or your entire service/class menu, and configure your campaign to send from 1 to 30 days before the customer's visit. Customers will receive this campaign only if they are scheduled for the applicable service or class. For instance, if you set up a before-visit campaign that only applies to "Hair Coloring" appointments, your customers won't receive this campaign before, for example, a "men's haircut." This campaign will also not apply to appointments or classes booked before this campaign was created. The After Visit campaign type allows you to automatically send an email to customers who you recently checked out for a service or class. The message can include anything from a call to action to rebook the appointment, a feedback survey form, post-visit care information, and so on. Configure after-visit campaigns to only apply to a specific service or class or your entire service/class menu, and configure your campaign to send immediately or up to 30 days after checking out a customer. Customers will receive this campaign only if they are checked out for the selected services or classes. For instance, if you set up an after-visit campaign for a "Couples Massage" to be sent out 30 days after a visit, but you forget to check out the customer from Vagaro, the 30-day countdown will only begin after you have checked out the customer in the system. This campaign will also not apply to appointments or classes checked out before this campaign was created.
ground_truth: An Email Blast campaign is a one-time message sent to a specific or filtered group of customers, while automatic campaigns, such as Birthday or Lost Customer campaigns, continuously send emails based on set criteria until deactivated.

question: How can I restore customer profiles deleted on or after a specific date using Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Restore a Deleted Customer | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000296213-Restore-a-Deleted-Customer]
Use Undo Delete to bring back customer profiles you removed by mistake, such as a profile you cleaned up during the wrong session or a duplicate you didn't mean to remove. Choose a date, and Vagaro restores every customer profile deleted on or after that date. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To restore a deleted customer: Select More → Reports → Customers . In the top-right corner, select Action → Undo Delete . Select the date from which you want to restore customers, then select Proceed . Note The further in the past you select the date, the more customer profiles will be restored. You will need to delete any unwanted customer profiles restored from this proress. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To restore a deleted customer: Select Reports → Customers . Select Undo Delete . In the Undo Delete pop-up, select the date from which you want to restore customers, then select Proceed . Note The further in the past you select the date, the more customer profiles will be restored. You will need to delete any unwanted customer profiles restored from this proress. In the confirmation pop-up, select OK to confirm the number of customers restored.
ground_truth: To restore customer profiles, select the date from which you want to restore customers and confirm the action. On a phone, go to More → Reports → Customers, then choose Action → Undo Delete, select the date, and proceed. On a computer or tablet, go to Reports → Customers, select Undo Delete, choose the date, and click Proceed, then confirm with OK.

question: How can I add color options as variants to a product on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add Variants to Products | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29333769252379-Add-Variants-to-Products]
Create variants for your products that allow customers to customize their purchases on your Vagaro Listing Page and MySite . Instead of creating a new product for each option, use variants to offer different styles, colors, types, or sizes. For example, you can sell a shampoo in 8.5 oz, 16 oz, and 33.8 oz bottles as one product with volume options. You can also combine multiple variant types, like color and volume for a nail polish, to give customers more flexibility.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses, and you must already have products set up in your inventory .

You can create a new product in your inventory or edit an existing one and add variants to it, allowing customers to further customize their purchase.

On a Phone To add a variant: Select More → Settings → Management . Select the product to go to its Product Details , then in the top-right corner, select Action → Edit . You can also create a new product instead. Under Variants , select Add Options . Enter your Option Name . If you want to use a template instead, select Quick Add Option and choose a template in the list. Notice how a list of prepopulated Variants appears. Next to Display Option As , select Right-Arrow > , then choose an option in the list: Chips : Display the options for this product as selectable boxes. Dropdown : Display the options for this product as a dropdown list. Image : Display the options for this product as selectable images. You can then add an image to each option by selecting Upload . Color Swatch : Display the options for this product as selectable colors. You can then choose a color for each option by selecting Plus + . Here's how each option will look on your product's listing page. Enter a name for your variants, like "small" or "blue." To add an additional variant, select Add option . To change the order of the variants, select Reorder , select Move , drag it to a new spot, and then select Done . To remove a variant, select X next to it. Select Save → Save to update your product. Repeat these steps to add additional variants to your product. All possible variant combinations will be shown under your product in your Inventory, and when checking out that product, your customer can make a selection based on your variants. By default, any variants you add will copy over the price, product images, quantity, and any other tracking you set for that product. You can customize each variant as necessary.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a variant: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . In the top-right corner, select Action → Edit to edit it. You can also create a new product instead. Under Variants , select Add Options . Enter the variant's Name . If you want to use a template instead, select Quick Add and choose a template in the list. Notice how a list of prepopulated Variants appears. In the Display Option as list, choose an option: Chips : Display the options for this product as selectable boxes. Dropdown : Display the options for this product as a dropdown list. Image : Display the options for this product as selectable images. You can then add an image to each option by selecting Upload . Color Swatch : Display the options for this product as selectable colors. You can then choose a color for each option by selecting Pencil . Here's how each option will look on your product's listing page. Enter a name for your variants, like "small" or "blue." To add an additional variant, select Add Option . To change the order of the variants, select Move and drag them to a new spot. To remove a variant, select X next to it. Select Save → Save to update your product. Repeat these steps to add additional variants to your product. All possible variant combinations will be shown under your product in your Inventory, and when checking out that product, your customer can make a selection based on your variants. By default, any variants you add will copy over the price, product images, quantity, and any other tracking you set for that product. You can customize each variant as necessary.
ground_truth: To add color options as variants, select 'Color Swatch' as the display option, then choose a color for each variant by selecting Plus + or Pencil, depending on the device you're using.

question: How can I view or reprint a customer's receipt from the Transaction List Report?
context:
[Article: Transaction List Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347940-Transaction-List-Report]
to be included in the report, or Select All . Transaction Type : Select one or more transaction types to include in the report. Checked Out By : Select one or more employees who checked out transactions, or Select All . CC Transaction Mode : Select one or more credit card transaction modes. Discount : Select one or all discounts that were applied to transactions. Deposit Status : Select one or more deposit statuses to include in the report. Search : Search and select specific products by barcode, brand, product, product type, or transaction ID. Show refunded items only : Whether to show only refunded transactions. Include Past Employees : Whether to include entries of past employees in the report. Sort By : Organize your report based on specific criteria. Review the transactions in the report. You can also perform the following tasks: Sort the transactions by selecting the column headers at the top of the report. View the transaction's receipt by selecting the transaction ID in the Checkout Date/Checkout By/Transaction ID column. Alternatively, scroll to the far right of the report, then select Receipt . You can then reprint or send it to the customer via email. View the customer's profile by selecting the customer's name in the App.Date/Customer column. Refund the customer by scrolling to the far right of the report, then select Refund . If the transaction was paid using a credit card, prevent the card from being charged again at your business (for example, if the customer reported the card as stolen) by scrolling to the far right of the report, then select Block Card . At the bottom of the screen, you can select: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only).

After running the report, you can view the transaction details of all purchases made during the date range you set. A separate item will be listed for every item purchased, fee charged, and transaction refunded.
ground_truth: Select the transaction ID in the Checkout Date/Checkout By/Transaction ID column or scroll to the far right of the report and select Receipt to view, reprint, or send the receipt to the customer via email.

question: How can I confirm a customer's attendance for an upcoming class session using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Register and Manage Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29078318111387-Register-and-Manage-Class-Attendees]
Registered customers will either need to confirm that they'll be attending the class or, depending on the business's settings, the business will need to Accept their registration request. You'll only need to accept registration requests if you have Require Acceptance for Online Booking turned on in your Online Appointment Rules . Your customer will receive a notification after they're approved.

On the Vagaro Pro App To manage class attendees: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select the session name in the menu. Select an attendee and choose a status: Accept : Approve a customer's class registration request. Confirm : Confirm that the customer will be attending the upcoming class session. The customer can confirm themselves through automatic notifications sent to their phone or email. Note If your attendee's status is Requested , then they're not officially an attendee and need to be accepted.

On a Computer To manage class attendees: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the class repeats, select Edit only this occurrence → Edit Class . Under the Attendee list, select Action next to an attendee and choose an option: Accept : Approve a customer's class registration request. Confirm : Confirm that the customer will be attending the upcoming class session. The customer can confirm themselves through automatic notifications sent to their phone or email. Note If your attendee's status is Requested , then they're not officially an attendee and need to be accepted. Select Save .

If you have Require Acceptance for Online Booking turned on in your Online Appointment Rules , customers must be approved by the business after registering online. Customers who requested a class session will appear as "Requested" on the class's attendee list. You can Deny a customer's request for reasons such as if the class is full or they have a history of not showing up for their class. Denying an attendee will remove them from the attendee list, and they will be notified of the denial.

On the Vagaro Pro App To manage class attendees: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select the session name in the menu. Select an attendee with the "Requested" status, then select Deny . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, continue to mark the attendee as denied. If the customer is enrolled in multiple classes that day, cancel the customer’s enrollment for all classes or only the ones you select. If an attendee fully paid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose to give a refund. See Refund Prepaid Classes to learn more. Enter an optional Message to Attendee about why you denied the request, then choose to DON'T NOTIFY ATTENDEE or NOTIFY ATTENDEE . The attendee will be removed from the list.

On a Computer To manage class attendees: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the class repeats, select Edit only this occurrence → Edit Class . Under the Attendee list, select Action → Deny for the attendee. Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, continue to mark the attendee as denied. If the customer is enrolled in multiple classes that day, cancel the customer’s enrollment for all classes or only the ones you select. If an attendee fully paid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose to give a refund. See Refund Prepaid Classes to learn more. Enter an optional Message to Attendee about why you denied the request, choose whether to Notify Customer , and then select Deny Enrollment . Select Save . The attendee will be removed from the list.
ground_truth: To confirm a customer's attendance using the Vagaro Pro App, select the class session on the calendar, tap the session name in the menu, select the attendee, and then choose 'Confirm' to verify that the customer will attend the session.

question: How can an administrator sign an employee's pending form using the Unsigned Forms report?
context:
[Article: Sign Forms From the Unsigned Forms Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/40257469908891-Sign-Forms-From-the-Unsigned-Forms-Report]
With Vagaro Forms, you can require an employee’s signature , such as a provider signing off on a treatment consent form. When you send a form to a customer, only the customer fields display. Any employee signature fields remain hidden. After the form is submitted, any employee assigned to sign it will receive a notification in the Notification Panel or can view it in the Unsigned Forms report. For internal forms, employees can sign immediately. If you’re an administrator and need an employee to sign, open the form from the Unsigned Forms report. The system will show which employees can sign and prompt them to log in. You can hand the device to the employee, have them enter their credentials, and provide their signature securely. On a Phone The Unsigned Forms report shows a list of all forms that still require an employee signature. The report includes details such as which employee is authorized to sign, the customer linked to the form, and the date the form was originally completed. From the Unsigned Report, you can view To view unsigned forms: Go to More → Reports → Customers → Unsigned Forms/SOAPs . The system will show you any unsigned forms for the current month. Optionally, to view a different month, change the Date Range of the report. Optionally, to filter your report, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set your filters, and then select Run Report : Employees : Filter the report for specific employees. Customer : Filter the report for specific customers. Form Name : Filter the report for specific forms. Signature Status : Filter the report to view recently signed forms instead of unsigned forms. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, select the multi-location businesses or all locations in a group you want to review. Sort By : Sort the report by specific criteria, like form name and customer. Select the form to be signed. If you don't have permission to sign this form, a screen will appear prompting another employee to sign in. Sign the form, then select Save . Optionally, to export your report, select Action , then choose an option: Star or Remove Star : Add or remove a bookmark to this report in the Starred list at the top of the More → Reports menu. Print : Print your report if your device is connected to a printer. Export PDF or Export Excel : Save the report to a PDF or Excel file. The employee's signature is stored in the customer's form response. You can view form responses from the customer's profile or the Form dashboard . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro The Unsigned Forms report shows a table of all forms that still require an employee signature. The report includes details such as which employee is authorized to sign, the customer linked to the form, and the date the form was originally completed. To view unsigned forms: Log in to Vagaro, then go to Reports → Customers → Unsigned Forms/SOAPs . The system will show you any unsigned forms for the current month. Optionally, change the date range and report filters, then select Run Report . You can select the following filters: Date Range : Change the date range for this report. Employees : Filter the report for specific employees. Customer : Filter the report for specific customers. Advanced Filters : Optionally, select any additional filters to further refine the report. Form Name : Filter the report for specific forms. Signature Status : Filter the report to view recently signed forms instead of unsigned forms. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, select the multi-location businesses or all locations in a group you want to review. Next to the form to be signed, select Action → Sign Form . If you don't have permission to sign this form, a popup will appear prompting another employee to sign in. Sign the form, then select Save . At the bottom of the screen, you can select: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only). The employee's signature is stored in the customer's form response. You can view form responses from the customer's profile or the Form dashboard .
ground_truth: An administrator can open the form from the Unsigned Forms report, which shows the employees authorized to sign. If the administrator has permission, they can log in, sign the form, and then select Save.

question: What permissions does an employee need to view and edit their own time card entries?
context:
[Article: Configure Access Levels and Employee Permissions | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977275541531-Configure-Access-Levels-and-Employee-Permissions]
Reports > Appointments , Inventory , and Messaging/Billing sections. Modify : N/A

Ability to run others' reports

View : Employees can view other employees’ reports related to service, class, and product sales, as well as the reports in the Reports → Customers → Gift Cards Reports > Appointments , Inventory , and Messaging/Billing sections. Modify : Employees can change the provider credited with an appointment on the Transaction List .

Multi-Location Reports : These permissions apply only to businesses that have multiple locations . To view the Multi-Location filter in report screens, employees must be added to other locations to view that location’s reports, and they will need View access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to Run Others' Reports .

View : Employees can view Sales reports for other business locations linked to their account. Modify : N/A

Time Card Screen : Employees can always add time entries using the clock in/out function on a computer, Pay Desk, or PayPro.

View : Employees can view historical time card entries for all employees in Reports → Customers → Time Card screen, as well as add manual entries through the report. Previous entries cannot be edited. Modify : Employees can edit or delete other employees’ previous time card entries.

Ability to edit own time card

View : N/A Modify : Employees can edit or delete their own entries in the Reports → Employees → Time Card screen.

Gift Certificate : Gift cards created in the Gift Certificates report instead of at Checkout are not paid for by customers; they're just given to the selected recipient as store credit.

View : Employees can view all cards created by the business in the Reports → Customers → Gift Cards screen. Modify : Employees can create new cards and edit/void existing ones .

Packages : This permission controls the ability to manage individual packages sold to customers. To manage access to the base package settings, see the Settings permissions for Packages .

View : Employees can view existing and previous packages sold to customers in Reports → Customers → Packages screen. They can also view usage and renewal history. Modify : Employees can make edits to existing customer packages. This includes modifying the price, auto-renew limit and frequency, expiration date, and balance for included services and classes. They can also cancel customer packages.

Memberships : This permission controls the ability to manage individual memberships sold to customers. Access to base membership settings is managed in Settings permissions for Memberships .

View : Employees can view existing and previous memberships sold to customers in the Reports → Customers → Memberships screen, as well as usage and renewal history. Modify : Employees can edit existing customer memberships, including the price, auto-renew limit and frequency, expiration date, and balance for included services, classes, and products. They can also cancel customer memberships.

Cancellations & No shows Report : Employees will also need View access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to Run Others' Reports to see cancellation history.

View : Employees can view customer cancellations, no-shows, and denied or deleted appointments/classes. Modify : N/A

Control his/her own Push Notification History : Employees will always be able to view their own push notification history.

View : N/A Modify : Employees can delete records from their own push notification history.

Control Other People's Push Notification History

View : Employees can view other employees' push notification history. Modify : Employees can delete records of other employees’ push notification history. View access for Manage Others’ Profile is also required to view other employees’ push notifications.

Pending Shipment Report :

View : Employees can view pending shipments and shipping history for customer product purchases in the Reports → Inventory → Pending Shipments screen, but they cannot change shipping status or view breakdowns of orders. Modify : Employees can change shipping status and add tracking information for product shipments to customers, as well as view breakdowns of orders.

Ability to Run Own Payroll : Employees will also need Modify access to run others’ payroll to mark payroll paid in the Payroll screen or delete records in the Payroll History screen .

View : Employees can view their own payroll settings and reports in the Reports → Employees → Payroll , Reports > Payroll , Payroll Configuration , and Payroll History . Modify : Employees can change payroll configuration settings and modify their own hourly pay/commission structure.

Ability to Run Others' Payroll : Employees will also need Modify access for Ability to Run Own Payroll to run their own payroll to mark payroll as Paid or delete records in the Payroll History screen.

View : Employees can view other employees’ payroll settings and reports in the Reports → Employees → Payroll , Payroll Configuration , and Payroll History screens. Modify : Employees can change payroll configuration settings and modify other employees’ hourly pay and commission structure.

Dashboard : Employees will need View access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to Run Others' Reports to see sales information.

View : Employees can view the Dashboard and all widgets. The data shown will depend on their access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to
ground_truth: Employees can view their own time card entries without additional permissions, but to edit or delete their own entries, they need Modify access in Reports → Employees → Time Card.

question: How do I add an employee profile on my phone using Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add an Employee Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977275440795-Add-an-Employee-Profile]
When you add an employee profile, a Vagaro account is automatically created for them. The account activates when they log in for the first time using the email address you entered. Once their profile is set up, only the employee can edit their personal information. If they forget their password, you can change it for them , or they can reset it themselves .

Every employee, regardless of position, should have their own Vagaro account and be assigned an access level. This keeps your business account secure, helps track who made changes, and protects your business’s and customers’ personal and financial information.

There is an additional monthly charge for each calendar you add, up to seven licenses. After seven licenses, you can add more employees at no extra cost. If there aren’t enough licenses for all employees, you’ll be prompted to purchase another. The additional monthly charge for each bookable calendar differs by country:

United States : $10 per month Canada : $15 per month United Kingdom : £10 per month Australia : $15 per month
