Important Multi-location businesses will need to complete additional setup when creating employee profiles .

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

On a Phone To add an employee profile: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Note If your business has multiple locations , employee profiles from all businesses will appear. Select Add Employee . Enter the employee's First Name , Last Name , Email , and Confirm Email . Only these fields are required; you can fill out any optional information as needed: Note If an employee already has a Vagaro account, they will be prompted to enter their Vagaro password, and that account will be added to your business. Under the Personal Information heading, enter the following information: Profile Picture : Optionally, upload a photo of this person so your customers can see what your team member looks like. Gender : Personal employee information that is not shared with customers. Address Line 1 : The employee's street number and name. As you type, the address line will autofill with the address's city and zip code. To change the employee's country of residence, select the flag and choose the country. Address Line 2 : The employee's room, unit, suite, or apartment number if they live in a multi-unit building. Birthday : Personal employee information that is not shared with customers Phone Number : Send text messages and notifications about their customers’ appointments. Note Employees who have toggled off text notifications in their employee profiles will not receive text messages about appointment notifications or other text messages from your business. Show contact email and phone on Vagaro : Whether to make this team member's information visible to customers on your Vagaro Listing Page in the Staff tab. Under the Company Information heading, enter the following information: Start Date : The day your employee started working at your business. Note Once added to their profile, an employee's Start Date can only be modified by the business owner or the employee's manager ( Reports To ). Reports To : This employee's manager. By default, the business owner is selected and will need to be changed if authorization is required for employee actions, such as addin a personal task or paid time off on their calendar. Employee Bio : A personal description of the employee, up to 1000 characters long, about the employee that will be viewable by customers on the business's Vagaro Listing Page . For example, share the employee's experience and training and why they are qualified to perform their services. Under Business Notifications , toggle whether your want the employee to receive Email Notifications , Text Notifications , and/or Push Notifications . Note Employees who have toggled off email notifications in their employee profiles will not receive any of these emails, text messages, or notifications. Under Marketing Notifications , toggle whether you want the employee to receive Marketing Emails from my Business , Vagaro Emails , or Vagaro Text Notifications . Select Next . Enter the employee's Login Information settings: Username : Automatically filled with the employee's email address. Password and Confirm Password : Only the employee will know what the password is. Security Question and Security Answer : If this employee contacts Vagaro Support, the agent will verify their identity by asking the selected question, and they must provide the answer entered. Enter the employee's Employee Access settings: Employee Type : The type of Vagaro user your employee is at your business. Service Provider : Team members who manage their customers and appointments on the Vagaro calendar and perform services. They may have different working Hours than the business, and they can choose to allow Online Booking and Share Calendar . There is a license fee for each service provider added, other than the business owner's, for up to seven calendars, after which it is a flat fee to add an unlimited number of service providers. Account Admin : Team members, such as receptionists, accountants, and cleanup staff, who do not provide services to customers. This employee
ground_truth: To add an employee profile on your phone, select More → Settings → Employee Profiles, then tap Employees to view all profiles, and choose Add Employee. Enter the employee's First Name, Last Name, Email, and Confirm Email, along with any optional information needed.

question: How does adding emojis or non-Latin characters affect the character limit per message segment?
context:
[Article: How Vagaro Calculates Credit Usage for Text Messages | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/27469123270555-How-Vagaro-Calculates-Credit-Usage-for-Text-Messages]
When you send a text through Connect by Vagaro or as part of a text campaign , the cost for each message is measured in credits from your Text Messaging plan . The number of credits required for each message depends on how many segments it contains. A segment is a portion of your message that fits within the character limits set by SMS networks, and each segment costs one credit. Short messages fit into one segment, but longer ones are split into multiple segments so they can be sent and reassembled on the recipient’s device. How Text Messages are Calculated Messages are divided into segments, and segments are divided into characters. Characters include letters, numbers, spaces, symbols, and emojis. Standard letters, numbers, and symbols such as $, #, and @ count as one character each. Basic emojis count as two characters, modified emojis (like skin tones) can count as four, and complex emojis made of multiple joined symbols can count as 15 or more characters. Each segment has a character limit, and the number of characters per segment depends on the characters used in your message. Using emojis, certain special characters, and non-Latin languages (such as Chinese) can reduce the number of characters allowed per segment. Message Type Characters per one-segment message Characters per credit for multi-segment messages Maximum Length Standard Message ( GSM-7 ) 160 153 1,600 Message with Emojis and Unsupported Languages* ( UCS-2 ) 70 67 700 * As soon as one emoji, special, or non-Latin character, like Chinese or Japanese, is added to a message, the character limits change. How Longer Messages are Sent SMS transmits a 161-character standard message in two segments: one with 153 characters and a second with 8 characters. If you're unsure how many credits your message will require, copy and paste your message into this Message Segment Calculator . Sending Messages to Multiple Customers at Once How Images and GIFs Affect Credit Usage Adding media, like images and GIFs, changes how credits are calculated. Instead of being charged per segment, media messages use a flat rate: Country Credit Cost per Message Character Limit United States and Canada 3 credits Up to 1,600 Australia 15 credits Up to 1,600 United Kingdom Not supported n/a Important Recipients must have a mobile data plan to receive media. Tips to Manage Credit Usage If you’re running low on credits or planning a large text campaign, check how many credits you have left and how many your message will use before sending it. While creating your campaign, the number of credits required per message appears directly below the Compose Text box. Keep messages within the single-segment limit (160 characters for standard text, 70 for messages with emojis). Avoid unnecessary emojis or special characters. Review the segment counter before sending campaigns. Use media only when it adds clear value to your message.
ground_truth: Adding emojis or non-Latin characters reduces the number of characters allowed per segment, with standard messages allowing 160 characters per segment and messages with emojis or unsupported languages allowing 70 characters per segment.

question: How can I copy a direct link to book a specific service or class on Vagaro using a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Create Links to Things You Sell | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4565143139739-Create-Links-to-Things-You-Sell]
Every service, class, product, membership, and package you create in Vagaro has a corresponding direct link or URL on your Vagaro Listing Page for customers to add to their shopping cart and check out right away. This link can also be shared with your customer directly through a social media post or on other Vagaro platforms like MySite , Connect by Vagaro , and text and email messages and campaigns. The link can also be specific to book with and purchase from specific employees. Prerequisites : At least one service, class, membership, product, or package must be created to copy the respective link for it. Copy a Link to a Service or Class Copying and providing the unique URL for a service or class will take customers directly to your Vagaro Listing Page to book it for today's date and show the availability of their selected employees. Customers can change the date and employee before they finalize their booking. On a Phone To copy the direct link to book one of your services or classes: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Services or Classes . Select the service or class, then select Share Link . In the Create LInk screen, you can: Select Employees to choose who this link can be used to book with. Note Only employees assigned to perform the service or class can be selected. Select Test Link to ensure the link goes to the correct place. Select Share Link , then copy the link or send it to someone to someone directly. On a Computer, Pay Desk, or PayPro To copy the direct link to book one of your services or classes: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. Optionally, to view your class menu, select the Classes tab. Select the Action menu next to service, then select Create Link . In the Create Link popup, you can: Select the Employees list to choose the employee this link can be used to book with. Select Test Link to make sure the link goes to the correct place. Select Copy Link . Copy the Link to a Product Customers who select the link to your product will automatically have it added to their shopping cart on your Vagaro Listing Page for quick checkout. On a Phone To copy the direct link to purchase one of your products: Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Inventory . Select the product in your inventory. In the top-right corner of the screen, select the Action menu, then select Share Link . Optionally, select Test Link to ensure the link goes to the correct place. Select Share Link , then copy the link or send it to someone to someone directly. On a Computer, Pay Desk, or PayPro To copy the direct link to purchase one of your products: Go to Settings + Inventory → Management . Select the Action menu for the product, then select Create Link . Optionally, select Test Link to ensure the link goes to the correct place. Select Copy Link . Copy the Link to a Membership Customers who select the link to your membership will automatically add it to their shopping cart on your Vagaro Listing Page for quick checkout. Note Copying a link to a package can be performed only on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro versions of Vagaro and requires Credit Card Processing and Online Shopping Cart . To copy the direct link to purchase one of your memberships: Go to Settings → Things We Sell → Memberships . Select the Action menu for the membership, then select Create Link . Optionally, select Test Link to ensure the link goes to the correct place. Select Copy Link . Copy the Link to a Package Customers who select the link to your membership will automatically have it added to their shopping cart on your Vagaro Listing Page for quick checkout. Note Copying a link to a package can be performed only on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro versions of Vagaro and requires Credit Card Processing and Online Shopping Cart . To copy the direct link to purchase one of your packages: Go to Settings → Things We Sell → Packages . Select the Action menu for the package, then select Create Link . Optionally, select Test Link to ensure the link goes to the correct place. Select Copy Link .
ground_truth: On a phone, go to More → Settings → Service/Class Menu, select Services or Classes, choose the service or class, then select Share Link. In the Create Link screen, tap Share Link to copy the link or send it directly.

question: How do I add pages to a folder in my MySite drop-down menu?
context:
[Article: MySite: Create a Drop-Down Menu in Your Navigation Bar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26945941352987-MySite-Create-a-Drop-Down-Menu-in-Your-Navigation-Bar]
Create folders on your MySite to group pages together in a drop-down menu in your navigation bar. Your folder will appear like this on your top navigation bar: Prerequisites : This feature requires MySite. To set up your first website, see Getting Started with Vagaro MySite . To create a drop-down menu in your navigation menu: Select MySite . In the left design panel, select the Pages tab. Select Add Folder . Enter a name for your folder. Add pages to your new folder: Hover over a page, select the Move button, and then drag that page and drop it under the folder. Under the folder, select Add Page to create a new page. Select Save . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: Hover over a page, select the Move button, and then drag that page under the folder in the Pages tab.

question: What is the typical timeframe for a business to dispute a chargeback after being notified in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: What are Chargebacks, and How Do I Prevent Them? | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360016551733-What-are-Chargebacks-and-How-Do-I-Prevent-Them]
A chargeback happens when a cardholder contacts their bank or credit card company to dispute a transaction. If the claim is valid, the bank reverses the payment and deducts the funds from the merchant's account with the chargeback fee .  You can either accept the chargeback (treat it like a refund) or dispute the chargeback . If you submit documentation to dispute the chargeback, we will forward your response to the issuing bank.  The issuing bank is responsible for reviewing your rebuttal to the chargeback and making the ultimate decision to reverse or allow the Chargeback to stand.  If the issuing bank decides to reverse the chargeback, they will remit the funds through the payment system to be deposited back into your processing account (this can take several days to complete). While chargebacks protect consumers from unauthorized or fraudulent transactions, they can be challenging for merchants, often resulting in lost revenue and fees. If you have further questions, contact our Support Team .

Businesses often face chargebacks for reasons such as:

Fraud : The customer claims the transaction was unauthorized. Product/Service Issues : The customer was unhappy with the quality or delivery of goods or services. Merchandise/Service Not Received : The customer claims they did not receive the product or service they paid for. Customer Confusion : The customer doesn't recognize the charge on their statement. No Refund Policy Disputes : The customer disagrees with or was unaware of your business policies .

Vagaro does not initiate a chargeback and does not have control over the final decision.  We will assist you in submitting your rebuttal to the chargeback . When submitting your dispute, we prepare the information that is stored on our system as part of your rebuttal.  If you do not accept or dispute the chargeback in time, we will submit the documentation that we have access to on our systems related to the transaction in response to the Chargeback.  There is no guarantee this information will be sufficient on its own to win the rebuttal.

The timeframe from when the chargeback is filed and the time limit for you to dispute is short, so it's important to be vigilant. The timeline from when a chargeback is filed to when you need to dispute a chargeback is as follows:

File a Chargeback : Depending on the card network (Visa or MasterCard), customers typically have 120 days from the transaction date to file a chargeback. Business Dispute : Once a chargeback is filed, your business is notified in Vagaro , and the original transaction amount plus the chargeback fee is deducted from your next deposit. Once notified, you have around eight days to dispute ; otherwise, a dispute will automatically be submitted to you. The timeframe may differ depending on the card network. Businesses in the United States may have their chargebacks auto-defended by our processing partners in some circumstances. Auto-defended businesses will receive a provisional credit covering the amount disputed by the customer. Note If your chargeback isn't automatically defended, businesses that dispute a chargeback themselves may receive provisional credit in some cases, especially with MasterCard card holders. Bank Investigation : The customer's bank reviews the evidence submitted by both parties and will decide whether to uphold or reverse the chargeback. This process can take weeks or months to process. Dispute Resolution : If the customer's bank rules in your favor, the amount disputed will be deposited back into your account with your daily batch deposits. The chargeback fee will not be refunded. If you lose the dispute, any provisional credits will be deducted from your deposits.
ground_truth: Once notified in Vagaro, you have around eight days to dispute the chargeback.

question: How long does it take to receive my cash advance after approval from Vagaro Capital?
context:
[Article: Apply for Vagaro Capital in Canada | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21897083104539-Apply-for-Vagaro-Capital-in-Canada]
It will take two business days* to complete the review process and receive your cash advance. When you're approved, Vagaro Capital deposits your funds into the bank account used for credit card processing. You will receive a notification in your Notification Panel when you receive your funds.

You can also view your application status on the Vagaro Capital Settings page:

Phone : Select More → Settings → Vagaro Capital . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Vagaro Capital .

Changing your bank account after receiving funding with Vagaro Capital is not recommended .

While unlikely, you are not guaranteed the cash advance that you apply for. You may be denied funding or receive a lower amount than requested based on your credit profile and processing volume.

*In June 2023, the percentage of approved customers funded in 48 business hours was 75%.

After paying 50 to 100% of your Vagaro Capital funding, you may be eligible for renewed funding. When eligible, you will receive the following notification in your Notification Panel .

To learn more, see Renew Vagaro Capital Funding FAQ .

Vagaro Capital Help & FAQs Vagaro Blog: Unlock Business Growth With Vagaro Capital
ground_truth: It takes two business days to complete the review process and receive your cash advance after approval.

question: How can I manually set my business hours on Vagaro if I am not using employee working hours?
context:
[Article: Set Your Business Hours | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/34418132184091-Set-Your-Business-Hours]
Set your business hours in your Business Profile settings to show when you're open or closed on your Vagaro Listing Page . Note The Business Hours setting does not appear if you have only one Calendar license , usually the business owner's. Instead, the business owner's or employee's working hours is used instead. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . To set your business hours: Go to your Business Profile : Phone : Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Business Profile . Scroll down to Business Hours . Optionally, toggle Use Employee Working Hours to base the business's hours on your employees' hours . You cannot manually set working hours from the Business Profile if you toggle this setting. You can also manually set what days and times your business is open: Day of week : Choose the days of the week your business is open. Opens at : Choose what time your business opens each day. Closes at : Choose what time your business closes each day. Select Save .
ground_truth: To manually set your business hours, go to your Business Profile settings, scroll down to Business Hours, select the days your business is open, and specify the opening and closing times for each day, then click Save.

question: How can a business add or update an appointment note for an existing appointment in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Manage Appointment Notes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010452434-Manage-Appointment-Notes]
Appointment notes help keep track of special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for an upcoming appointment. These notes can be added by either the business when booking the appointment or by the customer when they book their appointment online, but they are visible only to the business. Any appointments with a note are marked with an Appointment Note icon on the Calendar. Here's an example of how a customer would enter an appointment note online. Appointment Notes differ from Customer Notes because they contain information about the customer and are stored in their profile. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Add a Note When Booking an Appointment You can add a note to an appointment when you're scheduling it. On the Vagaro Pro App To add an appointment note at the time of booking: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Select a time slot on the Calendar, then select New Appointment . Choose a service, customer, and any other details for this service. Enter an Appointment Note . Select Book . On a Computer To add an appointment note at the time of booking: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Select a time slot on the Calendar, then select New Appointment . Choose a service, customer, and any other details for this service. Enter an Appointment Note . Select Book . Add or Edit an Appointment Note to an Existing Appointment If you want to add a note to a customer's existing appointment, you can do so from the calendar. You can also edit any existing notes. On the Vagaro Pro App To add or edit a note to an existing appointment: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . Under Appointment Notes , enter or update your customer's note. Select Save . On a Computer To add or edit a note to an existing appointment: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Note . Under Appointment Notes , enter or update your customer's note. In the top-right corner of the popup, select X to save. Viewing an Appointment Note You can view appointment notes on the Calendar. Any appointments with a note will show an Appointment Note icon in the appointment timeslot. On the Vagaro Pro App To view appointment notes: Select an appointment on the Calendar. The note appears below the customer's name and appointment summary. On a Computer To view appointment notes: Hover over an appointment on the Calendar to view the appointment's summary. The appointment note is located at the bottom of the popup.
ground_truth: To add or update an appointment note for an existing appointment, go to the Calendar, select the appointment, then choose Edit (on the app) or Note (on a computer), enter or update the note under Appointment Notes, and then save.

question: How can I add a new item to my store's list if I only want to keep track of its label and price, without selling it online?
context:
[Article: Add a Product to Sell In-House | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/16713434779291-Add-a-Product-to-Sell-In-House]
If you're creating a product but don’t plan to sell it online or track its quantity , only the product's barcode, name, brand, and price are required.

On a Phone To add a product: Select More → Settings → Management . In the top-right corner, select Action → Add → Product . Scan your product's barcode with your camera or enter it in the text box. Select Next to continue. Note Scanning a barcode that Vagaro recognizes will automatically populate its name, brand, and images into your product's details. If you want to make changes, you can edit the product as needed. Enter your Product Information : Enter the Name of your product so it's easy to identify when checking out customers. This name will also appear on your Vagaro Listing Page if you sell products online . Choose a Brand from the preset brand options or add a new one . Brands help categorize your inventory and make it easier to filter products in reports and on your Vagaro Listing Page . In the Category list, choose the categorization that best fits your product. Categories group products by type, which makes it easier for customers to browse and for you to manage inventory and sales reports. Choose a Vendor . You can create a new vendor if you haven't already. If you don't have a vendor, select No Vendor . Optionally, under Variants , select Add Options to add varieties of the same product . Under Pricing , configure the cost of your product and whether you offer points: Enter the Business Cost and Selling Price of your product. Tip Business Cost isn't the price you're selling the product for; it's what you paid to stock it. Your sales reports will use Business Cost to calculate profit. In the Tax list, choose any tax rates that apply to this product. Enter the Points Given and Points to Redeem if you have a points system . Select Save . Your new product will appear in the Inventory Management screen and can be added to the customer's cart at Checkout .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a product: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . In the top-left corner, select Add (or +) → Product Scan or enter your product's barcode, then select Next : With a Barcode Scanner : Plug the scanner into your device, press and hold the power button, and hold it up to the barcode until you hear a beep. The code will appear in the text box. Without a Scanner : Manually enter the product ID in the text box. On a Tablet or PayPro Mini : Select Scan , then point your device's camera at the barcode to scan it. Note Scanning a barcode that Vagaro recognizes will automatically populate its name, brand, and images into your product's details. If you want to make changes, you can edit the product as needed. Enter your Product Information : Enter the Product Name . This name will also appear on your Vagaro Listing Page if you sell products online . Choose a Brand from the preset brand options or add a new one . Brands help categorize your inventory and make it easier to filter products in reports and on your Vagaro Listing Page . In the Category list, choose the categorization that best fits your product. Categories group products by type, which makes it easier for customers to browse and for you to manage inventory and sales reports. Choose a Vendor . You can create a new vendor if you haven't already. If you don't have a vendor, select No Vendor . Optionally, under Variants , select Add Options to add varieties of the same product . Under Pricing , configure the cost of your product and whether you offer points: Enter the Business Cost and Selling Price of your product. Tip Business Cost isn't the price you're selling the product for; it's what you paid to stock it. Your sales reports will use Business Cost to calculate profit. In the Tax list, choose any tax rates that apply to this product. Enter the Points Given and Points to Redeem if you have a points system . Select Save . Your new product will appear in the Inventory Management screen and can be added to the customer's cart at Checkout .
ground_truth: You only need to input the product's barcode, name, brand, and price if you're creating it for in-house purposes without online sales or quantity tracking.

question: How can I sell a digital gift card with an auto-generated number using Vagaro on a computer?
context:
[Article: Sell Gift Cards | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18627015674011-Sell-Gift-Cards]
Selling gift cards in-house does not have the same requirements as selling them online. Any business using Vagaro can do this. You can sell your own physical gift cards or create digital gift cards that you can email to your customers or print out for later use.

On a Phone To sell a gift card: Go to the Checkout screen. Select a customer. Select Shopping Cart → Add Item → Gift . Enter a unique gift card number, then select Done : If you're selling a physical gift card, enter the Gift Card # or select Scan to use your device's camera to scan it. If you're selling digital gift cards, you can Auto Generate the gift card number. Enter the gift card's Price and choose a design. If you don’t want to assign a value, you can choose a Service/Class/Add-On instead. This limits the gift card so it can only be redeemed for the specific service, class, or add-on you select. Any designs you've uploaded will appear in Business Created ; otherwise, you can use any of Vagaro's template designs. You still need to select a design if you scanned a physical gift card. Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, then select Add to Cart → Done . Expires On : The expiration date of your gift card. Note Every state has its own regulations for gift cards. These regulations can vary from when they expire to whether they are redeemable for a cash value. To help you follow safe business practices and provide you with more information on these regulations, visit https://www.ncsl.org/financial-services/gift-cards-and-gift-certificates-statutes-and-legislation . Message : The message shown on the digital gift card. Send Now : Sends the gift card to the recipient after checkout. You can toggle this option off if you have a recipient selected and choose a scheduled delivery day and time. Email Gift Card to Recipient : Send this gift card to the recipient's email. Print Gift Card : Print this gift card. From Checkout , take payment for the gift card.

On a Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To sell a gift card: Go to the Checkout screen. Select a customer, then select Gift . Enter a unique gift card number, then select Done : If you're selling a physical gift card, enter the Gift Card # or select Scan to use your device's camera to scan it. Note If you're on a PayPro or Pay Desk, you won't see the option to scan. However, you can use a barcode scanner if you have one. If you're selling digital gift cards, you can Auto Generate the gift card number. Enter the gift card's Price and choose a design. If you don’t want to assign a value, you can choose a Service/Class/Add-On instead. This limits the gift card so it can only be redeemed for the specific service, class, or add-on you select. Any designs you've uploaded will appear in Business Created ; otherwise, you can use any of Vagaro's template designs. You still need to select a design if you scanned a physical gift card. Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, then select Done . Expires On : The expiration date of your gift card. Note Every state has its own regulations for gift cards. These regulations can vary from when they expire to whether they are redeemable for a cash value. To help you follow safe business practices and provide you with more information on these regulations, visit https://www.ncsl.org/financial-services/gift-cards-and-gift-certificates-statutes-and-legislation . Message : The message shown on the digital gift card. Send Now : Sends the gift card to the recipient after checkout. You can toggle this option off if you have a recipient selected and choose a scheduled delivery day and time. Email Gift Card to Recipient : Send this gift card to the recipient's email. Print Gift Card : Print this gift card. From Checkout , take payment for the gift card.

On a Computer To sell a gift card: Go to the Checkout screen. Select a customer, then select Gift Card . Enter a unique Gift Card Number : For physical gift cards, manually enter the number or scan the barcode with a barcode scanner. For digital gift cards, select Auto Generate . Enter the gift card's Amount and choose a Template Type . If you don’t want to assign a value, you can choose a Service/Class/Add-On instead. This limits the gift card so it can only be redeemed for the specific service, class, or add-on you select. Any designs you've uploaded will appear in Business Created ; otherwise, you can use any
ground_truth: To sell a digital gift card with an auto-generated number on a computer, go to the Checkout screen, select a customer, click 'Gift Card,' then choose 'Auto Generate' for the Gift Card Number, enter the amount, select a template type, and proceed with the sale.

question: How do I remove an attendee from a class session using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Register and Manage Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29078318111387-Register-and-Manage-Class-Attendees]
If a customer calls your business to cancel their class, you can cancel it directly from the Calendar. Marking an attendee as cancelled will remove them from the attendee list.

On the Vagaro Pro App To remove an attendee from a class: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select the session name in the menu. From the list of attendees, select the attendee to remove, then select Cancel . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, continue to mark the attendee as cancelled. If the customer is enrolled in multiple classes that day, cancel the customer’s enrollment for all classes or only the ones you select. If an attendee fully paid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose to give a refund. See Refund Prepaid Classes to learn more. In Cancel Enrollment , enter the following information: Message to Attendee : The reason why their session was cancelled. This message will appear in an email, text, and push notification to your customer if you choose to notify them. Cancellation Fee : A fee charged to your customer because they cancelled their class. You can enter a fee, or a value may be prepopulated if you have automatic cancellation fees configured. See charge a cancellation fee to learn more. Notify Attendee : Notify your customer of the cancellation via email, text, and push notifications. You can't turn off this setting if you charge a cancellation fee. Add to Cancellation History : Mark this cancellation in their profile so you know which customers cancel the most. If you entered a Cancellation Fee , choose a payment method. Note Sending an invoice or going to the checkout screen will automatically mark the attendee as cancelled once checkout is successful or the invoice is sent. You can skip the next step. Select Cancel Enrollment or Charge Fee . If you have a fee automatically entered, you can select Do Not Charge . Cancel any other attendees as needed, then select Save to return to the calendar. The attendee was removed from the class session. If a fee is charged, a receipt will be sent to the customer's email as well.

On a Computer To remove an attendee from a class: Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the class repeats, select Edit only this occurrence → Edit Class . Under the Attendee list, select Action → Cancel for the attendee to remove. Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, continue to mark the attendee as cancelled. If the customer is enrolled in multiple classes that day, cancel the customer’s enrollment for all classes or only the ones you select. If an attendee fully paid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose to give a refund. See Refund Prepaid Classes to learn more. In Cancel Enrollment , enter the following information: Message to Attendee : The reason why their session was cancelled. This message will appear in an email, text, and push notification to your customer if you choose to notify them. Cancellation Fee : A fee charged to your customer because they cancelled their class. You can enter a fee, or a value may be prepopulated if you have automatic cancellation fees configured. See charge a cancellation fee to learn more. Notify Attendee : Notify your customer of the cancellation via email, text, and push notifications. You can't turn off this setting if you charge a cancellation fee. Add to attendee's cancellation history : Mark this cancellation in their profile so you know which customers cancel the most. Select Cancel Enrollment or Charge Fee . When a cancellation fee is required, the checkout screen appears, allowing you to collect the fee before completing the cancellation. Cancel any other attendees as needed, and then select Save to return to the calendar. The attendee will be marked as cancelled.
ground_truth: To remove an attendee using the Vagaro Pro App, select the class session on the calendar, open its details, select the session name, then choose the attendee to remove and tap Cancel. Complete any optional steps, then mark the attendee as cancelled and save.

question: What is the fee to restore my files after deactivating Vagaro Drive?
context:
[Article: Restore Deleted Drive Files | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/35864572615451-Restore-Deleted-Drive-Files]
When you deactivate Vagaro Drive , the system deletes all stored files from your account, making them no longer accessible. If you want to access those files again, you must reactivate Vagaro Drive and restore your files. Restoring your files requires a one-time $50 fee. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses that previously activated Vagaro Drive . To restore your files: Go to Vagaro Drive : Phone : Select More → Settings → Vagaro Drive . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Vagaro Drive . Select Get Started . Select the storage plan you prefer, then select Buy . The Restore Your Deleted Files popup will appear. Select Restore Files . Important If you choose Not Now , your stored files will be deleted, and you will not be able to retrieve them later .
ground_truth: The fee to restore your files is a one-time $50 charge.

question: How do I create a time off request on the Vagaro calendar?
context:
[Article: Request Time Off From Your Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21475806122267-Request-Time-Off-From-Your-Calendar]
Creating time off requests is like creating a personal task on your calendar and shown in the same color. When you request time off, you will see a Time Off Summary that shows you the Current Balance of your time off, current Request hours, and Total Hours Remaining after your request has been approved. If your employer’s time off policy allows for unlimited time off, your Total Remaining Hours will be shown as a negative number to track how many hours you've taken. Note Customers will still be able to book with you until your time off request has been approved by your manager. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To request time off from your calendar: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Go to the day you want to add the task , select any timeslot to open the menu, and then select Personal Task . In the popup, provide the following information: Event Type : Select Request Time Off . Time Off : Select the time off type that you are requesting. Note If you can't see the time off type you are requesting, contact your employer; you may not have been assigned to a time off policy yet (requires your employer to be subscribed to Vagaro Payroll ). From and To : Select the start and end dates of the time off request. From and To : Select the start and end times for the requested days off. Add Comment : Enter any comments or notes about the requested time off, and add any files if needed. Select Request . A message at the top of the screen states, “Your time off request has been submitted. You should hear from your employer shortly.”
ground_truth: To create a time off request, go to the Calendar screen, select the desired calendar, choose the day and timeslot, select Personal Task, then Event Type: Request Time Off, specify the time off type, start and end dates and times, add comments if needed, and finally select Request.

question: How can I reward my clients for sharing their opinions about my business online?
context:
[Article: Give Customers Points for Reviews | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000922014-Give-Customers-Points-for-Reviews]
In addition to giving points to your customers for referring new clients and paying for services and classes , you can also let customers earn points by leaving a review on your Vagaro Listing Page . Depending on your settings, customers can redeem their points for the full or partial amount of points you require to purchase additional services, classes, and even products. Tip To prevent unknown users and other people who've never visited your businesses from leaving reviews, you can limit reviews to only verified customers of your business in your Online Appointment Rules . Note Customers can earn points for service bundles, memberships, and packages, but they cannot redeem points to purchase them. Points cannot be earned or redeemed for Daily Deals. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . To give customers points for leaving a review on your Vagaro Listing Page: Go to Taxes & Checkout . Vagaro Pro App : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Things We Sell → Taxes & Checkout . Web Version : Go to Settings → Things We Sell → Taxes & Checkout . Scroll down to the Other Payment Methods section. Enter the amount of points you want to give your customers if they write a review: Select Save .
ground_truth: You can give customers points for leaving a review on your Vagaro Listing Page, which they can later use to purchase services, classes, or products.

question: How can I add a Vagaro Class to my class menu on a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Add a Vagaro Class or Workshop | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977244070811-Add-a-Vagaro-Class-or-Workshop]
Your Vagaro Classes and workshops define your business type with keywords in their names and descriptions so customers find you and your classes more easily in the Vagaro Marketplace. Classes and workshops are set up and scheduled the same way, except that customers attend just one class session , whereas customers attend all sessions in a workshop . If you can't find a suitable Vagaro Class name or want a more creative name, you can set up custom classes . You can sell them individually, in a bundle , package , or as part of a membership both in-house and on your Vagaro Listing Page .

Note Your classes will not show on your calendar or on your Vagaro Listing Page until they've been scheduled on your calendar and the service provider allows online booking .

While managing your menu, you can search your classes by entering part or all of a word to see exact and partial matches at the top of the left-side menu, then select the one you want to update. Alternatively, press Ctrl +K on a PC, or press Command + K on a Mac to move your cursor to the Search bar.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

On a Phone To add a Vagaro Class or workshop to your class menu: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Classes to view your class menu. At the top of the screen, verify that the class is being created for the correct employee, select the Filters button to choose another employee, or select the shown name to change it to another employee. At the bottom of the screen, select Add , and then select Class . Select Vagaro Class . Alternatively, select Custom Class to use a different name. Scroll through the list of classes, select the ones that you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the classes' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the class's category, select the class you just added. Add the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your class after you have completed setting up your class menu): Class Name : The name of the class. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Class because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the class teaches and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Class because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Sell Price and Live Stream Price : The cost of one class session in-house or online and must be greater than the Business Cost . Optionally, select Add Price Break to allow customers to book additional classes at a discounted rate. For example, the cost of one Pilates class could be $30, but you could entice customers to return for multiple sessions by lowering the cost of a class to $25 if they also pay for the next 6 sessions in one transaction. Duration : Amount of time blocked off on the service provider's calendar to teach the class. Select Save . Repeat steps 3 to 9 to add additional classes to your menu. To rearrange the order of the classes or change their categories on your Vagaro Listing Page, drag the class's handle into place.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a Vagaro Class or workshop to your class menu: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. Select the Classes tab. Select Add , and then select Class . Select Vagaro Class . Alternatively, select Custom Class to use a different name. Scroll through the list of classes, select the ones that you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the classes' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the class's category, select the class you just added. Add the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your class after you have completed setting up your class menu): Class Name : The name of the class. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Class because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the class teaches and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Class because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Price and Live Stream Price : The cost of one class session in-house or online. Optionally, select Add New Session to allow customers to book additional classes at a discounted rate. For example, the cost of one Pilates class could be $30, but you could entice customers to return for multiple sessions by lowering the cost of a class to $25 if they pay for the next 6 sessions. Select the employee(s) to teach the class. Classes can also be assigned to employees in their Employee Profile by selecting Services below their profile picture. Performed by :
ground_truth: To add a Vagaro Class on a mobile device, select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu, then tap Classes to view your menu. At the top, verify the correct employee, use Filters to select another if needed, then tap Add and choose Class. Select Vagaro Class or Custom Class, pick the classes to add, and tap Save.

question: How can I edit a recurring class session on the Vagaro Pro App and what happens if I choose to edit only this occurrence?
context:
[Article: Edit Class Sessions and Workshops From the Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360023385054-Edit-Class-Sessions-and-Workshops-From-the-Calendar]
You can edit class sessions directly from the calendar. For example, you can update the start time, instructor, or duration of a class. If the class is part of a repeating series or a workshop , you can update multiple sessions at once instead of modifying each session individually. Recurring classes are marked with a Repeat icon on the calendar. In addition to editing classes, you can also reschedule them , change their statuses , remove them from the Calendar , and add attendees . Prerequisites : This feature requires a scheduled class on the calendar. On the Vagaro Pro App To edit a class session or workshop on the calendar: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the class repeats or is part of a workshop, choose to Edit This Occurrence or Edit Series . Note Keep in mind that editing an individual class or appointment that's recurring will unlink it from the series, and any future changes to the series will have no effect on this appointment or class. You can identify unlinked items on your Calendar by looking for the No Repeat icon. From the Class Details tab, make changes to the class as needed. If you're editing an individual class session, manage the attendees you've already registered , or select Add Attendee to register more . Go to the Attendees tab to manage the customers you've already registered or select Add Attendee to register additional attendees. Note If you changed any Class Details , make sure you save before registering any new attendees. You can only go to the attendees tab if you're editing an individual class session. Go to Class Details , choose whether you want to Notify Attendees , and then select Save . On a Computer To edit a class session or workshop on the calendar: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If the class repeats or is part of a workshop, choose to Edit only this occurrence or Edit the series . Note Keep in mind that editing an individual class or appointment that's recurring will unlink it from the series, and any future changes to the series will have no effect on this appointment or class. You can identify unlinked items on your Calendar by looking for the No Repeat icon. Make changes to the class or workshop as needed. If you're editing an individual class session, you can manage the attendees you've already registered , or select Add Attendee to register more . To not notify attendees, toggle off Notify Attendee . Leaving this toggle on will send a push notification, an email, and a text message to your customer. Select Save .
ground_truth: To edit a recurring class session on the Vagaro Pro App, select the class on the calendar, then choose 'Edit' and select 'Edit This Occurrence' to modify only that specific session. If you choose to edit only this occurrence, it will unlink from the series, and future changes to the series will not affect this session.

question: What should I do if a customer doesn't arrive for their appointment and I want to make sure they are billed for missing it?
context:
[Article: Check In, Check Out, or No-Show Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022751974-Check-In-Check-Out-or-No-Show-Class-Attendees]
Sometimes, customers don't show up to their class sessions. If you want to discourage that behavior, mark your customers as a no-show and charge a no-show fee to their credit card.

You can mark attendees as no-shows with the Group Check-In feature. This allows you to mark multiple attendees as a no-show simultaneously and give them all a message. However, you cannot charge a No-Show fee with this method. You'll need to go to the attendee list and individually mark each missing attendee as No-Show.

If multiple attendees don't show up to their appointment, you can mark them all as No-Show from group check-in.

Note These steps cover how to mark multiple attendees as no-shows without charging a no-show fee.

On a Phone To mark an attendee as a no-show: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the Calendar, then select Check-In . A list of all attendees for this class will appear. Select No-Show for each attendee who didn't arrive for the class. Select Apply to mark the attendees as no-show. The No-Show Enrollment popup will appear. Enter an optional message to all attendees that no-showed. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. If you don't want to send a message, unselect Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment . If any attendees haven't paid for their class yet, one customer will be randomly selected and added to the Checkout screen. You'll need to check out each attendee individually if multiple attendees haven't paid for their class yet.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To mark an attendee as a no-show: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the Calendar, then select Check-In . A list of all attendees for this class will appear. Select No-Show for each attendee who didn't arrive for the class. Select Apply to mark the attendees as no-show. The No-Show Enrollment popup will appear. Enter an optional message to all attendees that no-showed. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. If you don't want to send a message, unselect Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment . If any attendees haven't paid for their class yet, one customer will be randomly selected and added to the Checkout screen. You'll need to check out each attendee individually if multiple attendees haven't paid for their class yet.

When you mark an individual customer as no-show, you can also charge a fee to their card to deter and prevent future no-shows.

Tip No-show and cancellation fees are at risk of being charged back by the customer. To prevent chargebacks , we recommend charging a deposit before the service .

On a Phone To charge a no-show fee: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select the session name in the menu. The Attendee list will appear. Select the attendee to be changed, then select No-Show . Enter an optional message to all attendees who did not show up. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. Enter a No-Show Fee by entering the fee amount or selecting a percentage of the total ( Fee % ). Charge a no-show fee by selecting Please select a payment method . If you have Credit Card Processing , send an invoice to the customer or use a card on file. Use one of the customer's packages or membership visits to pay for the fee. If you don't have Credit Card Processing , use another payment method from the Checkout screen. Select Save & Apply when you're ready to charge the fee. If you want to notify this customer of the no-show, select Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To charge a no-show fee: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If this is a repeat class session, select Edit only this occurrence to view the list of attendees for the class. Under the Attendee list, select Action next to an attendee and choose an option: Select No-Show . Enter an optional message to all no-show attendees. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. Enter a No-Show Fee for your customer: If you want to notify this customer of the no-show, select Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment or Charge No-Show Fee . If you charged a no-show fee, the Checkout popup will display for you to check the attendee out.
ground_truth: You can mark the attendee as a no-show and enter a fee to charge their credit card, either individually or for multiple attendees, to discourage future absences.

question: How can I prevent customers from booking appointments for family members or friends on my Vagaro listing?
context:
[Article: Block Family & Friend Bookings | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/31455291960987-Block-Family-Friend-Bookings]
Customers who book with your business from your Vagaro Listing Page can also book an appointment for a family member or friend . If you don't want customers to book for others, you can turn this feature off in your Online Appointment Rules settings. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . To block family and friend bookings: Go to Online Appointment Rules : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Appointment Rules . In the Booking section, turn off Allow Family and Friends Booking . Select Save .
ground_truth: Go to Online Appointment Rules in your settings and turn off 'Allow Family and Friends Booking,' then save the changes.

question: How can I ensure cookies are enabled on my iPhone Safari browser?
context:
[Article: Enabling Cookies | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204346900-Enabling-Cookies]
What are computer cookies? A cookie is a piece of data that a website puts on the device so the website can remember you when you visit again. Cookies are stored on your computer by websites you visit and contain information such as site preferences or login status. Computer cookies are intended to enhance the experience of Internet navigation. Click the link below for information on cookies in these browsers: Microsoft Edge Google Chrome Firefox Safari Safari On your iPhone or iPad, go to your phone Settings . In the left side menu, select Safari . Scroll down to Privacy & Secuity and make sure Block all Cookies is turned off. See this article from Apple support on how to enable cookies on Mac: Google Chrome Go to Chrome's tools menu , then Settings . Go to the Privacy and Security tab. Select Cookies and other site data . Set your options for cookies. Click the help icon for more information. Firefox Go to the Firefox tools menu . Select Options . Select the Privacy & Security tab. Scroll down to Cookies and Site Data . Click the Learn More links for detailed instructions. Note Cookies are enabled by default in Firefox. Microsoft Edge Go to the tools menu . Go to the Cookies and site permissions tab. Under All permissions , look at the settings in Cookies and site data . Cookies are allowed in Edge by default.
ground_truth: Go to your phone Settings, select Safari from the left side menu, then scroll down to Privacy & Security and make sure Block all Cookies is turned off.

question: If I want to keep in touch with my clients even when they're not logged into Vagaro, what should I consider?
context:
[Article: Is Connect by Vagaro Free? | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26168595985179-Is-Connect-by-Vagaro-Free]
While Connect by Vagaro does not require a Text Messaging plan to reply to customer messages or chat with coworkers, we highly recommend purchasing at least the small text plan to utilize all the feature's functionality. This way, you can send direct SMS messages to your customers without relying on them being active on Vagaro.com or the app. The logic for when a chat will or will not use your plan is the following: Chatting with team members (Free): Chatting with team members at your business is free and does not require a text marketing plan. Note that you can start a chat with a team member only if they have an employee profile created at your business . Multi-location businesses can chat with employees at other locations in their organization as well. Customer receives or sends a message as a text message (Uses text plan): A creditwill be deducted from your plan for each message you exchange with your customer, including any messages they send you. All messages initiated by the business will be sent as text message and will use your text plan. Note More than one message may be deducted from your text plan, depending on the length of the message sent/received, if any emojis or unsupported languages were used, or if any images were added. See here to learn more. Customer starts a chat through Vagaro (Free): Chats initiated by your customers through your booking page will not incur charges or require a Text Marketing plan. Once the customer ends the chat and closes the page, a messaging plan will be required to continue chatting with them. Important Your text messaging plan will be used if a customer replies to any Text Marketing campaigns or sends a direct text message to the number provided by Vagaro. Customer receives a message through Vagaro (Free): Any replies or chats to your customer while they are active on the Vagaro website or app will not use your text plan. Businesses without a text messaging plan will see a popup if a text plan is needed to continue messaging the customer. However, having a Vagaro Text Marketing plan lets you message your customers all the time instead of needing them to be active on Vagaro.com or the Vagaro app.
ground_truth: You should consider purchasing a text messaging plan, as it allows you to send direct SMS messages to your customers regardless of their activity on Vagaro.

question: If I stop using my calendar app with Vagaro, will my upcoming appointments still show up?
context:
[Article: Sync Vagaro with Google Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/31275501148699-Sync-Vagaro-with-Google-Calendar]
Vagaro's Google Calendar integration keeps your Vagaro and Google calendars aligned in real time with instant syncing. By connecting your accounts, you can organize all appointments, tasks, and class sessions in one place, ensuring nothing gets overlooked. The process allows for flexibility, including syncing past events, sharing details between calendars, and customizing privacy settings for added control.

Important Vagaro currently supports calendar syncing only with Google Calendar. If your business previously connected Vagaro to Outlook or Apple Calendar, your appointments will continue to sync as long as those connections remain active. However, once you disconnect an Outlook or Apple Calendar integration, it cannot be reconnected.

Prerequisites : This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses with a Google Calendar account.

In Vagaro, you have the option to Sync Google Calendar to Vagaro and/or Sync Vagaro to Google Calendar . The logic for syncing events and appointments from Vagaro or Google Calendar differs depending on where the event originated.

Note If you disconnect your calendar from Vagaro, turn off syncing, or reassign it for any reason, all Google events will be removed from your calendar, unless they were converted to a service or class.

Syncing Vagaro -> Google Syncing Google -> Vagaro Personal tasks, appointments, and class sessions are transferred over to Google as Calendar Events , and any changes to those events will not sync back to Vagaro. Repeating appointments in Vagaro will also repeat in Google Calendar. Customer profiles added to your appointments are not added to the event as a Guest . Blocked-off timeslots on your calendar will be brought over with the "busy" visibility setting in Google Calendar. Personal tasks that don't block customer bookings will be synced over with the "free" visibility setting . Any events marked as "free" will allow customers to book during that timeslot. Google Calendar Events are transferred over to Vagaro as a Personal Task; any changes made to that personal task will stay in sync until it is converted to a service or class in Vagaro. Repeating events in Google Calendar will also repeat in Vagaro. The emails of any customers invited to your event as a Guest will be brought over to Vagaro when converting the Personal Task to an appointment or class. Information added to the event, like the date, time, and description, will be brought over to Vagaro. Any events marked as "busy" will block customer booking for that timeslot on Vagaro. Any events marked as "free" will allow customers to book during that timeslot.
ground_truth: No, if you disconnect your calendar, all Google events will be removed from your calendar unless they were converted to a service or class.

question: How can I check in a customer using the Check-in app without a membership card or QR code?
context:
[Article: Check In Customers From the Check-in App | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41484332880795-Check-In-Customers-From-the-Check-in-App]
The Check-in app makes it easy to welcome customers as soon as they arrive. Whether they’re scanning a membership card, entering their name, or signing in for an appointment or class, the app helps you manage the process quickly. You can also check in appointments, prompt customers to complete required forms, or cancel appointments and classes when needed. Prerequisites : This feature requires the Vagaro Check-in app . Check Your Customers In Check in your customers directly from the check-in app. Once successfully checked in, their visit will be recorded in the Check-in Log report and in their customer profile in the Check-Ins tab. An upcoming appointment or class is not required to check in. For example, if you sell a gym membership, you can track who's coming and going from your business without needing to book services or classes. To check in your customers: Log in to your business account from the Check-in app. Bring up the customer's account through one of the following ways: With Membership Card or QR Code : Use your device's camera to scan your customer's membership card or QR code . Without Card or QR Code : Select Check in , enter your customer's name and select them, have them enter their password, and then select Check In . Depending on your settings , the option to purchase a new membership may appear. Have your customer choose a membership or select Skip , if applicable. Once, checked in. The customer's profile will show any services or classes they have scheduled for that day. You can help them check in their bookings or schedule a new one for them . Mark an Appointment or Class as Checked-in To mark an appointment or class as checked-in: After bringing up the customer's profile, next to their booking, have them select Check-in . Optionally, if a form is required, it will prompt them to fill it out. Once you've checked in, a successful check-in popup will display. From the popup, select Done . The appointment or class has been checked in. It will show a Checked In status from the Check-in app, and the appointment or class will be marked as Show in Vagaro as well. Once the customer completes their class, you can check them out from Vagaro, if applicable. Cancel an Appointment or Class for Them To cancel an appointment or class for your customer: After bringing up your customer's profile, next to their booking, have them select Action . The button to check in the appointment will change to Cancel Appointment or Cancel Class . Have them select Cancel Appointment or Cancel Class . Have them select OK to confirm the cancellation. The appointment or class will be removed and can no longer be checked in.
ground_truth: Select Check in, enter the customer's name, select them, have them enter their password, and then select Check In.

question: How can I set up a repeat session or service with my favorite provider without creating a new appointment each time?
context:
[Article: Rebook a Service or Class- for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008506194-Rebook-a-Service-or-Class-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
Easily rebook your favorite services or class sessions on the Vagaro app or website, whether you're scheduling a follow-up appointment with a preferred provider or reserving another class session.

To rebook a class or service, you'll need to sign in to your Vagaro customer account . If you don't have a Vagaro account, you can contact the business, and they will send you your login information.

Did you enjoy the service you received from a business? You can rebook that service with the same service provider directly from your profile.

Vagaro App To reschedule an appointment: Log into your Vagaro account on the Vagaro app . At the bottom of the screen, select Appointments . On the Past tab, select Rebook in the appointment. Choose a timeslot that works for you: Tip Don't see an appointment time that works for you? If the business allows, you can add yourself to the waitlist . Selecting a timeslot will bring you to the booking screen. Finalize your appointment: If you're booking for someone else, select a family, friend or pet in Who Are You Booking For . Under About your appointment , enter any notes or requests to the business. Review and accept the business's cancellation policy (if applicable). Note Please enter your credit card information if the business requires deposits, credit card holds, or prepay for bookings. Some businesses may require a deposit or card on file before scheduling an appointment. You'll need to enter or use a credit card on file or use another payment method to pay for the deposit. Select Book or Request to schedule the appointment.

Web Version same comments To reschedule an appointment: Log into your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . In the top-right corner, hover over or select your profile, then select Appointments . On the Past tab, select Rebook in the appointment to begin booking your new appointment. Choose a timeslot that works for you: Tip Don't see an appointment time that works for you? If the business allows, you can add yourself to the waitlist . Selecting a timeslot will bring you to the booking screen. Finalize your appointment: If you're booking for someone else, select a family, friend, or pet in Who Are You Booking For . Under About your appointment , enter any notes or requests to the business. Review and accept the business's cancellation policy (if applicable). Note Please enter your credit card information if the business requires deposits, credit card holds, or prepay for bookings. Some businesses may require a deposit or card on file before scheduling an appointment. You'll need to enter or use a credit card on file or use another payment method to pay for the deposit. Select Book or Request to schedule the appointment.
ground_truth: You can rebook a service or class directly from your profile if you enjoyed the previous experience with the same provider.

question: How can I make it easier for clients to see what my place looks like and get a sense of atmosphere?
context:
[Article: Vagaro Features Included in Your Base Subscription | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977274427803-Vagaro-Features-Included-in-Your-Base-Subscription]
Booking Widget : A widget on your independent business website that customers can use to book an appointment on your Vagaro Listing Page . You can customize its appearance and get the code to embed the widget and enable the booking function on your site.

Daily Deals : Offer discounted services, classes, memberships, bundles, and packages to promote your business. These deals will appear on the Vagaro Marketplace and your Vagaro Listing Page . You can also post the deal on your social media pages.

Email Marketing* : Get 1,000 free credits to send custom emails directly to your client base. You can select from a group of high-quality designs and templates and customize the messages. When you're ready, select whether to send the emails to all or just a filtered list of customers once or as recurring, automated emails.

Get Featured : Bump your business to the top of the Vagaro Marketplace to show off your business and get found by new customers when they search for services in your area.

Mobile App : The Vagaro app that customers can download at the App Store and Google Play for customers to find and book with your business on Vagaro.

Portfolio : An online album of up to three photos to showcase your services and promote them on your Vagaro Listing Page and the Vagaro Marketplace . If approved, your images may also be featured on Vagaro's Social Media pages.

Social Media Integration : Link your Vagaro Listing Page or add a custom widget on your social media accounts so that customers can book an appointment when they visit your business on TikTok , Facebook , Instagram , Yelp , X (formerly Twitter) , Pinterest , LinkedIn , YouTube , and even your blog site.

Venue Gallery : Show customers how your business looks inside and outside your facility. You can add up to 100 photos, including a logo image, automatically added to your receipts, forms, and Vagaro Listing Page .
ground_truth: You can add a venue gallery with up to 100 photos, including images of your interior, exterior, and logo, which will be displayed on your listing and receipts.

question: How do I set the TSP 100 Printer as the default printer in Windows after installation?
context:
[Article: Star ETHERNET Printer Setup - Windows Computers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347360-Star-ETHERNET-Printer-Setup-Windows-Computers]
Installing the Printer Drivers Select the link below to download the drivers: https://vsupport.azureedge.net/forums/tsp100_v750_lite.zip Extract All. Open the folder and double-select Autorun.exe . Select Installation on the screen that pops up. Select I Accept on the next screen that appears. Select Yes on the User Access Control screen. From there, follow the instructions on the wizard to finish installing the drivers. Printer Configuration in Windows 8 Connect the printer to the router using the supplied ethernet cable, then turn on the printer. Enter LAN & Bluetooth Setup Tool in the search box at the bottom of the screen. Select the option once it appears. A pop-up may appear, asking you if you want to make any changes to your device. Select Yes . A new window will pop up, and the system will conduct an automatic search for the printer. After it searches and locates the printer, select Add printer queue . Setting up The Default Printer Search for Control Panel in the search box and select it to open the Control Panel . Select View devices and printers . Right-select the TSP 100 Printer icon and select Set as Default Printer . Once the TSP 100 Printer is set as default, right-select the icon again, then select Printer Properties . When the Printer properties menu appears, select Device Settings . On the Device Settings tab, follow the settings below, then select OK . Setting up the Receipt on Firefox From Firefox, go to www.Vagaro.com Select on the More drop-down menu. Select Options . Select Privacy & Security . Under the Permissions section, select Exceptions . Enter Vagaro.com in the Address of website field, then select Allow . Select Save Changes . Printer Settings Configure the Page Setup before printing receipts. Select the More drop-down menu. Select Print . Select Page Setup . In the Format & Options tab, set the following: Make sure Orientation is set to Portrait Scale should be set to 100% Select Shrink to fit Page width Deselect Print Background (colors & images) Select the Margins & Header/Footer tab. Change these settings: Set the Top and Bottom margins to 0.25 Set the Left and Right margins to 0.0 Set all of the Headers & Footers to --blank-- Select OK at the bottom of the window. Your printer is now ready to use.
ground_truth: Open Control Panel, select View devices and printers, right-click the TSP 100 Printer icon, and choose Set as Default Printer.

question: Where can I change the employee booking order that appears on my Vagaro Listing Page?
context:
[Article: Change Employee Booking Order | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204736614-Change-Employee-Booking-Order]
Employee booking order determines the order shown when a customer books on your Vagaro Listing Page . This setting does not affect the order that your employees are listed on the Staff tab, which is managed on the Website Employee Lineup screen . There are four ways that your employees can be listed or selected when customers search for an appointment: First Available : Automatically select the first service provider with the soonest availability for the customer's service. Website Employee Lineup : Show employees in the order you've selected on the Website Employee Lineup screen . Best Reviewed : Show employees starting with the employees who have the highest star ratings at the top. Alphabetical Order : Show your employees in alphabetical order by first name. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. Online Booking must be enabled for customers to book appointments with them from your Vagaro Listing Page. On a Phone To select your employee booking order: Select More → Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Scroll down to the Booking section. Select your preferred Online Employee Booking Order in the list. Select Save . The change is reflected on your Vagaro Listing Page . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To select your employee booking order: Select Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Scroll down to the Booking section. Select your preferred Online Employee Booking Order in the list. Select Save .
ground_truth: You can change the employee booking order by going to Settings → Online Appointment Rules, scrolling down to the Booking section, selecting your preferred Online Employee Booking Order, and then clicking Save.

question: If I want to reward my team based on how much they sell, but only after a certain sales amount, what should I do?
context:
[Article: Set Up Commissions for Products | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21476003547419-Set-Up-Commissions-for-Products]
Setting up commissions for your products incentivizes your employees and service providers to sell more if they bring in more revenue. In the Payroll Configuration screen, you can set up commissions as a fixed percentage, an amount based on how much revenue employees bring in, or what an employee sold during a pay period.

Tiered Commission by Revenue : Pay commissions as a fixed percentage or amount based on how much money the employee brought in. Commission by Product : Pay commissions as a fixed amount based on what products the employee sold.

Commissions are paid based on when a booking is performed, not when a customer pays for the service. For example, consider this business that has a bi-monthly pay schedule, and the current pay period is January 1-15. A customer books an appointment for January 20 but pays for it right away on January 3. This means that the service provider will receive their commission at the end of the next pay period (January 16-31) because the appointment will occur after the immediate pay period.
