Important If any employee is set up for Tiered Commission by Revenue , only the settings on that tab will apply to that employee. For hourly employees, toggle on Only Pay Hourly Rate or Commission (Whichever is Larger) to pay only the commission amount if the commission earned during a pay period is more than the total hourly pay, and if the total hourly pay during a pay period is more than the commission earned, then only the total hourly pay will be given. Do not set up commissions for employees with merchant accounts. If an employee has their own merchant account, the full amount of service or class sales will be deposited into their bank account. Setting up commissions for these employees will result in payment into their merchant account as well as receiving commissions.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. At least one employee profile must already be added and your Payroll Configuration must already be set up. In the US, Vagaro Payroll is recommended to pay your employees by direct deposit and withhold their income taxes.

This option allows you to customize multiple percentages or amounts based on how much an employee charges for a product.

Note Tiered Commission by Revenue is the default; product-specific commissions will override the revenue commission.

To set up tiered commission by revenue:

Select Reports → Payroll Configuration . If you subscribe to Vagaro Payroll , ensure your employees have been added to the Payroll tab. Scroll to the bottom of the screen, and then select the Products tab. The Tiered Commission by Revenue tab will be the default selection. Add additional tiers by selecting +Add . Provide a top range for each tier and enter commission percentages per employee. (You can also enter flat commission amounts.) For example, consider Anna who sold $600 worth of products during the current payroll period. If you set up Tier 1 commission for revenue earned between $0.00 and $499.99 and a Tier 2 commission for revenge earned between $500 and $9999.99, Anna would receive the Tier 2 commission of 40%. You can add an unlimited number of tiers and assign them to any or all of your employees. To copy the same commission rate amount to other tiers or employees, select the left/right and up/down arrows. To delete a tier, hover over it, then select the Delete icon. Optionally, toggle on the Only Pay Hourly Rate or Commission (Whichever is Larger) switch, then select an option: Apply to all hourly pay rates : If the commission earned during a pay period exceeds the total hourly pay, then only the commission will be paid. If the total hourly pay during a pay period exceeds the commission earned, then only the hourly pay will be paid. Apply to specific pay rates : Whether only Select Pay Rates (created on the Hourly Pay tab) in the list will be paid the larger of earnings by their hourly rate or commissions. Paid Time Off ( Vagaro Payroll only): Whether to withhold PTO if the commission is greater than the total number of hours worked and their PTO balance, or to always give PTO in addition to the greater of their commission earned or total hourly pay. In the same example above, you can choose to withhold paying the employee's PTO because the earned commission is more than what would be earned during PTO PTO will not be paid if the commission is greater than the combined total of hours worked and the PTO balance. The employee will always receive PTO in addition to the larger of their commission earned or total hourly pay. Select Save . Important ONLY use Save and Run Payroll if you're ready to run the payroll at the same time you save the settings.
ground_truth: You can set up tiered commissions that apply different percentage rates or fixed amounts depending on sales ranges, so employees earn more as they reach higher sales tiers.

question: How can I register an attendee for multiple class sessions at once using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Register and Manage Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/29078318111387-Register-and-Manage-Class-Attendees]
If attendees want to sign up for more than one class session, you can register them for all of them directly from the Calendar. No need to book each session one at a time.

Before registering attendees for multiple sessions, confirm that the class is set up as recurring or as a workshop . If it’s not, you won’t be able to add the customer to more than one session.

On the Vagaro Pro App To register attendees for a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. If the attendee isn't sure which class they want to book, you can search all your class availability . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Add Attendees . Select the customers to register, then select Next . Tip If the attendee doesn't have a profile at your business, you can create one for them by selecting + in the top-right corner. Enroll the attendees in multiple class sessions: Enter the Number of Sessions you want to add the attendees to and set the Start Date for their first class. Choose a Frequency for the attendees. Weekly : Choose a frequency, such as every two or three weeks, then select the days of the week you want to sign the customer up for. Monthly : Choose the day of the month you want to repeat. Optionally, add any add-ons and payment information: To save a credit card on file, select the Card button next to the attendee's profile. To charge a deposit, enter a Deposit Due for your attendee. This option will not be available if you register multiple attendees at once. To add an add-on to the attendee's sessions, select + Add-Ons . Add your attendees: To register your attendees and check them out later, select Add Attendees . To register your attendee and prepay for their class, select Add & Checkout . Note You can't check out multiple attendees at the same time.

On a Computer To register attendees for a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. If the attendee isn't sure which class they want to book, you can search all your class availability . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Add Attendees . Select the customers to register, then select Next . Customers already enrolled will have "(enrolled)" next to their profile. If the attendee doesn't have a profile, select Create New to make one for them. Optionally, add any add-ons and payment information: To save a credit card on file, select the Card button next to the attendee's profile. To charge your attendee a deposit, enter a Deposit Due for your attendee. This option will not be available if you register multiple attendees at once. To add an add-on to the attendee's sessions, select + Add-Ons . Enroll the attendees in multiple class sessions: Enter the Number of Sessions you want to add the attendees to and set the Date for their first class. Choose a Repeat frequency for the attendees. Week : Choose a frequency, such as every two or three weeks, then select the days of the week you want to sign the customer up for. Month : Choose the day of the month you want to repeat. Add your attendees: To register your attendees and check them out later, select Add Attendees . To register your attendee and prepay for their class, select Add & Checkout . Note You can't check out multiple attendees at the same time.
ground_truth: To register an attendee for multiple sessions, select the class, choose Add Attendees, enter the number of sessions and start date, set the frequency (weekly or monthly), and then add the attendee. You can also add add-ons and payment info before completing registration.

question: How can I filter my email campaign list to only include customers within a specific age range?
context:
[Article: Filter Audience for Email Campaigns | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412639225883-Filter-Audience-for-Email-Campaigns]
your name, grayed out, to indicate that you cannot select other employees for the campaign. Birthday : Gender Age Between : Adjust the slider to select an age range (from 0 to 110) for customers to receive the campaign. Has or Doesn't Have Membership : Filter your list to only send to customers with selected Memberships. Past Services/Classes/Packages : Filter the list by customers who've had or taken a specific service or class or purchased a particular package , and select the field under Past Services/Classes/Packages . Then, use checkboxes to select options to include in the filter, and select Apply . Exclude Customers with Failed Phone Numbers : Filter the list to exclude customers with an invalid phone number . question: If I decide I don't want an upcoming message to go out anymore, how can I stop it from being sent?
context:
[Article: Edit Email and Text Campaigns | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26168603160091-Edit-Email-and-Text-Campaigns]
Cancel a scheduled campaign if you no longer want it to send or if you’ve created a new version.

On a Phone To cancel a scheduled campaign: Go to More → Email & Text Marketing → Campaigns . Select Queue to view all scheduled campaigns. Swipe left on the campaign, select Cancel , and then select Yes to confirm. The campaign will not be sent to customers.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To cancel a scheduled campaign: Go to Marketing , then select Create Campaign under Email & Text Marketing . Filter by Sending Queue to only view campaigns that haven't been sent out yet. Next to the campaign to be edited, select Action → Cancel , then select Yes to confirm. The campaign will not be sent to customers.
ground_truth: You can cancel the scheduled campaign through your device by accessing the marketing options, finding the campaign in the queue, and confirming the cancellation.

question: How do I manually configure my Star wireless printer to connect to my Wi-Fi network using an Android device?
context:
[Article: Star WIRELESS Printer Setup - Android Devices | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000256554-Star-WIRELESS-Printer-Setup-Android-Devices]
This article explains how to install, connect, and troubleshoot the Star w printer with an Android device. For setup of the Star wireless printer on other devices, refer to these articles: IOS Mobile Devices Mac Desktop/Laptop Computers Windows Desktop/Laptop Computers Note Due to the limitations of third-party libraries on Amazon devices, Star receipt printers are not supported on Amazon Fire Tablets and Kindles. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Physical Setup To set up and plug in the receipt printer: Connect the power cable to the inlet on the back of the TSP100iii W, and then connect the plug of the power cable into the outlet. On the left-hand side of the device, flip the switch on and check that the blue LED light is lit up. Next, you should insert a roll of paper. Check that the power is on. Push the lever on the top to open the cover. Note When using a paper roll with a 58mm width, install the supplied paper guide inside the printer. The way you insert paper is important. Insert the paper roll with the paper edge pulling towards the floor rather than the ceiling. See the illustration below: Pull the end of the paper straight out and over the front of the printer and push down on both sides of the printer cover to close. Connect the Printer to Your Wi-Fi Network To connect the printer to your Wi-Fi network: On the back of the printer and under the wire cover, press and hold the PAIR button until the blue LED light starts flashing. Press the WPS button on your router. When the printer connects to the network, it will print the connection information, and the blue LED light will change from flashing to solid. Note If a connection cannot be made, just follow the next steps. Configure the Printer to Connect to Your Network If the receipt printer did not connect to your Wi-Fi network, you need to manually configure the connection. Ensure the printer is OFF . Press the FEED button while switching the printer ON to perform a self-print. Release the FEED button when self-printing has begun. On the printout, look for the MAC address under "Network Configuration." On your Android device, go to the Settings screen, select Wi-Fi , and make sure it is toggled ON . In the device list at the bottom, take note of the last 6 digits of the MAC address, then select the device to connect. Once connected, "Connected, no internet" will appear under the SSID indicating that the connection is established. Note When connecting with this method, Internet access is not available on that device. Open your device's web browser, such as Safari, and enter 192.168.10.1 in the address field. (If the IP address of the printer was changed, enter the IP address that appears under "Current IP Parameters Status" on your test print). In the upper left-hand side of the screen, select the More (3 lines) menu, then select Login . Log in with the following credentials. Name : root / Password : public The following message appears when you are using the default password. DO NOT CHANGE THE PASSWORD . Select Cancel to keep the login information the same. You will be prompted to change your password after logging in, DO NOT CHANGE YOUR PASSWORD . After login, select WLAN Settings . Select Infrastructure Mode , enter your internet's SSID and Security Key and select Submit to connect the printer to your network. Make sure to set Security as WPA2/AES . When you select Submit , you will be prompted to select Save . Do not select Save at this step. Select IP Parameters . Select Static . Enter the IP Address , Subnet Mask , and Default Gateway. The IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway that are shown in this image are an example. The local IP address is the one your router assigns to your device and uses to communicate with your network. A static IP address may be IPv4 or IPv6 in your network profile information. To access the local IP address on your Android device, navigate to Settings → Network & Internet → Wi-Fi . You will see the settings icon next to the Wi-Fi network name. Select Settings , then select Advanced . Scroll down to locate the IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway. Select Submit . Select Save . Select Execute . Your printer will restart and will stay connected to your network. Print a Test Receipt To print a test receipt: Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Reports Select Transaction List . Run the report, then select a transaction. Select Receipt . Confirm and select Print . If the printer is connected correctly, it will print out the receipt. If not, refer back to the instructions on how to connect the device to the WiFi network. Example of a test print:
ground_truth: To manually configure your Star wireless printer on Android, ensure the printer is OFF, press the FEED button while turning it ON to print network info, then connect your Android device to the printer's Wi-Fi (not internet). Open a browser and go to 192.168.10.1, log in with username 'root' and password 'public', select WLAN Settings, choose Infrastructure Mode, enter your SSID and Security Key, set Security to WPA2/AES, then go to IP Parameters, select Static, and input your IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway. Save and execute the settings; the printer will restart and connect to your network.

question: What steps do I need to take before deleting my Vagaro customer account?
context:
[Article: Close Your Vagaro Account - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260806204150-Close-Your-Vagaro-Account-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
Note If you're a Vagaro Business owner, you must cancel your Vagaro subscription . If you're an employee or a booth renter, make sure the account owner deletes your profile and calendar from their Vagaro account. An account is required to book with businesses on Vagaro. If you're no longer actively visiting businesses on Vagaro, you can close your customer account. After closing your account, you can also request that your information be deleted from Vagaro . Prerequisites : Before you can delete your account, make sure you have cancelled all future appointments or classes , cancelled your recurring memberships or packages , and settled outstanding invoices and IOUS. Note You may need to contact the business directly to fully cancel certain packages or memberships. If you're having trouble, please contact Vagaro Support . On the Vagaro App To delete your Vagaro account: Open the Vagaro app , then select the Profile button at the bottom of the screen. Log into your Vagaro customer account . Under Account , select My Profile Scroll down to Account Management section, then select Delete Account . In the confirmation popup, select Delete Account . On a Computer Go to https://vagaro.com and select Login at the top-right corner of the screen. Hover over your profile icon in the top-right corner, then select My Profile . Scroll down to Account Management section, then select Delete Account . In the confirmation popup, select Delete Account .
ground_truth: Before deleting your Vagaro account, you must cancel all future appointments or classes, cancel any recurring memberships or packages, and settle all outstanding invoices and IOUs.

question: How can I keep track of any special requests or important details for my upcoming visit?
context:
[Article: Rebook an Appointment | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/221194188-Rebook-an-Appointment]
return request, or return non-request. Each option allows you to track whether the customer is returning and whether they requested a specific employee. Appointment Notes : Any notes that should be known about this appointment. Appointment notes help keep track of special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for an upcoming appointment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. You can also attach any files, forms, or images relevant to the appointment. Vagaro Drive is required to save files to your Vagaro business account. Notes : View and add any notes you've added in the customer's profile . A customer note is a comment that an employee or service provider can add to a customer's profile for business purposes, such as reminders, allergy information, or an explanation of a customer's assessment and treatment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. History : The customer's previous bookings. : The customer's credit card information on file . You can enter or update the information if needed. Select Book . The appointment is added to the calendar.
ground_truth: You can add Appointment Notes to record any special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments related to the appointment.

question: If I need to change the device that handles my card payments, how do I disconnect the current one from my system?
context:
[Article: Using the WisePad 3 Mobile with Vagaro Pro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402883300891-Using-the-WisePad-3-Mobile-with-Vagaro-Pro]
The WisePad 3 Mobile is Vagaro's credit card (EMV) reader for businesses in Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia to check out clients securely and process credit card payments with the Vagaro Pro app . For US-based customers, refer to Pair the Credit Card (EMV) Reader with Vagaro Pro . Note This device is for iOS and Android phones and tablets only and cannot be connected to a PC or laptop. The WisePad 3 credit card reader is incompatible with the PayPro . You will need the WisePOS E instead, which is available from the Vagaro Online Store . Prerequisites: This hardware is only available for Vagaro businesses in Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia. Process Payments Using the WisePad 3 Mobile Important For Canadian business owners, if your customer is using an Interac card, they will only be asked to enter their PIN if the transaction is over $100. To process a payment using the WisePad 3 Mobile: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Select Connect . Note If you're at a location where multiple Vagaro businesses have credit card readers, you may see more than one reader you can pair with. To prevent connecting to the wrong reader and charging a customer from a different Vagaro business, check the serial number on the back of the WisePad 3 to make sure you pair with the correct reader: Select Pair . You will see a confirmation message when the reader has been successfully paired. Select Pay by Card . If your business asks for tips: Ask the customer to select a Tip option. Select Split to split the tip if multiple service providers have been added to the Checkout screen. If you selected Split , enter the amount for each employee, and then select Continue . Select Next . Ask the customer to sign on the screen, then select Charge Card . Select or dip the card into the reader to process the payment. Unassign a Reader When you connect a chip reader to a device, it assigns the reader to that specific device. If another business attempts to use that reader, they'll see a popup stating that the reader is assigned to another business. This means that the other business must unassign the reader first. To unassign a reader: Open the Vagaro Pro app and log in, then select More → Connected Devices . At the bottom of the screen, select Unassign Reader to disconnect the reader from the device. In the Unassign Reader popup, select Unassign . Update the Firmware Firmware updates keep your WisePad 3 current and secure with the media it connects with. When there is an update, you'll see a notification beside it in the More menu. To update the firmware of the WisePad 3: Open the Vagaro Pro app and log in, then select More → Connected Devices . Select Install Now . When the update has been completed, the device will auto-connect and display its latest firmware information.
ground_truth: Open the Vagaro Pro app, go to More → Connected Devices, then select Unassign Reader and confirm to disconnect the device.

question: What should I avoid including in my text campaigns to prevent them from being flagged as spam?
context:
[Article: Text Campaign Best Practices | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/15282860216475-Text-Campaign-Best-Practices]
Text marketing is a powerful tool to connect with your clients, but it's essential to ensure your messages are delivered successfully and help drive traffic to your business. This guide will help you craft effective campaigns that resonate with your audience and comply with carrier guidelines. Please see here for more details on Twilio's messaging guidelines. Insert a Marketing Link Use the Insert Link button when building your campaign to link directly to your services, classes, or booking page. This makes it simple for customers to book appointments. Use Vagaro AI Let Vagaro AI help you create engaging campaigns tailored to your customers. Keep It Short and Sweet Send clear, concise messages. Most customers will skim long texts. Add Images/GIFs Enhance your text campaigns with images or GIFs to capture attention and make your message stand out. Target Active Customers Filter your campaigns for clients who have visited your business recently. This helps avoid being blocked by customers who haven’t engaged in a while. Filter Your Audience Target specific customer groups rather than sending campaigns to your entire list. This allows for more personalized and relevant messaging. Avoid "Spammy" Formatting and Phrases Stay away from all caps, excessive punctuation, unconventional capitalization, and phrases that trigger spam filters, such as: "BOOK NOW!!!," "FRee SeRVice," "I <3 U," "100% FREE," "Extra Cash," or "No Credit Check" can cause your message to be flagged by spam filters. Keep your language professional and trustworthy. Avoid Sending Too Many Messages Space out your campaigns and avoid sending duplicates to prevent customers from blocking your number. Avoid Restricted Content Do not send campaigns containing restricted terms, including: Hemp, Ganja, Green Leaf, 420, CBD THC, CBD-infused, Delta-8, Delta-9, or any other slang related to cannabis. Even if CBD products are legal in your state, these messages will be denied. Limit Emojis Use no more than one or two emojis to add personality without triggering spam filters. Don't Add Business Information Avoid adding your address, phone number, or email to the text. Customers can find this on your website. Avoid Mentioning Prices Skip listing deals and service prices, as these can be flagged. Be Professional Use respectful language and avoid offensive or slang terms to protect your brand’s image. Important Any Text Campaigns that fail for any reason are not subject to refunds.
ground_truth: You should avoid using all caps, excessive punctuation, phrases like 'BOOK NOW!!!' or 'FRee SeRVice,' spam trigger words such as 'I <3 U' or '100% FREE,' restricted terms related to cannabis like 'Ganja' or 'CBD,' too many emojis, business contact info, and price listings.

question: How can I connect my Vagaro campaign to my Facebook page for posting?
context:
[Article: Send a One-Time Email Blast to Your Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412637791387-Send-a-One-Time-Email-Blast-to-Your-Customers]
option to connect your Vagaro account with Facebook and post this campaign on your page. Toggle Send Now off to Schedule a date and time for when you want to send out this campaign. Agree to the acknowledgment, then select Announce when you're ready to send.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To create this campaign: Go to Marketing , then select Create Campaign under Email & Text Marketing . On the marketing dashboard, select Create Campaign . Next to Select Delivery Method , choose Email . Enter a Campaign Email Subject . This will appear in the subject line of the email your customer receives. Select the Vagaro AI button to enhance your subject with AI. Select the Emoji button to add emojis to the subject. Select Insert Name , then select First Name or First & Last Name to auto-populate the customer's name in the subject. Select the Email Blast campaign type. Select the recipients for this campaign: All Customers : Send this campaign to all customers in your Customer List. Filtered List : Send this campaign to a specific, filtered list of customers. Specific Customers : Not available for this campaign type. Under Who Will Receive Your Campaign , review which Customers or Employees will receive your campaign, and see who Opted Out of text messages from your business. You can select Edit next to each item to view and remove customers from the list. Note Customers who have Opted Out have configured their notification settings not to receive messages or have been blocked from online booking at your business. The system will not send text campaigns to customers in this list. Customers who texted "STOP" to previous campaigns also appear in the Opted Out list and will not receive this campaign. Scroll to Schedule and choose an option: Send Now : Send this campaign as soon as you announce it. You can toggle Post On Facebook to connect your Facebook account to Vagaro and post your campaigns on your page. To Post On Facebook , make sure you've set up the Facebook integration ; otherwise, you will get an error. Schedule : Select Date & Time for when you want to send this text campaign. Select Next to begin drafting your email campaign. Choose a template for your email. Each category has different templates, so we recommend looking through all of them before choosing one. Optionally, in the top-right corner of the email editor, select Style to customize the colors and border of your campaign. Select the box next to Page Background to change its color. Select the box next to Body Background to change its color. Select the box next to Border to change its color. You can also change the border thickness as well. By default, each template includes two images, buttons, and social links. You can optionally Move , Edit , or Delete them as needed. To add a module to the message, drag and drop it from the sidebar onto the design window. The Style tab on every edit pop-up provides background and border colors, border size, and padding options. Changes to the background and border color won't take effect unless you select OK in that dialog. Text modules will open a text editor where you can enter and format text for your email, insert customer names , add hyperlinks, and style the text box with background and border colors. Image modules allow you to upload an image or select one from your business's Image Gallery . You can make any image function as a link by adding a URL in the Link to box. Uploading an image will open the Vagaro Image Editor , where you can edit your image. You can also hover over or select an image selected from the gallery, then select Edit to open the editor. The Buttons module allows you to create buttons that link to pages on your Vagaro Listing page and more. You can set button color, height, width, rounded corners, text color, font, and size and add preset or custom links. Each Button module can have up to three buttons, but only the ones you configure will show on the message. Note Adding links to your campaigns allows for tracking the click-through rate and how many customers booked through the link . These metrics are only tracked for Vagaro links; Vagaro will not track clicks to external websites outside of Vagaro. The Social Buttons module lets you add links to social media accounts (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, etc.) The Themes drop-down list gives you six link style selections. Any change to the Theme is reflected in the links shown on the page and the preview. Preview your campaign: Select the Preview or Preview tabs to view how this campaign will look like on a phone or computer. Select Send Email Preview to send a preview of this campaign to your email. Warning Sending an email preview will pull from your Email Marketing plan. Select Save for Later to save this campaign as a draft. Announce your campaign by agreeing to the acknowledgment at the bottom of the screen, then select Announce .
ground_truth: You can connect your Vagaro account with Facebook and post your campaign on your page by toggling the 'Post On Facebook' option during campaign setup, provided you have set up the Facebook integration beforehand.

question: Can I get more funding after I have paid between 50% and 100% of my current Vagaro Capital funding?
context:
[Article: US: Vagaro Capital Help & FAQ | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/16378030395291-US-Vagaro-Capital-Help-FAQ]
Can I Get More Funding After Finishing My Payments? Yes. Businesses that have paid 50 to 100% of their funding may be eligible for renewal. You will receive a notification in your Notification Panel when you're eligible.

Do I Pay Interest on My Funding? There is no interest accrued with Vagaro Capital, just a fixed fee.

Is There a Max Timeframe That My Funding Must Be Paid Off No, Vagaro Capital is not a loan. There is no fixed term.

How do payments work? After you receive your cash advance, Vagaro Capital automatically withdraws daily payments (Split Payments) from your credit/debit card transactions. Vagaro Capital calculates the payment based on the percentage (Payment Speed) that was agreed upon when submitting your Vagaro Capital application. This means that payments will fluctuate depending on your daily credit/debit card earnings.

Are There Any Late Fees If I Miss a Payment? No, there are no additional fees for late payments.

Can I Adjust My Split Percentage? No, the agreed-upon split percentage taken from each card transaction cannot be changed.

A Booth Renter with Funding Left My Business. What Happens to Their Repayments? If a Booth Renter with an outstanding payment moves to another Vagaro business, they can transfer their employee information (and Vagaro Capital balance) to the other business. If they are not leaving for another Vagaro business, the employee will still need to make payments toward their funding.

Where Can I See Information About My Payments You can review a Vagaro Capital Report from your Merchant Account. To get to the Capital Report : Vagaro Pro App : Select More → Credit Card Processing , then select the Capital Report tab. Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : In the top-right corner, select your profile in the top-right corner, select Vagaro Capital , and then select the Capital Report tab.

Can I Cancel My Vagaro Account Before Paying Off My Funding? No, you must pay off your Vagaro Capital balance before cancelling your Vagaro subscription or deactivating Vagaro Credit Card Processing.

What if I'm Struggling with Payments? If you are struggling to meet payments, please contact Vagaro support . We will always carefully consider the needs of our customers on an individual basis.
ground_truth: Yes, businesses that have paid 50 to 100% of their funding may be eligible for renewal and will receive a notification when they are eligible.

question: What must a renter have before they can be charged rent through the system?
context:
[Article: Set Up Automatic Rent Collection | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347790-Set-Up-Automatic-Rent-Collection]
Renters must already have an employee profile before they can be charged rent. If a renter's information is incomplete when rent is due, the system will automatically charge the owed rent when submitted. Similarly, if their primary and/or backup payment method fails, the system will automatically charge the owed rent when their payment goes through.

Note If the business changes the rent charge frequency, unpaid rent will not be collected from renters with a "Pending Request" status.

To add a renter:

Go to Rent Collection : Phone : Select More → Settings → Rent Collection . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Rent Collection . Scroll down to the Renters section, and then select + Add Renter . Select the Renter in the list and enter the Rent Amount . Select how you want to receive the renter's information: Request Renter Completion : Send an email and push notification to the renter so they can fill out their information on their own time and device. Fill Payment Information : Allow the renter to provide the required information directly on your device. Note If you choose to Require Rent Contract , you will need to fill out the form you attached after filling out the required information. Select Save or Send Request . To resend the rent request for information: On a Phone : Select the renter's name that says "Request Sent" below it, then select Resend Request . On the Web, Tablet, or Pay Desk : Select the Action menu in the Action column of the renter's name that says "Request Sent" below it, and then select Edit Payment Method .

For employees to see the Rent Collection tab in their profile, toggle on the Manage Own Profile permission in the Access Levels screen.

To set access levels for rent collection:

Go to Rent Collection : Phone : Select More → Settings → Rent Collection . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Rent Collection . On the Access Levels tab, select the Action menu of the desired role to add visibility for Rent Collection tab to, and then select Edit . At the bottom of the screen, scroll to Settings and set Manage Own Profile to Modify . For the Manage Employee Rent Collection setting, select View and Modify . Select Save .
ground_truth: Renters must already have an employee profile before they can be charged rent.

question: How can I switch between different views during a Vagaro live stream session?
context:
[Article: Attend a Live Stream Session - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4623722925723-Attend-a-Live-Stream-Session-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
to Frame : Have your video fit the entire frame. During the live stream session, you can perform the following tasks by selecting the corresponding buttons on the toolbar: : Switch between the Gallery, Speaker, and Presenter view. (Chrome and Safari Browser Only): Start sharing your screen. You can share a tab, window, or an entire screen. (Only Available on Web and iOS): Give yourself some privacy by blurring your background. : Start or stop the recording (the icon is red when recording). : Turn off your camera. : Turn off your microphone. : Open chat and view messages. : View or hide the Attendee Panel. : Open the settings panel for additional options. When the session is over, you can select Leave Class or Leave Appointment to send the session.
ground_truth: During the live stream session, you can switch between the Gallery, Speaker, and Presenter view by selecting the corresponding buttons on the toolbar.

question: How can I assign a gift card to a customer without taking payment in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Give Complimentary and Donation Gift Cards to Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/48220729318939-Give-Complimentary-and-Donation-Gift-Cards-to-Customers]
Adding a gift card from the Gift Card report lets you assign a gift card to a customer’s profile without taking payment. This is useful when you want to give a gift card as part of a promotion, provide one for a donation, or issue one as a goodwill gesture to make things right. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To add a gift card: Select More → Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-right corner, select Action → New Gift Card . Enter a unique Gift Card Number : For physical gift cards, manually enter the number or scan the barcode with a barcode scanner. For digital gift cards, select Auto Generate . Enter the gift card's Amount and choose a Template Type . If you don’t want to assign a value, you can choose a Service/Class/Add-On instead. This limits the gift card so it can only be redeemed for the specific service, class, or add-on you select. Any designs you've uploaded will appear in Business Created ; otherwise, you can use any of Vagaro's template designs. You still need to select a design if you scanned a physical gift card. Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, and when you've finished creating your gift card, select Next . Expires On : The expiration date of your gift card. Note Every state has its own regulations for gift cards. These regulations can vary from when they expire to whether they are redeemable for a cash value. To help you follow safe business practices and provide you with more information on these regulations, visit https://www.ncsl.org/financial-services/gift-cards-and-gift-certificates-statutes-and-legislation . Send On : Send the gift card to the recipient right after it's created. If you have a recipient selected, you can toggle this setting off to choose a specific delivery day and time. Message : The message shown on the digital gift card. Print Gift Card : Print this gift card. Email Gift Card to : Send this gift card to the recipient's email. A preview of the digital gift card will appear. If no additional changes are needed, select Add Gift Card . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a gift card: Select Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-left corner of the table, select New Gift Card . Enter a unique Gift Card Number : For physical gift cards, manually enter the number or scan the barcode with a barcode scanner. For digital gift cards, select Auto Generate . Enter the gift card's Amount and choose a Template Type . If you don’t want to assign a value, you can choose a Service/Class/Add-On instead. This limits the gift card so it can only be redeemed for the specific service, class, or add-on you select. Any designs you've uploaded will appear in Business Created ; otherwise, you can use any of Vagaro's template designs. You still need to select a design if you scanned a physical gift card. Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, and when you've finished creating your gift card, select Next . Expires On : The expiration date of your gift card. Note Every state has its own regulations for gift cards. These regulations can vary from when they expire to whether they are redeemable for a cash value. To help you follow safe business practices and provide you with more information on these regulations, visit https://www.ncsl.org/financial-services/gift-cards-and-gift-certificates-statutes-and-legislation . Send On : Send the gift card to the recipient right after it's created. If you have a recipient selected, you can toggle this setting off to choose a specific delivery day and time. Message : The message shown on the digital gift card. Print Gift Card : Print this gift card. Email Gift Card to : Send this gift card to the recipient's email. A preview of the digital gift card will appear. If no additional changes are needed, select Add Gift Card .
ground_truth: You can assign a gift card to a customer's profile using the Gift Card report by selecting 'New Gift Card,' entering the details, and choosing to send or print it, without processing a payment.

question: How can I change the name of a document I stored in my online file space without losing any details?
context:
[Article: Vagaro Drive Dashboard | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260800678490-Vagaro-Drive-Dashboard]
The Vagaro Drive Dashboard allows you to manage files. You can view, preview, rename, download, edit, or delete files and access detailed information, including names, sizes, and associated details.

To manage files:

Go to the Vagaro Drive dashboard . On the left side of the screen, choose the folder you want to view. Hover over the file, select the Action menu, and choose an action. Open : View the file and its information, such as the File Name , Form Name , Appointment Date , Size , Dimensions , and so on. Preview images, PDF, text (.txt), Word (.doc, .docx), and Excel (xls, .xlsx) files. Files from PowerPoint (.ppt, .pptx), Word (.docx), and video (.mpg and .mov) must be downloaded to view. You can also edit ( ), download ( ), and delete ( ) the image by selecting the button in the top-left corner. Select the Arrow < or > icons to cycle between different images. Rename : Opens a sidebar where you can rename the file and enter a file description. Download : Download the file. Info : See information on this file, such as File Name , Form Name , Appointment Date , Size , Dimensions , and so on. Edit Description : Opens a sidebar where you can rename the file and enter a file description. Delete : Remove this file from your drive. Files removed from your drive can't be recovered.

Images saved in your Vagaro Drive can be edited or marked up using Vagaro's two built-in image editors:

The Vagaro Image Editor is a built-in image editor that helps you work with photos and other images in your online business. You can resize, crop, flip, rotate, and add text, shapes, effects, and filters. The Vagaro Markup Editor allows you to draw on photos in Vagaro Drive. You can upload a picture of a client's face, for example. Using that image: draw circles and arrows, write text, add highlights and comments, etc.

To edit images:

Go to the Vagaro Drive dashboard . On the left side of the screen, choose the folder you want to view. Select the image you want to edit. In the top-right corner, select the Editor icon, then choose to edit the image in the Markup editor or Image editor . Modify your image as needed and save.

On the web version of Vagaro, you can copy the file address of videos and photos saved in your Vagaro Drive and then share it with your customers. This functionality is only available for images and videos.

To share files

Select the file you want to share. Right-click the image or video. Copy the file address: For images, select Copy Image Address . For videos, select Copy Video Address . Share the link with your customer.
ground_truth: You can rename the file by opening a sidebar where you can enter a new name and add a description.

question: How can I invite my acquaintances to join Vagaro without using keywords from the instructions?
context:
[Article: Refer a Friend in the United States | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204947034-Refer-a-Friend-in-the-United-States]
Join the Refer-a-Friend program and earn Vagaro Bucks when you refer a US-based business to Vagaro. To refer another business, send them a referral link to sign up for Vagaro. Once signed up, if they process $1,000 in credit cards within their first 60 days, both you and the referred business will receive 50 Vagaro Bucks. Vagaro Bucks are a token of appreciation we offer to our valued customers for referring someone to Vagaro. Each Vagaro Buck is worth $1 and will never expire. You can use these credits toward your subscription or redeem them in the Vagaro Online Store. You and the referred business will receive Vagaro Bucks 30 days after meeting the processing requirement. And for a little extra motivation, the business that refers the most paying members by year’s end will take home $1,000 USD! Note Your Vagaro Bucks are always applied to your Vagaro subscription first. If you want to use them toward Vagaro products or hardware, be sure to do so before your next billing date. Prerequisites : To become a valid referred business, your referral must activate Credit Card Processing at signup. Businesses that sign up for a free trial will not be considered. Referred businesses must be new to Vagaro and unrelated to existing locations. Refer Your Friends to Vagaro On the Vagaro Pro App To refer your friends to Vagaro: Select More → Refer & Earn Cash . Share your referral link: Copy Link : Copy your unique Vagaro referral link, then share it by pasting it in a post on social media or sending it in an email, text message, or messaging app. Share : Select Share to choose how you want to share your referral link. Make sure your friends sign up for Vagaro with your referral link. On a Computer To refer your friends to Vagaro: Go to More → Refer a Friend . Select Copy Link to copy your unique Vagaro referral link. Share your referral link by pasting it in a social media post or in an email, text message, or messaging app, and tell them about the benefits of using Vagaro! Make sure your friends sign up for Vagaro with your referral link. See Who You've Referred and Vagaro Bucks Earned From the Refer a Friend screen, you can see how many people you have referred in the Paying Members card. Select the card to see who signed up. Your current Vagaro Bucks balance appears in Vagaro Bucks Earned. Select the card to view when Vagaro Bucks were earned and when they were used. To get to the refer a friend screen: On the Vagaro Pro App : Go to More → Refer & Earn Cash . On a Computer : Go to More → Refer a Friend .
ground_truth: You can share your unique referral link through social media, email, or messaging apps to encourage others to sign up for Vagaro using that link.

question: How do I assemble the Vagaro handheld QR stand after receiving my barcode scanner?
context:
[Article: Build the Handheld QR/Barcode Scanner Stand | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360055994254-Build-the-Handheld-QR-Barcode-Scanner-Stand]
After receiving your barcode scanner, build the stand with it for easier placement. Prerequisites : Purchase the Vagaro barcode reader and scanner stand . To build the handheld QR stand: Rotate the cradle insert upwards. Spin the cradle 180 degrees. Invert the cradle so it's pointing down. Slide the cradle insert over the stand until you hear it select into place. Adust the cradle to the desired angle. For more information, watch the attached video.
ground_truth: To assemble the stand, rotate the cradle insert upwards, spin the cradle 180 degrees, invert it so it's pointing down, slide the insert over the stand until it clicks, and then adjust the cradle to the desired angle.

question: How can I pay a contractor using Vagaro Payroll and what are the steps to run the payroll report?
context:
[Article: Pay Your Contractors With Vagaro Payroll | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24718114971547-Pay-Your-Contractors-With-Vagaro-Payroll]
You can pay your contractors at any time by running the Payroll report and then submitting it for processing. Contractors will receive their funds according to your business's processing schedule. Any contractors who did not complete their onboarding to Vagaro Payroll cannot be paid. You can submit or cancel a payroll up until the cut-off time at 4 PM PT (or 7 PM ET) the day before pay day. Any payroll marked as "Pending" and "Paid" can no longer be canceled. Because of the required processing time, a notification will appear if the funds will not be deposited to their bank accounts in time for their pay day. Tip If a contractor who was invited to complete their payroll profile hasn't onboarded themselves by for their first pay day, you can Resend or Cancel an Onboarding Invitation to a Contractor and enter their information yourself. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To run and submit your payroll for a contractor: Go to the Payroll report: Phone : Select More → Reports → Payroll History . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Reports → Payroll . In the Which Employees Are Your Paying? screen, select Contractors . In the Which Payroll Do You Want to Run? screen, select Regular Payroll . At the top of the screen, configure your report filters, then select Run Report : Payroll Period : Select the pay period the contractors worked. Pay Date : The date when the contractors will receive their direct deposit, or the date to be shown on the paycheck. Contractors : Select the contractors to be paid. Optionally, to Include Past Employees , select Advanced Filters , and then select the checkbox. For each contractor you're paying: Scroll to the right side of the report, then select the Action menu. Select Edit Payroll . Enter the payment information for the contractor: The Gross Pay is automatically updated as you enter the contractor's pay information. Pay By : Whether to pay the contractor by Check or Direct Deposit . Note If you’re going to pay an employee outside of payroll (via a physical check), make sure you record it for tax purposes. Regular Hours : The number of hours that the contractor worked at the regular hourly rate . Overtime Hours (1.5x) : The number of overtime hours that the contractor worked and will be paid time-and-a-half the hourly rate . Overtime Hours (2x) : The number of overtime hours that the contractor worked and will be paid double the hourly rate . Tips : The amount of tips to pay the contractor. Bonus : The additional amount you'd like to pay the contractor. Reimbursement : Expenses that you're paying the contractor back for, such as extra supplies that they paid for out-of-pocket. Commission Total : The commission amount that you're paying the contractor. Select Save . To export or print this report, select an option in the bottom-right corner: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only). Review the report, and if the totals look correct to you, select Submit Report . In the Review and Submit Payroll popup, select each tab and review the payroll details to be submitted, then select Submit : Note These details do not include the amounts paid by check because they are not debited directly from your payroll. Company Pays : Lists the payments to each contractor, and a breakdown of the payment. Debited : Shows the amount to be debited from the business's bank account and deposited to your contractors' bank accounts. Payments made by check are not included in the debited amount. If you need to make any corrections, select the X in the upper-right corner of the popup, then update and resubmit the payroll. In the Payroll Submitted popup, review the confirmed amounts and dates, then close the popup.
ground_truth: To pay a contractor with Vagaro Payroll, go to Reports → Payroll (or More → Reports → Payroll History on phone), select Contractors, then choose Regular Payroll. Configure your report filters, select Run Report, and for each contractor, select Action → Edit Payroll to enter payment details, including pay method, hours, tips, bonuses, and reimbursements. After reviewing the report, click Submit to process the payroll.

question: How can an employee view their rental agreement online?
context:
[Article: View and Manage Employee's Rent Collection Information | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19376653373467-View-and-Manage-Employee-s-Rent-Collection-Information]
If an employee is assigned to an access level that allows them to Manage Own Profile , then they will see the Rent Collection tab in their profile. There, employees can sign their rental agreement, manage their payment methods, and view their rent payment history. If the employee still needs to sign their rental contract the status Pending appears, followed by the contract and a signature box. If the employee has already signed the contract online, the employee can select the contract's name, which also shows the signing date, to view the agreement. Note Signed rental agreements require the Forms feature. Prerequisites : This feature requires credit card processing to collect rent payments. On a Phone To view and update employee's rent collection information: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Select the employee's profile. In the employee's profile, select the Action menu, then select Rent Collection . Note If the employee works for multiple multiple locations , select the Action menu for the location to view rent collection for. To view your rental contract, select the document. To update your rent payment method: Scroll down to the Payment Method section. Under Primary Payment Method , select Edit Account Information , select Add New Bank Account , then follow the prompts. Under Backup Payment Method , select Edit Card Information , select Add New Card , then follow the prompts. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view and update employee's rent collection information: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . At the end of the row of the employee's profile, select the Action menu, then select Rent Collection . Note If the employee works for multiple locations , select the employee's blue Locations link, then select the Action menu for the location to view rent collection for. To update your rent payment method: Scroll down to the Payment Method section. Under Primary Payment Method , select Edit Account Information , select Add New Bank Account , then follow the prompts. Under Backup Payment Method , select Edit Card Information , select Add New Card , then follow the prompts.
ground_truth: An employee can view their rental agreement online by selecting the contract's name in the Rent Collection tab, which displays the signing date and allows them to view the agreement.

question: What criteria does Vagaro use to determine the target profile when merging duplicate customer profiles?
context:
[Article: Merge and Clean Up Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204348140-Merge-and-Clean-Up-Customers]
As your business grows, your customer list may fill up with inactive and duplicate customer profiles. Instead of regularly cleaning up your customer list by yourself, use the settings in Merge & Clean Up Customers to let Vagaro merge matching profiles and remove profiles with missing contact information.

Prerequisites: You must have at least two or more customer profiles already created .

Vagaro picks the target profile (the one that will be kept) in this order:

If only one profile has signed in to Vagaro, that profile will be kept. If both profiles have a card on file but only one has an email, the profile with the email will be kept. If both profiles have a card and an email, the profile with the most appointments and classes will be kept.

Keep the following in mind:

If both profiles have signed in to Vagaro, a lock icon appears on both, and they can't be merged from this screen. The lock keeps one business from overwriting another business's customer data. To merge locked profiles, email support@vagaro.com . Credit card information can't transfer between profiles during a merge. Make sure the card you want to keep is on the target profile before merging. Vagaro won't merge a customer profile that matches an employee or business owner.

Vagaro detects duplicate customer profiles by name and phone number and lists them on the Merge & Cleanup Customers screen, sorted by match score. Review each pair, choose which profile to keep, and merge them.

100% match : Both customers have the same phone number, first name, and last name. 80% match : Both customers have the same first and last name, but different phone numbers. 75% match Both customers have the same phone number and first name, but different last names. 40% match Both customers have the same phone number and last name, but different first names.

Note Vagaro only lists profile pairs that match by name or phone number. If two profiles you want to merge don't appear here, search for one of them on the Customers screen and merge them from there. If Vagaro doesn't find any matches, this list won't appear.

On a Phone To merge two customer profiles that Vagaro has identified: Select More → Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Under the pair of profiles you want to merge, select Merge . Review the From , To , and Result profiles to see how the profiles will be merged. Optionally, adjust the merge: Select Switch to change the To profile. Select a profile to view or edit it. Select Merge . The profiles are merged. Repeat the steps above for all other profiles you want to merge.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To merge two customer profiles that Vagaro has identified: Select Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Next to the pair of profiles you want to merge, select Merge . Review the From , To , and Result profiles to see how the profiles will be merged. Optionally, adjust the merge: Select Switch to change the To profile. Select Edit in the customer's profile to view or edit it. Select Merge . The profiles are merged. Repeat the steps above for all other profiles you want to merge.

Turn on Merge 100% Matched Customers to let Vagaro automatically merge duplicate customer profiles in the background within two hours after detecting them.

A 100% match means that both customers share the same phone number and first and last name.

On a Phone To set up automatic merging of duplicate customer profiles: Select More → Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Turn on Merge 100% Matched Customers . While Vagaro merges two customer profiles, a 'Merge in Progress' message appears above the match percentage.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To set up automatic merging of duplicate customer profiles: Select Reports → Merge & Cleanup Customers . Turn on Merge 100% Matched Customers . While Vagaro merges two customer profiles, a 'Merge in Progress' message appears above the match percentage.
ground_truth: Vagaro selects the target profile based on this order: if only one profile has signed in, that one is kept; if both have a card on file but only one has an email, the one with the email is kept; if both have a card and email, the profile with the most appointments and classes is kept.

question: What must be done before a custom service can be found on my Vagaro Listing Page?
context:
[Article: Add a Custom Service | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/22642423565211-Add-a-Custom-Service]
If none of the recommended Vagaro Services match what your business offers or you want to use a more creative name, you can add a custom service instead. You'll still be able to sell them together with Vagaro Services in a bundle , as a package , and in your memberships .

Note Each service must first be assigned to a service provider who accepts online bookings for customers to find them on your Vagaro Listing Page.

While managing your menu, you can search your services by entering part or all of a word to see exact and partial matches at the top of the left-side menu, then select the one you want to update. Alternatively, press Ctrl +K on a PC, or press Command + K on a Mac to move your cursor to the Search bar.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

On a Phone To add a custom service to your service menu: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Services . At the top of the screen, verify that the service is being created for the correct employee, or select the shown name to change it to another employee. At the bottom of the screen, select Add , and then select Service . Select Custom Service . The service is added to the list of services and classes. Enter the service's details: Service Name and Service Description , and Category . Resource : Any space or equipment limitations required to perform the service. Add-Ons Suggested : Service add-ons that customers may want to include with their service. Tax : Select any taxes applicable to this service. Business Cost : How much it costs your business to perform this service, such as supplies, materials, and rent. The cost must be less than what your service providers charge for this service. Cleanup Time : The amount of time blocked off after the service for the employee to clean up and prepare for the booking on their calendar. This reserved time is useful for back-to-back appointments. Mobile Services : Whether this service can be performed off-site from your business location. Show Online : Whether customers can see and book this service on your Vagaro Listing Page. Show Service Price Online : If customers can book this service on your Vagaro Listing Page, they can also see the service's price. Live Stream : Whether customers can attend this service remotely over the Internet. Price : How much the customer has to pay for the service. Price (with Tax) : Automatically calculated by Vagaro. Duration : Amount of time blocked off on the calendar to perform the service. Add Gap Time : Amount of time during a service available that can be used to provide service to another customer. Points Given : Number of points that a customer earns for completing the service. Points to Redeem : Number of points that a customer must redeem instead of cash for the service. Add Photo : Optionally, add a photo of the service or something that represents the service. Select Save . Repeat steps 3 to 7 to add additional services to your menu. To rearrange the order of the services or change their categories on your Vagaro Listing Page, drag the service's handle to their new place. Assign the service to an employee by updating their Employee Profile .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a custom service to your service menu: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. On the Services tab, select Add , and then select Service . Select Custom Service . Enter or update the service's details. Add Photo : Optionally, add a photo of the service or something that represents the service. Service Name , Description , and Category . Resource : Any space or equipment limitations required to perform the service. Business Cost : How much it costs your business to perform this service, such as supplies, materials, and rent. This cost must be less than the price charged for this service or workshop. Tax : Select any taxes applicable to this service. Suggested Add-Ons : The amount of time blocked off after the service for the employee to clean up and prepare for the booking on their calendar. This reserved time is useful for back-to-back appointments. Live Stream : Whether customers can attend this service remotely over the Internet. Mobile Services : Whether this service can be performed off-site from your business location. Cleanup Time : The amount of time blocked off after a service to clean up and prepare for the next service. This reserved time is useful for back-to-back appointments. Show Service Online : Whether customers can book this service and see its price on your Vagaro Listing Page. Select the employee(s) to perform the service. Performed By : Toggle on the switch beside the employee(s) who will perform the service. Price : How much the service costs to be performed by the service provider. The price charged must be greater than the Business Cost . Duration and Gap Time : Amount of time blocked off on the employee's calendar to perform the service, and optionally enter any
ground_truth: Each custom service must first be assigned to a service provider who accepts online bookings for customers to find it on your Vagaro Listing Page.

question: How do I delete a time off request from my Vagaro calendar?
context:
[Article: Delete a Time Off Request on Your Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/25141086757403-Delete-a-Time-Off-Request-on-Your-Calendar]
If you no longer wish to take time off on the date you selected, you can delete your request directly on your calendar. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To delete a time off request on your calendar: Go to the Calendar screen. Select the requested time off, and then select Delete . In the popup, select Yes to confirm that you want to delete your time off request.
ground_truth: Go to the Calendar screen, select the requested time off, then click Delete. In the popup, select Yes to confirm the deletion.

question: What should I do if I have set up deposits with an unsupported bank on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Unsupported Banks & Platforms on Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/30058314854555-Unsupported-Banks-Platforms-on-Vagaro]
The following online-only banks are not supported for deposits with Vagaro. If you add any of the banks below, you will not receive your funds. If you already set up deposits using one of the banks below, you must change the bank in your Merchant Account . Bancorp Bank Cash App Square Chime GO2bank Sutton Bank Stride Bank Lincoln Savings Bank GreenDot
ground_truth: If you have set up deposits with an unsupported bank, you must change the bank in your Merchant Account to ensure you receive your funds.

question: What should I do if I receive a suspicious email claiming to be from Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Avoid Phishing, Vishing, and Scams | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/28217516278043-Avoid-Phishing-Vishing-and-Scams]
Cybercriminals are constantly finding new ways to trick people into giving up sensitive information. These scams often target small businesses and their customers by pretending to be trusted companies, like Vagaro, banks, or payment providers. Knowing the warning signs of phishing, vishing, and social engineering can help you protect your business. Some examples we've seen include: Someone claiming ot be a Vagaro representative. Someone offering to feature your business on Vagaro's main page. Someone offering a promotion to advertise your business and increase your customers. Someone stating that Vagaro charges 30% for all new clients who book online and offering a one-time fee to waive this charge. Clicking even one link can compromise your account. A single click or an opened attachment can install malware that gives criminals access to your computer or device, where they can: Lock you out with ransomware until a fee is paid. Allow attackers to spread deeper into your company's servers or cloud storage. Steal confidential business information and customer data. Damage your business's reputation. These scams can look convincing, but knowing the warning signs of phishing, vishing, and other social engineering tactics will help keep your business safe. What is Phishing Phishing happens when scammers send fraudulent emails or text messages that look like they’re from a trusted source. These messages often: Ask you to click on a link to "verify" account details. Urge you to log in to fix an account issue. Include attachments that may install malware. If you receive an email that looks like it’s from Vagaro but asks for personal information or payment outside of the system, don’t respond. Instead, report it to support@vagaro.com . What is Vishing Vishing, or “voice phishing,” is when criminals use phone calls or voicemails to pose as trusted companies or even government agencies. They may: Claim there's a problem with your account or payment. Pretend to be Vagaro. Pressure you to act quickly, such as transferring funds or providing login details. Tip Vagaro will never call you to ask for your password, two-factor authentication code, credit card number, or bank account details. If you receive a suspicious call, hang up and contact us directly through the official support channels in your Vagaro account. All promotional announcements Vagaro sends can be found on the Vagaro software itself or from an email ending with @vagaro.com , like admin@vagaro.com or support@vagaro.com . What is Social Engineering? Social engineering refers to any tactic where criminals manipulate human trust instead of hacking technology. This includes phishing and vishing, but can also involve: Pretexting : Creating a fake scenario to gain your trust (for example, pretending to be from your business). Baiting : Offering something enticing (like “free software”) to trick you into downloading malware. Tailgating : Trying to physically enter a restricted area by following an employee. Scammers succeed by creating urgency, authority, or fear. For example, they might say, “Your account will be suspended unless you act now.” Recognizing these red flags is the best defense. Avoid Business Email Compromise (BEC) Scams When fraudulent communication happens in the workplace through an email that appears to come from a colleague, client, vendor, or other trusted source, it’s known as Business Email Compromise (BEC). In some cases, the attacker may have gained access to a legitimate email account that’s been compromised, making the message look even more convincing. While the impact of BEC can feel overwhelming, there are steps you can take to protect your business, your customers, and your employees: Verify payment or data requests with a phone call to a known contact. Avoid clicking on links or attachments from untrusted sources. Educate employees to report suspicious emails. Use strong passwords and multi-factor authentication on all email accounts. Regularly review your email account settings for unauthorized forwarding rules. How to Protect Yourself If you believe you’ve received a phishing email, a vishing call, or another type of social engineering attempt: Do not respond, click links, or download attachments. Forward suspicious emails to support@vagaro.com . Report vishing calls by noting the caller ID and details, then contact Vagaro Support directly. US : (925) 464-1932 ext. 2 UK : 020 3876 9138 ext. 2 Australia : (02) 8319 9138 ext 2 Canada : 778 897 2477 Alert your bank or credit card provider if you may have shared financial information. Set up a safe IP address at your business.
ground_truth: Do not respond, click links, or download attachments; instead, forward the suspicious email to support@vagaro.com.

question: How can I run an off-cycle payroll for contractors in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Run an Off-Cycle Payroll for Contractors | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/25191460540827-Run-an-Off-Cycle-Payroll-for-Contractors]
If you skipped payment to a contractor during a regular pay cycle, need to pay your contractors outside of your regular pay schedule, or want to give a bonus or reimbursement, you can run an off-cycle payroll. Unlike a regular payroll, an off-cycle payroll can be run anytime and paid to contractors within one to two business days. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To run an off-cycle payroll for contractors: Go to the Payroll report: Phone : Select More → Reports → Payroll History . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Reports → Payroll . In the Which Employees Are You Paying? screen, select Contractors . In the Which Payroll Do You Want to Run? screen, select Off-Cycle Payroll . At the top of the screen, configure your report filters, then select Run Report : Date Range : The start and end dates that the contractors are being paid for. Pay Date : The date when the contractors will receive their direct deposit, or the date to be shown on the paycheck. Contractors : Select the contractors to be paid. Optionally, to Include Past Employees , select Advanced Filters , and then select the checkbox. For each contractor in the list of payments, select Add Payment , to open the Update Pay popup and select the payment method, enter any other additional amounts to be included in the paycheck, then select Save . The Gross Pay is automatically updated as you enter the contractors' pay information. At the top of the screen, select Submit Payroll . In the Review and Submit Payroll popup, select each tab and review the payroll details to be submitted, then select Submit : Note These details do not include the amounts paid by check because they are not debited directly from your payroll. Company Pays : Lists the payments to each contractor, and a breakdown of the payment. Debited : Shows the amount to be debited from the business's bank account and deposited to your contractors' bank accounts. Payments made by check are not included in the debited amount. If you need to make any corrections, select the X in the upper-right corner of the popup, then update and resubmit the payroll. In the Vagaro Submitted popup, review the confirmed amounts and dates, then close the popup.
ground_truth: To run an off-cycle payroll for contractors in Vagaro, go to Reports → Payroll, select Contractors, choose Off-Cycle Payroll, configure your report filters, select the contractors, add payments, then review and submit the payroll in the popup.

question: How can staff members add a reminder or special instruction to a client's record before their visit?
context:
[Article: Manage Customer Notes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360007906613-Manage-Customer-Notes]
A note is a comment that an employee or service provider can add to a customer's profile for business purposes, such as reminders, allergy information, or an explanation of a customer's assessment and treatment; whereas an appointment note is specific to a scheduled appointment. Customer appointments on the calendar indicate that the customer has a note in their profile if there is a red or black note icon in the bottom-right corner of the appointment. There are four types of notes in Vagaro that may be added to a customer's profile:

General , Allergy , and Formula Notes ( ): These notes are intended for informational use that an employee should be aware of when booking an appointment for a customer. You can create as many of these notes as needed. Popup notes ( ): These notes will automatically appear in a popup message when booking a new appointment and when the customer is selected on the Checkout screen. Such notes are intended to alert the employee of important information about the customer or a reminder. For example, a pop-up note could be added if a customer still needs to fill out their forms, or a reminder to return a customer's sunglasses they left behind. You can add up to two pop-up notes.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

Customer notes are saved in their respective customer profiles. Here, they can also be updated and deleted as required

On the Vagaro Pro App To add a note to a customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. At the bottom of the screen, select Customers . Search for or select the customer. In the customer's profile, select the Notes tab. In the top-right corner of the screen, select the add (+) icon. Select the Note Type in the list, then enter the comment. Select Save .

On a Computer To add a note to a customer's profile: In the top menu, select Customers . Search for or select the customer. In the customer's profile, select the Notes tab. Select Create New . Select the Note Category , then enter the comment. Select Save .

Customer notes can also be added to a customer's profile when booking a new appointment.

On the Vagaro Pro App When booking an appointment in Vagaro Pro, customer profiles that already have a note will appear a note or popup note icon in Customers Notes . If there are both, then only the popup note icon will appear. To add a customer note when booking an appointment: On the calendar, select an available timeslot and create a New Appointment for the customer. Scroll down to Customer Notes and select it. In the top-right corner of the screen, select the add (+) icon. Select the Note Type in the list. Enter the text for the note. Select Save . Complete the customer's booking.

On a Computer When creating a new appointment on the web, an icon on the Notes button indicates if the customer has a note or pop-up note . If there are both, then only the popup note icon will appear. To add a customer note when booking an appointment: On the calendar, select an available timeslot and create a New Appointment for the customer. Search for or select the Customer . Below the customer's profile picture, select the Notes button. The customer's profile will appear on the Notes tab. Select Create New . Select the Note Category , then enter the comment. Select Save . Complete the customer's booking.

Customer notes can also be added to a customer's profile when updating an appointment that's already been scheduled.

On the Vagaro Pro App To add a customer note when editing an appointment: On the calendar, select the customer's appointment, then select Notes . In the top-right corner of the screen, select the add (+) icon. Select the Note Type in the list, then enter the comment. Select Save .

On a Computer When editing an existing appointment on the web, an icon on the Notes button indicates that the customer already has a note or pop-up note . If there are both, then only the popup note icon will appear. To add a customer note when editing an appointment: On the calendar, select the customer's appointment, then select Notes . Select Create New . Select the Note Category , then enter the comment. Select Save .
ground_truth: Staff can add a note to the client's profile, which can be a general comment, allergy info, or a reminder, either through the app or on a computer, and it will be saved in their profile for future reference.

question: How can I filter the Rebooking report to show data for a specific employee and location?
context:
