[Article: Run the Employee Rebooking Performance Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000510854-Run-the-Employee-Rebooking-Performance-Report]
The Rebooking report shows how many services or classes were booked and how many were rebooked on the Calendar or at Checkout during the selected timeframe. The report includes the following columns: Employee : The employee's name. Booked : The appointments booked with the employee in the date range, not counting rebookings. Rebooked : The number and percentage of appointments the employee rebooked at Checkout or on the Calendar. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone On the phone, the Rebooking report shows the data in a separate card for each employee. To run the Rebooking report: Select More → Reports → Rebooking . At the top of the screen, select the Date Range to report on. The report updates automatically each time you change the date range. Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set any of the following filters, and then select Run Report . Employees : Show data for all employees or a specific set of employees. Customers : Show data for all or a specific set of customers. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, show data for all locations or a specific set of locations. This option is available only if the Multi-Location Reports Access Level is set to View . Sort By : Reorder the report. Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Action , then: Select Star or Remove Star to bookmark or unbookmark. Select Print to get a hard copy of the report. Select Export PDF or Export Excel to download this report. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro The Rebooking report shows how often an employee is booked and rebooked in a table. To run the Rebooking report: Select Reports → Rebooking . Set the date range for your report: In the top-right corner, select Date Range to open the date picker. Use the date picker to choose a date range or a preset range like Yesterday or Last 7 Days . If you manually selected a date range, select Submit . Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set any of the following filters, and then select Run Report . Employees : Show data for all employees or a specific set of employees. Customers : Show data for all or a specific set of customers. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, show data for all locations or a specific set of locations. This option is available only if the Multi-Location Reports Access Level is set to View . Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set any of the following filters, and then select Run Report . Date Range : Show a specific time range. Employees : Show data for all employees or a specific set of employees. Customers : Show data for all or a specific set of customers. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, show data for all locations or a specific set of locations. This option is available only if the Multi-Location Reports Access Level is set to View . Optionally, sort, print, or export the report: Select a column header to sort the report. Select Print to get a hard copy of the report. Select Export to save the report to an Excel or PDF file (computer version only).
ground_truth: On a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro, you can set filters by selecting Filters in the top-right corner, then choosing the specific employee and location options before running the report.

question: How can I tell if my online booking site is connected to my Yelp page so customers can see the 'Book Now' option?
context:
[Article: Yelp Integration | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204228314-Yelp-Integration]
The Yelp Integration helps expand your online booking presence by directing customers from your Yelp listing to your Vagaro booking page by adding a "Book Now" button to your Yelp page. This makes it easier for customers to book with you while browsing Yelp. You can also track bookings from Yelp using the Source report . To set up your Yelp business, go to one of the following pages: Find & Claim Your Business Add Your Business Important If you have a Yelp business account set up, Vagaro will automatically turn this integration on for you, as long as you meet the requirements . Currently, the "Book Now" button will only display on the Yelp app on iPhones or iPads. Requirements To activate the Yelp integration, you must: Are located in the United States. Are not on the Vagaro free trial. Have an active Yelp Business account. Have Online Booking turned on. Ensure your business name, address, and phone number match on Yelp. Check Yelp Integration Status To see whether the integration has been activated: Go to your Business Profile : Phone : Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Business Profile . Make sure List Business on Yelp is turned on. If the Yelp integration is not turned on, then you've either turned off this integration before or don't meet the requirements . Deactivate the Yelp Integration To deactivate the Yelp Integration in Vagaro: Go to your Business Profile : Phone : Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Business Profile . Turn off List Business on Yelp . It may take up to 24 hours for the Yelp integration to be disconnected.
ground_truth: You can check your Business Profile settings to see if the 'List Business on Yelp' feature is enabled, which indicates your Yelp integration is active.

question: What happens if a Daily Deal does not meet Vagaro's quality standards during review?
context:
[Article: Create a Daily Deal for Services & Classes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204254384-Create-a-Daily-Deal-for-Services-Classes]
Keep in mind that Vagaro reviews all Daily Deals submitted for quality assurance before they appear on the Vagaro marketplace, which means the Vagaro team will edit the text for clarity, grammar, and spelling when necessary. If the deal does not fit Vagaro's standards, it will be denied. Denied deals will still be shown on your Vagaro Listing Page and Email/Text marketing campaigns. Ensure that you've read our best practices before continuing.

When you announce a Daily Deal, an Email Marketing campaign will be automatically sent to your existing customers, using the email allowance from your monthly limit. By default, this feature is enabled, but you can turn it off by toggling off the Send as Email option. It's important to note that businesses on the small Email Marketing plan won't be able to send Daily Deal emails when they reach their limit.

Note If you choose to send an email or text message with the Daily Deal, the message will be sent out as soon as you announce the campaign, even if you scheduled it for later.

On a Phone To create a Daily Deal: Select More → Daily Deals . Select Create Daily Deal . If you have created a deal before, select Create New in the top-right corner. Select a photo for your Daily Deal to be shown to customers: Select Category , scroll through the different image categories, like Barber or Graduation , and pick a theme that works best for your Daily Deal. Select Photo , then select an image for your deal. Enter details for your Daily Deal. Daily Deal Name : Check our Daily Deal guidelines to prevent denials. Description . (Max 500 characters) Important Your description must be at least 100 characters for approval. Fine Print : Include any requirements or limitations this deal has. (Max 500 characters) Set your Daily Deal Period : Start : When you want to announce your deal. Discount Has an Expiration Date : The date when customers can no longer use this deal. If a customer tries to book an appointment that falls after the expiration date, they can't apply the Daily Deal. Configure the Daily Deal's settings: Note You can only send a Daily Deal as a text on the web, tablet, and Pay Desk version of Vagaro. Limit Total Number of Uses : The total amount of times this deal can be used. Limit to One Use Per Person : Whether each customer profile can only use this deal once. Discount will be redeemable by : Whether new and/or existing customers can use your deal in-house and online. Note If you provide this deal to new customers only, no emails or notifications will be sent to your existing customers. By default, all customers can use this deal. Customers will only be considered "existing" if they have a Customer Profile in your Customer List ; otherwise, they are new customers. Discount will be applied to : Whether this deal can be applied to all applicable items in a cart or can be used for one item. Offered by : Limit the service provider(s) who offer this deal. Only Display on Daily Deals Page : Whether this deal will automatically apply to applicable items when customers shop online. In order to apply this deal, the customer will need to visit your Daily Deal page. Send as Email (on by default): Announce this deal with an email campaign. Send as Text : Announce this deal with a text campaign. This setting requires a Text Marketing plan . Select the Services & Classes Daily Deal type. For other Daily Deal types, see the following articles: Create Daily Deals for Gift Cards Create Daily Deals for Memberships & Packages Add services or classes to your deal: Select Service / Classes / Add-Ons . Select the services and/or classes to be applied to this deal. Select Confirm . Enter your deal's discount under Discount Amount : If you selected one service or class, enter the Price Before and the Price After the discount. If you selected multiple services and/or classes, enter the percent discount. Select Preview . After reviewing your deal, scroll down the Terms & Conditions , review the Daily Deal guidelines , and acknowledge. Select Announce . After announcing your deal, you can share it on Facebook.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To create a Daily Deal: Select Settings → Daily Deals . Select Create New Deal . Select the Services & Classes Daily Deal type. For other Daily Deal types, see the following articles: Create Daily Deals for Gift Cards Create Daily Deals for Memberships & Packages Select a photo for your Daily Deal to be shown to customers: Under Select Daily Category & Photo in the side menu, scroll through the different image categories, like Barber or Graduation , and pick a theme that works best for your Daily Deal. Select an image. Enter details for your Daily Deal. Daily Deal Name : Check our Daily Deal guidelines to prevent denials. Daily Deal Description . (Max 500 characters) Important Your description must be at least 100 characters for approval. Fine Print : Include
ground_truth: If a Daily Deal does not fit Vagaro's standards, it will be denied, but it will still be shown on your Vagaro Listing Page and in Email/Text marketing campaigns.

question: If I want to make sure my clients give me their contact details when they fill out a form, how can I set that up?
context:
[Article: Collect and Update Customer Contact Information With a Form | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24398221452571-Collect-and-Update-Customer-Contact-Information-With-a-Form]
Request your customers and employees to provide their contact information when filling out your form or SOAP note using the Contact module. The contact element can be used to update information in their customer profile or create a new one. Note The Contact module cannot be added to mandatory forms. Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To add the Contact module to your form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . Go to your form or SOAP note: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To create a SOAP note template , select Create New → SOAP Note . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . Under Form Elements , drag the Contact element into the form designer. The element's settings will appear on the right side of the screen. If you already have this element in the form designer, select it to make changes. Enter a Title for this element. Customize additional settings: To stop collecting certain information, such as an email address or phone number, clear the checkbox next to that option. To use this form to update or create new customer profiles at your business, turn on Update or Save Customer , then choose Create New Customer or Update Existing Customer . If you choose to update existing customers, your customers will be required to log in to Vagaro. Your customers must also enter their email if they want to create a new profile through the form. To make this element required, turn on Required Field . Publish your form: To save this form as a draft and publish it later, in the top-right corner, select Save as Draft . This form is saved in form management and can be published at a later time. To see a preview of your form and how it'll look for your customers, in the top-right corner, select Preview . You can select the Computer , Tablet , or Phone icon to see how your form will look on each device. To publish your form, go to Settings , then in the top-right corner, select Publish . Your form is saved in form management.
ground_truth: You can add the Contact module to your form and turn on the 'Required Field' option to ensure clients provide their contact information.

question: How do I download the Vagaro Pro app on my device?
context:
[Article: Download the Vagaro and Vagaro Pro App | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/27418205625883-Download-the-Vagaro-and-Vagaro-Pro-App]
Vagaro offers two different mobile apps, each designed for a specific audience: Vagaro App: The customer-facing app allows you to book appointments with businesses and check your upcoming appointments with them. Vagaro Pro App: The business-facing app, built for service providers, employees, and business owners. This is the main hub you can use to run a business, allowing you to manage appointments, check out customers, and run your reports. Both apps are free to download, but it's important to choose the right one based on whether you're a customer or a business. Download the Vagaro Customer App Keep Vagaro at your fingertips with the Vagaro app. Just like the web version , you'll be able to book appointments, register for classes, track your purchases, and explore the Vagaro Marketplace. To download the Vagaro app and log in: Go to your device's app store. On an Apple device (iPhone or iPad), open the Apple App Store . On an Android phone, open the Google Play Store . Enter Vagaro in the search box. Beside the Vagaro app, select Get (iPhone or iPad) or Install (on an Android phone). Note Do NOT select the Vagaro Pro app (red app with a white Vagaro logo), which is for businesses using the software. When the app has been installed, select Open . If prompted, allow Vagaro to access your device's location services. On an Android Device On an Apple Device If either Apple or Android asks permission to send Notifications , select Allow . Sign in to your Vagaro account , or create a new account . Download the Vagaro Pro App Manage your Vagaro calendar on the web, or download the Vagaro Pro app to work in-house and on-the-go. Unlike the customer version, the Vagaro Pro app is specifically for business owners and their employees to manage business operations, engage clients, and check out customers. You'll also be notified about new and upcoming appointments, waitlists, and business tasks that need your attention. To download the Vagaro Pro App Go to your device's app store. On an Apple device (iPhone or iPad) : Open the Apple App Store . On an Android device : Open the Google Play Store . Enter Vagaro Pro in the search box. Beside the Vagaro Pro app, select Get (iPhone or iPad) or Install (on an Android device). Note Do NOT select the Vagaro app (white app with a red Vagaro logo), which customers use to search for and book appointments. When the app has been installed, select Open . If prompted, allow Vagaro to access your device's camera. On an Android Device On an Apple Device If your phone asks for permission to send you notifications, select Allow . Log in to your Vagaro account , or create a new account.
ground_truth: To download the Vagaro Pro app, open your device's app store, search for 'Vagaro Pro,' and then select 'Get' on an Apple device or 'Install' on an Android device. After installation, open the app and log in or create a new account.

question: How can I include images or videos to help customers better understand my products?
context:
[Article: Sell Products Online | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360035414133-Sell-Products-Online]
to the product you want to change, then skip to step 4. Scan or enter your product's barcode, then select Next : With a Barcode Scanner : Plug the scanner into your device, press and hold the power button, and hold it up to the barcode until you hear a beep. The code will appear in the text box. Without a Scanner : Manually enter the product ID in the text box. On a Tablet or PayPro Mini : Select Scan , then point your device's camera at the barcode to scan it. Note Scanning a barcode that Vagaro recognizes will automatically populate its name, brand, and images into your product's details. If you want to make changes, you can edit the product as needed. Enter your Product Information : Enter the Product Name . This name will also appear on your Vagaro Listing Page if you sell products online . Choose a Brand from the preset brand options or add a new one . Brands help categorize your inventory and make it easier to filter products in reports and on your Vagaro Listing Page . In the Category list, choose the categorization that best fits your product. Categories group products by type, which makes it easier for customers to browse and for you to manage inventory and sales reports. In the Vendor list, create a new vendor or choose one you've already added. The vendor is the person or company you work with to supply the product. Assigning a vendor allows you to configure Purchase Orders and Drop Shipping. If you don't have a vendor, select No Vendor . Enter a Description of your product. Your Description helps customers understand what your product is when they browse your online inventory. Optionally, under Additional Details , select Add Detail , then choose or add a template. Additional Details are collapsible content you can add to your product listings. Under Upload Image , add an image or video to your product. Any image you add will be shown on your product listing page . Note If you added variants to your products, you will need to edit each variant instead of the main product. Optionally, under Variants , select Add Options to add varieties of the same product . Under Pricing , configure the cost of your product and whether you offer points: Enter the Business Cost and Selling Price of your product. Tip Business Cost isn't the price you're selling the product for; it's what you paid to stock it. Your sales reports will use Business Cost to calculate profit. In the Tax list, choose any tax rates that apply to this product. Enter the Points Given and Points to Redeem if you have a points system . Under Product Application , turn on Sell Product Online , then enter the Dimensions and Weight of your product. You only need to enter the product's dimensions and weight to sell products online for in-house pickup. If you ship products to your customers, turn on one of the options below: To offer free shipping to your customers, turn on Free Ground Shipping , then enter the cost for Shipping and Handling . By turning this on, you're confirming that your business will cover the shipping cost. Your reporting will factor in this cost when reviewing your sales, and your customers will be able to purchase your products with free shipping. To set a base shipping price for the customer's first item and additional items added to the cart, enter your base shipping price in 1st Item Shipping Price , then enter the other rate in the Additional Item Shipping Price . Select Save . Your new product will appear in the Inventory Management screen and can be added to the customer's cart at Checkout .
ground_truth: You can add an image or video to your product listing under the Upload Image section, and it will be displayed on your product's page to help customers see what you're offering.

question: How can I filter my email campaign recipients to only include customers who have been with my business since before a specific date?
context:
[Article: Filter Audience for Email Campaigns | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412639225883-Filter-Audience-for-Email-Campaigns]
All email campaign types allow you to filter your customer list based on a specific criteria, allowing you to create targeted email campaigns. For example, you can use these filters to:

Create a newsletter with General Tag . Customers who subscribe to your mailing list from your MySite will have a "subscribed" general tag added to their profile. Use this tag to filter only for these customers. Create a campaign that only sends to customers who have booked an appointment with me. ( Customers of Employees ) Create a campaign that automatically sends customers a promo code for a free service after they spend more than $1,000 at your business. ( Amount Paid Between ) Create a campaign that targets customers who've gone to your business since day one. ( Customer Since ) and more...

Note In the Vagaro Pro app, filters are limited to Gender , Customers Of , and Age Range . To use the full range of selection and filtering options, we recommend creating your campaign on the Web or Tablet version.

On a Phone To filter your customers: Start creating an email marketing campaign. If you're not sure how, see the following articles: Send a One-Time Email Blast to Your Customers Send Automatic Birthday Emails to Your Customers Reclaim Lost Customers with an Email Campaign Set Up Before-Visit Email Notifications & Reminders Set Up After-Visit Email Notifications & Reminders Choose a campaign type and select Apply on the Create Campaign screen. Doing so will open the Send To screen, where you can narrow down your campaign's recipient list. Gender Customers Of : Send to customers who've had a service with a specific service provider. Age Range : Adjust the slider to select an age range (from 0 to 110) for customers to receive the campaign. Configure the rest of your campaign and announce it.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To filter your customers: Start creating an email marketing campaign. If you're not sure how, see the following articles: Send a One-Time Email Blast to Your Customers Send Automatic Birthday Emails to Your Customers Reclaim Lost Customers with an Email Campaign Set Up Before-Visit Email Notifications & Reminders Set Up After-Visit Email Notifications & Reminders When creating a campaign, select Edit Filter next to Select Recipients . Doing so will open the Select Filtered Audience , where you can narrow down your campaign's recipient list to customers who fit certain criteria. Customer Since :The date a customer's profile was created or added to your business. Select Any if you don't have a preference. Select Before to target customers who've been with the business before a specific date. Then select the Date . Select After to message customers who've been with you after the selected Date . Last Visit : The customer's last checked-out appointment or purchase. Select Any if you don't have a preference. Select Before to target customers who last visited your business before a specific date. Then select the Date . Select After to send a message to customers who've visited you after the selected Date . Select Between for customer visits between a selected date range. No. of Apps Between : Send the message to customers who have a specific number of appointments. Enter 10 in the left field and 0 in the right, and the software will select customers who have had at least ten appointments. If you enter 10 in the left field and 10 in the right, the software will send to customers with exactly ten appointments. Amount Paid Between : Send the campaign to customers who spent a specific amount of money at your business. General Tag : A keyword or phrase added to a customer's profile and used as an identifier or as a way to find a customer. Enter a customer tag here to send the campaign to customers with that tag. Enter multiple tags separated by commas. Referred By : Limit the campaign to customers referred to your business by a specific client. Customers of Employees : Send to customers who've had a service with a specific service provider. Use checkboxes (or Select All) to select employees from the drop-down list. If you check Customers of Past Employees , the list of choices expands to include your past employees. If you have the access level of Employees Can Only Send Campaigns to Own Customers set, the drop-down list will show only your name, grayed out, to indicate that you cannot select other employees for the campaign. Birthday : Gender Age Between : Adjust the slider to select an age range (from 0 to 110) for customers to receive the campaign. Has or Doesn't Have Membership : Filter your list to only send to customers with selected Memberships. Past Services/Classes/Packages : Filter the list by customers who've had or taken a specific service or class or purchased a particular package , and select the field under Past Services/Classes/Packages . Then, use checkboxes to select options to include in the filter, and select Apply . Exclude Customers with Failed Phone Numbers : Filter the list to exclude customers with an invalid phone number . Exclude Customers with Failed Emails :
ground_truth: Use the 'Customer Since' filter, select 'Before,' and then choose the specific date to target customers who have been with your business since before that date.

question: Can I set an auto-renew limit for a package or a membership?
context:
[Article: Difference Between a Package and a Membership | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/49847995100187-Difference-Between-a-Package-and-a-Membership]
Packages and Memberships allow you to reward loyal customers by setting discounts for multiple services and classes. View the comparison table below to learn more about the differences between the two. Packages Memberships Include services and classes, and products Include services, classes, and products. Sell a set number of future visits for services and classes Apply discounts to a set number of visits Auto-renew can be based on time (days, months, years) or number of completed visits. Auto-renew can only be time based (days, months, years) Can include sales tax Can include sales tax Let you set an auto-renew limit Let you set an auto-renew limit Carry unused visits forward to the next billing cycle Carry unused visits forward to the next billing cycle Let you pause Auto-renew for all package holders or individual customers Let you pause Auto-renew for all membership holders or individual customers A package is a pre-bundled set of specific services, classes, or products that customers use over time. A membership applies discounts across eligible services, classes, and products instead of selling one fixed bundle. Example of when to use a package: This is applicable if you have a fitness center. You want to sell three cardio sessions at a discounted price to reward loyal customers and renew these sessions automatically based on a specific charge frequency. Example of when to use a membership : This is applicable if you own a massage therapy center and you want to reward loyal customers by adding a discount to multiple massage services, but you want to restrict this discount to 15 visits a month.
ground_truth: Yes, both packages and memberships let you set an auto-renew limit.

question: What should I do if my PayPro Mini isn't pairing with my Mac or PC after connecting via USB-C?
context:
[Article: Connect PayPro Mini to Mac or PC | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/33992723390235-Connect-PayPro-Mini-to-Mac-or-PC]
Known Issue with Chrome Browsers We’re aware of an issue in the latest Chrome and Edge updates that can prevent your device from pairing. Our team is working on a fix. In the meantime, follow the troubleshooting steps below. Open Vagaro in Chrome or Edge. Go to permissions: Chrome : Select View Site Information → Site settings . Edge : Select View Site Information → Permissions for this site . Change your permissions: Chrome : From the Apps on device list, select Allow . Edge : From the Sites can ask to connect to any device on your local network list, select Allow . Refresh your browser The PayPro Mini allows you to securely accept credit card payments on your Mac or PC. To connect your PayPro Mini, you'll need to install or update the Vagaro Pro Utility, plug in your device, and complete the pairing process. Once connected, your PayPro Mini will automatically pair with your computer whenever it's plugged in. Once connected, the PayPro Mini becomes an extension of your checkout. The screen stays idle until you add an item in Vagaro, instantly displaying the total. Customers can tap, insert, or swipe their card on the PayPro Mini screen when it's time to charge. They can also add a tip and choose to print a receipt—all from the device. Note Your computer and PayPro Mini can only be connected via USB-C and must always be plugged in to stay connected. You will not get the option to print a tip receipt if you use the PayPro Mini to check out customers. Prerequisites: This feature is available only in the US and requires PayPro Mini , an active Internet connection, and Credit Card Processing . You can't pair a PayPro Mini with a Chromebook or Pay Desk. Step One: Update the Vagaro Pro Utility You must download the Vagaro Pro utility on your device to pair with the PayPro Mini. If you already have the Vagaro Pro utility, make sure it's on version 1.2.1 or above (Windows) or 1.1.6 or above (Mac). To update the utility: Open Vagaro on the device you want to pair with. Go to the Checkout screen. Unplug the Vagaro Credit Card Reader, PayPro Go, or PayPro Mini from your device if you haven't already. In the bottom-right corner, select Pay By Card , then select PayPro Mini . A popup will appear, asking you to update the Vagaro Pro utility. If a popup does not appear, you're up-to-date. If there is an update, a popup will appear, prompting you to download the updated utility. Download the file. Open the file in your Downloads folder to install the updated utility. After installation is complete, you can pair your device and continue accepting credit card payments. Step Two: Plug Your PayPro Mini to Your Computer The PayPro Mini can only be connected to a computer through a USB-C cable. After connecting the two devices, you can pair them together. To connect your PayPro Mini: Press and hold the power button on the PayPro mini until it powers on. Using the USB-C cable included with your PayPro Mini, plug one end of the cable into the port on the back of the dock or directly into the PayPro Mini. Plug the other end of the USB-C cable into your computer. The PayPro Mini will make a "ding" sound and will show the onboarding screen. Step Three: Pair With Your PayPro Mini Pair your PayPro Mini to begin accepting credit cards. After pairing the two devices for the first time, your PayPro will automatically pair with your device whenever they're connected. To pair your PayPro Mini: Open Vagaro on the device you want to pair with. Go to the Checkout screen. In the bottom-right corner, select Connect , then select PayPro Mini in the menu. The Connect button will change to Pay With Card when successfully paired.
ground_truth: Ensure you have the latest version of the Vagaro Pro Utility (1.1.6 or above for Mac), update the utility if needed, and verify that your device is properly connected via USB-C. Also, check permissions in Chrome or Edge as instructed, and make sure your computer and PayPro Mini are always plugged in via USB-C for a successful connection.

question: How can I check which gadgets have been used to access an employee's account on my phone or computer?
context:
[Article: View Your Connected Devices | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/28278423726747-View-Your-Connected-Devices]
Use Connected Devices to see a history of the devices that have been used to log into the employee's account. Removing a device will require that the employee log in again to Vagaro on that device. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To view your connected devices: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Select the employee's profile. In the employee's profile, under Locations , select the Action menu, then select Password & Security . Note If the employee works for multiple locations , select the Action menu for the location to see the devices used to log into the employee's account for that location. Scroll down to the Connected Devices section. To remove a connected device: Select the devices to be removed. Select Delete In the popup, select Delete . Select Save . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view your connected devices: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . At the end of the employee's row, select the Action menu, then select Edit Profile . Note If the employee works for multiple locations , select the employee's blue Locations link, then select the Action menu to see the devices used to log into the employee's account for that location. Select the Password & Security tab. Scroll down to the Connected Devices section. To remove a connected device: Select the devices to be removed. Select Delete In the popup, select Delete . Select Save .
ground_truth: You can view the connected devices by navigating to the employee's profile settings, then accessing the Password & Security section to see the devices listed there.

question: How can I fill out a new form for a customer from their profile on Vagaro's web version?
context:
[Article: Fill Out a Form from a Customer's Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008820354-Fill-Out-a-Form-from-a-Customer-s-Profile]
Forms can be filled out in a customer's profile to include customer surveys, consent forms, liability waivers, and other forms to a customer's profile without requesting the customer to complete it. All of a customer's completed, incomplete, and duplicated forms are available on the Forms tab under the form's name. The date of when the form was completed or last updated is also shown shown beside that version of the form's name. Unfinished forms can be saved to be completed later, or if a form has already been filled out and submitted, it can be updated as long as an employee has not signed it. Signed forms will need to be duplicated to make changes. Prerequisites : This feature requires Forms , which is currently available only on the web version of Vagaro, and that you've created at least one form template. To fill out a form from a customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Forms tab. Note On a phone, swipe left on the top menu to see the Forms tab. Select the Fill New Form menu, then select the form to be filled out. In the popup select Fill Form . Fill in the form, then select Save .
ground_truth: Go to Customers, select the customer, click on the Forms tab, click 'Fill New Form', choose the form to be filled out, select 'Fill Form' in the popup, complete the form, then click 'Save'.

question: How can I make a form mandatory for customers when they book a specific service or class?
context:
[Article: Make Forms Mandatory for Your Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24398220401819-Make-Forms-Mandatory-for-Your-Customers]
By default, forms must be manually sent to customers from their profile or directly from the Calendar . If you’d rather have customers fill out a form before they arrive, you can require a form when someone books an appointment, when they purchase a specific membership, or require a form for all new customers. You can make multiple forms mandatory so customers must complete all required forms before booking or checkout. From your form's settings, you have the option to turn on these settings: Form is Mandatory for all Clients : Require every customer, new or existing, to complete this form. They'll be prompted to fill out this form when they book or buy anything online (except gift cards), or when you select their profile during checkout, create a new profile for them, send them an invoice, or book an appointment for them. Form is Mandatory When Booking and Form is Mandatory When Purchasing Membership will be hidden after turning on this setting. Form is Mandatory When Booking : Require customers to fill out a form every time they book a service or class. You can choose to apply this setting to your entire menu or just a few services. You can also require the form to be completed every time a customer books an appointment, even if they've already filled it out before. Form is Mandatory When Purchasing Membership : Require customers to fill out a form when purchasing a membership. This is especially useful if you need customers to sign a contract for membership or auto-renewal purposes. Any forms customers submit will be attached to their profile if they're logged in. You can ensure forms aren't submitted anonymously by requiring customer to log in . If the prompt to fill out a form appears when you're booking for the customer or checking out their items, you'll have the option for them to fill out a form from your device, or send it directly to them via email or phone. Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To make forms mandatory: Go to Forms . Create or edit a form: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . From the form designer, in the top-left corner, select Settings . Turn on the settings you want to configure your form: To require this form for all customers, turn on Form is Mandatory for All Clients . Form is Mandatory for All Clients and Form is Mandatory When Booking will not be available if you turn this setting off. To require this form for customers booking specific services and classes, turn on Form is Mandatory When Booking , then select the services and classes you want to require this form. If you want to require this form even when your customer has filled it out before, turn on Always Require Form . To require this form for customers when purchasing a membership , turn on Form is Mandatory When Purchasing Membership , then select the Memberships you want to require this form. If you want to require this form even when your customer has filled it out before, turn on Always Require Form . Make changes to your form as needed, then in the top-right corner, select Publish .
ground_truth: To require a form for specific services or classes, turn on 'Form is Mandatory When Booking' in the form's settings and then select the services and classes you want to require the form for.

question: How can I see which clients had their appointments canceled or marked as no-shows within a specific period?
context:
[Article: Cancellations and No-Shows Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000346733-Cancellations-and-No-Shows-Report]
Run the Cancellation & No-Shows report to view appointments that were denied, cancelled, deleted, or deemed as no-shows during a selected time range, as well as any changes to the appointment over the past 15 days. This report is useful to see which appointments were booked but ultimately were cancelled. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To run the Cancellations and No-Shows report: Select More → Reports → Cancellation & No-Shows . At the top of the screen, select the Date Range to report on. The data is automatically updated each time you select a different date range. To filter your report, select Filter , change the following filters as needed, and then select Run Report . Customer : Select one or more customers to include, or select All Customers . Event : Whether to list cancelled or no-show Appointments and/or Services . Appointment Status : Select one or more appointment statuses to include. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, select the multi-location businesses or all locations in a group you want to review. Sort By : Select the order in which to show the report's results. Optionally, to export your report, select Action , then choose an option: Print : Print your report if your device is connected to a printer. Export PDF or Export Excel : Save the report to a PDF or Excel file. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To run the Cancellations and No-Shows report: Select Reports → Appointments Summary . Optionally, at the top of the screen, set the filters for your report, then select Run Report . Date Range : Select the start and end dates of the report, then select Submit . Customer : Select one or more customers to include, or select All Customers . Event : Whether to list cancelled or no-show Appointments and/or Services . Advanced Filters : Optionally, select any additional filters to further refine the report. Status : Select one or more appointment statuses to include, or select All Status . Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, select the multi-location businesses or all locations in a group you want to review. To sort your report, select the column headers. To export or print this report, select an option in the bottom-right corner: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only). What's in This Report? The report will show the following information: Appointment Date : The date when the appointment or class occurred. Status : The status of the appointment. Status Changed Date : The date and time when the appointment status was last changed. Status ChangedBy : The employee who changed the status of the appointment. Comments : Any comments that were added to the appointment. Service Provider : The employee who provided the service. Service Name : The service or class that was booked. Customer : The name of the customer. History : Select the link to show the Timeline of all the changes that were made to the appointment over the past 15 days.
ground_truth: You can run the Cancellations and No-Shows report by selecting the appropriate date range and filters to view appointments that were denied, canceled, deleted, or considered no-shows during that time.

Customers of Employees : Send to customers who've had a service with a specific service provider. Use checkboxes (or Select All) to select employees from the drop-down list. If you check Customers of Past Employees , the list of choices expands to include your past employees. If you have the access level of Employees Can Only Send Campaigns to Own Customers set, the drop-down list will show only your name, grayed out, to indicate that you cannot select other employees for the campaign. Birthday : Gender Age Between : Adjust the slider to select an age range (from 0 to 110) for customers to receive the campaign. Has or Doesn't Have Membership : Filter your list to only send to customers with selected Memberships. Past Services/Classes/Packages : Filter the list by customers who've had or taken a specific service or class or purchased a particular package , and select the field under Past Services/Classes/Packages . Then, use checkboxes to select options to include in the filter, and select Apply . Exclude Customers with Failed Phone Numbers : Filter the list to exclude customers with an invalid phone number . Exclude Customers with Failed Emails : Filter the list to exclude customers with an invalid email address . Configure the rest of your campaign, then announce
ground_truth: You can use filters like 'Customer Since' to target clients based on when they joined and 'No. of Apps Between' to select customers with a certain number of appointments.

question: How do I set the Star TSP100III USB printer as the default printer on my Mac?
context:
[Article: Star USB Printer Setup - Mac Computers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347320-Star-USB-Printer-Setup-Mac-Computers]
This article will show you how to install, connect, and troubleshoot the Star TSP100III USB printer. See this article to set up a Star USB Printer with a Windows computer. Physical Setup Important The USB Star Printer, model TSP100 ECO, must have both the USB and the power cable connected. Once the power cable and USB cable are connected, the power light will appear. Connecting just the power cord will not power the device. USB Cable Make sure you are using the correct cable. The USB Cable looks like the image below and is included in the packaging. The port to the printer is marked A , and the port to the computer is marked B . Connect the USB cable to the printer using the USB Type-B port on the printer, then secure it by running the cable through the cable hook. Connect the USB cable to your computer. Power Cable Connect the included power cable to the inlet on the back of the TSP100III, then connect the power cable to the outlet. On the left side of the device, flip the power switch on and check that the blue LED lights up. Next, insert a roll of paper, check that the power is on, and then push the lever on the top to open the cover. If your paper roll is 58mm wide, install the supplied paper guide inside the printer. The direction you insert the paper roll is important. Insert the roll with the paper edge under the roll, not over the roll. See the illustration below. Pull the end of the paper straight out and over the front of the printer. Close the printer cover by pushing it down on both sides. Download and Install the Printer Driver Select on this link to download the driver: https://vsupport.azureedge.net/forums/tsp100_v750_lite.zip . Open the downloaded file. Open the folder labeled tsp100_v750_lite.zip . Open the folder labeled Mac . Open the folder labeled Cups . Unzip the folder labeled starcupsdrv-4.7.0_mac0.zip . Open the folder starcupsdrv-4.7.0_mac . Open the folder labeled Driver . Open starcupsdrv-4.7.0.pkg . Follow the instructions on the wizard to finish the driver installation process. Add the Printer and Set it up as Default Connect the printer to the computer using the supplied USB cable and power on the printer. Select the Apple icon on the top-left corner of the screen, then select System Preferences . Select Print & Fax . In the Default Printer drop-down box, select STAR TSP 100 . Printing a Sales Receipt and Changing Printer Settings Open Safari, go to the Vagaro website, then log in. At Customer Checkout , select Print Receipt . A printer menu appears. Make sure to expand the window by selecting on the drop-down arrow. Deselect the Print Headers and Footers box. Under Paper Size , select Manage Custome Sizes . Select the + icon under the list field. Rename Untitled to Vagaro . Follow the settings from the image and select OK to save. Select on the drop-down menu titled Safari , then select Features . From the Features drop-down menu, select Output Options . From the Features Sets drop-down menu, select Cut Length . Set Page and Document Cut to Partial Cut . On the Features drop-down menu, select Cash Drawer . (Skip this step if you don't have a cash drawer). On the Cash Drawer drop-down menu, select Open Drawer 1 . On the Cash Drawer 1 Pulse width menu, select 200 milliseconds . Make sure the cash drawer is plugged into the printer. On the Presets menu, select Save As and save settings as Vagaro . Test Print Test your setup by printing a receipt. In your Vagaro account, hover over Reports , then select Transactions List from the drop-down menu. Run a report and select any transaction. Select Receipt from the Action menu to print. Confirm and select Print . If the printer is connected correctly, it will print out your receipt. If not, refer back to the "Adding the Printer" section. Example of a test print:
ground_truth: Connect the printer to your Mac with the USB cable, turn it on, then go to System Preferences > Print & Fax, and select STAR TSP 100 from the Default Printer drop-down menu.

question: What are the processing rates for small merchants processing in-person payments via swipe, dip, or tap?
context:
[Article: United States - Credit Card Processing Rates and Fees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000595607-United-States-Credit-Card-Processing-Rates-and-Fees]
For every credit or debit card you process with Vagaro, our processing fees are deducted from the total before it's deposited into your bank account. The fees deducted depend on the type of transaction and whether you're on a Large or Small Merchant plan; you selected your plan when you signed up for Credit Card Processing. You can view all fee deductions and your net earnings from the Credit Card Deposits Report .

Vagaro does not allow passing on credit card processing fees to the customer because this practice is banned by some cardholders. See Surcharges and Convenience Fees before continuing.

Processing rates differ by country. If you are outside of the US, see the following articles:

Australia - Credit Card Processing Rates and Fees Canada - Credit Card Processing Rates and Fees United Kingdom - Credit Card Processing Rates and Fees

Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing .

Vagaro offers competitive processing rates and a wide range of other benefits with Credit Card Processing.

For businesses with a processing volume of less than $4,000 per month.

Swipe, Dip, or Tap Keyed-in Tap to Phone 2.6% + $0.10 per transaction 3.5%+ $0.19 per keyed-in transaction 2.6% + $0.16 per tap

There is no monthly fee required for Small Merchants.

For businesses with a processing volume of more than $4,000 per month.

Swipe, Dip, or Tap Keyed-in Tap to Phone 2.29% + $0.19 per transaction 3.5% + $0.19 per keyed-in transaction 2.29% + $0.25 per tap

There is a $10 monthly subscription for Large Merchants and additional FANF and the Mastercard Location fees are applied.

If you disable this feature before your billing date, you will still be charged for the feature at the end of your billing cycle, regardless of when you deactivated it.

Swipe, Dip, or Tap Transactions : Payments processed through a Credit Card Reader, PayPro Mini, or Tap to Phone when a card or digital wallet is swiped, dipped, or tapped in person.

Keyed-in Transactions : Payments processed using a saved card on file or by manually entering card details. This includes online payments, recurring payments for memberships and packages, and online purchases made with Google Pay or Apple Pay.

ACH Fee (1%) : Bank transfers through Rent Collection are subject to a 1% ACH fee. This fee applies only to bank transfers with Rent Collection; payments made with credit or debit cards will be charged the keyed-in fee. Buy Now, Pay Later Fee (6% + $0.30) : Any items paid for with Buy Now, Pay Later are subject to a 6% + $0.30 fee per transaction. This fee is charged to the business, not the customer. FANF Fee ( Variable ) : FANF stands for Fixed Acquirer Network Fee and is charged to large merchants only (Vagaro absorbs this fee for Small Merchants). If you have a monthly sales volume under $200, FANF will not apply. Businesses with a monthly volume of $200 – $1,250 will pay a FANF of 0.15% of the total volume instead of a fixed dollar amount. Mastercard Location Fee ($1.25 Monthly) : Mastercard charges $1.25 every month or $15 yearly to accept credit or debit cards at your location. This fee is absorbed by Vagaro for Small Merchants. ACH Return ($2.50) : Customers are required to keep banking information up to date. If the account information on file is wrong, through no fault of Vagaro Merchant Services (VMS), and cannot verify your bank account information based on your provided information, a $2.50 fee will be charged for any Rent Collection returned due to inaccurate bank account information. ACH Reject ($2.50) : If your account is frozen, your financial institution may reject deposits. Each rejected deposit ACH rent collection payment will automatically incur a $2.50 fee. This fee applies only to bank transfers with Rent Collection. 1st Chargeback ($7.50) : Upon a consumer initiating a chargeback, the consumer's financial institution immediately and without notice impounds the exact amount of funds at dispute from your account. We will impose a $7.50 fee, as well as provide details of the chargeback transaction and instructions/process on how to dispute it. 2nd Chargeback ($15.00) : In some cases, businesses may receive reversal credits for impounded funds. If your defense fails, MasterCard charges a $15 scheme fee for withdrawing the provisional credit from your account. This fee applies only to chargebacks from MasterCard cardholders, not other reversals.
ground_truth: For small merchants processing in-person payments via swipe, dip, or tap, the rate is 2.6% plus $0.10 per transaction, with no monthly fee required.

question: How can I filter the Payroll History report by payroll period on a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Payroll History Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003990314-Payroll-History-Report]
The Payroll History report is used to show all the payroll cycles that have been marked as "Paid" (or just "Submitted" if Vagaro Payroll has been activated). You can filter the report by year, by quarter (every three months), or for only a selected payroll period. You can also run this report if you need to undo, correct, or rerun a payroll by deleting it and rerunning it in the Payroll report .

Note Reporting of Payroll Frequencies : When running this report for a specific year, any payroll frequencies that ended in that year will be included. For example, a payroll period from Dec. 27, 2022, to Jan. 3, 2023, will be included in the 2023 report, not 2022. If you previously used Gusto for payroll processing and migrated to Vagaro Payroll , historical payroll information from before the migration will be visible only on www.gusto.com .

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. In the US, Vagaro Payroll is recommended if you want to pay your employees by direct deposit and withhold their income taxes.

Running this report generates a list of employees who were paid during the selected payroll frequency.

On a Phone To run the Payroll History report: Select More → Reports → Payroll History . Optionally, to filter your report, in the top-right corner, select Filters , then select Run Report : Employee Type ( Vagaro Payroll only): Select whether the employee is an Employee or Contractor . Payroll Type ( Vagaro Payroll only): Select whether the payroll was a Regular Payroll or Off-Cycle Payroll . Payroll Frequency : Select a frequency to review your payroll for, and then the pay period to review. By Year : Choose the year in the Select Year list. By Quarter : Select a year in the Select Year and which quarter in the Payroll Duration list. By Payroll Period : Select the Payroll Period date range that employees were paid for. Business Location : The business that the employee was paid for working at. Sort By : Select the criteria to sort the report by. Optionally, to export your report, select Action , then choose an option: Star or Remove Star : Add or remove a bookmark to this report in the Starred list at the top of the More → Reports menu. Print : Print your report if your device is connected to a printer. Export PDF or Export Excel : Save the report to a PDF or Excel file.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To run the Payroll History report: Select Reports → Payroll History . Optionally, at the top of the screen, set the filters for your report, then select Run Report . Employee Type ( Vagaro Payroll only): Select whether the employee is an Employee or Contractor . Payroll Type ( Vagaro Payroll only): Select whether the payroll was a Regular Payroll or Off-Cycle Payroll . Payroll Frequency : Select a frequency to review your payroll for, and then the pay period to review. By Year : Choose the year in the Select Year list. By Quarter : Select a year in the Select Year and which quarter in the Payroll Duration list. By Payroll Period : Select the Payroll Period date range that employees were paid for. Advanced Filters : Optionally, select any additional filters to further refine the report. Business Location : The business that the employee was paid for working at. Optionally, sort your employees' payroll information by selecting the column headers at the top of the report. At the bottom of the screen, you can select: Print : Get a hard copy of the report. Export : Save the report to an Excel or PDF file (web version only). To delete and rerun a payroll: Select Delete beside Get Report . Select Delete in the confirmation popup. Filter and run the Payroll report again.
ground_truth: On a phone, to filter the Payroll History report by payroll period, select Filters in the top-right corner, then choose Run Report, and select the desired Payroll Period date range.

question: What types of sales are included in the Service Sales total in the report?
context:
[Article: Run the Sales Summary Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000348493-Run-the-Sales-Summary-Report]
Each Type represents the following:

Service Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart service sales, based on checkout date. The total excludes points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, refunds, and membership, package, and gift card redemptions. Service Add-On Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart service add-on sales. The total excludes points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, and refunds. Class Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart class sales, based on checkout date. The total excludes points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, refunds, and membership, package, and gift card redemptions. Class Add-On Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart class add-on sales. The total excludes points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, and refunds. In-House Product Sales: The total revenue from product sales made at your business location. The total excludes points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, refunds, and membership, package, and gift card redemptions. Gift Card Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart gift card sales. The total does not include refunds. Membership Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart membership sales. The total does not include refunds. Package Sales: The total revenue from in-house or online cart package sales. The total does not include refunds. Rent Collection: The total rent collected from renters, not including commission deductions. Commission (Deducted from Rent Collection): The total commissions earned by employees from service, class, or product sales. Service Discounts: The total discounts applied to service sales, shown as negative values. Service Add-On Discounts: The total discounts applied to service add-on sales, shown as negative values. Class Discounts: The total discounts applied to class sales, shown as negative values. Class Add-On Discounts: The total discounts applied to class add-on sales, shown as negative values. Product Discounts: The total discounts applied to in-house or online cart product sales, shown as negative values. Gift Card Discounts: The total discounts applied to gift card sales, shown as negative values. Membership Discounts: The total discounts applied to membership sales, shown as negative values. Package Discounts: The total discounts applied to package sales, shown as negative values. Gift Card Redeemed: The total value of purchases paid with a monetary, service-, or class-based gift card, shown as a negative value. Package Redeemed: The total value of purchases paid with a package visit, shown as a negative value. Tax: The total taxes collected from transactions where the applicable tax rate was applied.

On a Phone You can drill into the report to see each transaction. Select a card to view its customer transactions. Optionally, work with the transactions: Select Search to find transactions by date, customer name, item sold, or transaction type. In the top-right corner, select Action , then select Print to get a hard copy, or Export PDF or Export Excel . Optionally, view a customer's receipt: Select a transaction to open its receipt. In the top-right corner, select Action , then select Print to resend or print the customer's receipt.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro You can drill into the report to see each transaction. If a Business Cost or Total Sale value is blue, select it to view the transactions for that row. Optionally, work with the transactions: Select a column header to sort by that column. In the Search by date, customer, employee, item sold, or transaction ID field, enter your search terms. Select Export or Print . Optionally, at the end of the transaction, select Receipt to view, reprint, or send the receipt to the customer via email.
ground_truth: The Service Sales total includes revenue from in-house or online cart service sales, based on checkout date, excluding points redemptions, business cost, tips, discounts, taxes, refunds, and membership, package, and gift card redemptions.

question: What does the 'Payroll Due' amount represent in the Employee Payroll Tab?
context:
[Article: Payroll History Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003990314-Payroll-History-Report]
from the gross pay total. Employee Benefits : The total amount deducted for employee benefits from the gross pay total. Payroll Due : The total amount paid to the employee for the pay period. Pay Method : If this line is listed, the employee was paid by direct deposit; select the link to view the employee's pay stub. If no pay method is listed, the employee was paid another way, and a pay stub will be available.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro The following information is shown for each employee: Employee : The first and last name of the employee. If you are using Vagaro Payroll , additional tags may also be shown: : This employee was Not Onboarded to Vagaro Payroll yet and cannot be automatically paid. : This employee had $0 Pay during this payroll period. Pay Rate / Hours Worked : The rate that the employee is paid per hour worked, and the total number of hours worked during the payroll period. OT Hourly Rate / OT Hours Worked : The rate that the employee was paid for overtime work , and the total overtime hours worked during the pay period. Paid Time Off : PTO Hourly Rate and number of PTO Hours Worked. Services : The total amount of revenue the employee earned by selling services. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Classes : The total amount of revenue the employee earned by selling classes. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Products : The total amount of revenue the employee earned by selling products. Service Tiered Revenue : The tiered commission amount earned for selling services. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Class Tiered Revenue : The tiered commission amount earned for selling classes. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Product Tiered Revenue : The tiered commission amount earned for selling products. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Commission by Service : The total amount of commissions earned for selling services. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Commission by Class : The total amount of commissions earned for selling classes. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Commission by Attendee : The total amount of commissions earned for the number of customers attending classes. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Commission by Product : The total amount of commissions earned for selling products. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when commissions were earned. Rent Collection : The amount collected from the employee to pay for rent. Commission Total : The total, combined amount of commissions earned for services, classes, attendees, and products. Wages : The total amount of money earned during regular hours worked. Calculated as Pay Rate x Hours Worked . OT Wages : The total amount of money earned during overtime hours worked. Calculated as OT Hourly Rate x OT Hours Worked . PTO Wages ( Vagaro Payroll only): The amount of wages earned during the employee's paid time off . Calculated as PTO Hourly Rate x PTO Hours Worked . Tips ( Vagaro Payroll only): The total amount of tips collected from customers. When viewing this report by Payroll Period, if the amount is more than $0.00, select the link to view when tips were earned. Reimbursements ( Vagaro Payroll only): The total amount reimbursed to the employee. Additional Earnings : The extra amount earned in addition to their hourly wages or salary. Gross Pay Total : The total amount earned by the employee Deductions : The total amount deducted from the gross pay total for business costs . Employee Taxes : The total amount deducted for employee taxes from the gross pay total. Employee Benefits : The total amount deducted for employee benefits from the gross pay total. Payroll Due : The total amount paid to the employee for the pay period. Pay Method : If this line is listed, the employee was paid by direct deposit; select the link to view the employee's pay stub. If no pay method is listed, the employee was paid another way, and a
ground_truth: The 'Payroll Due' amount represents the total amount paid to the employee for the pay period.

question: If I want to update the notes on a pending debt record, how would I do that?
context:
[Article: Manage IOUs from the IOU Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008144194-Manage-IOUs-from-the-IOU-Report]
If there are any Outstanding IOUs issued at your business, you can manage them from the report. Only Outstanding IOUs can be modified. Anything that's been paid off or voided can't be modified.

On a Phone To manage IOUs: Select More → Reports → IOU . Filter and run the report. Select the IOU you want to modify and choose an option: Edit : Change the Comment added to the IOU. Void : Void the IOU and remove the owed balance from their account. When voiding an IOU, you'll need to enter a Void Reason . Pay : Bring the customer's balance to the Checkout screen. This IOU will be marked as Paid in the customer's profile and the IOU report. View Customer Profile : Go to the customer's profile.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To manage IOUs: Select Reports → IOU . Filter and run the report. For the IOU to be modified, open the Action menu, then choose an option: Edit : Change the Comment added to the IOU. Void : Void the IOU and remove the owed balance from their account. When voiding an IOU, you'll need to enter a Void Reason . Pay : Bring the customer's balance to the Checkout screen. This IOU will be marked as Paid in the customer's profile and the IOU report.
ground_truth: You can select the IOU and choose the Edit option to change the comment added to the IOU.

question: If I want a unique symbol to represent my business, how does Vagaro help me get one made?
context:
[Article: Custom Logo Design Examples | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/221857707-Custom-Logo-Design-Examples]
Let our professional, in-house designers create a custom logo that sets you apart from your competitors and elevates your brand! Our logo design includes two rounds of edits/proofs. Additional edits/proofs are available for a small fee. If you are interested in purchasing a custom logo for your business, you can do so through the Vagaro Online Store . question: How long does it typically take to import my business information from Booksy to Vagaro after scheduling an import appointment?
context:
[Article: Import Your Business From Booksy to Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19007441402395-Import-Your-Business-From-Booksy-to-Vagaro]
Transferring your business to Vagaro is a simple process for any type of business, from large multi-location businesses to solopreneurs. Here's what you can do to move your business over to Vagaro: Self-Service Import : Vagaro provides a user-friendly app and software to easily transfer your customers yourself. All you need to do is upload an Excel file of your customers, and we'll do the rest for you. Free Import Appointment : If you would prefer the professionals to help you bring over your business, you can schedule a free appointment with our Imports Team, and they'll guide you through the entire process. Multi-Location Syncing : For businesses with multiple locations, Vagaro offers a free syncing feature that allows you to easily synchronize all your businesses to a flagship location . Prerequisites : You will need to sign up for a Vagaro business account before scheduling an import. What Can I Transfer Over From Booksy? Important Before scheduling your import appointment, you will need to request your business's files from Booksy. Data Can I Import? Employees X Contracts Contact Booksy Notes Contact Booksy Inventory X Services Contact Booksy Appointments Contact Booksy Packages X Memberships X Gift Cards Contact Booksy Credit Cards on File X Reviews Contact Booksy Request Your Free Import and Vagaro Walkthrough To start your import from Booksy, you'll need to schedule an import appointment with Vagaro and provide some information about your previous software. To book your import appointment: Go to Import Management : Phone : Select More → Settings → Import Management . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Import Management . Under Import from Other Software , select Import to schedule your appointment. In Where are you importing from , select your previous software, and then select Find a Time . Choose a time that works best for you, submit the necessary details, and then schedule your appointment. After scheduling your appointment, it typically takes one to three business days to import your information from Booksy to Vagaro. Our Imports Team will contact you when your account is ready. During your import appointment, you'll learn more about Vagaro and get set up. Getting Started with Vagaro Moving from one software to another can be daunting. We're here to make it easier for you. Here are some articles and resources to help you get started with Vagaro: Vagaro Resources & Training Vagaro Support & Services What's Included in Your Vagaro Free Trial Download the Vagaro Pro App Log In to Your Vagaro Account Customer Profiles Article What is the Benefit? Create Customer Profiles Develop your customer list and keep track of all your clients. Save Customer Credit Cards on File Save a customer's card on file to charge recurring memberships and packages, or just to make checking customers out that much easier. Create Email Marketing Campaigns to Your Customers Send your customers marketing emails to promote your business. Create Text Marketing Campaigns to Your Customers Send your customers text marketing messages to promote your business. Employee Profiles Article What is the Benefit? Set Up Your Employee Profiles Create a profile for each of your employees to manage their appointments. What Are Employee Types? Learn about the two employee types in Vagaro: Admin and Service Provider. Set Up Access Levels to Restrict Employee Access Limit what pages and settings your employees have access to. Assign Employees to Access Levels Assign your employees to different Access Levels. Service/Class Menu Article What is the Benefit? Create a New Service Show off all the different services you offer. Create a New Class Show off all the different classes you offer. Schedule a Service Appointment Schedule your appointments in-house or over the phone. Schedule a Class Session Schedule your class sessions in-house or over the phone. Credit Card Processing Article What is the Benefit? Accept Credit Card Payments With Vagaro's Credit Card Processing Easily accept credit and debit cards. Comes with a free credit card reader. Our Competitive Credit Card Fees and Rates Great rates, so you can focus on growing your business. Activate Buy Now, Pay Later Buy now, pay later option for your customers. Online Shopping Cart Article What is the Benefit? Create Your Online Store Sell products and take payments online with the Online Shopping Cart. Require a Deposit When Booking Online Charge an upfront deposit to discourage no-shows and last-minute cancellations. Require a Credit Card When Booking Online Require a credit card on file to book an appointment to help speed up the checkout process and protect against cancellations. Automatically Charge Cancellation Fees Charge cancellation fees whenever a customer cancels. Vagaro Marketplace Article What is the Benefit? Your Vagaro Listing Page Get appointments without having to lift a finger. Get Featured With Vagaro Make your business more visible on the Vagaro Marketplace. Set Up Daily Deals Advertise your discounts and deals on the Vagaro marketplace.
ground_truth: It typically takes one to three business days to import your information from Booksy to Vagaro after scheduling your appointment.

question: How can I view the history of changes made to a personal task on the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Schedule Personal Tasks and Closures on the Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010603213-Schedule-Personal-Tasks-and-Closures-on-the-Calendar]
Make changes as needed to your personal task.

On the Vagaro Pro App To edit a personal task: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Go to the task's date and time . Select the personal task on the calendar to see actions, then select Edit . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, a popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Edit This Occurrence or Edit Series . From the personal task, make changes as needed. To see all previous changes made to this personal task, select Action → History in the top-right corner. See View Personal Task History to learn more. Select Save .

On a Computer To edit a personal task: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Go to the task's date and time . Select the personal task on the calendar to see actions, then select Edit . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, an Edit Recurring Personal Task popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Edit only this occurrence or Edit the series . From the personal task, make changes as needed. To see all previous changes made to this task, select the Timeline tab. See View Personal Task History to learn more. Select Save .

Cancel a personal task at any time if your plans change.

On the Vagaro Pro App To delete a personal task: Select a task on the calendar to see actions, then select Delete . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, a popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Delete This Occurrence or Delete Series . Select Delete to confirm.

On a Computer To delete a personal task: Select a task on the calendar to see actions, then select Delete . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, a Delete Recurring Personal Task popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Delete only this occurrence or Delete the series . Select Yes or Delete Task(s) to confirm.

Editing a task allows you to access its history, even if it repeats. Task history can help you review an employee's reasons for scheduling time off or how often they block time off their calendar.

On a Phone To view changes to a personal task: Select the personal task on the calendar to see actions, then select Edit . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, a popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Edit This Occurrence or Edit Series . Select Action → History in the top-right corner. Your task timeline/history shows the following information: Appointment Date : Date and time of the task. Duration : The duration of the task. Online Booking : If online booking is blocked. Comment : Any comments added to the task. Service Provider : Who scheduled the task. Modified By : Who last edited the task. Modified Date : The date and time it was modified Action : Any actions made to the task, like creating or editing it.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view changes to a personal task: Select the personal task on the calendar to see actions, then select Edit . If the personal task is part of a repeating series, an Edit Recurring Personal Task popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Edit only this occurrence or Edit the series . Select the Timeline tab. Your task timeline/history shows the following information: Appointment Date : Date and time of the task. Duration : The duration of the task. Online Booking : If online booking is blocked. Comment : Any comments added to the task. Service Provider : Who scheduled the task. Modified By : Who last edited the task. Modified Date : The date and time it was modified Action : Any actions made to the task, like creating or editing it.
ground_truth: Select the personal task on the calendar, then tap Action → History in the top-right corner to see all previous changes made to the task.

question: How do I rebook an appointment from the Vagaro Pro App calendar?
context:
[Article: Rebook an Appointment | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/221194188-Rebook-an-Appointment]
From the Calendar, you can go back to previously checked-out appointments from the past and rebook them for your customers.

On the Vagaro Pro App To rebook from the Calendar Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Rebook . If the customer has multiple appointments on the same day, a pop-up will appear.
