From there, you can rebook multiple appointments at once. From the Rebook screen, confirm the appointment details, then select Next . Select a timeslot from an employee's availability. If there's no availability that day, you can go to another date. Review the details in the New Appointment popup. Add-Ons : Any add-ons applied to this service. Add-ons are extra items used to customize the service. Date : Day and time of the appointment. Price : The price the customer will need to pay. This is set to the default price set in your service menu . Duration : The amount of time needed to perform this service appointment, including gap processing time , cleanup time , and any add-ons. This is set to the default duration in your service menu . Resource : If you require a resource for a service, you'll need to select one. A resource on Vagaro is a bookable item, such as a room, chair, or piece of equipment, that can be assigned to appointments to manage availability and prevent double booking. Appointment Type: If you track customer retention , choose whether the customer is a new request, new non-request, return request, or return non-request. Each option allows you to track whether the customer is returning and whether they requested a specific employee. Deposit Due : The amount the customer must pay ahead of time to reserve this appointment. Appointment Files : Attach any files, forms, or images relevant to the appointment. Vagaro Drive is required to save files to your Vagaro business account. Appointment Notes : Any notes about this appointment. Appointment notes help keep track of special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for an upcoming appointment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. Customer Notes : View and add any notes you've added in the customer's profile . A customer note is a comment that an employee or service provider can add to a customer's profile for business purposes, such as reminders, allergy information, or an explanation of a customer's assessment and treatment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. Card on File : The customer's credit card information. You can enter or update the information if needed. Select Book . The appointment is added to the calendar.

On a Computer To rebook from the Calendar Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the appointment. Select the appointment on the calendar to open its details, then select Rebook . If the customer has multiple appointments on the same day, a pop-up will appear. From there, you can rebook multiple appointments at once. In Rebook Service , confirm the appointment details, then select Search . Select a timeslot from an employee's availability. If there's no availability that day, you can go to another date. Review the details in the Book Appointment with... popup. Add-On : Any add-ons added to this service. An add-on on Vagaro is an extra item that can be added to an appointment to customize the service. Date : The day of the appointment. Time : The time the appointment starts. Price : The price the customer will need to pay. This is set to the default price set in your service menu . Duration : The amount of time needed to perform this service appointment, including gap processing time , cleanup time , and any add-ons. This is set to the default duration in your service menu . Deposit Due : The amount the customer must pay ahead of time to reserve this appointment. Resource : If you require a resource for a service, you'll need to select one. A resource on Vagaro is a bookable item, such as a room, chair, or piece of equipment, that can be assigned to appointments to manage availability and prevent double booking. Appointment Type: If you track customer retention , choose whether the customer is a new request, new non-request, return request, or return non-request. Each option allows you to track whether the customer is returning and whether they requested a specific employee. Appointment Notes : Any notes that should be known about this appointment. Appointment notes help keep track of special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for an upcoming appointment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. You can also attach any files, forms, or images relevant to the appointment. Vagaro Drive is required to save files to your Vagaro business account. Notes : View and add any notes you've added in the customer's profile . A customer note is a comment that
ground_truth: To rebook from the Vagaro Pro App calendar, go to the Calendar screen, select the appointment to open its details, then tap Rebook. Confirm the appointment details, select a timeslot from an employee's availability, review the details, and then tap Book to add the appointment to the calendar.

question: How can I update a customer's package to change the number of visits in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Manage Customer Packages from the Packages Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4406235637275-Manage-Customer-Packages-from-the-Packages-Report]
All packages can be edited to make their settings specific to a customer. Not only can you update the package, but none of these changes will affect the original package created in the Packages screen.

On a Phone To update a customer's package in the Packages report: Select More → Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. Select the package to update, then select Edit Package . Update the customer's package information as required. Change Will Affect : Make changes to the Current package (immediately), or at the Next Renewal (if Auto Renew is enabled). Initial Visits : Select Edit Balance to change the visits balance. Amount : Change the value of the package. If you increase the amount, the customer must pay the difference. If you decrease the amount, the customer is owed a refund. Service/Class/Product : View the items available for the customer to redeem. Tip If only one item is added to the package, you'll have the option to change the service or class. Auto Renew : Turn this setting on or off to automatically renew this package based on Charge Frequency . Also, specify the Auto Renew Limit . Discount Initial Payments : Give your customer a discounted price for a specific number of billing cycles. Friends & Family Sharing : If the customer has family or friends added to their profile, select the customer's friends and family package that the package can be shared with. Select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To update a customer's package in the Packages report: Select Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. For the package to edit, select Action , then select Edit Package . Update the customer's package information as required. Change Will Affect : Make changes to the Current package (immediately), or at the Next Renewal (if Auto Renew is enabled). Initial Visits : Select Edit Balance to change the visits balance. Amount : Change the value of the package. If you increase the amount, the customer must pay the difference. If you decrease the amount, the customer is owed a refund. Service/Class/Product : View the items available for the customer to redeem. Tip If only one item is added to the package, you'll have the option to change the service or class. Auto Renew : Turn this setting on or off to automatically renew this package based on Charge Frequency . Also, specify the Auto Renew Limit . Discount Initial Payments : Give your customer a discounted price for a specific number of billing cycles. Friends & Family Sharing : If the customer has family or friends added to their profile, select the customer's friends and family package that the package can be shared with. Select Save .

Viewing a customer's package usage shows detailed information about the package, such as when it was purchased, its offerings, status, who has used it, and the activity that has occurred on it.

On a Phone To view a customer's package usage in the Packages report: Select More → Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. Select the package to view, then select History .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view a customer's package usage in the Packages report: Select Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. For the package, select Action , then select History .

You can tell whether a customer's package will auto-renew by looking at the Renewal Status column. Packages that have an Active renewal status will automatically renew based on the charge frequency in the Auto Renewal column.

On a Phone To pause or resume an auto-renewing package in the Packages report: Select More → Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. Select the package, then select Pause Auto Renew or Resume Auto Renew . Confirm the change. The package's Renewal Status will change to Paused or Active .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To pause or resume an auto-renewing package in the Packages report: Select Reports → Packages . All your customers' active packages will appear in the report. For the package, select Action , then select Pause Auto Renew or Resume Auto Renew . Confirm the change. The package's Renewal Status will change to Paused or Active .
ground_truth: To change the visits balance, select the package in the Packages report, then choose 'Edit Balance' to update the number of visits.

question: How do I group multiple same-day appointments for checkout on a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Group Customers at Checkout | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000311733-Group-Customers-at-Checkout]
If multiple customers book an appointment for the same day and one person wants to pay for the entire transaction, you can group the appointments in one checkout. Note Only customers with scheduled appointments on the same day can be grouped together. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To group multiple appointments: Select Checkout . Select Select Customer . Select In Today , select the customers to be grouped, and then select Group Customers . Select the customer who's paying for everyone. The selected customers' appointments are combined together and can be checked out in one transaction. Enter payment information, then select Checkout . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To group multiple appointments: Select Checkout . Select Select Customer . Select the In Today tab, choose the customers you want to group, and then Group Customers . Choose Who will pay . Select Save . The selected customers' appointments are combined together and can be checked out in one transaction. Enter payment information, then select Checkout .
ground_truth: On a phone, select Checkout, then Select Customer, choose In Today, select the customers to be grouped, and then select Group Customers. Choose the customer who will pay for everyone, enter payment information, and then select Checkout.

question: How can I reset my Vagaro password if I forgot it?
context:
[Article: Change or Reset Your Password | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204346860-Change-or-Reset-Your-Password]
You can update your Vagaro password at any time from your account settings or reset it if you’ve forgotten it. Prerequisites : You can't reset or change your password if you don't have a Vagaro account. Change Vagaro Account Password If you're logged in, you can change your password from your account settings. On the Vagaro App To change your password: Open the Vagaro app, log in, and then go to Profile → My Profile . Scroll down to the Account Management section, then select Change Password . Enter your Current Password . Enter your New Password , then enter it again to Confirm New Password . Note Your new password must be at least nine characters, including numbers, uppercase, and lowercase letters. Select Save Changes . On the Vagaro Pro App To change your password: Open the Vagaro Pro app, then log in to your account, Select More , select your profile picture (or initials), and then select Password & Security . Enter your Current Password . Enter your New Password , then enter it again to Confirm New Password . Note Your new password must be at least nine characters, including numbers, uppercase, and lowercase letters. Select Save . On the Web To change your password: Go to www.vagaro.com . Log in to your Vagaro Account. Go to your account settings: On a Phone or Tablet Browser : In the top-left corner, select the Vagaro icon, then select My Profile in the menu. On a Computer Browser : In the top-right corner, select your profile picture (or initials), then select My Profile . Scroll down to the Account Management section, then select Change Password . Enter your Current Password . Enter your New Password , then enter it again to Confirm New Password . Note Your new password must be at least nine characters, including numbers, uppercase, and lowercase letters. Select Save Changes . Reset Your Password If you can't remember your Vagaro password, you can reset your password and enter a new one. Note If you still can't log in after resetting your password or you're unsure what email you used for your account, contact Vagaro support . On the Vagaro App To reset your password: Open the Vagaro app , then select the Profile button at the bottom of the screen. The login screen will appear. Below the Password box, select Forgot Password . Enter your email address, then select Send . Check your email for a message from Vagaro. Open the email, then select Reset Password . Enter a new Password , re-enter it again in the Confirm Password box, then select Submit . You are returned to the Vagaro login screen. Log in to Vagaro with your new password. On the Vagaro Pro App To reset your password: Open the Vagaro Pro app. The login screen will appear. Below the Password box, select Forgot Password . Enter your email address, then select Send . Check your email for a message from Vagaro. Open the email, then select Reset Password . Enter a new Password , re-enter it again in the Confirm Password box, then select Submit . You are returned to the Vagaro login screen. Log in to Vagaro with your new password. On the Web To reset your password: Go to www.vagaro.com . Go to the log in screen: On a Phone or Tablet Browser : In the top-left corner, select the Vagaro icon, then select Login . On a Computer Browser : In the top-right corner, select Login . Below the Password box, select Forgot Password . Enter your email address, then select Send . Check your email for a message from Vagaro. Open the email, then select Reset Password . Enter a new Password , re-enter it again in the Confirm Password box, then select Submit . You are returned to the Vagaro login screen. Log in to Vagaro with your new password. Help Customers and Employees Log In to Vagaro If your employees or customers need help logging in, see the following articles: Help Change a Customer's Password Change an Employee's Security Question and Password Fix Problems With Your Password If you're having trouble resetting your password or can't sign in to your account, please contact our support team .
ground_truth: To reset your Vagaro password, open the Vagaro app or Vagaro Pro app, tap 'Forgot Password,' enter your email address, and then check your email for a message from Vagaro. Follow the link in the email to set a new password and then log in with it.

question: What is the primary purpose of a SOAP Note in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Using Forms vs. SOAP Notes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/39886751944987-Using-Forms-vs-SOAP-Notes]
Whether you're collecting client intake details or documenting patient information, Vagaro offers two tools to help you keep accurate, organized records: Forms and SOAP Notes. While they share some similarities, each is designed for a different purpose. Note One similarity between SOAP notes and forms is that customers can’t view their own responses unless you export and print them for the customer. What is a Form? Forms are customizable documents you can send to customers to collect information before, during, or after a visit. They’re often used for: Collecting contact details Collecting credit card information Client waivers Intake questionnaires Membership agreements Consent forms You can build forms from scratch or start with a template using the drag-and-drop editor. Forms can be sent via email or text, made mandatory for specific services or purchases, and stored in the customer’s profile for future reference. To create and fill out forms, the Forms feature must be activated. What is a SOAP Note? SOAP Notes are professional documentation tools commonly used in health, wellness, and personal care services. They follow the SOAP format: S - Subjective O - Objective A - Assessment P - Plan In Vagaro, SOAP Notes are stored directly in a client’s profile and are designed for internal use. They are not typically shared with the client unless you choose to print them. Creating and filling out SOAP Notes in a customer’s profile is free. However, creating reusable SOAP Note templates is a paid feature available through the Forms subscription. When to Use Each Use a Form when you need the client to provide information, agree to terms, or sign a document. Examples: health history intake, consent for treatment, or a membership contract. Use a SOAP Note when you want to keep professional records of a client session. Examples: massage therapy progress notes, hair treatment details, or personal training session logs.
ground_truth: The primary purpose of a SOAP Note in Vagaro is to serve as a professional documentation tool for internal use, recording details of a client session such as progress, treatment details, or session logs.

question: How can I make more employee schedules appear on my screen without losing clarity?
context:
[Article: Switch Between the Compact, Expanded, or Fit All Calendar View | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/47288222288923-Switch-Between-the-Compact-Expanded-or-Fit-All-Calendar-View]
On Vagaro, you can adjust how multiple employee calendars appear on-screen by changing your calendar view. You can choose Compact to display more calendars at once, Expanded to increase calendar width for clearer appointment details, or Fit All to resize calendars to fit the screen in the Day view . Prerequisites : This feature is only available on the computer version of Vagaro. To switch between the different views: Go to the Calendar screen. In the top-right corner, select your current view ( Day , Week , Month , or Agenda ) to open the menu. Choose a calendar view: To show additional calendars at once, select Compact view. If you select a large number of calendars, you may need to scroll horizontally to view all employees. To increase the width of each calendar for better visibility, select Expanded view. To adjust the layout to fit more calendars on the screen, select Fit All . This option is only available on the Day view . With this setting, you can select the Right-Arrow > to cycle through 25 calendars at a time.
ground_truth: You can switch to the Compact view to display more calendars simultaneously, or choose the Fit All option in the Day view to resize calendars to fit the screen.

question: How can I check if my Vagaro EMV reader is successfully connected on a Windows computer?
context:
[Article: Pair the Credit Card (EMV) Reader with a Computer | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360018745934-Pair-the-Credit-Card-EMV-Reader-with-a-Computer]
Known Issue with Chrome Browsers We’re aware of an issue in the latest Chrome and Edge updates that can prevent your device from pairing. Our team is working on a fix. In the meantime, follow the troubleshooting steps below. Open Vagaro in Chrome or Edge. Go to permissions: Chrome : Select View Site Information → Site settings . Edge : Select View Site Information → Permissions for this site . Change your permissions: Chrome : From the Apps on device list, select Allow . Edge : From the Sites can ask to connect to any device on your local network list, select Allow . Refresh your browser

With Vagaro's Credit Card Reader (EMV reader), you can easily accept Google Pay, Apple Pay, contactless, chip, and swipe payments. To start accepting payments, you must pair the reader with your phone, tablet, Pay Desk , or computer.

Vagaro does not integrate with other payment processors. Vagaro's EMV reader is available for customers in the United States. For international customers, please see Using the WisePad 3 Mobile with Vagaro Pro or Using the WisePOS E with Vagaro .

The credit card reader is not supported on Chromebook.

Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing .

Before pairing your credit card reader to your computer, you need to download the Vagaro Pro Utility.

Note You may need to use Administrator privileges in Windows to download and install this utility.

On a Windows Computer To download the utility: Go to the Checkout screen. In the bottom-right corner, select Pay by Card → EMV Reader . Open the file to begin installing. Agree to the license terms, then select Install . Select Finish when the installation is complete. You will see the Vagaro Pro Utility Application icon on the Taskbar or in the System Tray .

On a Mac Computer To download the utility: Go to the Checkout screen. In the bottom-right corner, select Pay by Card → EMV Reader . Locate the file in the Downloads folder and run it. On the Introduction screen, select Continue . On the License screen, select Continue . The only language choice available is English . Select Agree to accept the terms of the Software License Agreement . If there is more than one possible installation destination (hard drive), you will see a Destination Select screen; otherwise, the installer moves to the Installation type selection. Select Install . (There is no need to customize the installation.) Enter your system password, then select Install Software . A progress screen appears while the software is installed. If a popup requests access to your Downloads folder to delete the installer file, select OK .

To pair your reader:

Go to the Checkout screen. Open the Vagaro Pro utility. Turn on your credit card reader, plug the provided cable into the reader, and plug the other end into your computer. The Pay By Card button should change to blue. If it does not, select Pay By Card → EMV Reader to try pairing the device again.

On a Windows Computer Select the Vagaro icon in the system tray (in the bottom-right corner of the screen) to see the connection status between the Vagaro Checkout screen and the credit card reader. A Blue Dot means that the reader is connected to the Checkout screen of the Vagaro account and is ready to process customer transactions. Red Dot means that the reader is not connected to the Checkout screen of the Vagaro account and cannot process customer transactions. If you are having trouble pairing the reader, try troubleshooting the issue or contact Vagaro support for assistance. When successfully connected, Pay By Card will change to blue, and you can proceed with checkout.

On a Mac Computer Select the Vagaro icon on the right side of the menu bar (in the top-right corner of the screen) to see the connection status between the Vagaro Checkout screen and the credit card reader. A Blue Dot means that the reader is connected to the Checkout screen of the Vagaro account and is ready to process customer transactions. Red Dot means that the reader is not connected to the Checkout screen of the Vagaro account and cannot process customer transactions. If you are having trouble pairing the reader, try troubleshooting the issue or contact Vagaro support for assistance. When successfully connected, Pay By Card will change to blue, and you can proceed with checkout.
ground_truth: Click the Vagaro icon in the system tray; a Blue Dot indicates the reader is connected and ready to process transactions, while a Red Dot means it is not connected.

question: How do I add a custom field to a form or SOAP note in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add a Custom Field to a Form or SOAP Note | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/48620720637083-Add-a-Custom-Field-to-a-Form-or-SOAP-Note]
Add custom fields to forms and SOAP notes to gather data from form responses. To ensure the data is collected and added to the correct profile, turn on Require Vagaro Login in your form's settings. For example, to collect a customer’s emergency contact information, add an emergency custom field to a form or SOAP note. When the customer submits the form, their response is saved to their profile. If they complete the form for a family member, friend, or pet , the information is saved to that profile instead. Prerequisites : This feature is only available on the computer, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro versions of Vagaro. See Manage Customer Custom Fields to learn how to create custom fields. To add a custom field to a form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . Go to your form or SOAP note: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To create a SOAP note template , select Create New → SOAP Note . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . In the form designer, select the Custom Field element and drop it into the form builder. The element settings will appear on the right side. From the Select Field list, choose the custom field you want to add. Note If you decide to add a new custom field from this menu, any changes you made to this form will be lost. To display a different title specifically for this template, enter an Alternate Title . To make this field required, turn on Required Field . Publish your form: To save this form as a draft and publish it later, in the top-right corner, select Save as Draft . This form is saved in form management and can be published at a later time. To see a preview of your form and how it'll look for your customers, in the top-right corner, select Preview . You can select the Computer , Tablet , or Phone icon to see how your form will look on each device. To publish your form, go to Settings , then in the top-right corner, select Publish . Your form is saved in form management.
ground_truth: In the form designer, select the Custom Field element, drop it into the form builder, and then choose the custom field from the Select Field list.

question: When can the cash drawer balance be viewed in Vagaro, and does it include past dates?
context:
[Article: Enter the Daily Cash Drawer Opening Balance | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347780-Enter-the-Daily-Cash-Drawer-Opening-Balance]
The cash drawer balance tracks how much cash is currently in your till. Enter the daily opening balance so Vagaro can calculate how much cash you've made so far today. The first employee to access the Checkout screen each day must enter the opening balance before processing transactions, and you can view the balance throughout the day in the Transaction List summary view at the top of the Transaction List report . Once the opening balance is entered, it can't be changed. The balance resets at midnight each day, so a new amount must be entered the next day. You can enter a balance only for the current day, not for a past or future date.` Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Require a Starting Cash Drawer Balance Each Day The setting to require the starting cash drawer balance each day must be enabled for the first user to be prompted to enter the day's cash amount. If no transactions have yet been processed for the day, the first employee to access the Checkout screen will be prompted to enter the balance after the switch has been turned on. On a Phone To require the starting balance of your cash drawer to be entered each day: Select More → Settings → Taxes & Checkout . Scroll down to Require starting balance for cash drawer and toggle on the switch. Select Save . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To require the starting balance of your cash drawer to be entered each day: Select Settings → Taxes & Checkout . Scroll down and toggle Require starting balance for cash drawer . Select Save . Enter the Daily Cash Drawer Opening Balance When the Checkout screen is first accessed for the day by any employee, they will be prompted to enter how much money is currently in the cash drawer. On a Phone To enter the opening balance of the cash drawer: Go to the Checkout screen. In the prompt, enter the amount in the cash drawer, then select Save . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To enter the opening balance of the cash drawer: Go to the Checkout screen. In the prompt, enter the amount in the cash drawer, then select Save . View Your Cash Drawer Balance The cash drawer balance can be viewed at any time of the day in the Transaction List summary view at the top of the Transaction List report screen. The amounts are automatically updated when transactions are processed. Note The Transaction List report shows the cash drawer balance for the current day only. There isn't currently a way to view the opening cash drawer balance for a past date or enter a closing cash drawer amount. On a Phone To view your cash drawer balance for today: Select More → Reports → Transaction List . The system automatically runs the report for today's transactions. At the top of the screen, select the Money Earned / Drawer Balance heading. Today's Cash Drawer Balance will appear. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view your cash drawer balance for today: Select Reports → Transaction List . The system automatically runs the report for today's transactions. At the top of the screen, select the Down-arrow in the Money Earned / Drawer Balance heading.
ground_truth: The cash drawer balance can be viewed at any time of the day in the Transaction List summary view, but it only shows the current day's balance and does not include past dates.

question: How do I add envelopes or sleeves to my Vagaro gift card order?
context:
[Article: Order Gift Cards from Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/30847952225179-Order-Gift-Cards-from-Vagaro]
to the Vagaro Store : Phone : Select More , go to the Purchase Vagaro Products banner, and then select Buy Now . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select More → Purchase Vagaro Products . Computer : Select More → Vagaro Store . At the top of the screen in the Your Custom Cards banner, select Order Now . The order form will open in an Internet browser window. Select the Envelopes & Accessories tile. At the top of the screen, select View Stock Envelope Options . Under each sleeve option, Select quantity for each type to order. At the bottom of the screen, select Continue . Select Save and Continue to Checkout . In the Cart screen, review and submit your order. If you have a Coupon Code , enter it, then select Apply Coupon . Verify the price, quantity, and order total, then select Process to Checkout . Enter your Billing Details , Shipping Details , and Credit Card information. Optionally, enter any Order notes that you'd like to bring to our attention. Select Place Order .
ground_truth: Click on the Envelopes & Accessories tile, then select View Stock Envelope Options, choose the quantity for each sleeve option, and click Continue to proceed with your order.

question: How can I make sure customers are notified when their class starts after they've checked in?
context:
[Article: Activate the Check-In App | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360038981033-Activate-the-Check-In-App]
Although the Check-In app can be downloaded only on a tablet, it can be activated on any device in the Vagaro Pro app, on the web, or in the Check-In app itself.

To activate the Vagaro Check-In app:

Go to Check-In App . On a Phone : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . On the Web, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . Note If you're activating the Check-In app on a tablet in the app, you will need to log in first. Select Get Started to activate the feature. Configure the Check-In app's settings. Color Theme : Select a color for the buttons to display in the app. Logo : Select your business logo to show on the Check-In app's home screen. If you already selected an image, the app will automatically select it; otherwise, you can add in the app or change it by selecting the Logo image. Automatically Return to Home : Select how long the kiosk can be idle before showing the home screen again. You can select 15, 30, 60, or 90 seconds. Allow Customers to Check-In After Class : Whether customers can check in even after a class has ended, and how long afterward they can still do so. Play Sound : Whether to play a sound if a customer's action was Success performed, such as checking in or purchasing a membership, or a Failure , such as not being able to check in or payment for a membership was not approved. You can also select None to not play any sound for either event. Membership Purchase : Whether you require customers to have a membership when they book an appointment or register for a class at a kiosk by selecting one of the following options: None : Your customers can book appointments or register for classes at a kiosk without needing a membership. Required : Customers who try to book an appointment or sign up for a class but don't have a membership will be prompted to purchase one at the kiosk. If you select this option, also choose whether to Prompt to scan membership card after purchase , which requires you to provide plastic membership cards for your customers to scan. Optional : Customers don't require a membership when checking in, but they will be presented with a list of your memberships they can purchase right away at the kiosk, or Skip this screen and complete their check-in. Membership Required to Check-In : Whether you require your customers to have an active membership to check in at a kiosk and to prompt customers who don't have a membership to purchase one in the Check-In app. If this switch is toggled on, you can also enter a Message to Customers with Inactive Memberships (optional) and whether to Allow Customers to Purchase or Renew Memberships . Display a Custom Message After Customer Check-In : Whether to show a custom Message After Check-In , which can contain any notes or announcements you want to tell customers before the class starts. Confirm Customer Mobile Number During Check-In : Whether to prompt the customer to enter their phone number after they've checked in so they can be notified when the class begins. Hide Customer List Upon Check-In : Whether to allow customers to check in by selecting their name in a list of attendees. If this toggle is switched on, customers will be prompted to enter their email address and password to check in. If this toggle is switched off, customers can search for and select their name in the list to check in, but they still must also enter their password. Kiosk Management : Rename or delete a kiosk that has already been set up. Select Save .

To download the Check-In app on your tablet:

Go to your device's app store to get the Vagaro Check-In app. On an Apple device (iPhone or iPad) : Open the Apple App Store . On an Android device : Open the Google Play Store . When the app has been installed, select Open . If prompted, allow Vagaro to access your device's camera. Log in to your Vagaro account .

To update the Check-In app's settings:

Go to the Check-In app's settings screen. On a Phone : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . On the Web, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . In the Check-In App : Select the Settings icon, log in, then select Settings . Update the app's settings as needed. Select Save .
ground_truth: You can enable the Confirm Customer Mobile Number During Check-In option to prompt customers to enter their phone number, so they can receive notifications when their class begins.

question: What are the prerequisites for a business to be approved to Get Featured on Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Get Featured on the Vagaro Marketplace | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977275777947-Get-Featured-on-the-Vagaro-Marketplace]
After you've finished setting up your business on Vagaro, it's time to show off what you offer. Activating the Get Featured premium feature is one of the best ways to highlight your business in the Vagaro Marketplace to make you more visible to customers at the top of the screen and in your customers' search results. Once you're approved to Get Featured, List Your Business on Vagaro will be enabled, and your business will be shown on the Vagaro Marketplace. The cost to Get Featured varies by country: $10 USD/month $15 CAD/month £0 GBP/month (free) $15 AUD/month Prerequisites: This feature is for businesses in the Barber, Beauty, Fitness, Wellness, Self-care, Pet Grooming, and similar industries. Other businesses, such as car washes, carpentry and construction, retail, mechanics, food services, and cleaning services, may be denied. Business Account Requirements Our Design Team reviews all requests to Get Featured in one business day. During the review process, we may need to add or change your logo image to meet our image standards , and complete or update key information missing from your business account. After you've been approved, your business will be listed on the Vagaro Marketplace and any changes you make to your business account information will be resubmitted for our review to ensure it still meets our minimum requirements. To be approved to Get Featured, you need to have the following information in your business account: Your business type must be set up to offer services and classes in the barber, beauty, fitness, wellness, self-care, pet grooming, and similar industries. A business description in your Business Profile At least one spoken language selected in your Facility Information At least one accepted payment method selected in your Facility Information A service cancellation policy and/or class cancellation policy A logo or high-quality photo of your space, your work, or your team set as your business logo For example, the following logo images would be approved for a business to be featured: The following logo images would be replaced by our Design Team because they do not meet our logo image requirements : Activate Get Featured To activate Get Featured on Vagaro: Go to Get Featured : Phone : Select More → Settings → Get Featured . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Get Featured . Select Get Started , or if you're in the free trial, select Try it Free . Your request to get featured is currently pending approval. This review can take up to one business day. Once it's approved, the List Your Business on Vagaro setting will automatically turn on if it wasn’t already.
ground_truth: The prerequisites include having a business type that offers services in industries like barber, beauty, fitness, wellness, self-care, or pet grooming; a business description in your profile; at least one spoken language and accepted payment method selected; a cancellation policy; and a logo or high-quality photo that meets image standards.

question: What is the cost of activating the Xero integration for a Vagaro business in USD?
context:
[Article: Activate the Xero Integration | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005756128-Activate-the-Xero-Integration]
The Xero integration is a premium feature that saves you time by exporting your sales transactions, taxes, tips, and refunds to your Xero account , as well as automatically reconciling your books by matching payments and fees against your bank statement. Vagaro syncs all transactions at 2:00 AM so they're ready for your review in the morning. Note Xero only pulls the previous two months from Vagaro when you start the integration. It may take up to 48 hours for your business information to be synced the first time you activate the Xero integration. All businesses can activate the Xero integration for an additional cost per month: $10 USD/month $40 CAD/month $0 GBP/month (free) $40 AUD/month Note If you turn off this feature before your billing date, you will still be charged at the end of your billing cycle, regardless of when you deactivated it. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. Your Xero account must already be set up and ready to integrate with Vagaro. To activate the Xero integration: Go to Integrations : Phone : Select More → Settings → Integrations . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Integrations . In the Xero tile, select Learn more . Select Connect to Xero . Enter your Xero login credentials, then select Login . Select Allow access . Select the Sync Start Date to select when to start syncing the reports. You can also optionally add a Nickname that your Vagaro transaction data will be listed under after information has been synced. Under Match your Vagaro transactions to your Xero Accounts , match each Vagaro sales type with your "REVENUE" class account in Xero. Directions on the right side describe how to add new accounts, if the ones offered don't work for you. Under Match your Vagaro Payments to your Xero Accounts , match each Vagaro payment type with your "Current Asset" type account in Xero. Directions on the right side describe how to add new accounts, if the ones offered don't work for you. In the Tax Rate list, select Auto Look Up for Vagaro to use the tax rate you defined in your Xero account. Toggle on the Sync transactions after fees are deducted switch to synchronize your transactions to show deposit to your bank account with fees already deducted. If this toggle is switched off, your transactions will be shown without any deductions. Select Save to start syncing between Vagaro and Xero.
ground_truth: The cost is $10 USD per month.

question: How can I get my cash drawer to open automatically when I print a customer's bill?
context:
[Article: Set Up Cash Drawer with the Vagaro Receipt Printer | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/34789265649179-Set-Up-Cash-Drawer-with-the-Vagaro-Receipt-Printer]
Connecting your cash drawer to your receipt printer is simple. Use the provided RJ12 cable (or the built-in cable on newer devices) to connect them. Once set up, the cash drawer will open automatically each time a receipt is printed. Prerequisites : This feature requires a Vagaro receipt printer and cash drawer . To set up the cash drawer with the receipt printer: Turn off your receipt printer. Connect your cash drawer to your printer with the provided (RJ12) cable. Carefully flip the drawer upside down and plug the cable into the port located at the bottom of the cash drawer. Note Some newer devices already have this plug built in. Skip this step if so. Plug the other end of the cable into the back of the printer. Turn on your receipt printer. Pair your printer to your computer or phone , if you haven't already. Print a receipt .
ground_truth: Connecting the cash drawer to the receipt printer with the RJ12 cable will cause it to open automatically each time a receipt is printed.

question: How can I write a review for a business I visited using the Vagaro app?
context:
[Article: Write a Review - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003312174-Write-a-Review-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
A review allows you to provide feedback to a Vagaro business about its services, employees, and customer service. Prerequisites : You cannot write a review for a business you've never visited. Write a Review on The Business Listing Page Go to a business's Vagaro Listing Page to write a review. On the Vagaro App To write a review: Log into your Vagaro account in the Vagaro app . Go to the business's Vagaro Listing Page . Scroll down to Reviews , then select Write a Review . Select a rating for each category. Enter some optional information for your review: Select your service provider : Choose to leave a review for a specific service provider and will auto-populate if you wrote a review from a previous appointment. Leave a review for (employee name) : Enter a review for that employee. Leave a review for the business : Leave a review for the business. Share Your Look or Result : Add up to three photos of the service you received or the business. Select Post Review . On a Computer To write a review: Log in to your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . Go to the business's Vagaro Listing Page . Select Write a review . Select a rating for each category. Enter some optional information for your review: Select your service provider : Choose to leave a review for a specific service provider and will auto-populate if you wrote a review from a previous appointment. Leave a review for (employee name) : Enter a review for that employee. Leave a review for the business : Leave a review for the business. Add up to 3 photos : Add photos of the service you received or business. Share on Facebook : Share this review on Facebook. You'll be prompted to sign in to your Facebook account after posting your review. Select Post Review . Write a Review from a Completed Appointment If you really liked the service you received, you can write a review about that appointment. On the Vagaro App To write a review: Log into your Vagaro account in the Vagaro app . At the bottom of the screen, select Appointments . On the Past tab, find the business you want to review, then select Write a Review . Select a rating for each category. Enter some optional information for your review: Select your service provider : Choose to leave a review for a specific service provider and will auto-populate if you wrote a review from a previous appointment. Leave a review for (employee name) : Enter a review for that employee. Leave a review for the business : Leave a review for the business. Share Your Look or Result : Add up to three photos of the service you received or the business. Select Post Review . On a Computer To write a review: Log in to your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . In the top-right corner, hover over or select your profile icon, then select Appointments . On the Past tab, find the business you visited, and then select Write a Review . Select a rating for each category. Enter some optional information for your review: Select your service provider : Choose to leave a review for a specific service provider and will auto-populate if you wrote a review from a previous appointment. Leave a review for (employee name) : Enter a review for that employee. Leave a review for the business : Leave a review for the business. Add up to 3 photos : Add photos of the service you received or business. Share on Facebook : Share this review on Facebook. You'll be prompted to sign in to your Facebook account after posting your review. Select Post Review .
ground_truth: Log into your Vagaro account in the Vagaro app, go to the business's Vagaro Listing Page, scroll down to Reviews, select Write a Review, choose a rating, optionally select a service provider or employee, add photos if desired, and then tap Post Review.

question: What role should I assign to Vagaro when inviting them to my Apple Developer account?
context:
[Article: Set up an Apple Developer Account | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360033330154-Set-up-an-Apple-Developer-Account]
As the last step in setting up your Apple Developer Account, you’ll need to invite Vagaro to join your account and publish your branded app on your behalf.

Go to https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/users and log in. Select the blue + button. Complete the New User form with this information: First Name : Vagaro Last Name : Design Email : The email you need to send the invite to will be sent to you by the design team via email. Please refer to your email to find which one to add. In the Roles section, select the Admin checkbox. In the Additional Resources section, verify that Access to Certificates , Identifiers , and Profiles are checked. Note This access is required, as Vagaro cannot upload or manage your app without it. If Access to Certificates, Identifiers, and Profiles is not listed under Additional Resources , contact Apple Developer Support to edit your account type: https://developer.apple.com/contact/submit Select Invite .

Apple will send an email invitation to the Vagaro Design Team, who will accept the invite within 1 business day. The Vagaro Design Team will verify the account permissions and notify you if there are any remaining action items regarding your Apple Developer Account.

What is a D-U-N-S number?

The D-U-N-S Number is a unique nine-digit number that identifies each physical location of your business. It is widely used as a standard business identifier and is provided by Dun & Bradstreet (D&B). For more information, select here .

Can I sign up as an individual instead of an organization?

Apple does not currently allow individual accounts to add additional users and grant them access to Certificates, Identifiers, and Profiles. This access is required for the Vagaro team to upload your Branded App to your account, so for a Vagaro Branded App, you must register as an Organization.

How much does it cost to enroll in the Apple Developer Program?

To sign up for the Apple Developer Program, you must pay a $99 (USD) annual publisher fee, which Apple charges. This fee is the same for individual and organizational accounts and is paid yearly.
ground_truth: You should select the Admin checkbox in the Roles section when completing the New User form.

question: How do I choose where I want the appointment to happen when I book a mobile service?
context:
[Article: Book a Mobile Service (House Call) - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360055718674-Book-a-Mobile-Service-House-Call-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
After finding the business you want to book with, you can begin scheduling an appointment.

On the Vagaro App To schedule a service: On the business's listing page, select Services . All the services the business offers will appear. Expand the categories to see more services. Find the Mobile Service you want to book, then depending on how the business is set up, select one of the book buttons: Book Now : Book the appointment on the business's calendar. Request : This business requires acceptance for all appointments. You'll need to request the appointment before being scheduled. Add to Cart : This business allows you to prepay for appointments before booking. Add the service to your cart and continue shopping. Note Optionally, if the business has any add-ons you'd like to add to your service, select them, then then select Continue . Choose your service location, then select Continue . At the Business's Location : Have the service performed at the business. At My Location : Have the service performed at the address in your profile . Different Location : Enter another location and have the service provider meet you there. Choose an employee and date for your appointment, then choose a timeslot that works for you: Select Change to filter the scheduler for a different service, specific employee(s), and the day you want to book. You can also select Add Service to book up to six services at once. Under the This Week heading, swipe left and right to move through the different weeks, then select a date to view more appointments. Select a timeslot to schedule the appointment. Tip Don't see an appointment time that works for you? If the business allows, you can add yourself to the waitlist . Selecting a timeslot will bring you to the booking screen. Finalize your appointment: If you're booking for someone else, select a family, friend or pet in Who Are You Booking For . Under About your appointment , enter any notes or requests to the business. Review and accept the business's cancellation policy (if applicable). Some businesses may require a deposit or card on file before scheduling an appointment. You'll need to enter or use a credit card on file or use another payment method to pay for the deposit. Select Book or Request to schedule the appointment.

On a Computer To schedule a service: Select the Services tab. Find the Mobile Service you want to book, then depending on how the business is set up, select one of the book buttons: Book Now : Book the appointment on the business's calendar. Request : This business requires acceptance for all appointments. You'll need to request the appointment before being scheduled. Add to Cart : This business allows you to prepay for appointments before booking. Add the service to your cart and continue shopping. Note Optionally, the business may have additional add-ons you can add to your service. Choose any add-ons you want to add, then select Continue . Choose your service location, then select Continue . At the Business's Location : Have the service performed at the business. At My Location : Have the service performed at the address in your profile . Different Location : Enter another location and have the service provider meet you there. Choose an employee and date for your appointment, then choose a timeslot that works for you: At the top of the screen, filter the scheduler for a different service, specific employee(s), and the day you're looking to book. Select Add Service to book up to six services at once. Select Search after making changes to the appointment you want to book. Under This Week , select the Left < and Right > buttons to move through the different weeks to view more appointments availability. Select a timeslot to book that appointment. Tip Don't see an appointment time that works for you? If the business allows, you can add yourself to the waitlist . Selecting a timeslot will bring you to the booking screen. Finalize your appointment: If you're booking for someone else, select a family, friend, or pet in Who Are You Booking For . Under About your appointment , enter any notes or requests to the business. Under Service Location , you can change where the service is being performed. Review and accept the business's cancellation policy (if applicable). Some businesses may require a deposit or card on file before scheduling an appointment. You'll need to enter or use a credit card on file or use another payment method to pay for the deposit. Select Book or Request to schedule the appointment.
ground_truth: You can select whether the service takes place at the business, at your profile's address, or another location you specify.

question: How can I manually split a tip among multiple employees during checkout in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Split a Tip at Checkout | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347750-Split-a-Tip-at-Checkout]
Tipping is a simple way for customers to show appreciation, and it can be a boost to your income. In Vagaro, tips paid by cash , check , or gift card need to be entered manually during checkout. Credit card payments, on the other hand, automatically prompt the customer to select a tip amount or print a tip receipt, making the process quick. If the checkout includes services from multiple employees, Vagaro will automatically split the tip based on the price of the service each person performed. You can also manually split tips at checkout to reward a specific provider.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

Vagaro allows businesses to accept tips through various devices, including phones, tablets, Pay Desk, PayPro , and computers. On touchscreen devices, customers can choose from four preset tip options during checkout or enter a custom amount, including $0.00 if they choose not to tip. These tip options can be customized in your Checkout Settings . When using a computer with a receipt printer , you can print a tip receipt for the customer to manually write in a tip. If you're using a PayPro Mini with a computer, the tip screen appears directly on the PayPro Mini. For setups with only a computer, you’ll need to ask the customer for their desired tip amount, then enter it manually during checkout.

Note Credit Card Processing is required to accept credit cards with Vagaro.

On the Vagaro Pro App If you have a touchscreen device, you can hand the device to your customer so they can select a tip. When you charge their credit card at checkout, four tip options will appear for them to choose from. You can customize these tip options in your Checkout Settings . To accept a tip: Select Checkout , or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Charge the customer's card. An Add a Tip screen will appear with the preset tip options you set in your Checkout Settings . You can charge a customer's credit card by using a card on file , through Tap to Phone , or by using Vagaro hardware, like a credit card reader or PayPro Mini . In the top-left corner, select Split , have the customer enter the amount to tip each provider, and then select Apply . Note The option to split a tip will only display if multiple employees are selected at checkout . From the Add a Tip screen, have the customer select Charge Card . If a signature is required, they'll need to select Next , sign for the transaction, and then select Charge Card . Depending on your Checkout Settings , the option to print or send a receipt will appear. Have the customer choose an option. After charging the customer and printing a receipt, the What would you like to do next? popup will appear. In the popup, you can rebook the customer for another appointment or go back to the calendar.

On a Computer To accept a tip: Select Checkout , or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Charge the customer's card. You can charge a customer's credit card by using a card on file , or by using Vagaro hardware, like a credit card reader or PayPro Mini paired with your computer. In the Customer Checkout popup, select Split Tip , enter the amount to tip each provider, and then select Apply . If you have tip receipts enabled, you'll need to print the receipt first before entering the tip. Note The option to split a tip will only display if multiple employees are selected at checkout . Select Charge Card . If a digital signature is required, they'll need to sign first. Depending on your Checkout Settings , the option to print or send a receipt will appear. Have the customer choose an option. After charging the customer and printing a receipt, the What would you like to do next? popup will appear. In the popup, you can rebook the customer for another appointment or go back to the calendar.
ground_truth: During checkout, select 'Split' and have the customer enter the amount to tip each provider, then click 'Apply'. This option only appears if multiple employees are selected at checkout.

question: How can I encourage clients to buy something online before or after their appointment using my messages?
context:
[Article: Add a Promo Code to Email Notifications | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360037452234-Add-a-Promo-Code-to-Email-Notifications]
Adding a promo code to your automated notifications and reminders can incentivize your customers to make a purchase on your Vagaro Listing Page even before or after their scheduled appointment. When you create a discount , you can also add a corresponding promo code of your choice that can be applied in-house , or it can be used exclusively online. When you're ready to announce this promotion, you can include the promo code into the automatic appointment notifications that your customers receive before and after their visits as well as in your marketing text messages and emails . Prerequisites: This feature requires the Online Shopping Cart to allow customers to make purchases on your Vagaro Listing Page , and you must have already created the promo code for the service or class . To add a promo code to your email notifications: Go to Email & Text Notifications . On a Phone : Go to More → Settings → Booking → Email & Text Notifications . On the Web, Tablet, or Pay Desk : Go to Settings → Booking → Email & Text Notifications . Update your message with your promo code in the Include this message in appointment emails field. Select Save .
ground_truth: You can include a special discount code in your automatic email and message alerts, which may motivate clients to make a purchase on your Vagaro Listing Page either before or after their scheduled visit.

question: What hardware components are included in the box when I purchase PayPro?
context:
[Article: Set Up PayPro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/27745463426203-Set-Up-PayPro]
PayPro is Vagaro's point-of-sale (POS) hardware system, in addition to or replacing Vagaro on your phone, tablet, or computer. The tilting screen allows you to check out customers and add items to the cart. You can then flip it over to face your customers for payment. A provided PayPro hub allows you to plug in four USB devices, an Ethernet cable, and a cash drawer and/or receipt printer. For the best experience, we recommend using PayPro with the Vagaro credit card reader and wireless receipt printer, both of which are available in the Vagaro Online Store .

Optionally, for US businesses, PayPro can be connected with PayPro Mini to provide a secondary screen for customers and function as a portable POS device around your facility.

Note For customers in Canada, the UK, and Australia, PayPro is incompatible with the WisePad 3 credit card reader. You will need the WisePOS E instead, which is available from the Vagaro Online Store .

Prerequisites: This feature requires PayPro and an active Internet connection.

The PayPro hub is included only with the main PayPro unit and is not sold separately. It supports up to four USB peripherals and provides an ethernet port for a hardwired Internet connection and an RJ12 data port.

You can plug in the following Vagaro hardware into the PayPro hub, all of which can be purchased from the Vagaro Online Store :

USB Hardware Barcode scanner Credit card (EMV) reader (US only) WisePad 3 or WisePOS (Canada, UK, and Australia only) USB/Bluetooth/wireless mouse USB/Bluetooth/wireless keyboard RJ12 Hardware Cash drawer Vagaro receipt printer or Star printer

PayPro includes the following hardware in the box :

The main PayPro unit with a tilting screen PayPro hub AC adapter AC adapter cable

To set up PayPro:

Lay PayPro on its side, then plug the USB-C end of the PayPro hub into one of the ports under the base of the PayPro unit. Press the cable firmly into the grooves and through one of the notches on the side of the base, depending on which side you plan to plug into the PayPro unit. This will keep your PayPro level and prevent the device from resting on the cable. Turn the PayPro unit back upright, then plug the end of the AC adapter into the PayPro hub. Plug the power cable into the AC adapter. Plug the power cable into a power outlet.

When you power on your PayPro device for the first time, the Vagaro Setup Wizard will guide you through the initial setup to ensure Vagaro Pro functions correctly and can connect to the Internet.

To run the Vagaro Setup Wizard:

Press and hold the power button on the device until it powers on. On the Welcome screen, select Start . Select a language, then Next . Select a timezone, then select Next . Note If PayPro is connected to the business's router with an ethernet cable plugged in, Vagaro Pro will automatically download any necessary updates and then prompt you to begin onboarding. To connect to the internet, select a network, then select Next . If required, enter the network’s Password , then select Connect . After the device has connected to the network, select Next . When PayPro has connected to your Wi-Fi network, select Next . The device will begin downloading and installing the latest version of Vagaro Pro. When the Setup Wizard is complete, the Welcome screen appears.
ground_truth: The box includes the main PayPro unit with a tilting screen, the PayPro hub, an AC adapter, and an AC adapter cable.

question: How do I set up automatic cancellation or no-show fees for services in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Automatically Charge a Cancellation or No-Show Fee | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/17733260320539-Automatically-Charge-a-Cancellation-or-No-Show-Fee]
Charging a cancellation fee can help prevent loss of income and discourage customers from cancelling appointments at the last minute or not showing up. You can manually charge these fees or automatically charge them when customers cancel their appointment in the Vagaro customer app or your Vagaro Listing Page , or if you mark them as a no-show . Make sure you add your cancellation policy to your Vagaro Listing Page to protect your business from customer chargebacks when they dispute your cancellation fee. Even so, you can also require a deposit at the time a customer books a service, which are more protected and less likely to be disputed. Note Vagaro only accepts and processes deposits and fees from Vagaro Merchant Services. Before you can charge cancellation and no-show fees, you need to enable Capture Credit Card in your Online Shopping Cart settings to require customers to provide a credit card number when they book a service or class online. Once enabled, you can run the Cancellation & No-Show report to monitor how many appointments your customers have cancelled or missed, and view the fees you've collected by filtering the Transaction List report to show only the Cancellation Fee transaction type. Important Ensure that you have enabled Customers Can Cancel Appointments in your Online Appointment Rules . If customers can't cancel their appointments by themselves, you will have to manually charge your cancellation/no-show fee. Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing and the Online Shopping Cart . Set Up Cancellation/No-Show Fees for Services To set up credit card capture to charge cancellation/no-show fees for services : Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Services and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage based on the service price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer appointments : Customers who don't have a customer profile at your business. Old customer appointments : Customers who already have a customer profile at your business. Mobile service appointments : Customers who schedule a mobile service appointment Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. Enter your Required Appointment Cancellation Fee Policy to describe your business's cancellation policy . This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save . Set Up Cancellation/No-Show Fees for Classes and Workshops To set up credit card capture and cancellation/no-show fees for classes : Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Classes and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage of the class price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer class sign ups : Customers who have never taken the class before. Old customer class sign ups : Customers who have already taken the class. Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. In the Required Class Cancellation Fee Policy box, enter a cancellation policy for your business. This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save .
ground_truth: To set up automatic cancellation or no-show fees for services, go to Online Shopping Cart > Settings > Online Shopping Cart, select 'Capture Credit Card' under 'Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Services,' and enter the minimum service price, fee amount (percentage or fixed), and cut-off time. Then, choose the customer types required to provide a credit card and save the settings.

question: How can business owners restrict employees from changing prices or applying discounts during checkout in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Limit Employees' Ability to Change Prices and Discounts | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412317816731-Limit-Employees-Ability-to-Change-Prices-and-Discounts]
Using Access Level settings , business owners can limit their employees' ability to change pricing and enter discounts for products, services, classes, memberships, packages, and gift cards during checkout. An access level setting can also control employees' access to manage discount settings (create new discounts and edit existing ones.)

There are two Access Levels you can use:

Settings > Discounts Checkout > Customer Checkout

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

The following access levels are used to limit employees' ability to change prices and apply discounts:

Discounts Access Level The Discounts access level is found under Settings . You can set it to View or Modify . Employees with Modify access can create, edit, and delete discounts, while those with View can only see the page itself and use the Edit function to view details of a particular discount. Employees with no access level set for Discounts will see a warning page when they go to Settings → Things We Sell → Discounts : Those with only View access will see a warning when they try to use the Duplicate or Delete functions from the More menu or if they try to change the Active switch. View access employees can use the Edit function to see the details of a discount but cannot make changes to any settings, and they will see the same warning if they try to save their changes in the edit screen.

Customer Checkout Access Level The Customer Checkout access level specifies what can be done with prices and discounts during checkout. With Modify set to on (green), the Modify Price and Discount settings appear for you to set. You have several checkout areas to select from: Services Classes Products Gift Cards Memberships Packages And can control what employees can do for Price Reductions and Discounts for each area. Choices for each are: Cannot Modify Can Modify Can Modify With Limit Set these individually as needed. Note that Can Modify With Limit is set only by percentage, though the system will calculate the effect of a change in a dollar amount based on the percentage entered here. When an employee with this access level set to either Cannot Modify or Can Modify With Limit tries to change the sales price or discount during checkout, they will be required to get the approval of an employee who can modify the price or discount. Here's how that works: We'll use an employee with the following settings on this access level: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Go to Checkout and select a customer. Select a service, service provider, and start time to add to the cart. Change the service price to $60.00, or add a discount of 20% (remember they can only apply a 5% discount from the access settings), and the cart now reflects the change: For this example, we'll use a cash payment, so enter $60.00 in Cash and select Checkout . The Approval Required screen pops up. Select Next . An employee with Can Modify access can scan their employee QR code or select their icon and sign in. Once they enter their password and select Approve , the checkout is completed. This workflow is the same whether the employee has a limit set (percentage) or has the Cannot Modify option set. The approving employee must have either Can Modify or Can Modify With Limit (set for a higher limit than the employee doing the checkout) to approve the transaction. Note Preset and Daily Deal discounts are not subject to these limitations. No approval is needed if an employee limited to offering a 5% discount uses a 15% Preset or Daily Deal discount. However, a Preset discount that employees can only offer with selected Access Levels will kick off the approval process. Approve Price Changes on the Calendar Employees whose Access Levels have the Cannot Modify or Can Modify with Limits set under Checkout cannot change the price of a service from the Calendar without approval. Here's the process for changing the price from the Calendar: Select an upcoming appointment on the Calendar, and then select Edit . Lower the price (or lower it more than the allowed percentage from the Can Modify with Limits setting). Select Save . Select Next in the Approval Required popup. Select an employee who can approve the price change or have them scan their employee QR code and sign in. Once they enter their password and select Approve , the price change will be saved.

Access Level Required to Offer Discounts When creating preset discounts, you can limit employees' ability to offer that discount at checkout by Access Level. Follow these steps. Select Settings → Discounts . Set your discount with a name, description, and start date/time. Set the options to allow for this discount. Set Access Level Required to Offer Discount (In-House Only) to On (green). Select which access levels will be allowed to offer the discount. Finish creating your discount, and then select Save . To apply a limited discount at checkout for employees who do not have the access level needed: After adding an item to which the discount can apply, select the Apply a Discount drop-down list and select the discount. You'll
ground_truth: Business owners can restrict employees by setting Access Levels under Settings > Discounts Checkout > Customer Checkout, choosing options like View or Modify for discounts, and setting specific permissions for Price Reductions and Discounts for each area, which may require approval for changes.

question: How can I access my business's payroll documents in Vagaro Payroll on a computer?
context:
[Article: View Your Business's Payroll Information | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24718151586203-View-Your-Business-s-Payroll-Information]
The time it takes to list all of your business's Vagaro Payroll documents depends on how long you have used Gusto and Vagaro Payroll and how many documents are available. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To view your business’s payroll documentation: Go to Vagaro Payroll : Phone : Select More → Settings → Vagaro Payroll . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Vagaro Payroll . Select the Documents tab. In the row of the document to view, select Download . Check the folder where your files are downloaded to find and open the document.
ground_truth: On a computer, go to Settings → Vagaro Payroll, then select the Documents tab, find the document you want to view, and click Download.

question: What steps should I follow to cancel my Vagaro subscription on a computer or tablet?
context:
[Article: Cancel or Pause Your Vagaro Subscription | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347110-Cancel-or-Pause-Your-Vagaro-Subscription]
Only the business account owner can cancel or pause a Vagaro subscription. If you have multiple locations, those accounts will remain active. To pause your subscription, you’ll need to cancel your account and reactivate it when you’re ready to resume. Before you cancel your account, we recommend exporting or printing your sales data and customer list from your business records. All merchant accounts associated with your business will also be closed unless they are used at another Vagaro business. Cancel Your Vagaro Subscription After your account is closed, you will be logged out and will receive an email confirming the cancellation. To cancel your Vagaro subscription: Go to Manage Subscription : Phone : Select More → Settings → Manage Subscription . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Manage Subscription . Scroll down to Cancel Subscription , then select Cancel My Vagaro Subscription . Provide the following information, then cancel: Select the reasons why you are cancelling your Vagaro subscription. Enter any feedback or suggestions you'd like to share with us. Carefully read the cancellation policy, select I agree to the cancellation policy , and then select Cancel Vagaro Account . Select Cancel Subscription to confirm. Note When reactivating your account, keep the following in mind: Accounts that have been deactivated due to non-payment will be charged for the outstanding balance as soon as you reactivate, along with a $5 US/CA/AU (£5 GBP) late fee. You'll have a chance to review the outstanding balance, verify your billing address, and select the payment method before you can restart your subscription. If your account was not closed due to non-payment and has been closed then reactivated, you will be charged for your subscription again. Export Your Reports (optional) For your records, you should export your reports before canceling your Vagaro subscription. Saving your Vagaro reports requires that you run each one individually, then export the data to a PDF or Excel file or print it out. If you forget to export your reports before closing your Vagaro account, you will have to log in to reactivate your account, retrieve them, and then deactivate it again. On a Phone To export your reports: Select More → Reports , then select a report. Optionally, select the Filter button and choose your filters to refine the report, then select Run Report . Select the Action button to save your report: Print : Print your report if your device is connected to a printer. Export PDF or Export Excel : Save the report to a PDF or Excel file.
