Adding a separator between modules on your forms and SOAP notes can make your form more visually appealing and easier to read by clearly dividing your elements into sections. For example, a separator followed by a title can identify a section that is for employee-only use, or indicate a text-only section that does not require any input from the reader. When your customers and employees view your form, they will see the separator as a horizontal line. Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To add a separator to your form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . Go to your form or SOAP note: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To create a SOAP note template , select Create New → SOAP Note . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . Under Form Elements , drag the Separator element into the form designer. The element's settings will appear on the right side of the screen. If you already have this element in the form designer, select it to make changes. Publish your form: To save this form as a draft and publish it later, in the top-right corner, select Save as Draft . This form is saved in form management and can be published at a later time. To see a preview of your form and how it'll look for your customers, in the top-right corner, select Preview . You can select the Computer , Tablet , or Phone icon to see how your form will look on each device. To publish your form, go to Settings , then in the top-right corner, select Publish . Your form is saved in form management.
ground_truth: To add a separator, go to Forms, select your form or SOAP note, then drag the Separator element into the form designer under Form Elements.

question: What permissions are needed for an employee to view and modify payroll settings and reports?
context:
[Article: Configure Access Levels and Employee Permissions | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977275541531-Configure-Access-Levels-and-Employee-Permissions]
hourly pay/commission structure.

Ability to Run Others' Payroll : Employees will also need Modify access for Ability to Run Own Payroll to run their own payroll to mark payroll as Paid or delete records in the Payroll History screen.

View : Employees can view other employees’ payroll settings and reports in the Reports → Employees → Payroll , Payroll Configuration , and Payroll History screens. Modify : Employees can change payroll configuration settings and modify other employees’ hourly pay and commission structure.

Dashboard : Employees will need View access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to Run Others' Reports to see sales information.

View : Employees can view the Dashboard and all widgets. The data shown will depend on their access for Ability to Run Own Reports and Ability to Run Others' Reports permissions. Modify : Employees can also add, rearrange, or remove Dashboard widgets.

Failed Payment Report :

View : Employees can view failed package or membership renewals in the Reports → Customers → Failed Payments screen. Modify : N/A

Review Management Report :

View : Employees can view customer reviews and responses to reviews in the Reports → Customers → Review Management screen. Modify : Employees can post public responses to customer reviews that will also be shown on the business's Vagaro Listing Page .

Deposit Report

View : Employees can view all deposit reports and merchant information. Modify : N/A

Paid Time Off :

View : Employees can view all time off requests in the Reports → Employees → Time Off screen. Modify : Employees can approve or deny time off requests .
ground_truth: Employees need View access to see payroll settings and reports, and Modify access to change payroll configuration settings, hourly pay, and commission structures.

question: Does creating or editing my Vagaro components in Vagaro automatically update my MySite?
context:
[Article: MySite: FAQ | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/35429794470555-MySite-FAQ]
This FAQ covers questions Vagaro users have asked about MySite. If a question has not been answered in this FAQ, please leave a comment below or contact our Support Team .

Some general questions about MySite, including the difference between your MySite and Vagaro Vagaro Listing Page.

How can I set up my website? You can set up your new website on your phone, tablet, computer, or Pay Desk. See here to learn more.

Does MySite sync with my Vagaro business? Yes, you can use Vagaro components to sync your products, services, and staff to your website.

How can I edit the information in a Vagaro component? Vagaro components sync directly with your Vagaro business, so you don't need to update them in MySite. To make changes to your Vagaro components, all you need to do is edit the related setting. For example, let's say you want to update the Facility Information component, you would need to go to Facility Information in your Vagaro account and make changes.

Can I create my own color palette for quick use? Yes, you can create your own color palette with up to 10 colors. See Set Global Site Stylings to learn more.

Does Vagaro MySite change my Vagaro Listing Page? No, your Vagaro MySite doesn't affect your listing in any way. MySite is separate from your listing page. Customers will not automatically navigate to your MySite when they visit your booking page from the Vagaro marketplace or Vagaro Customer app.

What's the difference between MySite and my Vagaro Listing Page? Your Vagaro Listing Page can be found on the Vagaro Marketplace and uses Vagaro's branding and color theme. Your listing page is generated for all Vagaro businesses for free; however, it can't be customized. Vagaro MySite is your own professional, custom website. It has a completely different URL from your booking page and can look completely different. You can customize your website as much as you want and tailor it to your business's brand.

What is the subscription price for MySite? Is it included in my Vagaro subscription? For your own custom business website, Vagaro MySite is only an additional $20 (US), £15 (UK), or $30 (CA/AU) per month.

How many fonts are available on MySite? Can I upload my own fonts? Yes, you can upload your own fonts to MySite. You can upload your fonts with the .woff2, .2off, .ttf, or .otf supported file types. Vagaro MySite also has over 100 pre-loaded fonts available.

How does MySite sync with my Vagaro business? Within the MySite editor, you can place Vagaro components on your website. Components are the building blocks that make up the visual elements of your site, like images, business hours, and your service menu. Vagaro components sync from your Vagaro business and require no maintenance once added. For instance, if you add the Facility Information Vagaro component, it will appear with all the information set in your Vagaro settings exactly as it appears on your Vagaro Listing Page. Any changes you make to the Facility Information within Vagaro will automatically be reflected on your website.

Can I test out MySite? Yes, we've designed the MySite builder so anyone can get started and learn how to use it before making a decision. You will only be charged for MySite when you publish.

Where can I find my website's site map? Currently, there isn't a site map generated for your MySite.

Can customers purchase products from MySite? If you've set up your Inventory in Vagaro, yes, you can sell products on your MySite. You can use the Products Vagaro component to set this up on MySite.

Are there any contracts for MySite? How can I deactivate it? No, there are no contracts with MySite. You can activate and deactivate it at any time. To deactivate MySite: Select MySite . Select the Settings tab in the left Design panel. Select General . Select Deactivate Website Builder and confirm deletion.

Do I need web development experience to use MySite? No, MySite is a simple and intuitive builder that allows you to create a fully customized website in a few steps.
ground_truth: No, Vagaro components sync directly with your Vagaro business, so you don't need to update them in MySite. To make changes, you must edit the related settings in your Vagaro account.

question: What information can I include in the Appointment Notes section for a scheduled appointment?
context:
[Article: Schedule Multiple Appointments at Once | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010525613-Schedule-Multiple-Appointments-at-Once]
first service starts. Price : The total price the customer will need to pay for all services. This is set to the default price set in your service menu . Duration : The total amount of time needed to perform all services, including gap processing time , cleanup time , and any add-ons. This is set to the default duration in your service menu . Deposit Due : The amount the customer must pay ahead of time to reserve this appointment. Appointment Type: If you track customer retention , choose whether the customer is a new request, new non-request, return request, or return non-request. Each option allows you to track whether the customer is returning and whether they requested a specific employee. Appointment Notes : Any notes that should be known about this appointment. Appointment notes help keep track of special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for an upcoming appointment. Customers will not be able to see the information you enter. You can also attach any files, forms, or images relevant to the appointment. Vagaro Drive is required to save files to your Vagaro business account. Tip To make changes to the services you've added, select the box under Service with... . Select Book . The appointments are added to the calendar.
ground_truth: You can include any notes about special requirements, reminders, requests, or comments for the appointment, and attach relevant files, forms, or images.

question: How can a business owner collect a failed rent payment from an employee's profile on a computer or tablet?
context:
[Article: Collect Failed Rent Payments | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19361065995675-Collect-Failed-Rent-Payments]
If a rent payment fails, the system notifies both the business owner and renter. The business owner will receive a notification in their email and in their Notification Panel, and the renter will receive a notification via email, text, and in their Notification Panel. The business owner can collect payment in the Rent Collection screen, in the Employee Profile screen, or by running the Rent Collection report . Prerequisites : This feature requires credit card processing to collect rent payments. Collect Failed Rent Payments in the Rent Collection Screen To collect a failed rent payment in the Rent Collection screen: Go to Rent Collection : Phone : Select More → Settings → Rent Collection . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Rent Collection . Scroll down to the Renters List . On the Renter List , in the row of the employee who has a Failed status in the Status column, select the Action menu, then select Collect Payment . In the Collect Payment popup, verify the Amount Due , then select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, or new credit card). You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee. Verify the Amount Due . You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee for a late payment. Select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, credit card, or new add a new credit card). Select Collect . Refresh the screen to see the payment's Status marked as Paid . Collect Failed Rent Payments from an Employee's Profile Failed rent payments can also be collected by viewing the Rent Collection screen in an employee's profile. On a Phone Failed rent payments are shown with a red side border, while collected rent payments have a green side border. To collect an employee's failed rent payment: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Select the employee's profile, then select Rent Collection in the popup. Note If the employee works for multiple multiple locations , select the Action menu for the location to collect rent from. In the row of the payment that shows Failed in the Status column, select the Action menu, then select Collect Payment . In the Collect Payment popup, verify the Amount Due , then select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, or new credit card). You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee. Verify the Amount Due . You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee for a late payment. Select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, credit card, or new add a new credit card). Select Collect . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To collect an employee's failed rent payment: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . Select the employee's profile. Note If the employee works for multiple multiple locations , select the employee's blue Locations link, then select the Action menu for the location to collect rent from. Select the Rent Collection tab. At the end of the payment's row with Failed in the Status column, select the Action menu, then select Collect Payment . In the Collect Payment popup, verify the Amount Due , then select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, or new credit card). You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee. Verify the Amount Due . You can also increase the payment amount to include a fee for a late payment. Select how to Collect Payment By (cash, check, credit card, or new add a new credit card). Select Collect . Refresh the screen to see the payment's Status marked as Paid .
ground_truth: To collect a failed rent payment from an employee's profile on a computer or tablet, go to Settings → Employee Profiles, select the employee's profile, then click on Rent Collection. Find the payment with a Failed status, select the Action menu, then choose Collect Payment. Verify the Amount Due, select the payment method (cash, check, or credit card), and click Collect. Refresh the screen to see the payment marked as Paid.

question: What is required to enable the Book button on my Instagram business profile using Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Instagram Integration | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360005432413-Instagram-Integration]
Get customers to book directly from your Instagram business account with our free integration! This integration adds a Book button to your business profile that customers can select to schedule an appointment. You can also track the number of customers who've booked with you via Instagram in the Source report. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses and you must have an Instagram business account . To integrate Instagram with your Vagaro account: Open the Instagram app and select your profile in the bottom-right corner, then select Edit Profile . Under Public business information , select Contact options → Add an action button → Vagaro . Login to Vagaro with your credentials and enter your security code, if prompted. If you have a multi-location business, you'll be asked to select which one to use for the authorization process. Select one business, and then select Continue . On the Facebook access screen, select Authorize → Continue as [your name] . Select Continue as [your account name] . If your account is successfully connected, the Connect Website screen will appear. Your Instagram profile page now shows the Book Now button.
ground_truth: You must have an Instagram business account and connect it to your Vagaro account by following the integration steps, including logging into Vagaro and authorizing the connection.

question: What happens to a membership if I cancel it after refunding it in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Refund a Membership and Membership Visits | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18791547967515-Refund-a-Membership-and-Membership-Visits]
Refunding a membership will return the full sale amount to the customer, regardless of any visits or discounts already used. You'll also have the option to cancel the membership so that the customer can no longer use it. Cancelled memberships cannot be reactivated and must be repurchased if the customer wants to be a member again.

On a Phone To refund or cancel a membership: Go to Customers and select a customer. Scroll to the bottom of the screen, then select Refund to open the customer's transaction history. Select the membership to be refunded, then select Refund . Select Refund under the item. Enter the full or partial Refund Amount and the Tip amount to refund, if applicable, then select Next . Optionally, select Cancel Membership . If you do not cancel the membership, customers can still redeem or receive a discount on their leftover visits. Select Next . Optionally, split a refund between two payment methods, such as cash and a gift card, and then select Submit . Review the Refund Summary , then select Refund . Enter a reason for the refund, select whether to Print Receipt or Email Receipt , then select Continue .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To refund or cancel a membership: Go to Customers and select a customer. Select the Memberships tab. Select the Action menu for the membership to be refunded, then Refund Membership . Select the customer's preferred refund method. Unavailable methods are grayed out. Enter the full or partial Refund Amount . Optionally, if a tip was included, you can enter the Tip refund. Optionally, select Cancel Membership . If you do not cancel the membership, customers can still redeem or receive a discount on their leftover visits. Optionally, split a refund between two payment methods, such as cash and a gift card, then select Confirm . Enter the Reason for Refund , select whether to Email Receipt or Print Receipt , then select Refund .
ground_truth: If you cancel a membership after refunding it, the membership cannot be reactivated and must be repurchased if the customer wants to be a member again.

question: How do new staff members get started with the system after they're invited?
context:
[Article: Invite and Onboard Doctors and Medical Assistants to E-Prescribe | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/41118765054107-Invite-and-Onboard-Doctors-and-Medical-Assistants-to-E-Prescribe]
After an account is created, users can be added to and invited to use E-Prescribe.  The users who are invited will receive an email link to complete their setup.  For Medical Assistants like nurses, secretaries, IT staff, and other supporting users, the setup process is very simple.  The users essentially confirm their basic information and set a password.

For providers and doctors, the setup process has many more requirements. Any provider (prescriber with a DEA and/or NPI number added to ScriptSure) must undergo Identity Proofing (IDP) and be issued a token for prescribing controlled substances. A token is simply an app downloaded to the provider's cell phone OR a physical device that can be purchased.

Adding a new doctor or medical assistant will increase your subscription price, with the pricing being $40 per month per doctor and an additional $5 per month per medical assistant.

Note Be sure to use Google Chrome or Safari web browser when signing up. You may encounter issues with advancing through screens if you do not use one of these browsers.

Prerequisites : This feature requires that you've already set up your E-Prescribe account and that your doctor and/or medical assistant has an employee profile at your business .

To invite employees to E-Prescribe:

Go to Integrations : Phone : Select More → Settings → Integrations . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Integrations . Select Learn More under E-Prescribe . In the Employees list, select the employees you want to add as a Doctor and Medical Assistant . The monthly subscription cost will update based on the type of role you add. Select Invite Employees .

Your employees will receive a text message, push notification, and email prompting them to set up their E-Prescribe account.
ground_truth: They receive notifications via email, text, and push alert, guiding them to complete their account setup by confirming their details and creating a password.

question: What is the recommended process for creating a discount for cash payments in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: How to Offer a Discount for Cash | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/39069593249691-How-to-Offer-a-Discount-for-Cash]
A discount for cash is a reduction in price offered to customers who pay using cash instead of a credit or debit card. It allows businesses to avoid credit card processing fees and pass those savings on to the customer. The advertised price includes the cost of card fees, and the discount is applied only when the customer pays with cash. Instead of risking fines and card network violations associated with surcharges , businesses can legally and safely offer a discount for cash. This method is: Keeps pricing transparent by offering a discount for cash rather than adding a fee. Avoids negative customer perception of being 'penalized' for using cards. Legal and compliant in all 50 states. Makes marketing easier: 'Save with cash!' sounds better than 'Pay extra with a card.' Allowed on both credit and debit card transactions Free from registration with Visa and MasterCard No in-store signage required. To do this, businesses should first increase the prices of all services, classes, memberships, packages, and inventory items. This ensures the cost of processing is factored into every sale. Once the new pricing is set, businesses can then offer a discount, typically the same percentage as the price increase, to customers who choose to pay with cash. This setup enables businesses to offset processing fees without penalizing customers who pay with cash. After the price increase, the next step is to create a preset discount in Vagaro, such as “Discount for Cash,” and apply it across all relevant items. Separate discounts may need to be created for products, memberships, and packages. When a customer pays with cash, staff can apply this preset discount. Note We recommend following the instructions below on a computer or tablet because creating a preset discount is not available on the phone. Step One: Increase Your Prices To cover credit card processing fees, you’ll need to increase your prices, then offer a discount to customers who pay with cash. This requires updating the prices in your inventory, service and class menu, memberships, and packages. To update your pricing, check out the following articles on how to edit each item: Edit Your Service Prices Edit Your Class Prices Edit Membership Prices Edit Package Prices Edit Products in Your Inventory Step Two: Create a Preset Discount After increasing your business's prices, you'll need to create a discount that applies to all items at your business. A discount can only be created on the computer, tablet, PayPro, or Pay Desk version of Vagaro. To create a preset discount: Select Settings → Discounts . Select New Discount . From the New Discount screen, enter a Discount Name , like "Discount for Cash", then enter a Description . Scroll down to the bottom of the screen, then select Add . All your services, classes, and add-ons will appear in the popup. In the popup, select Select All → Create Group . All your services and classes will be grouped together so you can enter a single discount for all of them. In the table with all your services and classes grouped together, enter a percentage-based discount based on the amount you increased your services by. Optionally, if you sell products at your business, select the Products tab and repeat steps three to five. Save Save Note The steps above covered how to create a discount for cash for services and classes. If you sell memberships and packages, see Create a Discount for Things You Sell to learn more. Step Three: Apply the Discount at Checkout After creating the preset discount, you can then apply the discount whenever a customer comes in and pays with cash. On the Vagaro Pro App To apply a preset discount: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Next to Discount , select Add , and then select the "Discount for Cash" preset discount Select Done to return to checkout. Complete the checkout. On a Computer To apply a preset discount: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. In the Apply a Discount or Fee list, select the "Discount for Cash" discount. Complete the checkout.
ground_truth: First, increase your prices across all services, classes, memberships, and products to cover processing fees. Then, on a computer or tablet, go to Settings → Discounts, create a new discount named 'Discount for Cash,' select all relevant items to group them, and enter a percentage discount equal to your price increase. Finally, apply this preset discount at checkout when a customer pays with cash.

question: How can I update a customer's email address on their profile using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Edit a Customer's Profile | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/212228518-Edit-a-Customer-s-Profile]
You can edit a customer’s name, email address, phone number, and other details directly in their profile. These changes apply only to the information your business has on file and will not change the email address or password the customer uses to sign in. If a customer needs help accessing their account, direct them to these steps to reset their credentials and regain access .

On the Vagaro Pro App To edit a customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. In the top-right corner, select Edit . Update the customer’s information as needed. First Name and Last Name : The customer's name. Gender : The customer's gender. Choose from male, female, and non-binary. Mobile : The contact phone number used to send appointment notifications. You can also add their Day Phone and Night Phone , but only the Mobile Number will receive text notifications and marketing. Invalid contact details will show an Invalid icon. Email : The email used for appointment reminders and marketing messages. Invalid contact details will show an Invalid icon. If the email you enter is associated with another Vagaro account, you will be prompted with the option to merge the accounts . Address : The customer's address. Referred By : The person who referred this customer to your business. This information is used only in your Customer List Report to track where your customers are coming from. General Tag : The keywords or phrases used to categorize or label your customer for reporting, discounts , and marketing campaigns . Point Balance : The customer's current points balance. You can update the balance as needed. Card on File : If you have Credit Card Processing active , you can save the customer's card for future purchases. You can manage this by adding a new card or removing any existing cards on file. Customer Since : The date when this person became your customer. Send Email Copy : The recipient who receives copies of this customer’s appointment details and notifications. Optionally, select Online Booking to select the booking permission you want: Allow : The customer can book with you at any time. Require Acceptance : The customer requires approval for every booking. Their appointment remains pending until the service provider approves it . Block : The customer is blocked from booking with the business entirely. Optionally, turn on the options under Appointment Reminders to manage what types of notifications this customer gets . SMS Text : Control whether the customer receives text updates for their appointments. Email : Control whether the customer receives email updates for their appointments. Push Notification : Control whether the customer receives in-app updates for their appointments. Note Even if these settings are turned on, customers who have opted out of notifications will not receive any messages. Select Save .

On a Computer To edit a customer's profile: Go to Customers and select a customer. In the top-right corner, select Edit Profile . Update the customer’s information as needed: Email Address : The email used for appointment reminders and marketing messages. Invalid contact details show an Invalid icon. If the email you enter is associated with another Vagaro account, you will be prompted with the option to merge the accounts . First Name and Last Name : The customer's name. Mobile Phone : The contact used to send appointment notifications. You can also add a Day Phone and Night Phone , but only the Mobile Number will receive text notifications and marketing. Invalid contact details will show an Invalid icon. Birth Date and Gender : The customer's personal information. Customer Since : The date when this person became your customer. CC Appointment Email to : The recipient who receives copies of this customer's appointment details and notifications. General Tag : The keywords or phrases used to categorize or label your customer for reporting, discounts , and marketing campaigns . Referred By : The person who referred this customer to your business. This information is used only in your Customer List Report to track where your customers are coming from. Address Line 1 : The customer's address. Address Line 2 is the apartment number or suite number. Optionally, use the Online Booking list to select the booking permission you want: Allow : The customer can book with you at any time. Require Acceptance : The customer requires approval for every booking. Their appointment remains pending until the service provider approves it . Block : The customer is blocked from booking with the business entirely. Optionally, use the Appointment Reminders to select the notification types the customer can receive: Email : Control whether the customer receives email updates for their appointments. SMS/Text : Control whether the customer receives text updates for their appointments. Push Notification : Control whether the customer receives in-app updates for their appointments. Note Even with these settings enabled, customers who have opted out of notifications will not receive any messages. Select Save . You can manage your customers' card on file and points balance directly from their profile as well.
ground_truth: Go to Customers, select the customer, tap Edit in the top-right corner, and then update the Email field with the new email address.

question: How can I temporarily stop customers from using a special offer without losing all the related data?
context:
[Article: View and Manage Your Discounts and Promo Codes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4412244402331-View-and-Manage-Your-Discounts-and-Promo-Codes]
If you no longer want your customers to use one of your discounts or make it temporarily unavailable for use, we recommend you deactivate it rather than delete it. Deleting a discount removes all statistics and revenue data for this discount. If the discount has not expired, eligible customers will not be notified that the discount is no longer valid.

To deactivate or reactivate a discount:

Select Settings → Discounts . To deactivate a discount, toggle off the switch in the discount’s Active column. To reactivate a discount, toggle on the switch in the discount’s Active column.

Deleted discounts cannot be recovered, and eligible customers will not be notified that the discount is no longer valid. For your own records, we recommend deactivating unwanted discounts instead of deleting them.

To delete a discount:

Select Settings → Discounts . For the discount to be edited, select the Action menu and then select Edit . Confirm that you want to delete the discount, and then select Delete .
ground_truth: You should deactivate the discount by toggling it off in the settings, which makes it unavailable without deleting it or losing its information.

question: How can I log into my Vagaro account using the Vagaro app on my mobile device?
context:
[Article: Log In to Your Vagaro Account | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260806204090-Log-In-to-Your-Vagaro-Account]
Log in to your Vagaro account from the app or at vagaro.com . Using a phone or tablet? We recommend downloading the Vagaro or Vagaro Pro app. The Vagaro Pro app is required to access your business account on mobile devices. Customer Account : Needed to book appointments with businesses that use Vagaro. If you’ve ever visited a Vagaro business, you likely already have an account. Business Account : Used by business account owners and employees to manage a Vagaro business. This account type is created when signing up for a Vagaro subscription or when an employee is added to a business . Note Already logged in as a business owner or employee? Go directly to your Vagaro Calendar: https://vagaro.com/merchants/calendar . Prerequisites : The steps below require a Vagaro account. Log In to Your Customer Account If you're a customer of a Vagaro business, log in to your Vagaro account to view your upcoming appointments or book a new one with a business. If you haven't created an account yet but previously booked with a business on Vagaro, contact the business, and they can send you temporary login details. On the Vagaro App To log into your Vagaro account: Download the Vagaro App . Open the app, then in the bottom-right corner, select Profile . Enter your Username or Email and Password , then select Login Alternatively, you can sign in with your Facebook, Google, or Apple account. If you don't remember your password, select Forgot your password? to reset your password . If you're prompted to enter a verification code, check your phone for a text message or email for an authentication code, enter it on the screen, then select Verify . You've successfully logged in to your Vagaro account. Check out the following articles to learn more about using Vagaro: Book an Appointment with a Business Find Deals on the Marketplace View Your Upcoming Appointments View Past Appointments Cancel or Reschedule Appointments On Vagaro.com To log into Vagaro.com: Go to https://vagaro.com . Go to the log in screen: On a Phone or Tablet Browser : In the top-left corner, select the Vagaro icon, then select Login . On a Computer Browser : In the top-right corner, select Login . Enter your Username or Email and Password , then select Login Alternatively, you can sign in with your Facebook, Google, or Apple account. If you don't remember your password, select Forgot your password? to reset your password . If you're prompted to enter a verification code, check your phone for a text message or email for an authentication code, enter it on the screen, then select Verify . You've successfully logged in to your Vagaro account. Check out the following articles to learn more about using Vagaro: Book an Appointment with a Business Find Deals on the Marketplace View Your Upcoming Appointments View Past Appointments Cancel or Reschedule Appointments Log In to Your Business or Employee Account Manage your Vagaro business from the Vagaro Pro app or vagaro.com. Whether you're ready to get started with your free 30-day trial of Vagaro or keep using Vagaro, just log into your account in the Vagaro Pro App or at www.vagaro.com . To access your business or employee account on a phone or tablet, you will need to download the Vagaro Pro app . On the Vagaro Pro App To log into the Vagaro Pro app: Download the Vagaro Pro app . Open the app and select Log In . Enter your Username or Email and Password , then select Login If you don't remember your password, select Forgot your password? to reset your password . If you're prompted to enter a verification code, check your phone for a text message or email for an authentication code, enter it on the screen, then select Verify . Your Calendar will be shown. On Vagaro.com To log in to a Vagaro business or employee account: Go to https://vagaro.com . In the top-right corner, select Login . Enter your Username or Email and Password , then select Login If you don't remember your password, select Forgot your password? to reset your password . If you're prompted to enter a verification code, check your phone for a text message or email for an authentication code, enter it on the screen, then select Verify . Your Calendar will be shown. To change the screen shown at login: In the top-right corner of the screen, hover over your profile icon. Hover over Open at Login , then select Calendar , Checkout , or Dashboard .
ground_truth: Open the Vagaro app, select Profile in the bottom-right corner, enter your Username or Email and Password, then select Login. If needed, you can also sign in with Facebook, Google, or Apple, and complete verification if prompted.

question: How can I temporarily remove someone from the upcoming payment cycle without firing them?
context:
[Article: Skip or Exclude an Employee from Payroll | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24718114838683-Skip-or-Exclude-an-Employee-from-Payroll]
If you need to skip or exclude an employee from payroll for reasons such as suspensions, firings, furloughs, sick leave, or extended maternity leave, you can remove them from a pay period and run an off-cycle payroll or include their pay in the next pay period. Alternatively, you can terminate the employee and rehire them when they return . Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To skip or exclude an employee from payroll: Go to the Payroll report: Phone : Select More → Reports → Payroll History . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Reports → Payroll . At the top of the screen, select your Payroll Closing Date , and then select the Employees to pay. Select Run Report . A list of your employees’ earnings and payments is generated. In the end of the row of the employee to skip, select the Action menu, and then select Skip Employee . The row of the employee is greyed out in the report. To add the employee back to that payroll, select the Action menu, and then select Unskip Employee .
ground_truth: You can exclude them from the current pay period using the payroll report, which will grey out their row and prevent their payment from being processed this cycle.

question: How can I include a customer's name in my lost customer text campaign message?
context:
[Article: Reclaim Lost Customers with a Text Campaign | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26168603819547-Reclaim-Lost-Customers-with-a-Text-Campaign]
The Lost Customer campaign type allows you to automatically send text messages to customers who haven't visited your business in a while. The message can include anything from a call to action to book an appointment, a promo code, a feedback survey form, and so on. A lost customer campaign can be set up to send messages up to 24 weeks (five and a half months) after your customers' last appointment, but these campaigns will not retroactively look back for any customers that were lost before creating this campaign. For instance, if you create a campaign for customers who haven't visited your business in two weeks, the system will not send out campaigns to anyone who hasn't visited in three or more weeks. The system will also not send out this campaign to customers who have future appointments.

Before sending out a text campaign, keep the following in mind:

Text campaigns are limited to text only. Videos and photos are not allowed, but links can be included. Lost customer campaigns will continue sending messages for as long as they remain active, so you only have to create one lost customer campaign and let it run continuously. If you need to change the campaign, you can always edit and reannounce it later . Campaigns will only be sent to customers with mobile phone numbers in their Customer Profile . Campaigns will not be sent to customers you have blocked from online booking. Campaigns may be rejected if they break Vagaro's text guidelines or are flagged by phone carriers. Vagaro will not refund text messages used for flagged or rejected campaigns. Campaign recipient lists will not include customers who have opted out of marketing on Vagaro, including customers who have replied "STOP" to your previous campaigns. Any customers with invalid phone numbers will not receive this campaign. You can't create a text campaign while the number provided by Vagaro is still being approved.

Prerequisites : This feature requires a text marketing plan with an approved phone number.

On a Phone To send a lost customers campaign: Select More → Email & Text Marketing → Create Campaign . Under Select Delivery Method , select Text . Enter a Text Campaign Name . This name is used to label your campaign and is not shown to customers. Select the Lost Customer campaign type, and adjust the slider to select how many weeks since a customer's last visit that the system will wait to send a message. Select Apply to continue. Specify the customers to include in the campaign by selecting the gender, customers of a specific employee, and age, then select Create Text . You can select Reset to clear all filters. In the campaign builder, compose a text message for the campaign. The text cannot exceed 306 characters. When composing a message, you can: Select the Vagaro AI button to enhance your message with AI. Select Insert Link to add a link to your booking page, service menu, products, forms, Daily Deal, etc. Note Adding links to your campaigns allows for tracking the click-through rate and how many customers booked through the link . These metrics are only tracked for Vagaro links; Vagaro will not track clicks to external websites outside of Vagaro. Select Insert First Name or Insert First & Last . The system will automatically put the customer's name into the message when it is sent. Select Next . A preview of your text message will appear. Optionally, select Send Text Preview to send a preview text to your phone. Warning Sending a text preview will pull from your Text Messaging plan . Enter the phone numbers you want to send the preview to. Select Add Number to add another phone number. Select Send Preview Text . Select Next . Schedule and announce your campaign: Schedule : Set a time for when this campaign will usually be sent out. By default, it is set to 8:00 a.m. Agree to the acknowledgment. Select Announce and confirm the Important Notice or Usage Alert that may appear.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To send a lost customers campaign: Go to Marketing , then select Create Campaign under Email & Text Marketing . On the marketing dashboard, select Create Campaign . Next to Select Delivery Method , select Text . Enter a Campaign Text Subject . This subject is used to label your campaign and is not shown to customers. Select the Lost Customer campaign type, and adjust the slider to select how many weeks since a customer's last visit that the system will wait to send a message. Select the recipients for this campaign: All Customers : Send this campaign to all customers in your Customer List. Filtered List : Send this campaign to a specific, filtered list of customers. Specific Customers : Not available for this campaign type. Optionally, set a Schedule for when this campaign will be sent out to customers. Select Next to begin drafting your text campaign. In the campaign builder, compose a text message for the campaign. The text cannot exceed 306 characters. Select the Vagaro AI button to enhance your message with AI. Select Insert Link to add a link to your booking page,
ground_truth: You can select the 'Insert First Name' or 'Insert First & Last' option in the campaign builder to automatically include the customer's name in your message.

question: How can I pick a different date or time for a future session if I want to change my plans?
context:
[Article: Rebook a Service or Class- for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008506194-Rebook-a-Service-or-Class-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
Want to schedule another class session? You can schedule another class session with the same instructor from your profile.

Vagaro App To rebook a class session: Log into your Vagaro account on the Vagaro app . At the bottom of the screen, select Appointments . On the Past tab, select Rebook in the class session. The list of available sessions for this class will be displayed. Choose the class session you want to book, then select Sign Up . Note The available times are based on the location you searched or your browser's location settings. You can change the displayed times by selecting the location next to Time Based On and entering the name of a different city. A popup will display to choose how many class sessions you want to book. In the popup, configure your class schedule, then select Next . Number of Attendees : Choose whether it's just you attending the class or if you want to sign up others as well. Number of Sessions : Choose the number of sessions you want to sign up for. Where would you like to take this class : Some businesses offer classes in-house or online. Choose how you want to attend, if applicable. Sign up every : Choose how often you want to attend this class. Select the weeks box to switch between a weekly or monthly frequency. For example, if the class is offered multiple days of the week, you can register for a class every other Friday. Note If you select a month frequency, you can enter the day of the month you want to schedule each month. For example, the fifth of every month. The field is not for scheduling every one, two, or three months. Start Date : Choose when you want to start attending your class. Note Optionally, the business may have additional add-ons you can add to your class. Choose any add-ons you want to add, then select Continue . Optionally, if you're booking the class for someone else, you can change Who you are booking for , then select Checkout to open the shopping cart. You can choose from family, friends, or pets you've added to your profile. To add a new profile, scroll down the list and select Add Family & Friends or Add Guest . On the shopping cart screen, you can remove items from your card or select Continue to checkout. Acknowledge the business's cancellation or refund policies, and then select Book to finish. If you are joining a live stream, select your current location to get the correct class time on your calendar. The business may require a card on file or a deposit to register for a class. You can use a card on file or another payment method like a gift card, package visit, or membership to pay for the deposit.

Web Version To rebook a class session: Log into your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . In the top-right corner, hover over or select your profile, then select Appointments . On the Past tab, select Rebook in the class session. The list of available sessions for this class will be displayed. Choose the class session you want to book, then select Sign Up . Note The available times are based on the location you searched or your browser's location settings. You can change the displayed times by selecting the location next to Time Based On and entering the name of a different city. A popup will display to choose how many class sessions you want to book. In the popup, configure your class schedule, then select Next . Number of Attendees : Choose whether it's just you attending the class or if you want to sign up others as well. Number of Sessions : Choose the number of sessions you want to sign up for. Where would you like to take this class : Some businesses offer classes in-house or online. Choose how you want to attend, if applicable. Sign up every : Choose how often you want to attend this class. Select the weeks box to switch between a weekly or monthly frequency. For example, if the class is offered multiple days of the week, you can register for a class every other Friday. Note If you select a month frequency, you can enter the day of the month you want to schedule each month. For example, the fifth of every month. The field is not for scheduling every one, two, or three months. Start Date : Choose when you want to start attending your class. Note Optionally, the business may have additional add-ons you can add to your class. Choose any add-ons you want to add, then select Continue . Optionally, if you're booking the class for someone else, you'll have the option to change Who you are booking for , then select Checkout to open the shopping cart. You can choose from family, friends, or pets you've added to your profile. To add a new profile, scroll down the list and select Add Family & Friends or Add Guest . On the shopping cart screen, you can remove items from your card or select Continue to checkout. Acknowledge the business's cancellation
ground_truth: You can select a new start date and choose from available class times based on your location or a different city if you prefer a different schedule.

question: How can I delete a payroll that has been marked as 'Paid' using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Payroll History Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003990314-Payroll-History-Report]
Deleting or rerunning a payroll that has been closed and marked as "Paid" is performed in the Payroll History screen, and then rerun the Payroll report .

Payrolls that have been closed and marked as "Paid" can be deleted or rerun if they need to be undone or corrected.

Note If you are using Vagaro Payroll , submitted payrolls can be cancelled the same day by 4 PM PST. After this cut-off time, contact Vagaro Support , and provide the employee name(s) and payroll amounts to reverse. If you are using our legacy Gusto integration , contact Gusto Support for assistance.

On a Phone To delete a payroll that has been marked as "Paid": Select More → Reports → Payroll History . Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Reports → Employees → Payroll History . In the Filters list, select the following options, then select Get Report : Payroll Frequency : Select By Payroll Period . Payroll Period : Select the payroll to delete. After the report has been generated, select the Filters button at the top of the screen. A Delete button appears below the Payroll Period list. Select Delete . In the confirmation popup, select Delete . To rerun the payroll for the payroll period, go back to the Payroll report .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To delete a payroll that has been marked as "Paid": Select Reports → Payroll History . In the Filters list, select the following options, then select Get Report Payroll Frequency : Select By Payroll Period . Payroll Period : Select the payroll to delete. After the report has been generated, a Delete button appears beside the Get Report button. Select Delete . In the confirmation popup, select Delete . To rerun the payroll for the payroll period, go back to the Payroll report .

This report shows a breakdown of each employee's paychecks for a selected payroll period, including commissions, tips, rent collected, taxes and other deductions, and reimbursements and benefits paid.

Businesses that have activated Vagaro Payroll will see multiple tabs at the top of the report to show what their company paid, the amounts that were taxed and debited from the employee's payroll, and a payroll receipt to confirm the total amount deducted from your business's bank account. Without Vagaro Payroll, businesses will see only the contents of the Employee Payroll tab without any tabs on the screen.
ground_truth: To delete a paid payroll using the Vagaro Pro App, go to More → Reports → Employees → Payroll History, select the payroll period in the Filters list, generate the report, then tap the Filters button at the top, and select Delete below the Payroll Period list. Confirm the deletion in the popup.

question: How can I require customers to have a membership before checking in at a Vagaro kiosk?
context:
[Article: Require Customer Memberships to Book at Check-In App Kiosks | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360019833193-Require-Customer-Memberships-to-Book-at-Check-In-App-Kiosks]
For businesses that require memberships for services, such as those in the spa and wellness industry, or for cases, such as those in the fitness and self-care industry, you can configure the Check-In app so that customers must have a membership to book an appointment or sign up for a class at a Check-In kiosk. If they don't have a membership, you can prompt the customer to purchase or renew their membership right at the kiosk, or enter a custom message they can purchase one by either speaking to an employee at your business or visiting your Vagaro Listing Page . Along with your memberships, you can also provide plastic membership cards , which also show off your business logo and branding , and make it easier for customers to check in by scanning their card at the kiosk instead of logging into their account. Tip To require customers to have a membership if they sign up for a class or book an appointment on your Vagaro Listing Page , go to the Online Appointment Rules screen and toggle on the Membership or Package Required to Book switch. Alternatively, if you don't require customers to have a membership when checking in, you can still show a list of your memberships that they can optionally purchase at the kiosk before completing their check-in. Prerequisites: This feature is available only on tablets and requires the Vagaro Check-in app , Credit Card Processing , and Online Shopping Cart . Also, at least one service or class and a membership , must already be set up, and all customers who check in at a kiosk must have a customer profile with your business. To require customers to have a membership when checking in at a kiosk: Go to the Check-In app's settings screen. On a Phone : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . On the Web, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Settings → Premium Features → Check-In App . In the Check-In App : Select the Settings icon, log in, then select Settings . Scroll down to the Membership Purchase setting and choose an option: None : Your customers can book appointments or register for classes at a kiosk without needing a membership. Required : Customers who try to book an appointment or sign up for a class but don't have a membership will be prompted to purchase one at the kiosk. If you select this option, also choose whether to Prompt to scan membership card after purchase , which requires you to provide plastic membership cards for your customers to scan. Optional : Customers don't require a membership when checking in, but they will be presented with a list of your memberships they can purchase right away at the kiosk, or Skip this screen and complete their check-in. Scroll down to the Membership Required to Check-In setting and toggle it on: Select whether a membership is required for customers to check in, and optionally enter a Message to Customers with Inactive Memberships . If this setting is toggled on, customers will be prompted that a membership is required to check in and will have to purchase the membership at the kiosk first. When they select Explore Memberships , they can select one in the list to purchase. If this setting is toggled off, customers are prompted that a membership is required to check in and have to purchase a membership either at your business or on your Vagaro Listing Page . Select whether to Allow Customers to Purchase or Renew Memberships at your kiosk. If this setting is toggled on, customers who check in with an expired or inactive membership will be prompted to renew their membership at the kiosk. When they select Renew Membership , they can select their expired membership to purchase again or select a different one. If this setting is toggled off, customers with expired or inactive memberships will also be prompted that their membership has expired. You can enter a custom message to tell them to speak to someone at your business or visit your Vagaro Listing Page . Select Save .
ground_truth: To require customers to have a membership when checking in, go to the Check-In app's settings, select 'Membership Required to Check-In,' toggle it on, and optionally enter a message for inactive memberships. This will prompt customers that a membership is required and they must purchase or renew it at the kiosk before checking in.

question: How can I sign up for a Vagaro workshop through Vagaro University?
context:
[Article: Vagaro Resources & Training | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001580613-Vagaro-Resources-Training]
Use Vagaro’s built-in resources to learn the platform, find answers, and get help when you need it. In this article, you’ll find ways to browse support articles, watch tutorial videos, and explore training options. If you still have additional questions or require further assistance, feel free to contact Vagaro Support at any time. Visit Vagaro University Vagaro University is our educational hub featuring comprehensive tutorials and workshops created by Vagaro experts. You'll dive into easy-to-follow content, from the fundamentals of Vagaro to strategies for effective marketing. Watch Our Free Tutorials If you'd like to learn how to use Vagaro at your own pace, watch our free tutorial videos. Created by our Vagaro experts and trainers, you'll learn how to use Vagaro features to run and manage your business. To watch our free tutorials: In a web browser, go to Vagaro University at mysite.vagaro.com/vagarouniversity. Below the Vagaro University banner, select a topic to view the tutorials available. Select a video to view. Sign Up for a Workshop Participate in one or more of our free workshops. Led by a Vagaro expert, each session provides an interactive walkthrough of the software with a Q&A session for you and fellow Vagaro users. To sign up for a workshop: In a web browser, go to Vagaro University at mysite.vagaro.com/vagarouniversity. At the top of the screen, select Workshops . Under the Live/Upcoming heading, select Register Now for the workshop you want to attend. Select the session you'd like to attend. Tip All times are shown in Pacific Time. Select Show in My Time Zone to see when it will start in your local time zone. Fill out the required registration information, then select Register . Sign Up for One-on-One Training Vagaro University also offers one-on-one training. During these personalized one-hour sessions, you'll work with one of our Vagaro experts over a video call to learn the Vagaro software and ask any questions you may have. Select from the following: One-hour training sessions : Pick the topic you want help learning. Package of three training sessions : Purchase a training bundle that includes three sessions for the price of two. 30-minute check-in service : Schedule a Vagaro expert to review your Vagaro settings and features with you. If you purchased a Vagaro membership, these four 30-minute check-ins are already included. Vagaro Membership : This monthly membership entitles you to 50% off each one-hour training session you book. You'll also get a dedicated technical trainer to guide you and provide suggestions tailored to your business. To sign up for one-on-one training: In a web browser, go to Vagaro University at mysite.vagaro.com/vagarouniversity. At the top of the screen, select Training & Membership . Find the training you're interesting in, then select Book Now At the top of the screen, complete the following: Optionally, choose a professional to work with. Select the date for the training. Select Search to check their availability. In the calendar, select one of the times shown. Dates that are greyed out cannot be booked. If none of the times are convenient for you, select another date on the calendar. In the checkout screen, enter the goals for the training and any other comments or topics you'd like to discuss, then select the level of experience you have with booking software. Select Submit . Watch Our Help Videos For more guided learning, visit the Vagaro Support YouTube channel at https://www.youtube.com/user/VagaroSupport . You’ll find video tutorials and topic-based playlists covering Vagaro features, setup steps, and everyday tools to help you run your business. Browse and Read Our Support Articles For step-by-step help, visit the Vagaro Help Center at https://support.vagaro.com . You’ll find support articles organized by topic, with instructions and information to help you set up Vagaro, use its features, and manage your business.
ground_truth: To sign up for a Vagaro workshop, go to Vagaro University at mysite.vagaro.com/vagarouniversity, select 'Workshops' at the top of the screen, under 'Live/Upcoming' click 'Register Now' for your preferred workshop, choose the session, and then fill out the registration form to complete your sign-up.
