question: What file formats are supported for uploading images to use with the Vagaro Markup Editor?
context:
[Article: Vagaro Markup Editor | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/11954030441115-Vagaro-Markup-Editor]
Vagaro Markup Editor allows you to draw on photos and SOAP Notes templates (face and whole-body). You can upload a picture of a client's face, for example. Using that image: draw circles and arrows, write text, add highlights and comments, etc. You can add notations to an uploaded photo, the SOAP Notes Face, and SOAP Notes Advanced templates. Prerequisites : You must have Vagaro Drive to use the Vagaro Markup Editor. Accessing the Vagaro Markup Editor You can access the Vagaro Markup Editor when creating SOAP Notes and on image files that have been saved (using Vagaro Drive) to a customer's profile (appointment, customer, and forms.) Editable file formats are .jpg , .png , and .gif . When you upload an image file using a form or SOAP Notes, only the markup editor is invoked. When you use the Open option on the customer's Files page (go to Customers , select a customer and then select the Files tab), you'll have a choice of markup or image editor to use. (See [link to image editor article] for more.) You can also invoke the markup editor for image files on Vagaro Drive, in the Customers area for appointment, customer, and form files, and in the Business area. To open a file in the Markup editor: Locate the image file and select the Action menu. Vagaro Pro Web Open the file: Vagaro Pro Web Note Vagaro Pro opens the image in the markup editor. The Web version opens a detail screen for the file: Web Version : Select the Edit icon and then select Markup editor. The image in the Markup editor looks like this: Vagaro Pro Web Using Markup Editor Tools On the Vagaro Pro app , the center grouping of tools is compressed for space with a More (3 vertical dots) menu to reveal the last three options: Here are all the notation types you can add to images using the Vagaro Markup editor : Rectangle Draw a rectangle on templates or uploaded images Apply color, line width, and line style to the rectangle Resize, move, and rotate Freehand draw/line Draw a line on templates or uploaded images Apply color and line width to the freehand line Resize, move, and rotate Arrow Draw an arrow or straight line on templates or uploaded images Apply color, line width, and line style to the arrow/line Apply arrowhead styles (both ends, left or right, none) Resize, move, and rotate Text tool Add text anywhere on templates or uploaded images Apply color and select font Resize, move, and rotate Circle tool Add a circle to templates or uploaded images Apply color to the outline Apply color fill (or no fill) Apply line width and line style Apply opacity level to circle (affects both line and fill) Resize, move, and rotate Highlighter tool Add a highlight rectangle to templates or uploaded images Apply color and opacity level Resize, move, and rotate Comment tool Add a comment box with a pointer to templates or uploaded images Apply color to text and comment box Select font for text Reposition the pointer Resize, move, and rotate the comment box Other actions The selection arrow lets you select to select a previously added notation select the trash icon to delete the selected notation The undo arrow steps backward, removing the last change you made select the checkmark to close the image or template and save your changes Returns you to the SOAP note screen select the X to close the image or template and discard your changes Returns you to the SOAP note screen Tool selections Color selector (all except the circle tool) Line width selector Line style selector Arrow type selector Font selector Circle tool color selector Opacity level selector To move, resize or rotate a notation, select the notation and use the handles to resize, or select in the middle of the notation and drag to reposition. (We're using images from SOAP Notes Face for our examples.) Here's a sample of each tool used on the SOAP Notes face diagram: Important You cannot change the notations entered during your editing session once you save the image. You can invoke the markup editor again and add more notations.
ground_truth: The supported file formats are .jpg, .png, and .gif.

question: What must I do to restore a deleted employee's appointments in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Restore Deleted Employees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/35899760858907-Restore-Deleted-Employees]
You can restore a deleted employee's profile at any time. All reporting history and tax forms will stay with the account and will not be lost. However, future and previous appointments will not be restored. To get the appointments restored, you'll need to contact support and pay a one-time restoration fee of $50. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To add an employee profile: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Add Employee . Enter the required details for the employee profile, then select Next . The Existing Employee Profile Found popup will appear if a profile is found. Tip Ensure that the email you enter is the same as the previously deleted profile. In the popup, select Add Employee . Have the employee enter their login information, then select Login Enter any additional changes to their profile, then select Next . Confirm the employee's Login Information and Employee Access , then select Next . Set the employee's working hours, then select Save . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add an employee profile: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Add Employee . Enter the required details for the employee profile, then select Next . The Existing Employee Profile Found popup will appear if a profile is found. Tip Ensure that the email you enter is the same as the previously deleted profile. In the popup, select Add Employee . Have the employee enter their login information, then select Login Enter any additional changes to their profile, then select Next . Select the employee's Employee Type and Employee Access settings, then select Next . Assign the employee to services and/or classes, then select Next . Set the employee's working hours, then select Save .
ground_truth: To restore a deleted employee's appointments, you need to contact support and pay a one-time restoration fee of $50.

question: If I decide to stop sharing customer details across my locations, will the customers who made purchases at multiple sites still be linked in the system?
context:
[Article: Share Information Between Multi-Location Businesses | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/7515489788187-Share-Information-Between-Multi-Location-Businesses]
Sharing customers between grouped locations will sync:

New customers from all locations within a group. Merged customers within a group. Edited customers within a group, like changing the profile name or phone number.

When you first sync customers, the customer lists for all locations you selected are merged, resulting in multiple locations having the same customer lists. Customers that are deleted at one business will not be removed from other locations. Let's use an example of two locations: Location X and Location Z. Their customer lists look like this:

After grouping these locations and syncing their customers, they will have the same customers in their lists.

Set up Customer Sharing To share customers: Select Settings → Multi-Location . Next to the group you want to modify, select the Action menu, then select Edit Group . Under the Shared Settings heading, toggle Share Customers on. In the Share Customers From list, select all locations you want to share with this group. Note If you select locations from another multi-location group, their customers will be shared with this group without affecting that location's customer list. To sync between businesses in a group: Points : Whether points can be earned and redeemed at all locations where customers are shared. General Tags : Whether general tags associated with customer profiles will be visible at all locations where that customer is shared. Gift Cards : Whether gift cards can be redeemed at all locations where customers are shared. Form Responses : Customer form responses and SOAP notes will be shared at all locations where customers are shared. Note If any of the locations in the group do not have Forms activated , a popup will prompt you to activate this feature for those locations. Syncing forms between all your businesses can take up to 2 hours. Memberships : Whether memberships can be redeemed if they share the same qualifying services, classes or products. Packages : Whether packages can be redeemed if they share the same qualifying services or classes. Vagaro Drive : Whether to share uploaded files to all locations. Important If packages and memberships to be shared between locations do not have the exact same package/membership name, services, classes, or products that they include, they will not be synced. Select Save .

Stop Syncing Customers If you turn off customer sharing or remove a location from a group, the system separates and restores each business's customer list back to its original configuration. The exception is if a shared customer has made a purchase at one of the other businesses in the group. For example, if a customer from Location X makes a purchase with Location Z while both locations are sharing customers, turning off sharing will remove all customers shared except that profile. To turn off customer sync for a group: Select Settings → Multi-Location . Next to the group you want to modify, select the Action menu, then select Edit Group . Toggle Sync Customers off: Select Disable in the confirmation dialog box. Select Save to finish.

How Employee Reviews Are Shared When you Reviews selected in your Share Customers settings, reviews for employees that work in more than one location are shared on the booking page for each location. If you add an employee to a new location that's part of a group, their existing reviews will be replicated on the new location's booking page. Reviews are shared throughout the entire multi-location setup, including between different groups. Location : On your Vagaro Listing Page , the About tab shows an overall rating (from all ratings in all reviews) and a link showing the number of reviews. When a review for an employee from a location is added, the rating is calculated to include the latest ratings, and the number of reviews is incremented. When a customer selects the link to see the location reviews, they'll see only ones that were written for services performed at this location. Employee : On the Staff tab of your Vagaro Listing Page and when a customer is offered multiple employee choices when booking a service, all reviews for an employee, regardless of location, appear. When a new review is added for an employee who works at more than one of your locations, the overall rating changes to add the new rating into the overall calculation, and the number of reviews increments for that employee - in ALL locations. Removing employees from a location or removing a location from a group will affect the employee's reviews: Removing an Employee From a Location : Location/Venue Reviews All reviews for the location are maintained in the About tab, even after the employee is removed. The rating from that review is kept as part of the overall rating calculation for the location. Employee Reviews The employee is no longer shown on the Staff tab. This means that reviews for the deleted employee are not shown, either. However An employee review written while they were in Business B is still shown with that employee's reviews on Business A 's staff page. Removing a Location From a Group : Location/Venue Reviews All reviews for the location are maintained in the About tab. The rating from that review is kept as part of the overall rating calculation for the location. Employee Reviews The employee is shown
ground_truth: Yes, if a shared customer has made a purchase at another business in the group, their profile will remain linked even after customer sharing is turned off.

question: If I move my clients' details to a different account, will their information be erased from my current setup?
context:
[Article: Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import FAQ | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/4403315263643-Vagaro-to-Vagaro-Import-FAQ]
Vagaro lets you easily import and transfer your information from one Vagaro business to another. Whether you’re opening a new location, joining a new business, or handing off operations as you close down, you can seamlessly move your services, classes, customers, customer notes, future appointments, and Vagaro Drive files to your new account. Depending on what information you want to transfer over, see one of the following articles: Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Share Your Customer List with Another Vagaro Account When you just want to share customer profiles with another Vagaro business, you can copy over customer contact info, notes, and any attached Vagaro Drive files. The customer data is duplicated in the other account and remains in your own system. Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Transfer an Employee Who's Starting Their Own Business If an employee is leaving to open their own business, the business account owner can create the new business in Vagaro and transfer over the employee’s customers, contact details, notes, appointments, services, and classes. Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Transfer Employee and Customer Data to Another Account When an employee joins an existing Vagaro business, the current owner can transfer the employee’s customers, appointment data, Vagaro Drive files, service menu, and other relevant information. Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Transfer Your Data From a Previous Employer on Vagaro Employees who have moved to a new Vagaro business can log into the account and request a transfer of their reviews, menu, contact information, notes, files, etc. They choose which items to bring over when confirming the request. Important Considerations Before Transferring Contact customers : Employees moving appointments to a different or new (their own) Vagaro business must contact their customers with appointments to inform them of the change. They can use a text or email marketing campaign to notify their customers. Inactive or suspended target business : If you request the transfer to occur on a date in the future, be aware that if the target business becomes inactive or suspended (due to non-payment, for example), the transfer will not go through. Free trial vs. paying businesses : In Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Transfer an Employee Who's Starting Their Own Business , where the transferring business owner is creating a new business for their employee, the following applies: If the transferring business is in a free trial period , the new business will start in a free trial . If the transferring business is in a paid monthly plan , the new business will immediately be in a paid plan with no free trial period. Cancellation : Transfer requests cannot be cancelled. If you requested a transfer by mistake, contact Vagaro support for options to roll back a transfer. Requests to roll back a completed Vagaro to Vagaro import may incur a service fee. How is Information Handled in a Vagaro to Vagaro Import? Depending on the information included in a transfer request, it may be moved or copied to the target business. Note When data is moved to the target business, it is deleted from the transferring business. Data that is copied over remains unchanged. Data Copied or Moved? Future Appointments Moved Over to Target Business Past Appointments Moved Over to Target Business Appointment Files (Vagaro Drive) Moved Over to Target Business Customer Contact Information Copied Over to Target Business Customer Notes Moved Over to Target Business Customer Files (Vagaro Drive) Copied Over to Target Business Services Copied Over to Target Business Membership Copied Over to Target Business Package Copied Over to Target Business Reviews Moved Over to Target Business Form Copied Over to Target Business How are Vagaro Drive files Transferred Over If your business has Vagaro Drive configured, the option to transfer appointment and customer files will be shown. If the other business does not have Vagaro Drive or enough storage, a popup will appear prompting them to activate Vagaro Drive or upgrade. How are Services Mapped Over? If you transfer over appointment and class data, the employee or business owner will need to map the imported services and classes to their menu. This process helps align differences between two businesses. If a new business is created for their employee, services and classes are imported without the need to map. If the other business has no services or classes, your menu will be imported without the need to map.
ground_truth: No, when customer data is copied over, it remains in your original system while also being available in the new account.

question: How do I share customer profiles between locations in my multi-location Vagaro setup?
context:
[Article: Set Up Connect by Vagaro for Multi-Location Businesses | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26308416978459-Set-Up-Connect-by-Vagaro-for-Multi-Location-Businesses]
Connect by Vagaro with multi-location functionality helps enterprise businesses manage customer communication from one place or through multiple locations. You can share customer profiles and sync your text marketing toll-free number across multiple locations. Plus, if you're subscribed to Vagaro AI , your chatbot can manage messages from all locations—keeping the experience personalized. You can also send booking links for individual locations or let customers choose which one to visit. Prerequisites: This feature requires your business to be set up as a multi-location within a group . Share Customers Between Locations From your multi-location settings, share customer profiles between locations, allowing you to send messages to a larger list of customers. To share customers between locations: Go to Settings → Business → Multi-Location . Select the multi-location group to be edited. Toggle on Share Customers . In the Sync Customers from , choose which locations you want to sync. Choose what information you want to sync over from customers. Select Save . Sync Text Plan Between Locations Sync your text plan and toll-free number from one flagship location to all locations within a group. With all locations synced together, incoming messages from all locations will be funneled to the selected flagship, and only the flagship will be able to send and view customer messages. Note All sub-locations can still access and send messages to coworkers, and any individual text plans used for text marketing will not be affected. To set up Connect by Vagaro for multi-location: Go to Settings → Business → Multi-Location . Select the multi-location group to be edited. Scroll down to Sync Text Plan for Connect and toggle it on. In the Choose Text Plan Location menu, choose the phone number to be synced across all locations. Note This list will only display flagships. If you haven't assigned a flagship, toggle on Sync Settings and choose one. Select Save . All sublocations will no longer have access to Connect by Vagaro. Connect AI Functionality Between Synced Locations Set up a personal chatbot for your business with Vagaro AI that takes over customer communication by providing automated responses to messages on Connect by Vagaro . Vagaro AI will respond to messages from your Vagaro Listing Page and via SMS. Enabling the Sync Text Plan for Connect setting will change how your chatbot functions. Instead of having an individual chatbot for each location, Vagaro AI will prompt customers to choose a location they want to book with. Enabling Vagaro AI with Connect by Vagaro will charge an additional monthly subscription on a tiered pricing system. Number of Locations United States (per month) Canada (per month) United Kingdom (per month) Australia (per month) 1 $10 $15 £10 $15 2-5 $20 $30 £20 $30 6-20 $30 $45 £30 $45 21-100 $50 $75 £50 $75 101-500 $160 $240 £160 $240 501+ Contact Sales Contact Sales Contact Sales Contact Sales Send Links for All Locations or an Individual Location Businesses that sync their text plan will notice that a Choose Booking Link Type popup appears when generating links for messages. To generate a link: Go to Connect by Vagaro: On a Phone or Tablet : Download or open the Connect app . On the Web : Log in to your Vagaro business, then select the Chat Bubble button. Create a new chat or select an ongoing chat. Open the links menu: On a Phone or Tablet : In the bottom-left corner, select the Plus + icon, then select Vagaro Links . On the Web : In the chat box, select the Link button. Choose the link to generate, like Book With Us 24/7 , Visit Our Page , and so on. The Choose Booking Link Type popup will appear. In the popup, choose an option, then select Add Booking Link . All Locations : When the customer clicks the link, they'll be prompted to choose the location they want to view. Single Location : Choose a specific location you want the customer to see. Send the message to the customer.
ground_truth: Go to Settings → Business → Multi-Location, select the group to edit, toggle on Share Customers, choose the locations to sync from the Sync Customers from menu, select the customer information to sync, and then click Save.

question: How can I update my profile photo in the Vagaro app?
context:
[Article: Edit Your Customer Profile - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003183914-Edit-Your-Customer-Profile-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
You, the customer, can update your customer profile by logging into the Vagaro app or Vagaro.com . A business may also send you a form in an email or text message for you to update your information, which will automatically update your profile information in their system. To update your customer profile at any time: Go to your Profile screen. In the Vagaro app : Select the Profile Icon at the bottom of the screen. At Vagaro.com : In the top-right corner of the screen, select your profile picture, then select My Profile . Optionally, add or update your Profile Photo by selecting Choose a file then selecting a photo from your Camera or Files . If you already have a profile picture, you can delete it and upload a new one. Update the rest of your profile information as needed. At the bottom of the screen, select Save Changes .
ground_truth: In the Vagaro app, go to your Profile screen, select 'Choose a file,' then select a photo from your Camera or Files to add or update your profile photo.

question: How can I prevent new customers from booking online with my salon?
context:
[Article: Block New Customers from Booking Online | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19065503469595-Block-New-Customers-from-Booking-Online]
If you're a busy salon with an exclusive clientele, you may want to use Block New Customers from Booking Online . With this setting enabled, only existing customers can book with the employees you set. Also, you can apply this setting to one, some, or all providers at your business. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . What Classifies a Customer as "New" The system will block your customer from booking if they follow these criteria: They don't have a customer profile in the business's Customer List . They have no history of Confirmed appointments. They haven't been checked out for an appointment. The employee hasn't performed a service or class for this customer. Customers can request an appointment if the business has the Waitlist feature turned on. Block New Customers To block new customers from booking: Go to Online Appointment Rules : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Appointment Rules . Under the Booking heading, toggle on Block New Customers from Online Booking . Open the Employees Not Accepting New Customers list and select the employees who will not accept appointments for new customers. Important If a customer has a history with one employee at a business and this feature is on for all employees, they will only be able to book appointments with that one employee. Select Save .
ground_truth: To block new customers from booking online, go to Online Appointment Rules, then under the Booking heading, toggle on 'Block New Customers from Online Booking' and select the employees who will not accept new customer appointments, then save your changes.

question: How can I make sure my new staff member's bank account details are correct before I start paying them through direct deposit?
context:
[Article: Add an Employee to Your Payroll | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/21475805714075-Add-an-Employee-to-Your-Payroll]
Adding an employee to your payroll requires that you have already created an employee profile for them. You will then manually enter their information into Vagaro, or you can invite them to onboard themselves using their own device on their own time. Regardless of how you onboard an employee, they will have to digitally sign a Tax Withholding form (Form W-4) and a Direct Deposit Authorization form in their own Vagaro account to be included in the next payroll; otherwise, you'll have to issue them a check each time you run payroll. Employees will also need to provide you with all their paystubs for the current tax year so you can enter their payroll information. Tip If you migrated your Gusto account, you can quickly map their Gusto profile to their Vagaro employee profile. Note Employees who are rehired as contractors will need another employee profile to be set up and associated with their payroll profile. A separate profile keeps their paperwork distinct between being that of an employee or a contractor. It may take two to three business days for newly added employees to be verified on your payroll. Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US on the web, tablet, Pay Desk, and PayPro. Invite an Employee to Fill Out Their Profile When you invite an employee to fill out their profile, they will receive an email from Vagaro asking them to enter their personal information, select how they want to receive their payments from you, and then sign and submit their completed Form W-4. To invite an employee to fill out their payroll profile: Select Reports → Payroll Configuration . Scroll down the screen to the Payroll tab. Select Add Employee to Payroll . In the Add Employees to Payroll popup, select the employee(s) to add to payroll, then select Add Employees . Notice that the button also shows the total monthly payroll cost as you select employees. At the end of the row of the employee you just added, select Setup Payroll . In the What Kind of Employee? popup, select Employee , then select Next . In the Finish Setup popup, select Invite Employee to Fill out Profile , enter their basic information, then select Next . In the Employee Added confirmation popup, select Done to return to the Payroll Configuration screen, then select Save . The employee will receive an email requesting them to sign and submit their Form W-4: Tip If an employee who was invited to complete their payroll profile hasn't onboarded themselves by for their first pay day, you can Resend or Cancel an Onboarding Invitation to an Employee and enter their information yourself. In the Payroll Configuration screen, set up the employee's pay: Set Up Hourly Pay Rates Set Commission for Services Set Commission for Classes Set Commission for Products Enter the employee's paystub information . If you selected multiple employees to add to your payroll, repeat steps 5 to 10 to onboard the others. Fill Out an Employee's Profile If the employee is with you or you already have their information, you can enter their personal information, then select or enter their payment details. To add an employee to your payroll by filling out their profile: Select Reports → Payroll Configuration . Scroll down the screen to the Payroll tab. Select Add Employee to Payroll . In the Add Employees to Payroll popup, select the employee(s) to add to payroll, then select Add Employees . Notice that the button also shows the total monthly payroll cost as you select employees. At the end of the row of the employee you just added, select Setup Payroll . In the What Kind of Employee? popup, select Employee , then select Next . In the Set Up Profile popup, select Fill Employee Profile Now , then select Next . Enter the employee's Basic Info , then select Next . Enter the Employee's Payment Details , then select Next . Direct Deposit : Pay the employee by depositing their funds in their bank account. To verify that the employee owns that bank account after you enter their bank information, they must check their bank statement for two micro transactions amounts that you will need to enter in the Verify Bank Account screen. Those amounts will be credited back to their bank account within three business days. Check : Pay the employee with a check for each pay period. In the Employee Added confirmation popup, select Done to return to the Payroll Configuration screen, then select Save . In the Payroll Configuration screen, set up the employee's pay: Set Up Hourly Pay Rates Set Commission for Services Set Commission for Classes Set Commission for Products Enter the employee's paystub information . If you selected multiple employees to add to your payroll, repeat steps 5 to 12 to onboard the others.
ground_truth: Your employee will need to check their bank statement for two small transactions we send to verify ownership, and then you will enter those amounts into the system for confirmation.

question: What are the iPhone model and iOS version requirements to use Tap to Pay with Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Tap to Phone on iPhone (Australia and the United Kingdom) | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/23139000838299-Tap-to-Phone-on-iPhone-Australia-and-the-United-Kingdom]
Tap to Phone allows customers to pay by simply tapping their contactless-enabled credit card, debit card, or mobile device (such as a smartphone or smartwatch) on a compatible device—no credit card reader is needed. Tap to Phone supports all major credit and debit cards with the NFC icon and digital wallets, like Google Pay, Apple Pay, and Samsung Pay. You can start accepting credit cards as soon as you sign up for Vagaro Credit Card Processing by connecting your Apple ID to your merchant account, which only takes a few moments. Tap to Phone is currently available in the United States, the United Kingdom, and Australia. This feature is not available to businesses in Canada at this time. Prerequisites : This feature requires Credit Card Processing and a compatible iPhone. Tap to Phone is not available on an iPad. Processing Rates & Compatible iPhones Vagaro businesses using Tap to Pay will pay the following processing fees per transaction: United Kingdom : 1.15% + £0.27 per Tap to Pay transaction. Australia : 1.90% + $0.12 per Tap to Pay transaction. Tap to Pay requires an iPhone XS or later and running iOS 16.7 or later; however, updating to the latest iOS version is recommended for best performance. See the following articles to learn more: See what iOS your iPhone is running Update your iOS In the United Kingdom, depending on your issuer, the customer may need to insert their card into your Vagaro Credit Card Reader because of the Strong Customer Authentication requirements. The card will decline if this is the case. Have them try another card or payment method or charge them with the credit card reader. Set Up Tap to Pay in Vagaro for the First Time Setting up Tap to Pay on your iPhone requires you to link your Apple ID to Vagaro. After connecting your ID, you'll be ready to accept payments right away. You can connect up to three devices with the same Apple ID. To set up Tap to Pay: Go to the Checkout screen. In the bottom-left corner, select Pay by Card → Tap to Pay on iPhone . Note Make sure you've turned off your reader before starting. In Tap to Pay on iPhone. Now Available , select Get Started . Link your Apple ID with Vagaro. Select Continue with This Apple ID to link with the Apple ID shown on the screen. Select Use a Different Apple ID to use another Apple ID. Once the system has linked your account, you'll see instructions on using Tap to Pay with Vagaro. Select Next to finish the setup process. The Pay by Card button will change from black to blue. Charge a Customer with Tap to Pay If you've already set up Tap to Pay on your device, you can process payments from the Checkout screen. Important When charging credit cards with tap to phone, ensure your Vagaro Pro app and iOS version are up-to-date. Also, remove any personal credit cards near or attached to your phone to avoid accidental charges. To charge a customer with Tap to Pay: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. In the bottom-left corner, select Tap to Pay . The Tip screen appears. Note If you don't see Tap to Pay , make sure you have connected to Tap to Pay or unassigned your credit card reader . Have the customer enter their tip amount. The Tap to Pay screen appears. Show your phone to the customer and have them tap their device or card on the front of your device. When the process is complete, the Approved screen appears. Optionally, send the receipt to the customer.
ground_truth: Tap to Pay requires an iPhone XS or later running iOS 16.7 or later, with updating to the latest iOS version recommended for best performance.

question: How many Google Calendars can I sync with Vagaro at the same time?
context:
[Article: Sync Vagaro with Google Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/31275501148699-Sync-Vagaro-with-Google-Calendar]
To sync your Calendar with Vagaro, you'll need to link your Google account with Vagaro. Keep in mind that you can only link your Google Calendar with one Vagaro business at a time.

On a Phone To sync your calendar with Google: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles, then select the employee's profile from the list. From their profile, next to your business's name, select Action → Integrations . Note If multi-location is configured, select Action for the location you want to modify. Under Sync Calendar , select Sync Google Calendar . Select the checkboxes for the setting you want to turn on: To bring over past events from Google Calendar, select Import past events from Google calendar , then choose a Starting From date. Keep in mind that you can only sync up to three years in the past. To bring over appointments, classes, and personal tasks from Vagaro to Google Calendar, select Sync Vagaro to Google Calendar . To bring over Google Calendar Events to Vagaro, select Sync Google Calendar to Vagaro . To hide the names of any calendar events brought over from Google, select Make imported Google events private to other employees . Select Connect Calendar . When you Connect Calendar , the system will begin syncing your two calendars and will show Syncing in Progress . This will take a few moments to complete.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To sync your calendar with Google: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . Next to the employee's profile, select Action → Integrations . Note If multi-location is configured, select the Locations link next to their profile, then select Action → Transfer Employee Data for one of the locations. Under Sync Calendar , select Sync Google Calendar . Sign in with Google and give Vagaro access to your account. Once successfully connected, the Connect Calendar popup appears. Choose the Google Calendar you want to sync. Keep in mind that only one calendar can be shared at a time. Select the checkboxes for the setting you want to turn on: To bring over past events from Google Calendar, select Import past events from Google calendar , then choose a Starting From date. Keep in mind that you can only sync up to three years in the past. To bring over appointments, classes, and personal tasks from Vagaro to Google Calendar, select Sync Vagaro to Google Calendar . To bring over Google Calendar Events to Vagaro, select Sync Google Calendar to Vagaro . To hide the names of any calendar events brought over from Google, select Make imported Google events private to other employees . Select Connect Calendar . When you Connect Calendar , the system will begin syncing your two calendars and will show Syncing in Progress . This will take a few moments to complete.
ground_truth: You can only link and sync one Google Calendar with Vagaro at a time.

question: Where will my Daily Deal be visible once it's approved?
context:
[Article: Create a Daily Deal for Services & Classes | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204254384-Create-a-Daily-Deal-for-Services-Classes]
Daily Deals are a great way to advertise your business to new customers and help bring back customers who haven't come in for a while. Creating a Daily Deal is free, and Vagaro does not take any commission. Once announced and approved, the deal appears on the Vagaro Marketplace , your Vagaro Listing Page , and MySite , allowing for more visibility of your business.

Prerequisites : This feature requires your business to be listed on Vagaro.com .
ground_truth: Once approved, the Daily Deal appears on the Vagaro Marketplace, your Vagaro Listing Page, and MySite.

question: How can I see how much each team member earned from sales over a certain period?
context:
[Article: Run the Employee Sales Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000409134-Run-the-Employee-Sales-Report]
The Employee Sales report shows how much each of your employees sold over a date range. See the number of services, classes, products, memberships, and packages sold, their prices, and the tips, taxes, and fees collected. Select an employee’s name to view their individual transactions.

All sales figures shown are after discounts and refunds. Deductions for points are not included.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

Service Provider : The employee’s name. Appts : Appointments checked out with the employee. Unique Customers : Different customers checked out with the employee. Customer Visits : Times a customer checked out with the employee. May be lower than Appts if a customer checked out multiple appointments in one visit. Services : Services checked out with the employee. Services Sales : Value of those services. Services Add-Ons : Service add-ons checked out with the employee. Service Add-On Sales : Value of those add-ons. Classes : Classes checked out with the employee. Class Sales : Value of those classes. Class Add-On Sales : Value of class add-ons checked out with the employee. Products : Products checked out with the employee. Avg Product Qty Sold Per Visit : Average products sold per customer visit (products sold ÷ customer visits). Product Sales : Revenue from products checked out with the employee. Avg Product Sales Per Visit : Average product sales per customer visit (product sales ÷ customer visits). Memberships : Memberships checked out with the employee. Membership Sales : Value of those memberships. Gift Cards : Gift cards checked out with the employee. Gift Card Sales : Value of those gift cards. Packages : Packages checked out with the employee. Package Sales : Value of those packages. Tips : Tips intended for the employee. Taxes : Tax collected on the employee’s transactions. Fees : Fees collected on the employee’s transactions. Total : All money collected from the employee’s sales, tips, taxes, and fees.

On a Phone On a phone, the Employee Sales report shows a separate card for each employee's sales transactions for the selected date range. To run the Employee Sales report: Select More → Reports → Sales . At the top of the screen, select the Date Range to report on. The report updates automatically each time you change the date range. Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set any of the following filters, and then select Run Report . Employees : Show data for all employees or a specific set of employees. Customers : Show data for all or a specific set of customers. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, show data for all locations or a specific set of locations. This option is available only if the Multi-Location Reports Access Level is set to View . Include Past Employees : Include data for employee profiles removed from your business. Appts. From-To : Show transactions by the date the appointment was completed, regardless of when the payment was made. Note This filter differs from Date Range . Date Range filters transactions by the date the payment was processed. Sort By : Reorder the report.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro The Employee Sales report lists the services, classes, and products that an employee has sold during the date range. To run the Employee Sales report: Select Reports → Sales . Set the date range for your report: In the top-right corner, select Transaction Date to open the date picker. Use the date picker to choose a date range or a preset range like Yesterday or Last 7 Days . If you manually selected a date range, select Submit . Optionally, in the top-right corner, select Filters , set any of the following filters, and then select Run Report . Employees : Show data for all employees or a specific set of employees. Customers : Show data for all or a specific set of customers. Business Location : If your business has multiple locations, show data for all locations or a specific set of locations. This option is available only if the Multi-Location Reports Access Level is set to View . Include Past Employees : Include data for employee profiles removed from your business. Appointment Date : Shows transactions by the date the appointment was completed, regardless of when the payment was made. Note This filter differs from Transaction Date . Transaction Date filters transactions by the date the payment was processed. Optionally, sort, print, or export the report: Select a column header to sort the report. Select Print to get a hard copy of the report. Select Export to save the report to an Excel or PDF file (computer version only).
ground_truth: You can run the Employee Sales report, select the desired date range, and view the details for each employee's sales, tips, taxes, and fees collected during that time.

question: How can I add a Date and Time element to my form in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add the Date and Time Element | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24398221576603-Add-the-Date-and-Time-Element]
Ask your customers and employees using the Date and Time element to select or enter a date, time, or date range in response to a question you ask them, up to 200 characters long on your form or SOAP note. For example, customers can provide their availability for an appointment or select the date when they last had a procedure performed. When filling out a form, your customers and employees will be able to select a date and/or time to answer your question. Date Time Date and Time Date Range Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To add the Date and Time element to your form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . Go to your form or SOAP note: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To create a SOAP note template , select Create New → SOAP Note . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . Under Form Elements , drag the Choose One element into the form designer. The element's settings will appear on the right side of the screen. If you already have this element in the form designer, select it to make changes. Enter a Title for this element. Next to Date Options , choose where you'll require your customer to enter a Date , Time , Date and Time , or Date Range . Optionally, to make this element required, turn on Required Field . Publish your form: To save this form as a draft and publish it later, in the top-right corner, select Save as Draft . This form is saved in form management and can be published at a later time. To see a preview of your form and how it'll look for your customers, in the top-right corner, select Preview . You can select the Computer , Tablet , or Phone icon to see how your form will look on each device. To publish your form, go to Settings , then in the top-right corner, select Publish . Your form is saved in form management.
ground_truth: Go to Forms, create or edit a form, drag the Choose One element into the form designer, then set the Date Options to include Date, Time, Date and Time, or Date Range.

question: What should I do if I want to verify my bank account during the Plaid verification process but my bank is not supported?
context:
[Article: United States - Activate Credit Card Processing | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/230401007-United-States-Activate-Credit-Card-Processing]
After choosing your Vagaro hardware, you must enter some information about your business in the Verify Your Business Information form. If you aren't sure what to enter, the sections below can help clarify. Once you've filled out all your information, select Continue at the bottom of the form.

What Does "Type of Merchant" Mean? The "type of merchant" is your business's structure and the legal designation for your business required by the government. Sole Proprietor (Self-Employed) : Someone who owns and runs a business alone; however, if you select Sole Proprietor , this does not mean you have to work alone. Limited Liability Company (LLC) : A business type that protects business owners from personal responsibility for their business's debts and liabilities while providing better tax advantages than a sole proprietor. Private Corporation (Privately Owned Business) : Your business must file Articles of Incorporation to create a business entity with the IRS. Public Corporation (Publicly Traded on the Stock Market) : Your business must file Articles of Incorporation to create a business entity with the IRS.

What Is My "Legal Business Name"? For all other business types, however, you'll need to register a business name with the IRS. The business name entered here needs to be registered with the IRS. Sole Proprietor businesses do not need to enter a Legal Business Name. Learn more from the Small Business Administration . If you do not know what your registered Legal Business Name is, please contact the IRS at (800) 829-4922, then press 1, 1, and then 3 in the phone menu.

What Is An EIN? The Employer Identification Number ( EIN ) is a unique nine-digit number assigned by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to businesses working in the U.S. It's used to identify taxpayers required to file various business tax returns. Learn more from the IRS . Sole Proprietorship businesses do not require an EIN to submit their application . See How to Apply for an EIN .

What Is a "Business Category"? Your Business Category is based on the services and products you sell. Select the best industry type that fits your service/class offerings. If you are a medical office, use FDA-approved medical devices, perform medical services, or accept FSA and HSA payments, you must select Med Spa , Medical Services and Health Practitioners , or Chiropractors, Chiropractic Offices .

What Does "Monthly Credit Card Processing Volume" Do? Your processing volume determines your merchant size: Small Merchant (less than $4000) or Large Merchant (more than $4000). These two merchant account types have different processing rates and fees. You should review our Credit Card Processing Fees and Rates before continuing.

After you fill out your business information, the system will begin the Plaid verification process. Vagaro has partnered with Plaid to provide your business better security and a more streamlined signup process.

To complete the Plaid Verification Process:

Enter your basic information: Phone number , Full name , Date of birth , Home address , and Social Security Number . Once you've entered your information, applicants signing up on a computer will see a QR code on their screen. Open your phone's camera app, point your camera at the QR code, and select the link that appears to continue the signup process on your device. If you're having trouble, you can Send link to phone . Do NOT close this page as you'll need to come back later. Select a document type and take a picture of the front and back of your ID. Plaid will then begin verifying your documents. Once verified, Plaid will request a Selfie Check . Take a selfie with your device. Plaid will verify your picture.

Log in to your online banking through Plaid. This helps verify and set up your merchant account for bank deposits and prevent failed deposits and application denials. You have two options to connect your bank account:

Login to Bank with Plaid : Search for your bank and log in through Plaid to easily verify your banking details. Manually Enter Bank Details : Enter your banking account and routing information manually.

Unsupported Banks & Platforms The following online-only banks are not supported for deposits with Vagaro. If you add any of the banks below, you will not receive your funds. If you already set up deposits using one of the banks below, you must change the bank in your Merchant Account . Bancorp Bank Cash App Square Chime GO2bank Sutton Bank Stride Bank Lincoln Savings Bank GreenDot
ground_truth: If your bank is not supported for deposits with Vagaro, you need to change the bank in your Merchant Account after setting up deposits with an unsupported bank.

question: How can I import my existing product list into Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Add a Product to Sell In-House | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/16713434779291-Add-a-Product-to-Sell-In-House]
Selling products is a simple way to increase your revenue and upsell customers during their appointments. Vagaro centralizes everything you need to manage your retail items in one place, including inventory tracking, product details, and reporting. You can also enable product tracking to receive helpful alerts, such as low-stock notifications and reminders when it is time to place a purchase order, so your shelves stay stocked.

Vagaro includes a built-in database of popular brands and products. When you scan an item that Vagaro recognizes, its details and images are automatically added to your inventory.

If you already have a product list from another software, you can import it into Vagaro. Simply organize your product list in an Excel file, then email it to support@vagaro.com . If you have no products added to your inventory, Vagaro provides an example Excel file on the Inventory Management screen that can help with the import.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .
ground_truth: You can import your existing product list into Vagaro by organizing it in an Excel file and emailing it to support@vagaro.com. If you have no products added yet, Vagaro also provides an example Excel file on the Inventory Management screen to assist with the import.

question: How can I change the layout of my popup to have the message appear horizontally or vertically?
context:
[Article: MySite: Create Popups and "Subscribe to Mailing List" Forms | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26034492181787-MySite-Create-Popups-and-Subscribe-to-Mailing-List-Forms]
Change the Layout In your Pop-Up settings, next to Layout , you can choose between the Horizontal and Vertical configuration of your message. Changing the layout will change how your popup appears and will affect whether the image appears at the top or side. Vertical Horizontal

Add an Image In your Pop-Up settings, under Image , you can choose one image from your device, your Venue Gallery , or from Unsplash. To choose from thousands of royalty-free images on Unsplash, select Search Online Library . To upload an image from your device, select Add Photo . You can also select from images you've previously uploaded to your MySite. To choose a photo from your Venue Gallery , expand Gallery Images and choose an image.

Change Where and When the Popup Appears You can customize which pages your popup is shown on and how frequently it will appear. To change this setting: Go to your Pop-Up settings. Under Advanced Settings , expand Display . Under Show Pop-Up , choose an option: Any First Page : Display on the first page your customer lands on. Certain Pages : Display on select pages. When you choose Certain Pages , a list will appear for you to select your desired pages . In the Timing list, set the delay for when the popup will appear. In the Frequency list, choose how often this popup will display to visitors. If you only want this popup to display once, select Don't Show After Subscribing . Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .

Change the Styling To change the styling of your popup: Go to your Pop-Up settings. Under Advanced Settings , expand Style , then customize your popup by changing the following settings: Title Text : Choose a preset header styling for the Title . Text Color : Change the color of the Title . Description Text : Choose a preset paragraph styling for the Description . Description Color : Change the color of the Description . Main Button : Choose a preset button styling for the main button. Secondary Button : Choose a preset button styling for the secondary button. Background Color : Change the color of the popup's background. Preview and publish the changes to your MySite: To preview your MySite, go to Publish , then select Preview . To publish your website, go to Publish , then select Publish .
ground_truth: In your Pop-Up settings, next to Layout, you can choose between Horizontal and Vertical configurations, which will change how your message appears and whether the image is at the top or side.

question: Where can employees and contractors find their paystubs and tax forms in Vagaro Payroll?
context:
[Article: Vagaro Payroll FAQ | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/34800129419675-Vagaro-Payroll-FAQ]
log in to it to manage benefits for your employees . Otherwise, if you migrated from another payroll software or created a new payroll, contact our support team to help you set up a Gusto account to manage their benefits.

How do I switch my payroll system from Gusto to Vagaro Payroll? Switching your payroll system from Gusto to Vagaro Payroll only requires you to migrate your Gusto account . Your subscription for this feature will not begin until you complete your migration to Vagaro Payroll.

How soon will I be able to run payroll after switching to Vagaro Payroll? You can run your first payroll immediately after migrating to Vagaro Payroll; it may take up to 10 minutes depending on how many employees and contractors you have.

If a customer is refunded for a service, class, or product, is the commission automatically removed? Yes, depending on how the service , class , or product and its deposit were paid, the commission will be removed from the same or future pay period.

If I switch to Vagaro Payroll, will I still be billed by Gusto? After you switch to Vagaro Payroll, you will be billed at your normal billing cycle by Vagaro. If you are billed by Gusto after migrating to Vagaro Payroll, contact Vagaro Support .

Should I run payroll in Vagaro or Gusto after the migration? After your migration from Gusto to Vagaro Payroll is complete, you must perform all of your payroll actions in Vagaro Payroll, managing PTO policies , requesting and approving PTO , as well as running and submitting payroll .

What if I'm currently using the Gusto integration? If you're currently using the Gusto integration, it will be automatically disabled after activating Vagaro Payroll, and you will be billed for Vagaro Payroll instead.

What if I set up Vagaro Payroll without connecting my Gusto account? If you set up Vagaro Payroll without connecting your Gusto account, just contact Vagaro support . We will reset the onboarding process so you can migrate your existing Gusto account .

Where do employees and contractors get their paystubs, tax forms, and other documents? If a business uses Vagaro Payroll, employees and contractors can download their paystubs, tax forms, and other documents in their Employee Profile on the Payroll tab under the Documents heading.

Why does Vagaro Payroll need me to upload my employees' previous paystubs? You only need to upload your employees' previous paystubs if you're switching from a payroll system other than Gusto or creating a new payroll in Vagaro Payroll. Your employees' paystubs are needed to prevent duplicate tax forms from your previous payroll software and from the IRS. For example, if you switched to Vagaro Payroll in February, you need to upload paystubs starting from January until the date you first run Vagaro Payroll.

Will switching to Vagaro Payroll cancel my Gusto account? Switching to Vagaro Payroll will not cancel your Gusto account. It will remain active and is still required to use Vagaro Payroll.

Is a business required to upload previous paystubs? No, you are not required to upload previous pay stubs.

My employee has not received their pay. What do I do? There may be a delay. Please wait until 5 PM PST for the funds to be deposited. If they haven't received funds after 5 PM PST, please contact Vagaro support.

Can I cancel a submitted payroll? We can no longer cancel payroll if it's after 4:00 pm PST.

Can I run payroll for a pay period in the past? Yes, you can run an off-cycle payroll that allows you to pay out your employees for a previous pay period. We do not recommend running a regular payroll for a missed payroll.
ground_truth: Employees and contractors can download their paystubs, tax forms, and other documents in their Employee Profile on the Payroll tab under the Documents heading.

question: What happens during the first sync of a flagship's settings to other locations?
context:
[Article: Multi-Location Flagship Syncing | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/19644014118939-Multi-Location-Flagship-Syncing]
The first time the flagship syncs its settings, it will override the same settings at all other locations—regardless of whether those settings are locked or unlocked.

The matrix below shows what happens when a flagship syncs its service menu for the first time. Let's say that a flagship wants to sync a service, Women's Haircut, over to another business.

Field From Flagship to be Synced Is The Service on Both Businesses' Menu? Is the Field Locked? Will it Sync and Overwrite? Service Name Yes Yes Yes Cleanup Time No No Yes Service Description No Yes Yes House Call Yes No Yes

Note The system will sync over a new service if the Service Name and Category don't match any services at the synced business. We recommend that both businesses use Vagaro Services for optimized syncing. If you've previously synced your settings from a flagship and turned off Sync Settings , your next sync will be treated as a first-time sync.

After the flagship syncs its service menu for the first time, the logic changes: unlocked fields can be overwritten if they match the flagship business.

Let's say that a flagship synced a service, Women's Haircut, with a business in the last sync cycle.

The flagship has the following settings: Brief Description of the service. Cleanup Time is set to 15 minutes. House Call is enabled. The location has the following settings: Service Description is synced with the flagship and uneditable. Cleanup Time is set to 15 minutes. House Call is disabled.

The matrix below shows what will happen if the flagship modifies these fields before the next sync cycle.

Field From Flagship to be Synced Did the Flagship Modify the Field? Is the Field Locked? Did Field Match the Flagship During the Last Sync? Will it Sync and Overwrite? Service Name Yes Yes Yes Yes Cleanup Time Yes No Yes Yes Service Description No Yes Yes N/A (No Change) House Call Yes No No No
ground_truth: During the first sync, the flagship's settings override the same settings at all other locations, regardless of whether those settings are locked or unlocked.

question: How do I add selectable options to the Choose One element in my form?
context:
[Article: Clear Cache and History on a Web Browser | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204346890-Clear-Cache-and-History-on-a-Web-Browser]
To clear the cache and history on a web browser if the device is running slower than usual, select one of the links below: Microsoft Edge Firefox Google Chrome Safari (Mac Only) Microsoft Edge Launch the Microsoft Edge browser. Click the action menu (3 horizontal dots) and then scroll down and select Settings . Select Privacy, search, and services on the sidebar menu. Then, under Clear browsing data , click Choose what to clear . Select the checkboxes next to each item you want to clear, including Browsing history , Download history , Cookies and other site data , and Cached images and files . Select a time range: last hour, last 24 hours, last 7 days, last 4 weeks, or all time. Click Clear now to finish. Close all browser windows and restart Microsoft Edge . Firefox Launch the Firefox browser. Click and select Settings or Options (depending on Firefox version.) Click Privacy & Security . Go to the History section on the page. Then click Clear History . Click the drop-down next to Time Range to clear. Select Everything . Select Browsing & Download History , Form & Search History , Cookies, and Cache. Then click Clear Now . Close all Firefox windows and restart. Google Chrome Launch the Google Chrome browser. Click the menu. Hover over More Tools . Then select Clear browsing data . Select the Advanced tab. Select the Time range from the drop-down list. For example, if you select All time , it will clear all your past browsing data. Select the items you want to remove. Then click CLEAR DATA . Close all browser windows and restart Google Chrome . Safari (Mac Only) Launch the Safari browser. Click Safari at the top of the screen. Then select Clear History . Select a time range. For example, if you select all history , it will clear all your past browsing data. Click Clear History . Close the browser once you're finished clearing the history. Then launch a new Safari browser window.

[Article: Add a Policy for Unlimited Time Off | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/24718143049243-Add-a-Policy-for-Unlimited-Time-Off]
A policy for unlimited time off allows employees to request as much time off as they want, whether for sick leave or paid time off. Employees added to unlimited time off policies will always show that they have 0 hours when viewing the list of employees for the policy, even if the Payroll tab in their employee profile shows that they have accrued hours. After a time off policy have been created, employees can submit time off requests , and you will be able to approve or deny them . Prerequisites: This feature requires Vagaro Payroll and is available only to businesses in the US. To add a policy for unlimited time off: Go to Time Off Policy : Phone : Select More → Settings → Time Off Policy . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Time Off Policy . Go to Time Off Policy . On a Phone : Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Settings → Employees → Time Off Policy . On the Web, Tablet, or Pay Desk : Go to Settings → Employees → Time Off Policy . Select + Add Time Off Policy . Under the Policy Details heading, provide the following information: Name of Policy : The name of the time off policy or a brief description of it. Type of Time Off Policy : Whether the time off policy is for employees to request Sick Leave to address their health or Paid Time Off (for example, a day off or vacation). Note If an employee is terminated, any unused sick time will not be paid out to employees in their final paycheck. Is there a limit to how much time employees can take off : Select No, It’s Unlimited . Select Continue . Add employees to this time off policy. Select + Add Employees . In the Add Employees To Payroll popup, select the employees to add to payroll. Select Continue . Enter the time off Balance in hours that employees should currently have. Select Finish .

Request your customers and employees to provide their contact information when filling out your form or SOAP note using the Contact module. The contact element can be used to update information in their customer profile or create a new one. Note The Contact module cannot be added to mandatory forms. Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To add the Contact module to your form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . question: How can I deactivate a package for all customers in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Star WIRELESS Printer Setup - Windows Computers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000258854-Star-WIRELESS-Printer-Setup-Windows-Computers]
This article will give you info on how to install, connect, and troubleshoot a wireless printer when using a windows computer.

Helpful Links

For IOS Mobile Devices For Android Devices For Mac Desktop/Laptop Computers

Article Content

Physical Setup Installing the Drivers Connect Through WPS Connect Manually Perform Master Reset Configure the Utility Test Print

Turn on the main power unit. Connect the included power cable into the inlet on the back side of the TSP100iii W. Connect the plug of the power cable into the outlet. On the left side of the device, flip the switch to On and check that the blue LED light is lit up. Next, insert a roll of paper. Check that the power is on. Push the lever on the top to open the cover. Note When using a paper roll with a 58mm width, install the supplied paper guide inside the printer. Insert the paper roll with the paper feeding from underneath (not over the top of the roll.) See the illustration below: Pull the end of the paper out straight and over the front of the printer and push down on both sides of the printer cover to close.

An installed driver is required for both the WPS and Manual connection. Once installed, follow the steps that work best for your internet setup.

Select the link below to download the drivers: https://vsupport.azureedge.net/forums/tsp100_v750_lite.zip Select the following zip file: tsp100_v750_Lite.zip Double-select the zipped file to open it. Double-select Autorun.exe . Select Extract all . Select Extract . Double-select Autorun.exe (the file with the CD icon.) Select Installation . Select I Accept . Select Next . Select Next . Select Next . Select Install . Select Finish .

Now that you have the printer physically set up, connect it to your network. On the back of the printer and under the wire cover, press and hold the PAIR button until the blue LED light starts flashing. Press the WPS button on your router. When the printer connects to the network, it will print the connection information, and the blue LED light will change from flashing to solid.

Note When a connection cannot be made, start from step 3 and perform the settings again.

When your device is connected through WPS, go to Configure the Utility .

Note If WPS failed, go through these steps

First, make sure the printer is off. Then perform a self-print to get the MAC address of the printer. To perform a Self-Print, press and hold the FEED button ( 1 ). Do not let go, but then turn the power on ( 2 ). Take your finger off the FEED button when self-printing has begun. On this printout, you can check the MAC address from Network Configuration . Select the network icon in the taskbar, and from the connectable devices list, refer to the last 6 digits of the MAC address. Select the matching devices, then select Connect . No Internet, open will appear under the selected device, and the connection will be established. Next, log into the Web Configuration. Open a web browser and enter the IP address that appears under Current IP Parameters Status from your test print in the address field. In this example, the IP address is 192.168.10.1 . Select Login . Note If you don't already have a login, the default login name and password are as follows: Login name : root / Password : public. You will be prompted to change your password after logging in. DO NOT CHANGE YOUR PASSWORD . After login, select WLAN Settings . Select Infrastructure Mode , enter your internet's SSID and Security Key and select Submit to connect the printer to your network. Make sure to set Security to WPA2/AES . Select Submit . Select Save . Select Execute . Your printer will restart and will stay connected to your network.

If this is successful, follow the steps below.

In certain cases, you may be required to perform a master reset.

Hold down the RST button on the back of the printer while turning the printer on.

Note You should hear an initialization sound from the printer, indicating the printer has reset. The printer should then flash and be ready to print.

question: How do I create a new partial-use product in Vagaro using a GS1 QR code on a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Star WIRELESS Printer Setup - iOS Devices | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000238613-Star-WIRELESS-Printer-Setup-iOS-Devices]
This article explains how to install, connect, and troubleshoot the Star w printer with an iOS device.

For setup of the Star wireless printer on other devices, refer to these articles:

For Android Mobile Devices Mac Desktop/Laptop Computers Windows Desktop/Laptop Computers

Note Due to the limitations of third-party libraries on Amazon devices, Star receipt printers are not supported on Amazon Fire Tablets and Kindles.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

To set up and plug in your receipt printer:

Connect the included power cable into the inlet on the back side of the TSP100iii W, then plug the power cable into the outlet. On the left-hand side of the device, flip the switch to On and check that the blue LED light is lit up. Next, you should insert a roll of paper. Check that the power is on. Push the lever on the top to open the cover. Note When using a paper roll with a 58mm width, install the supplied paper guide inside the printer. Insert the paper roll with the paper feeding from underneath (not over the top of the roll.) See the illustration below: Pull the end of the paper out straight and over the front of the printer and push down on both sides of the printer cover to close.

To connect your receipt printer to your Wi-Fi network:

On the back of the printer, under the wire cover, press and hold the PAIR button until the blue LED light starts flashing. Press the WPS button on your router. When the printer connects to the network, it will print the connection information, and the blue LED light will change from flashing to solid.

Note When a connection cannot be made, follow the steps below.

Star wireless receipt printers are no longer available from the Vagaro Store.

Ensure the printer is OFF . Press the FEED button while switching the printer ON to perform a self-print. Release the FEED button when self-printing has begun. On the printout, look for the MAC address from "Network Configuration." On your iOS device, go to Settings → Wi-Fi and make sure that Wi-Fi is toggled ON . In the device list at the bottom, take note of the last 6 digits of the MAC address, then select the device to connect. A message appears indicating that the connection to the Internet fails. Select Join Anyway . Note When connecting with ad-hoc mode, Internet access is not available on that device. The SSID will appear under Wi-Fi. Open your device's web browser, such as Safari, and enter 192.168.10.1 in the address field. (If the IP address of the printer was changed, enter the IP address that appears under "Current IP Parameters Status" on your test print). On the upper left-hand side of the screen, select the More (3 lines) side menu, then select Login . Log in with the credentials below. Name : root / Password : public The following message appears when you are using the default password. DO NOT CHANGE THE PASSWORD . Select Cancel to keep the login information the same. You will be prompted to change your password after logging in, DO NOT CHANGE YOUR PASSWORD . After login, select WLAN Settings from the left-hand side. Select Infrastructure Mode , enter your internet's SSID and Security Key , then select Submit to connect the printer to your network. Make sure to set Security to WPA2/AES . When you select Submit , you will be prompted to select Save . Do not select Save at this step. Select IP Parameters . Select Static . Enter the IP Address , Subnet Mask , and Default Gateway . The IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway that are shown in this image are an example. The local IP address is the one your router assigns to your device and uses to communicate with your network. To access the local IP address on your iOS device, navigate to Settings → Wi-Fi and select the information icon next to the three wireless bars icon. Scroll down and locate IP Address , Subnet Mask , and Default Gateway . Select Submit . Select Save. Select Execute . Your printer will restart and will stay connected to your network.

In some instances, you may be required to perform a master reset.

Hold the RST button on the back of the printer while turning the printer on.

Note You should hear an initialization sound from the printer, indicating the printer has reset. The printer should then flash and be ready to print.

Test your setup by printing a receipt. Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Reports . Select Transaction List . Run a report, then select a transaction. Select Receipt . Confirm and select Print . If the printer is connected correctly, it will print out the receipt. If not, refer to the section that details connecting the device to the WiFi. Example of a test print:

question: Why should I avoid changing the description of a Vagaro Class?
context:
[Article: Send Notifications and Reminders to Your Customers | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000439594-Send-Notifications-and-Reminders-to-Your-Customers]
Vagaro can automatically send appointment notifications and reminders to your customers and clients one time via email, text, and push. These messages are used to give the details of an appointment, confirm the booking, and remind customers before their appointment, depending on your business’s and clients’ settings. Resending future appointment reminders or notifications needs an Email Marketing plan , Text Marketing plan , or Connect by Vagaro to send a direct message. Tip If customers aren’t getting their notifications, you can help them with their notification settings in their Vagaro customer account. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . To select the notifications and reminders your business sends to your clients: Go to Email & Text Notifications . On a Phone : Go to More → Settings → Booking → Email & Text Notifications . On the Web, Tablet, or Pay Desk : Go to Settings → Booking → Email & Text Notifications . Configure the notifications and reminders you want your customers to receive. Appointment Detail : Whether to send a notification containing the details of your customer's appointment. If the customer booked the appointment online, they will only see a confirmation with the appointment details on the screen and a push notification; no text message will be sent; however, if the customer booked the appointment online for someone on their Family & Friends list , the family or friend will receive both a push notification and a text message at the number provided for the booking. A notification will also be sent if you add a service at checkout. Important Customers who booked an appointment, class, or workshop on your Vagaro Listing Page will not receive a text Appointment Detail notiification via text; they will only see a confirmation message on the screen that their appointment was booked. They will receive all other reminders, if you have them enabled. Confirmation Request : Whether to send a request notification to customers to confirm their upcoming booking, and how far in advance to send the request. If the customer confirms, the appointment's status will automatically be updated on the calendar as Confirmed . Appointment Reminder : Whether to remind customers of their upcoming appointment, and how far in advance the reminder is to be sent. No action is required from the customer. HIPAA Compliant Notifications : Whether to hide appointment details in your notifications and reminder messages. Vagaro has worked with the Compliancy Group to comply with HIPAA Privacy Rules in your text messages, push notifications, and emails to your clients. Send Welcome Email to New Customers : Whether to send customers a welcome email containing instructions about creating their Vagaro customer profile with you and booking appointments. They will receive this email when you add their customer profile in-house. If they weren't already your customer, they will receive this email after they book their first appointment with you online. Send Receipts to Customers for Membership/Package Auto-Renewal : Whether to automatically send customers a receipt when their memberships or packages are automatically renewed. Select Save .
