question: Who is authorized to change an employee's security question and password in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Control Employee Access to Connect by Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26294654932763-Control-Employee-Access-to-Connect-by-Vagaro]
Connect by Vagaro is available for all businesses, booth renters, and employees using Vagaro. This means that your employees or booth renters can use your account to send and reply to customer messages, which may incur charges for the business account. If you want to restrict or block your employees' ability to send messages and reply to customers, you'll need to update your employees' Access Levels to restrict access to Connect by Vagaro. Note Restricting employee access to Connect by Vagaro will only prevent employees from viewing and/or replying to customer messages. Even if they've been restricted, employees can still chat with other employees at your business through Connect by Vagaro. Messaging other team members does not incur any charges. Prerequisites : You must assign Access Levels to an employee before the restrictions apply. To restrict access to Connect by Vagaro: Go to Access Levels : Phone : Select More → Settings → Access Levels . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Access Levels . In the list of Access Levels, select the Action button for the access level to be modified, and then select Edit . Scroll down to Vagaro Connect (Customers) , and choose a configuration: Select View and Modify to allow employees to send, view, and reply to customer messages. Only select View to restrict employees from replying to customer messages. Deselect both options to block employees with this access from viewing and replying to customer messages. Select Save . Repeat the steps above for any other Access Levels.

[Article: Add a Service or Service Bundle to Checkout | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/22545023096219-Add-a-Service-or-Service-Bundle-to-Checkout]
A service is an item or work an employee provides for a single client, such as a haircut, a shave, or a massage. You can also group your services together, like a couple's massage, to create a service bundle . You or the customer can then schedule and pay for services and bundles on your Vagaro Listing Page , custom website , Calendar , or at Checkout. Important Adding a service or service bundle at checkout books the appointment on your Calendar, even if you haven't checked out yet. If your appointment notifications are turned on , your customer will also receive a booking notification. Prerequisites : Create at least one product in Inventory Management . Add a Service or Service Bundle to Checkout On a Phone To sell a service or service bundle at checkout. Go to the Checkout screen. Select Shopping Cart . Select Add Item , then Service . Fill out the required information: Service : The service you're checking out. If you're checking out a service bundle, you'll have multiple services selected. Add-Ons (Optional): Any add-ons the customer wants to add. Service Provider : The employee performing the service. Appointment Type (Optional): Set the appointment type for customer retention . Select Add , then select Done to return to the Checkout screen. Enter the customer's payment, then select Checkout . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To sell a service or service bundle at checkout: Go to the Checkout screen. At the bottom of the screen, select Service . Under Service , open the menu and select the service to be checked out. Fill out the required information: Service Provider : The service you're checking out. If you're checking out a service bundle, you'll have multiple services selected. Start Time : The time the service will be performed. Add-Ons (Optional): Any add-ons the customer wants to add. Select Appointment (Optional): Set the appointment type for customer retention . Select Add Service . Enter the customer's payment, then select Checkout . Remove an Item from Checkout and Cancel the Booking If you add the wrong service at checkout or your customer changes their mind, delete the appointment to avoid confusion. You can remove it from the Checkout screen or the Calendar . The steps below show how to remove it from the Checkout screen. On a Phone To remove an item from checkout: From the Checkout screen, select Shopping Cart . Swipe right on the service, then select the X button. Select Delete from checkout and calendar , then select Confirm . Note Removing this service also removes any associated addons. If you scheduled a service bundle, you'll need to repeat these steps for any other services added. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To remove an item from checkout: From the Checkout screen, for the service added to the customer's cart, select Action → Delete . In the popup, select Delete from checkout and calendar , then select Confirm . Note Removing this service also removes any associated addons. If you scheduled a service bundle, you'll need to repeat these steps for any other services added.

context:
[Article: Set Up Same-Day and Instant Payouts | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/36228232178203-Set-Up-Same-Day-and-Instant-Payouts]
Schedule same-day deposits to get your money the same day. Any days you don't select for a same-day deposit will follow the midnight batch schedule . The fee for same-day deposits is 1.75% per deposit, and is limited to $2,500 per day , with a minimum limit of $25 . Any remaining funds are grouped with the next batch time following the midnight batch schedule. You won’t be able to perform any additional instant payouts after your same-day deposit is batched.

To schedule a same-day deposit:

Go to your Merchant Account settings: Vagaro Pro App : Select More → Credit Card Processing . Computer : In the top-right corner, select your profile icon, then select Merchant Accounts . Optionally, if the business has multiple merchant accounts, you can change the selected account in the Merchant Account list. Scroll down to Deposit Settings and select the Same-Day Deposit option. Toggle the Day of the Week and set the Close of Day (time to receive funds). The Transfer Speed will appear on the deposit day and time. Tip To ensure you receive all the funds from your entire workday, set a two-hour buffer after your last checkout time. This gives the system enough time to capture and include all transactions in your payout. Select Save .

It will take one business day for the new deposit schedule to go into effect.

[Article: Calendar Icons and Appointment Status Colors Legend | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204347590-Calendar-Icons-and-Appointment-Status-Colors-Legend]
only used to track customer retention .

Appointment Status Legend

The color of the timeslots on your calendar indicates the current status of an appointment.

Requested (purple) If your business requires customers' online bookings to be approved , it stays in the requested status until it is approved or denied. Accepted (light blue) A requested appointment has been approved by the business and added to the calendar. If Require Acceptance for Online Booking setting in the Online Appointment Rules screen is off for this type of appointment, it will be automatically accepted without approval from the business. Awaiting Confirmation (yellow) Based on your business's customer notification settings , your clients will receive an appointment confirmation message within a certain number of hours or days before their appointment. This status shows that the confirmation request has been sent to the client and is awaiting their response. Confirmed (pink) The business has received confirmation , either verbally or a response through a notification , that the client will be there for their appointment. Show (light green) Indicates that a client arrived for their appointment. No Show (red) The business manually selects this status on the calendar screen to indicate that the customer did not show up for an appointment. You can also run the Cancellations & No-Shows report to see which customers missed their appointments and how often. Ready to Start (blue) Clients who used the Contactless Check-In feature have been notified that the business is ready for them and that they may enter the business for their appointment In Progress (green) The appointment has already begun. Complete (grey) Once the appointment is completed, use this status to show that the service has been performed and the appointment has been completed. Personal Task (beige) A Personal Task that an employee has scheduled but customers are still able to book appointments during those timeslots. Personal Task Blocked (tan) A Personal Task that an employee has booked that does not allow customers to book appointments during those timeslots.

context:
[Article: Star WIRELESS Printer Setup - iOS Devices | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000238613-Star-WIRELESS-Printer-Setup-iOS-Devices]
This article explains how to install, connect, and troubleshoot the Star w printer with an iOS device.

For setup of the Star wireless printer on other devices, refer to these articles:

For Android Mobile Devices Mac Desktop/Laptop Computers Windows Desktop/Laptop Computers

Note Due to the limitations of third-party libraries on Amazon devices, Star receipt printers are not supported on Amazon Fire Tablets and Kindles.

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses .

To set up and plug in your receipt printer:

Connect the included power cable into the inlet on the back side of the TSP100iii W, then plug the power cable into the outlet. On the left-hand side of the device, flip the switch to On and check that the blue LED light is lit up. Next, you should insert a roll of paper. Check that the power is on. Push the lever on the top to open the cover. Note When using a paper roll with a 58mm width, install the supplied paper guide inside the printer. Insert the paper roll with the paper feeding from underneath (not over the top of the roll.) See the illustration below: Pull the end of the paper out straight and over the front of the printer and push down on both sides of the printer cover to close.

To connect your receipt printer to your Wi-Fi network:

On the back of the printer, under the wire cover, press and hold the PAIR button until the blue LED light starts flashing. Press the WPS button on your router. When the printer connects to the network, it will print the connection information, and the blue LED light will change from flashing to solid.

Note When a connection cannot be made, follow the steps below.

Star wireless receipt printers are no longer available from the Vagaro Store.

Ensure the printer is OFF . Press the FEED button while switching the printer ON to perform a self-print. Release the FEED button when self-printing has begun. On the printout, look for the MAC address from "Network Configuration." On your iOS device, go to Settings → Wi-Fi and make sure that Wi-Fi is toggled ON . In the device list at the bottom, take note of the last 6 digits of the MAC address, then select the device to connect. A message appears indicating that the connection to the Internet fails. Select Join Anyway . Note When connecting with ad-hoc mode, Internet access is not available on that device. The SSID will appear under Wi-Fi. Open your device's web browser, such as Safari, and enter 192.168.10.1 in the address field. (If the IP address of the printer was changed, enter the IP address that appears under "Current IP Parameters Status" on your test print). On the upper left-hand side of the screen, select the More (3 lines) side menu, then select Login . Log in with the credentials below. Name : root / Password : public The following message appears when you are using the default password. DO NOT CHANGE THE PASSWORD . Select Cancel to keep the login information the same. You will be prompted to change your password after logging in, DO NOT CHANGE YOUR PASSWORD . After login, select WLAN Settings from the left-hand side. Select Infrastructure Mode , enter your internet's SSID and Security Key , then select Submit to connect the printer to your network. Make sure to set Security to WPA2/AES . When you select Submit , you will be prompted to select Save . Do not select Save at this step. Select IP Parameters . Select Static . Enter the IP Address , Subnet Mask , and Default Gateway . The IP Address, Subnet Mask, and Default Gateway that are shown in this image are an example. The local IP address is the one your router assigns to your device and uses to communicate with your network. To access the local IP address on your iOS device, navigate to Settings → Wi-Fi and select the information icon next to the three wireless bars icon. Scroll down and locate IP Address , Subnet Mask , and Default Gateway . Select Submit . Select Save. Select Execute . Your printer will restart and will stay connected to your network.

In some instances, you may be required to perform a master reset.

Hold the RST button on the back of the printer while turning the printer on.

Note You should hear an initialization sound from the printer, indicating the printer has reset. The printer should then flash and be ready to print.

Test your setup by printing a receipt. Open the Vagaro Pro App, then go to More → Reports . Select Transaction List . Run a report, then select a transaction. Select Receipt . Confirm and select Print . If the printer is connected correctly, it will print out the receipt. If not, refer to the section that details connecting the device to the WiFi. Example of a test print:

[Article: Close Out Your Payroll | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/33781998576027-Close-Out-Your-Payroll]
The Payroll report is used to automatically calculate and show businesses how much to pay their employees based on hourly pay , sales and commissions, and rent collected . If you have employees who work at multiple locations , you can run a single report instead of in batches or separately by location. After you've run the report, you can close out the payroll and mark it as Paid. All of your current and past payrolls can be viewed by running the Payroll History report .

Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses. In the US, Vagaro Payroll is recommended if you want to pay your employees by direct deposit and withhold their income taxes.

If you don't have Vagaro Payroll, you can mark Payroll as Paid. However, this action finalizes payroll for reporting purposes only, and you must pay your employees manually

Vagaro Pro App Select More → Reports → Payroll . Select the icon in the top-right corner, select the Payroll Closing Date using the icons, and then select the Service Provider(s) you want to review. If you do not select a Service Provider , all employees will appear. Note Expand advanced filters to Include Past Employees and run payroll for multiple locations. See here to learn more about multi-location payroll. Select the Sort By field to sort the report by Business Costs , Hourly Pay , Commission , and more. Select Run Report . The Payroll Due row, located at the bottom of an employee's card, shows the total amount owed to the employee. The Payroll Due total does not include income tax deductions . Once you have paid all your employees the Payroll Due , select the icon again, and then select Payroll Paid . This will save the Payroll Period in the Payroll History report. Note Once saved, you cannot close out the payroll period again without deleting the saved report in Payroll History . See this article to learn more.

Web Version Select Reports → Payroll . Select the Payroll Closing Date using the icons, then select the Service Provider(s) you want to review. If you do not select a Service Provider , all employees will appear. Note Expand advanced filters to Include Past Employees and run payroll for multiple locations. See here to learn more about multi-location payroll. Select Run Report . The final column, Payroll Due , shows the amount you owe each employee. The Payroll Due total does not include income tax deductions . Once you have paid all your employees the Payroll Due , select Payroll Paid . This will save the Payroll Period in the Payroll History report. Note Once saved, you cannot close out the payroll period again without deleting the saved report in Payroll History . See this article to learn more.

Ask your customers and employees using the Date and Time element to select or enter a date, time, or date range in response to a question you ask them, up to 200 characters long on your form or SOAP note. For example, customers can provide their availability for an appointment or select the date when they last had a procedure performed. When filling out a form, your customers and employees will be able to select a date and/or time to answer your question. Date Time Date and Time Date Range Prerequisites : This feature is only available on a computer, tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro version on Vagaro. Before continuing, check out the Create a Form Template article to learn more about how to build forms. To add the Date and Time element to your form or SOAP note: Go to Forms . Go to your form or SOAP note: To create a form template , in the top-left corner, select Create New → Form . To create a SOAP note template , select Create New → SOAP Note . To edit an existing form , select Action → Edit . To duplicate a form , select Action → Duplicate . Under Form Elements , drag the Choose One element into the form designer. The element's settings will appear on the right side of the screen. If you already have this element in the form designer, select it to make changes. Enter a Title for this element. Next to Date Options , choose where you'll require your customer to enter a Date , Time , Date and Time , or Date Range . Optionally, to make this element required, turn on Required Field . Publish your form: To save this form as a draft and publish it later, in the top-right corner, select Save as Draft . This form is saved in form management and can be published at a later time. To see a preview of your form and how it'll look for your customers, in the top-right corner, select Preview . You can select the Computer , Tablet , or Phone icon to see how your form will look on each device. To publish your form, go to Settings , then in the top-right corner, select Publish . Your form is saved in form management.

[Article: Automatically Charge a Cancellation or No-Show Fee | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/17733260320539-Automatically-Charge-a-Cancellation-or-No-Show-Fee]
Charging a cancellation fee can help prevent loss of income and discourage customers from cancelling appointments at the last minute or not showing up. You can manually charge these fees or automatically charge them when customers cancel their appointment in the Vagaro customer app or your Vagaro Listing Page , or if you mark them as a no-show . Make sure you add your cancellation policy to your Vagaro Listing Page to protect your business from customer chargebacks when they dispute your cancellation fee. Even so, you can also require a deposit at the time a customer books a service, which are more protected and less likely to be disputed. Note Vagaro only accepts and processes deposits and fees from Vagaro Merchant Services. Before you can charge cancellation and no-show fees, you need to enable Capture Credit Card in your Online Shopping Cart settings to require customers to provide a credit card number when they book a service or class online. Once enabled, you can run the Cancellation & No-Show report to monitor how many appointments your customers have cancelled or missed, and view the fees you've collected by filtering the Transaction List report to show only the Cancellation Fee transaction type. Important Ensure that you have enabled Customers Can Cancel Appointments in your Online Appointment Rules . If customers can't cancel their appointments by themselves, you will have to manually charge your cancellation/no-show fee. Prerequisites: This feature requires Credit Card Processing and the Online Shopping Cart . Set Up Cancellation/No-Show Fees for Services To set up credit card capture to charge cancellation/no-show fees for services : Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Services and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage based on the service price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer appointments : Customers who don't have a customer profile at your business. Old customer appointments : Customers who already have a customer profile at your business. Mobile service appointments : Customers who schedule a mobile service appointment Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. Enter your Required Appointment Cancellation Fee Policy to describe your business's cancellation policy . This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save . Set Up Cancellation/No-Show Fees for Classes and Workshops To set up credit card capture and cancellation/no-show fees for classes : Go to Online Shopping Cart : Phone : Select More → Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Settings → Online Shopping Cart . Scroll down to Require Deposit or Capture Credit Card at the time of Booking Classes and select Capture Credit Card . Provide the following information: For Bookings Greater Than or Equal To : Enter the minimum service price that you want to require a credit card. Cancellation / No-Show Fee : Enter a percentage of the class price or a fixed fee. Cancellation Fee Cut-Off Time : Enter the cut-off time (in hours). Customers who cancel before the set time period (e.g., 24 hours) will not be charged a fee. Require Credit Card for : Select at least one customer type that needs to provide a credit card number: New customer class sign ups : Customers who have never taken the class before. Old customer class sign ups : Customers who have already taken the class. Customer with no-shows or cancellations : Customers who have cancelled or missed an appointment or class at least once at your business. In the Required Class Cancellation Fee Policy box, enter a cancellation policy for your business. This text is also reflected and can be updated in the Facility Information and Online Appointment Rules screens. Select Save .

[Article: Manage IOUs from the IOU Report | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008144194-Manage-IOUs-from-the-IOU-Report]
If there are any Outstanding IOUs issued at your business, you can manage them from the report. Only Outstanding IOUs can be modified. Anything that's been paid off or voided can't be modified.

On a Phone To manage IOUs: Select More → Reports → IOU . Filter and run the report. Select the IOU you want to modify and choose an option: Edit : Change the Comment added to the IOU. Void : Void the IOU and remove the owed balance from their account. When voiding an IOU, you'll need to enter a Void Reason . Pay : Bring the customer's balance to the Checkout screen. This IOU will be marked as Paid in the customer's profile and the IOU report. View Customer Profile : Go to the customer's profile.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To manage IOUs: Select Reports → IOU . Filter and run the report. For the IOU to be modified, open the Action menu, then choose an option: Edit : Change the Comment added to the IOU. Void : Void the IOU and remove the owed balance from their account. When voiding an IOU, you'll need to enter a Void Reason . Pay : Bring the customer's balance to the Checkout screen. This IOU will be marked as Paid in the customer's profile and the IOU report.

context:
[Article: Business Review Standards in Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/205167680-Business-Review-Standards-in-Vagaro]
Hearing what your customers say is a great way to see how your business is performing! When a customer reviews the business or service provider, the business owner receives an email detailing what the customer wrote. The notification is sent to the email address of the Account Owner . No other employee receives it. An email is also sent when a review is changed. Below is an example of an email that the business receives. It includes the name of the customer, the business review, and the employee review. Remember, Vagaro is an official review site for Salons, Spas, and Gyms, as Yelp is for restaurants. The review process must be fair. Businesses cannot decide if a review is removed, so Vagaro evaluates all issues a business has with a particular review. Vagaro only removes reviews for four reasons: The review includes a personal attack against a member of a business. The review contains harsh or inappropriate language. The reviewer was not a customer of the business they posted a review on. The review violates Vagaro’s user agreement https://www.vagaro.com/user-agreement . Vagaro sets these standards so the general public knows they are getting a fair assessment of each business.

[Article: Cancel Classes and Workshops from the Business Calendar | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360009811873-Cancel-Classes-and-Workshops-from-the-Business-Calendar]
You can cancel class sessions directly from the calendar for any reason, such as if your instructor is sick or the class falls on a day your business is closed. If the class session repeats, you can delete all upcoming sessions at once.

Cancelling a class removes it from the calendar, so you may need to refund any attendees who prepaid.

Note You must cancel all workshop sessions at once. If you want to remove just one session, you can delete it instead of cancelling .

Prerequisites : This feature requires a scheduled class on the calendar.

You can cancel class sessions and workshops directly from the calendar for any reason, such as an instructor being unavailable or t.e class falling on a day your business is closed.

On the Vagaro Pro App To cancel a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Change Status → Cancel . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, the class will be cancelled. If the class is part of a repeating series, choose to Cancel This Occurrence or Cancel Series . If the session is part of a workshop, confirm that you want to Cancel All workshop sessions. Individual workshop sessions must be rescheduled. If any attendees have prepaid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose whether to refund them. If you do, see Refund Class Sessions . If the class has attendees, enter an optional message explaining why the class is being cancelled, then select Notify Attendees . Vagaro will automatically send your attendees a text and email notification about the cancellation. The class session is removed from your calendar.

On a Computer To cancel a class: Go to the Calendar screen, then select the calendar you want to view . Find the class session. Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Change Status → Cancel . Complete the optional steps, if applicable. If there are no additional steps, the class will be cancelled. If the class is part of a repeating series, a popup will appear. From there, you can choose to Cancel only this occurrence or Cancel the series . If the session is part of a workshop, confirm that you want to Cancel All workshop sessions. Individual workshop sessions must be rescheduled. If any attendees have prepaid or put down a deposit for the class session, choose whether to refund them. If you do, see Refund Class Sessions . If the class has attendees, choose whether to Notify Attendees , enter an optional message explaining why the class is being cancelled, and then select Cancel Class . Vagaro will automatically send your attendees a text and email notification about the cancellation. The class session is removed from your calendar.

[Article: How Cancellation/No-Show Fees Affect Payroll | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/17748494703131-How-Cancellation-No-Show-Fees-Affect-Payroll]
Employees receive their commission based on the commission rate you've set up for them for the service or class ; however, if a customer is charged a cancellation or no-show fee, your employee will still receive a commission for the cancellation fee. Tip Make sure your business has a clear cancellation policy to protect your business and reduce disputes and chargebacks . To discourage no-shows and cancellations, you can also charge a deposit when customers book in-house or online , or require your customers to sign a form acknowledging that they understand and accept your policy before they book with you. For example, if the cancellation or no-show fee is $25.00 : Cost of Service or Class Normal Commission Cancellation Fee Commission Reason $100 $10 The employee will receive $2.50 . The $10 commission is 10% of the $100 service or class. Vagaro uses the percentage of the cancellation or no-show fee to calculate the new commission. $100 $30 The employee will receive $7.50 . The $30 commission is 30% of the $100 service or class. Vagaro uses the percentage of the cancellation or no-show fee to calculate the new commission. $100 50% The employee will receive $12.50 . $12.50 is 50% of the $25.00 cancellation fee or no-show fee.

context:
[Article: Add a Service Category or Class Category | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977260870683-Add-a-Service-Category-or-Class-Category]
Grouping your services and classes into categories makes them easier to find and organize. Categories can also be color-coded so they can be quickly identified at a glance on the calendar. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . Add a Service Category On a Phone To add a service category to your service/class menu : Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Services to view your service menu. Important If you are experiencing problems with two-factor authentication on the web, reload the page and try again. On the Vagaro app, make sure you have updated the app to the latest version. If you still can't log in to Vagaro, contact Vagaro Support . Here's an example of an authentication code email message: Enter that code in the verify screen. If you enter the correctly, the Account Verified confirmation screen appears. Select Done to start using Vagaro. Each time you log in to a new device you will receive an email notification that a new or unrecognized device has logged into your account.

question: How can I view my Vagaro listing page on a mobile device?
context:
[Article: Add and Update Working Hours | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010304773-Add-and-Update-Working-Hours]
Working hours decide whether a customer can or can't book with you or your employees at a specific time. Customize work hours, set special service times, and make quick changes directly from the calendar. Keep your bookings organized and make sure clients know when you're available.

Prerequisites: Ensure you've created at least one employee with a bookable calendar .

From the Business Profile , the business owner can set the overall working hours for the business. These hours can either be based on the earliest start and latest end times across all employees or be entered manually. The business hours will appear on your Vagaro Listing Page so your customers know when they can book with you.

Note If the option to change business hours does not appear in the Business Profile, it means there is only one employee with a calendar at your business. In this case, the business hours are automatically set to that employee’s working hours. To update the hours, go to the owner’s or employee’s profile to change business hours.

On a Phone To set your business hours: Select More → Settings → Business Profile . Scroll down to Business Hours , and choose to toggle Use Employee Working Hours on or off. If you toggle this setting on, the system will update the business's days open, opening and closing hours based on all your employees' working hours. If you toggle this setting off, a table will appear below where you can configure the Day of the week the business is open, then enter your Open at and Closes at times. Select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To set your business hours: Select Settings → Business Profile . Scroll down to Business Hours , and choose to toggle Use Employee Working Hours on or off. If you toggle this setting on, the system will update the business's days open, opening and closing hours based on all your employees' working hours. If you toggle this setting off, a table will appear below where you can configure the Day of the week the business is open, then enter your Open at and Closes at times. Select Save .

From an employee's profile, modify their working hours to control when they can or can't be booked. If you are the sole employee of the business, the working hours you set for yourself will update your business hours.

On a Phone To add employee working hours: Select More → Settings → Employee Profiles . Select Employees to view all profiles. Select the employee's profile. In the employee's profile, select the Action menu for the location you want to change, then select Hours . Note If the employee works for multiple locations , select the Action menu for one of the locations. Modify your working hours: Toggle the days of the week the employee works. Select the day you want to modify, then select Edit . Modify the From and To to set your hours for that day. Modify the Services Available to customize what services are offered during this timeframe. You can also chunk out the day by selecting Add Time Range if a specific service is only performed at certain times. Select Update . Make adjustments to the other days of the week, or you can select the day you modified, then Copy Hours and Services To the rest of the week. Select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add employee working hours: Select Settings → Employee Profiles . At the end of the row of the employee's profile, select the Action menu, then select Hours . Note If the employee works for multiple locations , select the number of Locations , then for the location you want to change, select the Action menu, and then Hours . Toggle the Day of the week that the employee works, and then enter the Start and End times. You can also chunk out the day if a specific service is only performed at certain times. Tip Copy the working hours to all other days of the week by hovering over the day and then selecting Copy to All . Select Save .

context:
[Article: Vagaro-to-Vagaro Import: Transfer Your Data From a Previous Employer on Vagaro | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/26149979762587-Vagaro-to-Vagaro-Import-Transfer-Your-Data-From-a-Previous-Employer-on-Vagaro]
If you've started working at another business on Vagaro, you can transfer your data from that business over to your new employer from the Import Management screen. All you need to do is log in to your account with your new employer and request the transfer. Prerequisites: The employee must have an employee profile at both businesses. This type of transfer request can only be initiated by the person who wants to transfer their information over. Step One: Request to Transfer Data From Previous Employer The employee must first request that their previous employer transfer their data. On a Phone To request an employee data transfer: Select More → Settings → Import Management . Under Transfer Your Information from (Business Name) , select Request Transfer . Note This option only appears if you have an employee profile with the same email address at another Vagaro business. If you work at multiple Vagaro businesses, you’ll see separate transfer options for each one. In Confirm Transfer Request , select whether to transfer your reviews, menu, contact information, notes, files ( Vagaro Drive ), and so on. Note Selecting options like Future Appointments may require you to transfer additional information. Select the Transfer Date for when the data will be transferred. Select Request Transfer . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To request an employee data transfer: Select Settings → Import Management . Under Transfer Your Information from (Business Name) , select Request Transfer . Note This option only appears if you have an employee profile with the same email address at another Vagaro business. If you work at multiple Vagaro businesses, you’ll see separate transfer options for each one. In Confirm Transfer Request , select whether to transfer your reviews, menu, contact information, notes, files ( Vagaro Drive ), and so on. Note Selecting options like Future Appointments may require you to transfer additional information. Select the Transfer Date for when the data will be transferred. Select Request Transfer . Step Two: Approve Transfer Request The account owner at the receiving business must approve the transfer request before any data is moved. Only the business owner can review the items approved for transfer and provide final consent. If additional data is required, they must contact the originating business to request it. When the transfer is complete, the transferring business owner should delete the employee account from their business. To approve a data transfer: In the Notification Panel , look for the Employee Requested to Transfer Information notification. If you find it, select View . In Approve Transfer , review what's being transferred over, agree to the terms, and then select Approve Transfer . The employee’s data has been successfully transferred. If appointment data was included, they'll need to complete server mapping. Optional: Map Services After the transfer is approved, either the employee or the new business owner maps the employee’s services to match those from the original business. Note This only applies if the service and appointment data were transferred over. To map the imported services or classes: In the Notification Panel , look for the Employee Approved Transfer notification. If you find it, select Complete Service Mapping . In Service Mapping , match each of the transferred services or classes with one of the services or classes at your business. You'll also have the option to create a new service if needed. Select Import . The employee’s data has been successfully transferred.

question: How can I remove an attendee from a class session using the Vagaro Pro App?
context:
[Article: Check In, Check Out, or No-Show Class Attendees | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022751974-Check-In-Check-Out-or-No-Show-Class-Attendees]
Sometimes, customers don't show up to their class sessions. If you want to discourage that behavior, mark your customers as a no-show and charge a no-show fee to their credit card.

You can mark attendees as no-shows with the Group Check-In feature. This allows you to mark multiple attendees as a no-show simultaneously and give them all a message. However, you cannot charge a No-Show fee with this method. You'll need to go to the attendee list and individually mark each missing attendee as No-Show.

If multiple attendees don't show up to their appointment, you can mark them all as No-Show from group check-in.

Note These steps cover how to mark multiple attendees as no-shows without charging a no-show fee.

On a Phone To mark an attendee as a no-show: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the Calendar, then select Check-In . A list of all attendees for this class will appear. Select No-Show for each attendee who didn't arrive for the class. Select Apply to mark the attendees as no-show. The No-Show Enrollment popup will appear. Enter an optional message to all attendees that no-showed. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. If you don't want to send a message, unselect Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment . If any attendees haven't paid for their class yet, one customer will be randomly selected and added to the Checkout screen. You'll need to check out each attendee individually if multiple attendees haven't paid for their class yet.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To mark an attendee as a no-show: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the Calendar, then select Check-In . A list of all attendees for this class will appear. Select No-Show for each attendee who didn't arrive for the class. Select Apply to mark the attendees as no-show. The No-Show Enrollment popup will appear. Enter an optional message to all attendees that no-showed. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. If you don't want to send a message, unselect Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment . If any attendees haven't paid for their class yet, one customer will be randomly selected and added to the Checkout screen. You'll need to check out each attendee individually if multiple attendees haven't paid for their class yet.

When you mark an individual customer as no-show, you can also charge a fee to their card to deter and prevent future no-shows.

Tip No-show and cancellation fees are at risk of being charged back by the customer. To prevent chargebacks , we recommend charging a deposit before the service .

On a Phone To charge a no-show fee: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select the session name in the menu. The Attendee list will appear. Select the attendee to be changed, then select No-Show . Enter an optional message to all attendees who did not show up. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. Enter a No-Show Fee by entering the fee amount or selecting a percentage of the total ( Fee % ). Charge a no-show fee by selecting Please select a payment method . If you have Credit Card Processing , send an invoice to the customer or use a card on file. Use one of the customer's packages or membership visits to pay for the fee. If you don't have Credit Card Processing , use another payment method from the Checkout screen. Select Save & Apply when you're ready to charge the fee. If you want to notify this customer of the no-show, select Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To charge a no-show fee: Go to the Calendar . Select the class session on the calendar to open its details, then select Edit . If this is a repeat class session, select Edit only this occurrence to view the list of attendees for the class. Under the Attendee list, select Action next to an attendee and choose an option: Select No-Show . Enter an optional message to all no-show attendees. This message will be included in a text and email notification after you confirm the no-show. Enter a No-Show Fee for your customer: If you want to notify this customer of the no-show, select Notify Attendee . Select No-Show Enrollment or Charge No-Show Fee . If you charged a no-show fee, the Checkout popup will display for you to check the attendee out.

question: How do I set up a weekly pay schedule for my employees in Vagaro?
context:
[Article: Set up an Apple Developer Account | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360033330154-Set-up-an-Apple-Developer-Account]
To add your Branded App to the Apple Store so customers can download it, you must first create an Apple Developer Account , pay a $99 publisher fee (charged annually,) and invite Vagaro to join your account.

Before you begin . Submit an enrollment request . Confirm enrollment and submit payment information . Invite Vagaro to join your account . Frequently Asked Questions .

Apple’s app guidelines require that the business owner complete the signup steps and submit the required information to register for their developer program.

The Apple Developer Program requires an annual enrollment fee of $99 USD, which is not included in your Vagaro payments.

To ease the process, we recommend completing the following steps before registering for an Apple Developer Account:

Set up a publicly accessible business website with a domain name reflective of your business name. For example https://www.vagaro.com/studio123 https://www.studio123.com Set up a business email address connected to your domain name. Apple does not accept Gmail or other personal email addresses. For example: studio123@gmail.com info@studio123.com Register your business as a legal entity. See Legal Entity Guidelines for Apple Developer Accounts . Obtain a D-U-N-S number for your business. A D-U-N-S Number (short for Data Universal Numbering System) is a unique numeric identifier assigned to a business to verify the business’s identity and legal status. If your business is registered as a legal entity, you may already have a DUNS Number. To register for a D-U-N-S number: https://developer.apple.com/support/D-U-N-S/ To look up a D-U-N-S number: https://developer.apple.com/enroll/duns-lookup/#!/search Set up an Apple ID and enable 2-factor authentication: https://appleid.apple.com/account The Apple ID used when registering for the Apple Developer Program will be the same Apple ID used for billing, login, and other important communications about your developer account. For this reason, you may want to consider creating an Apple ID specifically for your business.

Go to Apple's Enrollment Page . Select Start Your Enrollment , then sign in with your Apple ID and password. Select Company/Organization in the entity type drop-down box and select Continue . Important Per Apple’s requirements, you must register as a Company/Organization. Registering as an Individual will prevent you from being able to grant Vagaro access to important account settings, meaning we will be unable to upload or manage your Branded App. Enter your legal entity name and D-U-N-S number exactly as shown on your D-U-N-S registration. If an error occurs, do not register as an individual account . Instead: Double-check that the legal entity name is entered with the exact spelling, punctuation, and capitalization as your D-U-N-S registration. Double-check that the D-U-N-S Number was entered correctly. Select the link in the error message to update your information with the D&B DUNS database, then try again in a few days. It can take up to a whole week for D&B to update the database with Apple. Complete the remaining fields that did not auto-populate based on your legal entity and D-U-N-S information. The Tax ID/National ID box is optional. Confirm that you have legal signing authority for your business. Based on your selection, Apple will contact you or a senior employee at your business to verify your enrollment. Select Continue . Review your information for accuracy, then select Submit . You will immediately get an email verifying that Apple has received your enrollment request. Apple is now reviewing your information and will send another email after they have finished verifying your enrollment.

After Apple has finished verifying your registration information, an enrollment acceptance email will be sent to the Apple ID used in the initial signup steps.

Select Review Now , then follow the prompts to complete your developer program payment.

Tip Opting in to auto-renew will keep your Developer Program registration current. Failure to renew your Developer Program registration will result in Apple removing your app from the App Store.

context:
[Article: Add a Vagaro Service | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/18977243959067-Add-a-Vagaro-Service]
When you add a Vagaro Service to your menu, you're also defining your business type with keywords in their names and descriptions. These keywords help customers find you and your services more easily in the Vagaro Marketplace, and you'll only have to update or add more details. Alternatively, you can set up custom services if you can't find a suitable Vagaro Service or want a more creative name. Sell your services by themselves, in a bundle , as a package , and in your memberships both in-house and on your Vagaro Listing Page . While managing your menu, you can search your services by entering part or all of a word to see exact and partial matches at the top of the left-side menu, then selecting the one you want to update. Alternatively, press Ctrl +K on a PC, or press Command + K on a Mac to move your cursor to the Search bar. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses . On a Phone To add a Vagaro Service to your service menu: Select More → Settings → Service/Class Menu . Select Services to view your service menu. At the top of the screen, verify that the Service/Class Menu is being created for the correct employee, select the Filters button to choose another employee, or select the shown name to change it to another employee. At the bottom of the screen, select Add , and then select Service . Select Vagaro Service . Alternatively, select Custom Service to use a different name. Scroll through the list of services, select the one you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the services' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the service's category, select the one you just added. Provide the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your service after you have completed setting up your service menu): Service Name : The name of the service. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Service because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the service offers and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Service because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Sell Price : The cost of the service if performed by that service provider. Duration : The amount of time to block off on the service provider's calendar to perform that service. Select Save . To rearrange the order of the services or change the category that they belong in, drag and drop the service's handle button into place. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To add a Vagaro Service to your service menu: Select Settings → Service/Class Menu . Your service menu will be shown by default. On the Services tab, select Add , and then select Service . Select Vagaro Service . Alternatively, select Custom Service to use a different name. Scroll through the list of services, select the one you want to add to your menu, then select Save . Notice that the services' categories were also automatically added; you can rearrange or edit them later. Under the service's category, select the one you just added. Provide the following information (you can edit these and other settings for your service after you have completed setting up your service menu): Title : The name of the service. We do not recommend changing the name of a Vagaro Service because it has been named using words that customers are most likely to search for. Description : What the service offers and what is included. We do not recommend changing the description of a Vagaro Service because it uses words that customers are most likely to search for. Select the employee(s) to perform the service. Performed By : Toggle on the switch beside the employee(s) who will perform the service. Price : How much the service costs to be performed by the service provider. The price charged must be greater than the Business Cost . Duration : The amount of time to block off on the service provider's calendar to perform that service. Select Save . Repeat steps 2 to 8 to update your other services. To rearrange the order of the services or change their categories on your Vagaro Listing Page , drag them to their new place.

question: What should I do if I want to make sure my client details stay updated without any issues?
context:
[Article: View Product History | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360007375774-View-Product-History]
Product History provides two functions: View all events related to individual products in your inventory: sales, purchases, reductions in inventory due to loss, damage, expiration, or business use. Correct data entry errors for purchases, sales, and other inventory changes. Prerequisites: This feature is available for all Vagaro businesses, and you must already have products set up in your inventory . What's in This Article? How to View Product History How to Edit Product History How to View Product History On the Phone, Pay Desk, or PayPro To view a product's history: Select More → Settings → Management . Select the product you want to view. You can filter or search through products if needed. Select the icon, then select History . You are now on the Product History screen. From this screen, you can review events that affected this product. Date : When a change was made. Status : What changed? For example, the product was Purchased , Sold , Lost , or Expired . Qty (Quantity) : How much of the stock was affected? Select the icon in the top-right corner to filter out products from history. Select a date range ( From Date and To Date ) to review history during a specific date range. Select a Type to filter through a product's history based on its status. For example, select Damaged to review how often you lose stock because of damaged products. You can also select each event to learn more. On a Computer or Tablet To view a product's history: Go to Inventory Management : Computer : Select Settings → Inventory . Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro : Select Inventory . Select the icon next to the product you want to review, then select History . You are now on the Product History screen. From this screen, you can review events that affected this product. Here are some important fields with definitions: Date : When the change was made. Status : What changed? For example, the product was Purchased , Sold , Lost , or Expired . Reason : Reason entered when the quantity was changed. Vendor : Purchase Order received from this vendor. Service Provider : Who sold the product? Modified Date : Date of change. Modified By : Who made this change? Cost : Cost (for business) to purchase this product. Selling Price : Cost (for customer) to purchase this product. Qty (Quantity) : How much of the stock was affected? You can also filter, organize, and even print/export your Product History from this screen: Enter a date range in the From Date and To Date fields to view Product History during a specific period. Expand the Type drop-down menu, then select one of the options to filter through a product's history based on its status. For example, select Damaged to review how often you lose stock because of damaged products. Export the result to an Excel spreadsheet or print a copy by selecting the Export and Print icons in the top-right corner. Note If you apply a date range or filters, select the Search button to update your report. Edit Product History Did you or an employee make a mistake while entering a change to inventory? Did you select the wrong Reason or add an incorrect note when subtracting items from your inventory? Incorrect product adjustments could affect your reports, such as the Inventory Summary report; however, you can easily fix these mistakes. On the Phone, Pay Desk, or PayPro From the Product History screen, select an event you want to update. Select Edit in the top-right corner. Make changes on the edit screen and select Submit when you're done. To cancel any changes, select the back arrow. Note What you can update depends on the Type of change. For example: Sold : You can only change the Service Provider . Refund : There is no edit option for a refund event. Changing Tax Rates : Updating the Tax Rate for a product will not affect previous sales/refunds shown in product history. On a Computer or Tablet From the Product History screen, select the Edit button next to the product you want to edit. Make any needed changes, then select Submit in the bottom-right corner. Note What you can update depends on the Type of change. For example: Sold : You can only change the Service Provider . Refund : There is no edit option for a refund event. Changing Tax Rates : Updating the Tax Rate for a product will not affect previous sales/refunds shown in product history.

[Article: Refer a Friend in the United States | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/204947034-Refer-a-Friend-in-the-United-States]
Join the Refer-a-Friend program and earn Vagaro Bucks when you refer a US-based business to Vagaro. To refer another business, send them a referral link to sign up for Vagaro. [Article: Resend or Print a Customer's Gift Card | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008229073-Resend-or-Print-a-Customer-s-Gift-Card]
When a customer loses their gift card, you have several ways to help them recover or replace it. Depending on how the gift card was sold, you can resend it to the recipient, locate it using the purchaser’s information, or transfer the remaining balance to a new physical gift card. Prerequisites : Sell a gift card to your customer. Resend a Gift Card to Its Recipient If your customer selected a recipient for their gift card, you can find it in their profile or from the Gift Card report. On a Phone To print or email a recipient's gift card: Select More → Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-right corner, select Filters . In Search Value , enter the recipient's name, then select Run Report . Select the gift card, then select View . In the top-right corner, choose an option: Print : Print a hard copy of the gift card. Email : Send an email receipt to the customer by entering one or more email addresses, separated by commas (,), then selecting Send Mail . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To print or email a recipient's gift card: Go to Customers , then select the recipient's profile. From their profile, go to the Gift Cards tab, find their gift card, and then select Action → View . In the Gift Card Receipt , choose an option: Print : Print a hard copy of the gift card. Send Email : Send an email receipt to the customer by entering one or more email addresses, separated by commas (,), then selecting Send Email . Resend Gift Cards Without a Recipient If a gift card does not have a recipient assigned, you can find it by searching for the purchaser in the Gift Card Report. On a Phone To print or email a recipient's gift card: Select More → Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-right corner, select Filters . Next to Search Filters , select Any and choose From in the list. Enter the purchaser's name in Search Value , then select Run Report . Select the gift card, then select View . In the top-right corner, choose an option: Print : Print a hard copy of the gift card. Email : Send an email receipt to the customer by entering one or more email addresses, separated by commas (,), then selecting Send Mail . On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To print or email a recipient's gift card: Select Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the Search Fields list, select From , then enter the purchaser's name in Search Value . Select Run Report . Find the gift card, then select Action → View next to it. In the Gift Card Receipt , choose an option: Print : Print a hard copy of the gift card. Send Email : Send an email receipt to the customer by entering one or more email addresses, separated by commas (,), then selecting Send Email . Transfer a Balance to a New Physical Gift Card If your customer loses their physical gift card and would like a new one, you can transfer the balance over to a new gift card and void the old balance. On a Phone To transfer a balance: Select More → Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-right corner, select Action → New Gift Card . Enter the Gift Card Number Enter the gift card's Amount and choose a Template Type . Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, and when you've finished creating your gift card, select Next . A preview of the digital gift card will appear. If no additional changes are needed, select Add Gift Card . The customer's new card has been loaded. To prevent the customer from using the old gift card, you can void it in the same Gift Card report. On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To transfer a balance: Select Reports → Gift Cards . The report displays all gift cards with outstanding balances. In the top-left corner of the table, select New Gift Card . Enter or scan the Gift Card Number . Enter the gift card's Amount and choose a Template Type . Optionally, choose the Recipient . If this person isn't on your customer list, you can leave this option blank or create a new profile for them. Fill in the other optional gift card fields, and when you've finished creating your gift card, select Next . A preview of the digital gift card will appear. If no additional changes are needed, select Add Gift Card . The customer's new card has been loaded. To prevent the customer from using the old gift card, you can void it in the same Gift Card report.

[Article: Check Out Customers with Affirm | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/10422553055899-Check-Out-Customers-with-Affirm]
Once Affirm is set up on Vagaro , you can let customers pay for services over time when you're checking out their services. Just send a secure payment link to your customer's phone or email, or they can scan a QR code shown on-screen. They'll sign in or up, pick their payment plan, and complete payment. Processing Fee : A 6% + $0.30 fee is applied per Affirm transaction. Affirm Customer Support : Customers can obtain assistance directly from Affirm at affirm.com/help . Transaction Limits : Customers can use Affirm for purchases ranging from $50 to $30,000. Affirm finances up to $20,000; for purchases exceeding this, customers may make a down payment of up to $10,000 to cover the difference. Deposit Time : Funds from Affirm transactions follow your regular batch schedule and are typically deposited within one business day. Disclaimer Payment options through Affirm are subject to an eligibility check, may not be available everywhere, and are provided by these lending partners: affirm.com/lenders. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Optionally, to split the transaction (e.g., Affirm + cash or gift card), enter the other payment method first. Note You can't split a transaction between a credit card and Affirm. Enter the amount to be paid with Affirm . Select Checkout . You'll then see the option to send a link via text/email or display a QR code. Either send the payment link to the customer's email and/or phone number, or have them scan the QR code shown on screen. It may take a few minutes for them to sign up or log in. A Successful Checkout message will appear upon completion of payment by the customer. If the customer is not approved, direct them to the Buy Now, Pay Later FAQ and use another payment method. On a Computer To accept payments with Affirm: Go to the Checkout screen, or check out the customer directly from the Calendar. Select a customer and add items to their shopping cart. For existing appointments or classes, the customer is already selected and ready for checkout. Optionally, to split the transaction (e.g., Affirm + cash or gift card), enter the other payment method first. Note You can't split a transaction between a credit card and Affirm. Enter the amount to be paid with Affirm . Select Checkout . You'll then see the option to send a link via text/email or display a QR code. Either send the payment link to the customer's email and/or phone number, or have them scan the QR code shown on screen. It may take a few minutes for them to sign up or log in. A Successful Checkout message will appear upon completion of payment by the customer. If the customer is not approved, direct them to the Buy Now, Pay Later FAQ and use another payment method.

[Article: Manage Gap Processing Time During a Service | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/360010299394-Manage-Gap-Processing-Time-During-a-Service]
When you book a service, its gap time can be edited on the Calendar after it's been scheduled. For example, you may want to adjust gap time if a client requests beforehand to get extra time to relax during a facial after a mask has been applied. Changing an appointment's gap time will not affect the default cleanup time for your other services or those in your other customers' purchased bundles.

Note Gap time cannot be edited on the Calendar unless it was set up in the service's service's duration in the Service/Class Menu screen.

On a Phone To edit an appointment's gap time from the Calendar: On the Calendar, select the appointment with gap time, then select Edit . If the appointment repeats , choose to Edit this Occurrence or Edit the Series . Scroll down to Duration , then select the time. Select Gap (Processing) , then use the spinners to update the amount of time, then select Done . Optionally, you can also update the Initial Duration and Finish Duration if needed. Select Apply . Update any other details, then select Save .

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To edit an appointment's gap time from the Calendar: On the Calendar, select the appointment with gap time, then select Edit . If the appointment repeats , choose to Edit this Occurrence or Edit the Series , then select Edit Booking . Select the box under Duration to update the appointment's allotted time on the calendar. In the Service Duration popup, adjust the Gap (processing) time in five-minute increments using the ̶ and + buttons, then select Apply . Optionally, you can also update the Initial Duration and Finish Duration if needed. Update any other details, then select Save .

Booking an appointment during another service's gap time is performed the same way as booking any other service by selecting an available timeslot on an employee's calendar.

On a Phone To book an appointment during another service's gap time: On the Calendar, select an available timeslot next to the timeslots for the other service's gap time. Tip Turn your phone sideways to landscape orientation for more space to select an available timeslot during a service's gap time. Schedule the appointment . If the appointment is longer than the allotted gap time, a popup alerts you that another booking is already scheduled for that timeslot. Select whether to Continue to Book or select Cancel to adjust the length of the service.

On a Computer, Tablet, Pay Desk, or PayPro To book an appointment during another service's gap time: On the Calendar, select an available timeslot next to the timeslots for the other service's gap time. Schedule the appointment . If the appointment is longer than the allotted gap time, a popup alerts you that another booking is already scheduled for that timeslot. Select whether to Continue to Book or select Cancel to adjust the length of the service.

[Article: Find Daily Deals on the Marketplace - for Customers of a Vagaro Business | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/115003661514-Find-Daily-Deals-on-the-Marketplace-for-Customers-of-a-Vagaro-Business]
Daily Deals are promotions for services, classes, memberships, packages, and gift cards offered by businesses near you. You can regularly find Daily Deals on the Vagaro Marketplace, which are showcased at the top of the screen if your current location is within 80 miles of the business. You can redeem deals using the Vagaro app , on Vagaro.com , or by visiting the business in person. Note Some Daily Deals are targeted at customers who are new to a business. If you are already a customer, you may see a warning message letting you know that the deal doesn't apply to you. If you continue, you can book the service/class, etc., but the Daily Deal discount will not be applied. Booking a Daily Deal will apply the discount at checkout. If the business doesn't require you to pay upfront online, the discount will be applied when you visit the business. Prerequisites : To book an appointment or class, you'll need to sign in to your Vagaro customer account . If you haven't created a Vagaro account but previously booked with a business, you can contact them to send your login information, or you can create a new profile with the business. Look for Deals on the Marketplace You can find all available Daily Deals in your area on the Vagaro Marketplace, where you can find new businesses while getting a good deal at the same time. Vagaro App To find a Daily Deal: Log into your Vagaro account on the Vagaro app . At the bottom of the screen, select the Home icon to go to the Vagaro Marketplace. Scroll down to Daily Deals , then swipe left to browse nearby Daily Deals , or select See all . If you choose to See all deals, you can: Select the Sort list in the top-right corner to view the Nearest deals to your location, or only deals that are Ending Soon . Select Filter at the bottom of the screen to reduce the businesses shown to those with specific types of services, amenities, languages spoken, accepted payment types, and businesses that offer mobile service appointments. Select Apply Filters to update the list of deals. Select Map at the bottom of the screen to view a map of all deals in your area. Select the deal you want. Web Version To find a Daily Deal: Log into your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . In the top-right corner, select Daily Deals to view the available Daily Deals in your area. Narrow down your search for deals: Select the Sort list to view the Nearest deals to you or only deals that are Ending Soon . Select Filters to filter by types of services, amenities, languages spoken, the types of payments accepted, and businesses that offer mobile service appointments. Select Apply Filters to update the list of deals. Find and select the Daily Deal you want. View a Specific Business's Deals You can also see the deals a specific business is currently offering by going to their Vagaro Listing Page. Vagaro App To view a business's deal: Log into your Vagaro account on the Vagaro app . Go to the business you want to view . Scroll down to the Daily Deals section and choose the deal you want. Web Version To view a business's deal: Log into your Vagaro account on Vagaro.com . Go to the business you want to view . Scroll down to the Daily Deals section and choose the deal you want.

context:
[Article: Vagaro Receipt Printer Troubleshooting | URL: https://support.vagaro.com/hc/en-us/articles/8842193251099-Vagaro-Receipt-Printer-Troubleshooting]
If you are having issues setting up your Vagaro Receipt Printer , follow the troubleshooting tips below for both the printer and your device. Printer Troubleshooting Printer Lights Blue light under cloud icon: printer is connected to a network Green light under power icon: printer is turned on Red light under caution icon: printer is out of paper, blocked with paper, or experiencing voltage issues. If the red light is flashing, your printer is overheating and must be turned off to cool. Flashing white light: The receipt is ready to take. Solid white light: Paper is running low. Before troubleshooting on your smartphone or computer, make sure you follow the steps below. Factory Reset Printer Turn the printer off, then turn it back on. Your device is fully powered on when you hear "Welcome to Intelligent Cloud Printing." Hold the Config Key (button next to the Ethernet port) for 10 seconds until you hear a voice command, "Press the Feed button to initiate a restart." Release the button and press the Paper Feed one. The printer will “beep” again and reboot, which resets all network configurations. Important It may take up to five minutes to finish the reset. After troubleshooting, remove and reconnect your printer from Vagaro Pro or Vagaro.com. Fix Printing Issues Press the Paper Feed button to see if the feed is working properly. Restart your printer by turning it off and on. Open the paper and try and reinsert the receipt paper. The receipt paper should roll under. Try another connection method, like USB or Bluetooth, to see if the issue might be your method of connection. Check if the Printer is Connected to Your Wi-Fi Network : Quickly double-press the Config Key to print the network settings page. Under [Network Test], check the network name after Link => SSID: (name). Make sure that the network names match the Wi-FI network you are currently on. If it doesn't, follow the steps in Factory Reset the Printer. If Current Network shows FAIL , try moving your device closer to your router or follow the steps in Factory Reset the Printer. Important After troubleshooting, remove and reconnect your printer from Vagaro Pro or Vagaro.com. Windows Troubleshooting Troubleshooting tips for Windows 11 devices. USB : Select the Windows icon. Search for Control Panel . Select Hardware & Sound . Find USB Printing Support in the USB container. If you do not see this device, there may be an issue with your USB cable. Select Update Drivers if the option is available. Wi-Fi : Make sure your Wi-Fi settings are enabled. Select the Windows icon. Search "Settings" and select Settings . Select Network & internet . Toggle Wi-Fi on and select Wi-Fi . Select Manage known networks . Select Forget next to your Wi-Fi network. Reconnect to your Wi-Fi. Mac Troubleshooting Troubleshooting tips for Mac Devices. Wi-Fi : Restart your computer. Restart your router. On your Mac, select the Apple icon and select System Preferences . Select Network . Select Wi-Fi . Select Advanced . Select your Wi-Fi network. Select the - button, select Remove , and then select Ok to confirm. Select Apply to save changes. IOS Troubleshooting Bluetooth : Go to Settings → Bluetooth . Toggle Bluetooth on. If your printer is already paired, select the (I) icon next to the device in My Devices and select Forget this device . Under Other Devices , select the Vagaro receipt printer (CloudPrint_xxxx) to pair. Wi-Fi : Restart your mobile device. Restart your router. On your device, navigate to Settings → Wi-Fi . Select the (I) icon next to your network. Select Forget This Network . Reconnect to your Wi-Fi network. Android Troubleshooting Troubleshooting tips for Android devices. Bluetooth : Make sure Bluetooth is turned on. Go to Settings → Connection preferences → Bluetooth . Toggle Bluetooth on. Distance matters! Try moving closer to the Vagaro receipt printer. The printer might not detect your device if you are far away. Pair with your device manually. Go to Settings → Connected devices → Saved Devices → See all . If you have already paired to the Vagaro receipt printer (CloudPrint_xxxx), select the gear icon and select Forget . Return to the Connected Devices screen. Select Pair new device and select the receipt printer. Pair with the printer. Wi-Fi : Restart your mobile device. Restart your router. On your device, navigate to Settings → Network & Internet → Wi-Fi . Toggle Wi-Fi on. Select your network. Select Forget Network . Return to Wi-Fi and reconnect to your network.
ground_truth: I don't have enough information to answer that confidently.
